There is an apocryphal story that the first time the Guinness family heard of this work occurred when Gosset died suddenly of a heart attack in 1937 and his mathematical friends approached the Guinness company to help pay the costs of printing his collected papers in a single volume. Whether this is true or not, it is clear from the memoirs of the American statistician Harold Hotelling, who in the late 1930s wanted to talk with “Student,” that arrangements were made to meet him secretly, with all the aspects of a spy mystery. This suggests that the true identity of “Student” was still a secret from the Guinness company. The papers by “Student” that were printed in Biometrika lay on the cusp of theory and application, as Gosset moved from highly practical problems into difficult mathematical formulations and back out into practical reality with solutions that others would follow.

尽管成就斐然，戈塞特却为人谦逊低调。在他的信件中，我们经常会看到诸如“我自己的研究只能提供对这件事的一个粗略了解”之类的表述，或者抱怨自己因某些发现而获得的赞誉过高，而“费舍尔实际上推导出了完整的数学公式……”之类的言论。人们缅怀戈塞特其人，称他是一位和蔼体贴、善解人意的同事，能够体察他人的情感困境。他于六十一岁去世，身后留下妻子玛乔丽（她是一位精力充沛的运动员，曾担任英国女子曲棍球队队长）、一个儿子、两个女儿和一个孙子。他的父母在他去世时仍然健在。

In spite of his great achievements, Gosset was an unassuming man. In his letters, one frequently finds expressions like “my own investigations [provide] only a rough idea of the thing,” or protestations that he was given too much credit for some discovery when “Fisher really worked out the complete mathematics … .” Gosset, the man, is remembered as a kind and thoughtful colleague who was sensitive to the emotional problems of others. When he died at the age of sixty-one, he left his wife, Marjory (who was a vigorous athlete, having been captain of the English Ladies Hockey Team), one son, two daughters, and a grandson. His parents were still alive at the time of his death.

学生的T检验

“STUDENT”’S T-TEST

即便没有其他贡献，所有科学家都应该感谢戈塞特，因为他发表了一篇题为《均值的可能误差》的短文，该文于1908年发表在《生物统计学》 （Biometrika）杂志上。正是罗纳德·艾尔默·费舍尔指出了这篇杰出论文的普遍意义。对戈塞特而言，他当时面对的是一个具体的问题，于是他利用晚上时间在家中，以一贯的耐心和细致来攻克它。找到解决方案后，他会将其与其他数据进行比对，重新审视结果，试图确定是否遗漏了任何细微的差别，考虑自己必须做出哪些假设，并反复计算他的发现。他预见了现代计算机蒙特卡罗模拟技术，即通过反复模拟数学模型来确定与其相关的概率分布。然而，当时他并没有计算机。他只能一丝不苟地手工计算，从数百个样本中取平均值，并绘制出相应的频率分布图。

If nothing else, all scientists are indebted to Gosset for a short paper entitled “The Probable Error of the Mean,” which appeared in Biometrika in 1908. It was Ronald Aylmer Fisher who pointed out the general implications of this remarkable paper. To Gosset, there was a specific problem to be solved, which he attacked in the evenings in his home with his usual patience and carefulness. Having discovered a solution, he checked it against other data, reexamined his results, tried to determine if he had missed any subtle differences, considered what assumptions he had to make, and calculated and recalculated his findings. He anticipated modern computer Monte Carlo techniques, where a mathematical model is simulated over and over again to determine the probability distributions associated with it. However, he had no computer. He painstakingly added up numbers, taking averages from hundreds of examples and plotting the resulting frequencies—all by hand.

戈塞特着手解决的具体问题是小样本问题。卡尔·皮尔逊通过对单一分布进行数千次测量，计算出了该分布的四个参数。他认为，由于使用了大样本，因此得到的参数估计值是正确的。费舍尔后来证明他错了。在戈塞特的经验中，科学家很少能拥有如此大的样本量。更常见的情况是，一项实验只能产生十到二十个观测值。此外，他还意识到这种情况在整个科学领域都非常普遍。在他给皮尔逊的一封信中，他写道：“如果你遇到的唯一一个使用过小样本进行研究的人，那你真是太特别了。正是在这个问题上，我开始与斯特拉顿（剑桥大学的一位研究员，他曾以一个只有4个样本的例子来说明问题！）进行交流。”

The specific problem Gosset attacked dealt with small samples. Karl Pearson had computed the four parameters of a distribution by accumulating thousands of measurements from a single distribution. He assumed that the resulting estimates of the parameters were correct because of the large samples he used. Fisher was to prove him wrong. In Gosset’s experience, the scientist seldom had the luxury of such large samples. More typical was an experiment yielding ten to twenty observations. Furthermore, he recognized this as very common in all of science. In one of his letters to Pearson, he writes: “If I am the only person that you’ve come across that works with too small samples, you are very singular. It was on this subject that I came to have dealings with Stratton [a fellow at Cambridge University, where] … he had taken as an illustration a sample of 4!”

皮尔逊的所有工作都基于一个假设：数据样本非常大，因此可以无误差地确定参数。戈塞特问道：如果样本很小怎么办？我们该如何处理计算中必然存在的随机误差？

All of Pearson’s work assumed that the sample of data was so large that the parameters could be determined without error. Gosset asked, what happens if the sample were small? How can we deal with the random error that is bound to find its way into our calculations?

戈塞特晚上坐在厨房的桌子旁，收集一小组数据，计算平均值和估计的标准差，然后将它们相除，最后在坐标纸上绘制结果。他找到了与这个比率相关的四个参数，并将其与皮尔逊偏态分布族中的一种进行匹配。他的重大发现是，你不需要知道原始分布中所有四个参数的精确值。前两个参数估计值的比率具有可以制表的概率分布。数据来源或标准差的真实值是多少都无关紧要。这两个样本估计值的比率就能让你得到一个已知的分布。

Gosset sat at his kitchen table at night, taking small sets of numbers, finding the average and the estimated standard deviation, dividing one by the other, and plotting the results on graph paper. He found the four parameters associated with this ratio and matched it to one of Pearson’s family of skew distributions. His great discovery was that you did not have to know the exact values of all four parameters of the original distribution. The ratios of the estimated values of the first two parameters had a probability distribution that could be tabulated. It did not matter where the data came from or what the true value of the standard deviation was. Taking the ratio of these two sample estimates brought you into a known distribution.

正如弗雷德里克·莫斯特勒和约翰·图基所指出的，如果没有这项发现，统计分析注定要陷入无限循环的困境。如果没有后来被称为“学生”回归[4]的这项发现，分析人员就必须先估计观测数据的四个参数，然后估计这四个参数估计值的四个参数，再估计每个估计值的四个参数，如此反复，永远无法得出最终结果。戈塞特证明，分析人员可以在第一步就停止计算。

As Frederick Mosteller and John Tukey point out, without this discovery, statistical analysis was doomed to use an infinite regression of procedures. Without “Student”’s t,[4] as the discovery has come to be called, the analyst would have to estimate the four parameters of the observed data, then estimate the four parameters of the estimates of the four parameters, then estimate the four parameters of each of those, and so on, with no chance of ever reaching a final calculation. Gosset showed that the analyst could stop at the first step.

戈塞特的研究存在一个基本假设：初始测量数据服从正态分布。多年来，随着科学家们对“学生t检验”的应用，许多人开始认为这个假设并非必要。他们发现，无论初始测量数据是否服从正态分布，“学生t检验”的分布都相同。1967年，斯坦福大学的布拉德利·埃夫隆证明了这一点。更准确地说，他找到了无需进行该假设的一般条件。

There was a fundamental assumption in Gosset’s work. He assumed that the initial set of measurements had a normal distribution. Over the years, as scientists used “Student”’s t, many came to believe that this assumption was not needed. They often found that “Student”’s t had the same distribution regardless of whether the initial measurements were normally distributed or not. In 1967, Bradley Efron of Stanford University proved that this was true. To be more exact, he found the general conditions under which the assumption was not needed.

随着“学生”统计量模型的发展，我们正逐渐滑入统计分布理论的应用领域。这种应用在科学界十分普遍，但却存在着深刻的哲学问题。这就是所谓的“假设检验”或“显著性检验”。我们将在后续章节中对此进行探讨。目前，我们只需指出，“学生”统计量模型提供了一种几乎人人都在使用的科学工具——即便真正理解它的人寥寥无几。

With the development of “Student”’s t, we are sliding into a use of statistical distribution theory that is widespread in the sciences but with which there are deep philosophical problems. This is the use of what are called “hypothesis tests,” or “significance tests.” We shall explore this in a later chapter. For the moment, we only note that “Student” provided a scientific tool that almost everyone uses—even if few really understand it.

与此同时，“亲爱的戈塞特先生”成了两位才华横溢却又水火不容的天才——卡尔·皮尔逊和罗纳德·艾尔默·费舍尔——之间的中间人。他与两人都保持着密切的友谊，尽管他经常向皮尔逊抱怨自己看不懂费舍尔写给他的东西。他与费舍尔的友谊始于后者还在剑桥大学读本科的时候。1912年，费舍尔的天文学导师[5]将他们介绍认识，当时费舍尔刚刚获得剑桥大学数学最高荣誉——数学竞赛优等生（wrangler）。他当时正在研究一个天文学问题，并撰写了一篇论文，在论文中他重新发现了“学生”戈塞特1908年的研究成果——年轻的费舍尔显然对戈塞特之前的工作一无所知。

In the meantime, “dear Mr. Gosset” became the middleman between two towering and feuding geniuses, Karl Pearson and Ronald Aylmer Fisher. He maintained a close friendship with both, although he often complained to Pearson that he did not understand what Fisher had written him. His friendship with Fisher began while the latter was still an undergraduate at Cambridge University. Fisher’s tutor[5] in astronomy introduced them in 1912, when Fisher had just become a wrangler (the highest mathematical honor) at Cambridge. He was looking at a problem in astronomy and wrote a paper in which he rediscovered “Student”’s 1908 results—young Fisher was obviously unaware of Gosset’s previous work.

在费舍尔给戈塞特看的那篇论文中，有一个小错误，被戈塞特发现了。他回到家后，发现费舍尔给他留了两页详细的数学推导。这位年轻人重新完成了原稿，并加以扩展，还指出了戈塞特犯的一个错误。戈塞特写信给皮尔逊说：“我附上了一封信，其中证明了我关于学生人数频率分布的公式……。您能否帮我看一下？我对三维以上的空间不太熟悉，即使我能理解……”费舍尔用多维几何证明了戈塞特的结论。

In this paper, which Fisher showed Gosset, there was a small error, which Gosset caught. When he returned home, he found waiting for him two pages of detailed mathematics from Fisher. The young man had redone the original work, expanded upon it, and identified an error that Gosset had made. Gosset wrote to Pearson: “I am enclosing a letter which gives a proof of my formulae for the frequency distribution of [”Student“’s t] … . Would you mind looking at it for me; I don’t feel at home in more than three dimensions, even if I could understand it otherwise … .” Fisher had proved Gosset’s results using multidimensional geometry.

在信中，戈塞特解释了他如何前往剑桥与一位朋友见面，这位朋友同时也是费舍尔在冈维尔和凯斯学院的导师，以及他如何结识了这位22岁的学生。他接着写道：“这位费舍尔先生发表了一篇论文，提出了‘一种新的概率准则’之类的东西。这是一种简洁明了，但据我理解，却完全不切实际且毫无用处的看待问题的方式。”

In the letter, Gosset explains how he went to Cambridge to meet with a friend who was also Fisher’s tutor at Gonville and Caius College and how he was introduced to the 22-year-old student. He goes on: “This chap Fisher produced a paper giving ’a new criterion of probability’ or something of the sort. A neat but as far as I could understand it, quite unpractical and unserviceable way of looking at things.”

在描述了他在剑桥与费舍尔的讨论之后，戈塞特写道：

After describing his discussion with Fisher at Cambridge, Gosset writes:

他回复了我两页纸，上面密密麻麻地写满了晦涩难懂的数学公式，他在其中证明了[后面跟着一组数学公式]……。我完全看不懂，就写信说等有时间再研究。我竟然真的把它带到湖区去了——结果弄丢了！

现在他把这个发给我了。我觉得，如果可以的话，或许你可以把证明过程写在便条里。它写得非常优美，也很有数学性，可能会吸引一些人……

To this he replied with two foolscap pages covered with mathematics of the deepest dye in which he proved [this is followed by a group of mathematical formulas] … . I couldn’t understand the stuff and wrote and said I was going to study it when I had time. I actually took it up to the Lakes with me—and lost it!

Now he sends this to me. It seemed to me that if it’s all right perhaps you might like to put the proof in a note. It’s so nice and mathematical that it might appeal to some people … .

就这样，二十世纪一位伟大的天才横空出世。皮尔逊在《生物统计学》（Biometrika）杂志上发表了这位年轻人的论文。三年后，在收到一系列居高临下的信件后，皮尔逊发表了费舍尔的第二篇论文，但前提是确保这篇论文会被视为对其一位同事工作的微不足道的补充。此后，皮尔逊再也没有允许费舍尔的论文发表在他的期刊上。费舍尔后来在皮尔逊引以为傲的许多成就中都发现了错误，而皮尔逊在《生物统计学》后来的社论中也经常提及“费舍尔先生”或“费舍尔先生的学生”在其他期刊论文中犯下的错误。所有这些都将在下一章中详细讨论。戈塞特将在接下来的章节中再次出现。作为一位和蔼可亲的导师，他为年轻一代进入统计分布的新世界做出了重要贡献，他的许多学生和同事都为这一新兴数学领域做出了重大贡献。尽管戈塞特本人谦逊地表示反对，但他本人也做出了许多影响深远的贡献。

Thus did one of the great geniuses of the twentieth century burst upon the scene. Pearson published the young man’s note in Biometrika. Three years later, after a sequence of very condescending letters, Pearson published a second paper by Fisher, but only after making sure that it would be seen as a minor addition to work done by one of Pearson’s coworkers. Pearson never allowed another paper by Fisher into his journal. Fisher went on to find errors in many of Pearson’s proudest accomplishments, while Pearson’s editorials in later issues of Biometrika often refer to errors made by “Mr. Fisher” or by “a student of Mr. Fisher” in papers in other journals. All of this is grist for the next chapter. Gosset will appear again in some of the succeeding chapters. As a genial mentor, he was instrumental in introducing younger men and women to the new world of statistical distribution, and many of his students and coworkers were responsible for major contributions to the new mathematics. Gosset, in spite of his modest protestations, made many long-lasting contributions of his own.

C章第四章

CHAPTER 4

翻动粪堆

RAKING OVER THE MUCK HEAP

拉1919年春天，29岁的唐纳德·艾尔默·费舍尔带着妻子、三个孩子和妻妹搬进了位于伦敦北部罗瑟姆斯特德农业实验站附近的一座老农舍。从很多方面来看，他的人生都算不上成功。他从小体弱多病，孤僻寂寞，而且视力严重受损。为了保护他的近视眼，医生禁止他使用人造光源阅读。他很早就对数学和天文学产生了浓厚的兴趣。六岁时，他就对天文学着迷。七八岁时，他就开始聆听著名天文学家罗伯特·鲍尔爵士的科普讲座。

Ronald Aylmer Fisher was twenty-nine years old when he moved with his wife, three children, and sister-in-law into an old farmhouse near the Rothamsted Agricultural Experimental Station, north of London, in the spring of 1919. By many measures, he could have been considered a failure in life. He had grown up as a sickly and lonely child with severe vision impairment. To protect his nearsighted eyes, the doctors had forbidden him to read by artificial light. He had taken early to mathematics and astronomy. He was fascinated with astronomy at age six. At ages seven and eight, he was attending popular lectures given by the famous astronomer Sir Robert Ball.

费舍尔进入著名的哈罗公学就读[6]，并在那里展现出卓越的数学天赋。由于学校不允许使用电灯，他的数学老师只能在晚上给他上课，而且不使用铅笔、纸或其他任何辅助工具。因此，费舍尔培养了深刻的几何意识。在后来的岁月里，他非凡的几何洞察力使他能够解决许多数理统计方面的难题。这些洞察力对他来说如此显而易见，以至于他常常无法让别人理解。其他数学家可能要花费数月甚至数年的时间才能证明一些费舍尔认为显而易见的事情。

Fisher matriculated at Harrow, the renowned public school[6] where he excelled in mathematics. Because he was not allowed to use electric light, his mathematics tutor would teach him in the evening without the use of pencil, paper, or any other visual aids. As a result, Fisher developed a deep geometric sense. In future years, his unusual geometric insights enabled him to solve many difficult problems in mathematical statistics. The insights were so obvious to him that he often failed to make them understandable to others. Other mathematicians would spend months or even years trying to prove something that Fisher claimed was obvious.

他于1909年进入剑桥大学，并于1912年荣获剑桥大学数学荣誉毕业生（Wrangler）的称号。剑桥大学的学生只有通过一系列极其困难的数学考试（包括口试和笔试）才能获得这一称号。每年只有一到两名学生能够获此殊荣，有时甚至没有学生获此殊荣。费舍尔在本科期间发表了他的第一篇科学论文，文中他用多维几何空间来解释复杂的迭代公式。在这篇论文中，他证明了此前极其复杂的计算方法实际上是这种几何学的简单推论。毕业后，他留校一年学习统计力学和量子理论。到1913年，统计革命已经渗透到物理学领域，而这两个领域的新思想已经足够成熟，可以开设正式的课程。

He entered Cambridge in 1909, rising to the prestigious title of wrangler in 1912. A student at Cambridge becomes a wrangler by passing a series of extremely difficult mathematics exams, both oral and written. This was something accomplished by no more than one or two classmen a year, and some years there were no wranglers. While still an undergraduate, Fisher published his first scientific paper, where complicated iterative formulas are interpreted in terms of multidimensional geometric space. In this paper, what had hitherto been an exceedingly complicated method of computation is shown to be a simple consequence of that geometry. He stayed for a year after graduation to study statistical mechanics and quantum theory. By 1913, the statistical revolution had entered physics, and these were two areas where the new ideas were sufficiently well formulated to produce formal course work.

费舍尔的第一份工作是在一家投资公司的统计部门，但他突然辞职去加拿大务农；第一次世界大战爆发时，他又突然辞职返回英国。尽管他符合入伍条件，但由于视力不佳，他未能服役。战争期间，他辗转于多所公立学校教授数学，但每次的经历都比前一次糟糕。对于那些在他看来显而易见却又难以理解的学生，他总是很不耐烦。

Fisher’s first job was in the statistical office of an investment company, which he left suddenly to do farmwork in Canada; again, he left his work suddenly to return to England at the beginning of the First World War. Although he qualified for a commission in the army, his poor eyesight kept him out of military service. He spent the war years teaching mathematics in a series of public schools, each experience worse than the previous one. He was short-tempered with students who could not understand what was to him obvious.

费舍尔诉卡尔·皮尔逊案

FISHER VERSUS KARL PEARSON

如前一章所述，费舍尔还在读本科时，就在《生物统计学》（Biometrika）杂志上发表了一篇论文。因此，他结识了卡尔·皮尔逊，皮尔逊向他介绍了确定高尔顿相关系数统计分布这一难题。费舍尔仔细思考了这个问题，将其转化为几何形式，并在一周内得到了完整的解答。他将解答提交给皮尔逊，希望在《生物统计学》杂志上发表；然而，皮尔逊无法理解其中的数学原理，于是将论文转交给威廉·西利·戈塞特，戈塞特也同样难以理解。皮尔逊知道如何针对特定情况获得该问题的部分解。他的方法涉及大量的计算，于是他安排生物统计实验室的工作人员计算这些特定情况下的解。在所有情况下，他们的计算结果都与费舍尔的更一般的解一致。尽管如此，皮尔逊仍然没有发表费舍尔的论文。他敦促费舍尔修改论文，降低其普遍性。皮尔逊拖延了费舍尔一年多，同时让他的助手（“计算员”）计算出一张包含参数选定值分布的大型表格。最后，他发表了费舍尔的研究成果，但只是作为一篇篇幅更长的论文的脚注，而这些表格正是他和一位助手在这篇论文中展示的。结果是，对于普通读者而言，费舍尔的数学推导仅仅是皮尔逊及其同事所做的更为重要、更为庞大的计算工作的附录。

While still an undergraduate, Fisher had a note published in Biometrika, as mentioned in the previous chapter. As a result, Fisher met Karl Pearson, who introduced him to the difficult problem of determining the statistical distribution of Galton’s correlation coefficient. Fisher thought about the problem, cast it into a geometric formulation, and within a week had a complete answer. He submitted it to Pearson for publication in Biometrika; Pearson could not understand the mathematics and sent the paper to William Sealy Gosset, who also had difficulty understanding it. Pearson knew how to get partial solutions to the problem for specific cases. His method involved monumental amounts of calculation, and he set the workers in his biometrical laboratory to computing those specific answers. In every case, they agreed with Fisher’s more general solution. Still, Pearson did not publish Fisher’s paper. He urged Fisher to make changes and to reduce the generality of the work. Pearson held Fisher off for over a year, while he had his assistants (the “calculators”) computing a large, extensive table of the distribution for selected values of the parameters. Finally, he published Fisher’s work, but as a footnote to a larger paper in which he and one of his assistants displayed these tables. The result was that, to the casual reader, Fisher’s mathematical manipulations were a mere appendix to the more important and massive computational work done by Pearson and his coworkers.

尽管《生物统计学》（Biometrika）是该领域首屈一指的期刊，费舍尔却再也没有在该期刊上发表过论文。此后几年，他的论文陆续发表在《农业科学杂志》（Journal of Agricultural Science）、《皇家气象学会季刊》（The Quarterly Journal of the Royal Meteorological Society）、《爱丁堡皇家学会会刊》（The Proceedings of the Royal Society of Edinburgh）和《心理研究学会会刊》（Proceedings of the Society of Psychical Research）上。这些期刊通常与数学研究并无关联。据一些认识费舍尔的人说，费舍尔之所以做出这样的选择，是因为皮尔逊和他的朋友们实际上将他排除在了数学和统计学研究的主流之外。另一些人则认为，费舍尔本人对皮尔逊的傲慢态度以及他未能将类似的论文发表在《皇家统计学会杂志》（Journal of the Royal Statistical Society，该领域另一份享有盛誉的期刊）上感到不满；因此，他转而投稿到其他期刊，有时甚至付费让期刊刊登他的论文。

Fisher never published another paper in Biometrika, although this was the preeminent journal in the field. In the following years, his papers appeared in the Journal of Agricultural Science, The Quarterly Journal of the Royal Meteorological Society, The Proceedings of the Royal Society of Edinburgh, and the Proceedings of the Society of Psychical Research. All of these are journals that one does not normally associate with mathematical research. According to some who knew Fisher, these choices were made because Pearson and his friends effectively froze Fisher out of the mainstream of mathematical and statistical research. According to others, Fisher himself felt rebuffed by Pearson’s cavalier attitude and by his failure to get a similar paper published in the Journal of the Royal Statistical Society (the other prestigious journal in the field); and he proceeded to use other journals, sometimes paying the journal to have his paper appear in it.

费舍尔是“法西斯分子”

FISHER THE “FASCIST”

R.A.费舍尔的一些早期论文数学性很强。他关于相关系数的论文（最终由皮尔逊发表）充斥着大量的数学符号，通常一页纸上至少有一半都是数学公式。当然，也有一些论文完全没有出现数学。其中一篇探讨了达尔文的随机适应理论如何足以解释最复杂的解剖结构。另一篇则推测了性取向的演化。他加入了优生学运动，并于1917年在《优生学评论》上发表了一篇社论，呼吁制定一项协调一致的国家政策，“提高专业人士和高技能工匠的生育率”，并抑制下层阶级的生育。他在文中指出，政府为穷人提供的福利政策鼓励他们生育并将基因传递给下一代，而中产阶级对经济保障的担忧则导致他们推迟结婚并减少家庭规模。费舍尔担心，最终结果是国家会为后代选择“最差”的基因，而淘汰“更好”的基因。优生学，即通过选择性育种来改良人类基因的运动，将主导费舍尔的大部分政治观点。二战期间，他被诬陷为法西斯分子，并被排挤出所有与战争相关的工作。

Some of these early papers by R. A. Fisher are highly mathematical. The paper on the correlation coefficient, which Pearson finally published, is dense with mathematical notation. A typical page is half or more filled with mathematical formulas. There are also papers in which no mathematics appear at all. In one of them, he discusses the ways in which Darwin’s theory of random adaptation is adequate to account for the most sophisticated anatomical structures. In another, he speculates on the evolution of sexual preference. He joined the eugenics movement and, in 1917, published an editorial in the Eugenics Review, calling for a concerted national policy “to increase the birth-rate in the professional classes and among highly skilled artisans” and to discourage births among the lower classes. He argued in this paper that governmental policies that provided welfare for the poor encouraged them to procreate and pass on their genes to the next generation, whereas the concerns of the middle class for economic security led to postponement of marriages and limited families. The end result, Fisher feared, was for the nation to select the “poorest” genes for future generations and to deselect the “better” genes. The question of eugenics, the movement to improve the human gene stock by selective breeding, would dominate much of Fisher’s political views. During World War II, he would be falsely accused of being a fascist and squeezed out of all war-related work.

费舍尔的政治观点与卡尔·皮尔逊截然不同。皮尔逊曾一度接近社会主义和马克思主义，他同情弱势群体，并热衷于挑战根深蒂固的“上层阶级”。皮尔逊的政治观点对他的科学研究几乎没有明显影响，而费舍尔对优生学的关注则促使他投入大量精力研究遗传学的数学原理。费舍尔从当时的新观点出发，即动植物的特定性状可归因于单个基因，而基因又可以以两种形式存在，在此基础上，他远远超越了格雷戈尔·孟德尔的研究成果[7] ，展示了如何估算相邻基因之间的相互影响。

Fisher’s politics contrast with the political views of Karl Pearson, who flirted with socialism and Marxism, whose sympathies lay with the downtrodden, and who loved to challenge the entrenched “better” classes. Whereas Pearson’s political views had little obvious effect on his scientific work, Fisher’s concern over eugenics led him to put a great deal of effort into the mathematics of genetics. Starting with the (at that time) new ideas that specific characteristics of a plant or animal can be attributed to a single gene, which can occur in one of two forms, Fisher moved well beyond the work of Gregor Mendel,[7] showing how to estimate the effects of neighboring genes on each other.

认为存在控制生命本质的基因这一观点，是科学整体统计学革命的一部分。我们观察到动植物的特征，这些特征被称为“表型”，但我们假设这些表型是基因之间相互作用的结果，而这些基因之间的相互作用概率各不相同。我们试图用这些潜在的、不可见的基因来描述表型的分布。在20世纪后期，生物学家确定了这些基因的物理本质，即遗传分子DNA的片段。我们可以读取这些基因，确定它们指导细胞合成哪些蛋白质，并将这些过程视为真实发生的事件。但我们观察到的仍然是各种可能性的散布，而我们称之为基因的DNA片段，正是从这种散布中推断出来的。

The idea that there are genes that govern the nature of life is part of the general statistical revolution of science. We observe characteristics of plants and animals that are called “phenotypes,” but we postulate that these phenotypes are the result of interactions among the genes with different probabilities of interaction. We seek to describe the distribution of phenotypes in terms of these underlying and unseen genes. In the late twentieth century, biologists identified the physical nature of these genes as segments of the hereditary molecule, DNA. We can read these genes to determine what proteins they instruct the cell to make, and we talk about these as real events. But what we observe is still a scatter of possibilities, and the segments of DNA we call genes are imputed from that scatter.

本书探讨了统计学革命，而R.A.费舍尔在其中扮演了重要角色。他以自己作为遗传学家的成就为傲，其著作中约有一半与遗传学相关。我们暂且搁置对费舍尔作为遗传学家的探讨，主要从他对一般统计技术和思想的发展的角度来审视他。这些思想的萌芽可以追溯到他早期的论文，但在20世纪20年代和30年代初他在罗瑟姆斯特德研究所工作期间得到了更充分的发展。

This book deals with the general statistical revolution, and R. A. Fisher played an important role in it. He was proud of his achievements as a geneticist, and about half of his output deals with genetics. We will leave Fisher, the geneticist, at this point and look at Fisher primarily in terms of his development of general statistical techniques and ideas. The germs of these ideas can be found in his early papers but were more fully developed as he worked at Rothamsted during the 1920s and early 1930s.

科研人员统计方法

STATISTICAL METHODS FOR RESEARCH WORKERS

尽管费舍尔当时并未受到数学界的关注，但他发表的论文和著作对农业和生物学领域的科学家产生了深远的影响。1925年，他出版了《科研人员统计方法》第一版。该书英文版共发行了十四个版本，并被翻译成法文、德文、意大利文、日文、西班牙文和俄文。

Although Fisher was ignored by the mathematical community during that time, he published papers and books that greatly influenced the scientists working in agriculture and biology. In 1925, he published the first edition of Statistical Methods for Research Workers. This book went through fourteen English-language editions and appeared in French, German, Italian, Japanese, Spanish, and Russian translations.

《科研人员统计方法》与以往任何数学书籍都截然不同。通常，数学书籍会列出定理及其证明，阐述抽象概念并将其概括，同时将它们与其他抽象概念联系起来。这类书籍即使包含应用实例，也往往是在数学理论被完整阐述和证明之后才出现。《科研人员统计方法》开篇便讨论了如何用数字绘制图表以及如何解读图表。第一个例子出现在第三页，展示了一个婴儿出生后前十三周每周的体重变化。这个婴儿是费舍尔的第一个孩子，他的儿子乔治。接下来的章节描述了如何分析数据，给出了公式，展示了实例，解释了这些实例的结果，并引出了其他公式。然而，所有公式都没有经过数学推导。它们都没有任何依据或证明。书中通常会详细介绍如何在机械计算器上实现这些公式，但并未给出任何证明过程。

Statistical Methods for Research Workers was like no other mathematics book that had appeared before it. Usually, a book of mathematics has theorems and proofs of those theorems, and develops abstract ideas and generalizes them, relating them to other abstract ideas. If there are applications in such books, they occur only after the mathematics have been fully described and proven. Statistical Methods for Research Workers begins with a discussion of how to create a graph from numbers and how to interpret that graph. The first example, occurring on the third page, displays the weight of a baby each week for the first thirteen weeks of life. That baby was Fisher’s firstborn, his son George. The succeeding chapters describe how to analyze data, giving formulas, showing examples, interpreting the results of those examples, and moving on to other formulas. None of the formulas is derived mathematically. They all appear without justification or proof. They are often presented with detailed techniques of how to implement them on a mechanical calculator, but no proofs are displayed.

尽管缺乏理论数学，或许正因为如此，这本书迅速被科学界接受。它满足了一项迫切的需求：即使是数学基础薄弱的实验室技术人员也能轻松上手。使用这本书的科学家们都认同费舍尔的论断。而审阅此书的数学家们则对书中那些未经证实的、大胆的论断持怀疑态度，许多人不禁疑惑他是如何得出这些结论的。

Despite, or perhaps because of, its lack of theoretical mathematics, the book was rapidly taken up by the scientific community. It met a serious need. It could be handed to a lab technician with minimum mathematical training, and that technician could use it. The scientists who used it took Fisher’s assertions as correct. The mathematicians who reviewed it looked askance at its audacious unproven statements, and many wondered how he had come to these conclusions.

二战期间，瑞典数学家哈拉尔德·克拉默（Harald Cramér）因战争与国际科学界隔绝，花费数日甚至数周时间审阅了费舍尔的著作及其已发表的论文，补充了证明过程中缺失的步骤，并推导出了未明确给出的证明。1945年，克拉默出版了《统计学的数学方法》一书，对费舍尔的大部分著作给出了正式的证明。由于费舍尔的著作浩如烟海，克拉默不得不从中择优选择，因此书中并未收录费舍尔的大量内容。克拉默的著作被用于培养一代又一代的数学家和统计学家，他对费舍尔著作的修订版也成为了标准范式。到了20世纪70年代，耶鲁大学的L·J·萨维奇（L.J. Savage）重新审视了费舍尔的原始论文，并发现了克拉默遗漏的大量内容。他惊讶地发现，费舍尔预见了其他人后来的工作，并解决了在 20 世纪 70 年代被认为尚未解决的问题。

During the Second World War, the Swedish mathematician Harald Cramér, isolated by the war from the international scientific community, spent days and weeks reviewing this book and Fisher’s published papers, filling in the missing steps of proofs and deriving proofs where none were indicated. In 1945, Cramér produced a book entitled Mathematical Methods of Statistics, giving formal proofs for much of what Fisher had written. Cramér had to choose among the many outpourings of this fertile genius, and a great deal that Fisher had written was not included in this book. Cramér’s book was used to teach a generation of new mathematicians and statisticians, and his redaction of Fisher became the standard paradigm. In the 1970s, L. J. Savage of Yale University went back to Fisher’s original papers and discovered how much Cramér had left out. He was amazed to see that Fisher had anticipated later work by others and had solved problems that were thought to be still unsolved in the 1970s.

但这一切在1919年都还未发生，当时费舍尔放弃了并不成功的教师生涯。他刚刚完成了一部鸿篇巨制，将高尔顿的相关系数与孟德尔遗传的基因理论相结合。这篇论文被英国皇家统计学会和皮尔逊在《生物统计学》（Biometrika）杂志上拒稿。费舍尔听说爱丁堡皇家学会正在征稿，希望发表在其会刊上，但作者需要自费。于是，他自掏腰包，将他的下一部重要的数学著作发表在了一份名不见经传的期刊上。

But all of this was still in the future in 1919, when Fisher abandoned his unsuccessful career as a schoolmaster. He had just finished a monumental work, combining Galton’s correlation coefficient and the gene theory of Mendelian heredity. The paper had been rejected by the Royal Statistical Society and by Pearson at Biometrika. Fisher heard that the Royal Society of Edinburgh was looking for papers to publish in its Transactions, but that the authors were expected to pay for the publication costs. Thus, he paid to have his next great mathematical work published in an obscure journal.

此时，卡尔·皮尔逊仍然对年轻的费舍尔印象深刻，于是向他发出邀请，让他担任高尔顿生物统计实验室的首席统计师。两人之间的通信十分友好，但费舍尔很清楚皮尔逊意志坚定、独断专行。他的首席统计师充其量只能从事一些由皮尔逊指示的详细计算工作。

At this point, Karl Pearson, still impressed by young Fisher, came through with an offer to take him on as chief statistician at the Galton Biometrical Laboratory. The correspondence between the two men was cordial, but it was obvious to Fisher that Pearson was strong-willed and dominating. His chief statistician would, at best, be engaged in detailed calculations that were dictated by Pearson.

罗瑟姆斯特德和农业实验

ROTHAMSTED AND AGRICULTURAL EXPERIMENTS

费舍尔还曾与罗瑟姆斯特德农业实验站站长约翰·罗素爵士联系过。罗瑟姆斯特德实验站是由一家英国化肥生产商建立的，选址在一座老农场上，这座农场曾属于这家化肥公司的原主人。那里的黏土土壤并不特别适合种植任何作物，但农场主们发现了一种将碎石与酸混合，生产出一种名为过磷酸钙的物质的方法。生产过磷酸钙的利润被用来建立一个实验站，用于研发新型人工肥料。九十年来，该实验站开展了各种“实验”，测试不同矿物盐组合以及不同品种的小麦、黑麦、大麦和土豆。这积累了大量数据，包括每日精确的降雨量和温度记录、每周的施肥量和土壤测量数据，以及每年的收成记录——所有这些数据都保存在皮面装订的笔记本中。虽然大多数实验的结果并不一致，但这些笔记本都被小心翼翼地保存在实验站的档案馆里。

Fisher had also been contacted by Sir John Russell, head of the Rothamsted Agricultural Experimental Station. The Rothamsted station had been set up by a British maker of fertilizer on an old farm that had once belonged to the original owners of the fertilizer firm. The clay soil was not particularly suited to growing much of anything, but the owners had discovered how to combine crushed stone with acid to produce what was known as Super-Phosphate. The profits from the production of Super-Phosphate were used to establish an experimental station where new artificial fertilizers might be developed. For ninety years, the station ran “experiments,” testing different combinations of mineral salts and different strains of wheat, rye, barley, and potatoes. This had created a huge storehouse of data, exact daily records of rainfall and temperature, weekly records of fertilizer dressings and measures of the soil, and annual records of harvests—all of it preserved in leather-bound notebooks. Most of these experiments had not produced consistent results, but the notebooks had been carefully stored away in the station’s archives.

约翰爵士审视了这浩如烟海的数据，心想或许应该雇人来分析一下这些数据，做些统计分析。他四处打听，有人推荐了罗纳德·艾尔默·费舍尔。他给费舍尔提供了一份为期一年、年薪一千英镑的工作；他无法提供更高的薪水，也无法保证这份工作能持续一年以上。

Sir John looked at this vast collection of data and decided that maybe somebody might be hired to see what was in it, take a sort of statistical look at these records. He inquired around, and someone recommended Ronald Aylmer Fisher. He offered Fisher a year’s employment at a thousand pounds; he could not offer more and could not guarantee that the job would last past that one year.

费舍尔接受了罗素的提议。他带着妻子、妻妹和三个孩子搬到了伦敦北部的乡村地区。他们在实验站隔壁租了一座农场，妻子和妻妹在那里照料菜园，并操持家务。他穿上靴子，穿过田野，来到罗瑟姆斯特德农业实验站，那里保存着九十年的数据，他要从事的工作，后来被他称为“翻粪堆”。

Fisher accepted Russell’s offer. He took his wife, sister-in-law, and three children into the rural area north of London. They rented a farm next door to the experimental station, where his wife and sister-in-law tended a vegetable garden and kept house for him. He put on his boots and walked across the fields to Rothamsted Agricultural Experimental Station and its ninety years of data, to engage in what he was later to call “raking over the muck heap.”

C第五章

CHAPTER 5

“作物变异研究”

“STUDIES IN CROP VARIATION”

E在我早期从事生物统计学工作时，有一次我去康涅狄格大学斯托尔斯分校拜访休·史密斯教授，探讨我遇到的问题。他送给我一份礼物，是一篇题为《作物变异研究。III. 降雨量对罗瑟姆斯特德小麦产量的影响》的论文。这篇论文长达五十三页，是一系列杰出的数学论文中的第三篇，第一篇发表于1921年的《农业科学杂志》第十一卷。产量变异是实验科学家的难题，却是统计方法的基础。在现代科学文献中，“变异”一词已很少使用，取而代之的是其他术语，例如“方差”，这些术语指的是分布的特定参数。

Early in my career as a biostatistician, and on one of my trips to the University of Connecticut at Storrs to talk over my problems, Professor Hugh Smith had a present for me. It was a copy of a paper entitled “Studies in Crop Variation. III. The Influence of Rainfall on the Yield of Wheat at Rothamsted.” The paper was fifty-three pages long. It is the third in a series of remarkable mathematical articles, the first of which had appeared in the Journal of Agricultural Science, vol. XI, in 1921. Variation in output is the bane of the experimental scientist but the basic material of statistical methods. The word variation is seldom used in the modern scientific literature. It has been replaced by other terms, like “variance,” which refer to specific parameters of distributions.

对于普通的科学用途而言，“变异”一词过于模糊，但在本系列论文中却很合适，因为作者以作物产量逐年和田间的变化为出发点，推导出新的分析方法。

Variation is too vague a word for ordinary scientific use, but it was appropriate in this series of papers, because the author used the variation in crop outputs from year to year and from field to field as a starting point from which to derive new methods of analysis.

大多数科学论文末尾都有长长的参考文献列表，列出了先前探讨过相关问题的论文。而本系列的第一篇《作物变异研究 I》却只有三篇参考文献：一篇指出1907年曾尝试将降雨量与小麦生长关联起来，但最终失败；另一篇是1909年发表的德语论文，描述了一种计算复杂数学公式最小值的方法；第三篇是卡尔·皮尔逊出版的一组表格。此前没有任何论文涉及本系列论文所涵盖的大部分主题。《作物变异研究》可谓独树一帜。其作者署名为R.A. Fisher，硕士，隶属于哈彭登罗瑟姆斯特德实验站统计实验室。

Most scientific papers have long lists of references at the end, identifying the previous papers that had addressed the problems discussed. “Studies in Crop Variation. I,” the first in this series, had only three references: one indicated a failed attempt made in 1907 to correlate rainfall and wheat growth; a second, in German, from 1909, described a method for computing the minimum of a complicated mathematical formula; and the third was a set of tables published by Karl Pearson. There was no previous paper dealing with most of the topics covered in this remarkable series. The “Studies in Crop Variation” were sui generis. Their author is described as R. A. Fisher, M.A., Statistical Laboratory, Rothamsted Experimental Station, Harpenden.

1950年，出版商约翰·威利(John Wiley)邀请费舍尔(Fisher)从他已发表的论文中精选出最重要的几篇，汇编成册。这本名为《数理统计文集》(Contributions to Mathematical Statistics)的文集，开篇是一张费舍尔当时的旧照，照片中的他头发花白，嘴唇紧抿，领带略微歪斜，白胡子也修剪得有些凌乱。照片上标注着“剑桥大学遗传学系，R.A.费舍尔”。《作物变异研究. I》(Studies in Crop Variation. I)是本书的第三篇文章。文章前有作者的一段简短说明，阐述了这篇文章的重要性及其在费舍尔著作中的地位。

In 1950, the publisher John Wiley asked Fisher if he would cull through his published papers and provide a selection of the most important, to form a single volume. The volume, entitled Contributions to Mathematical Statistics, opens with a contemporaneous photograph of a white-haired R. A. Fisher, his lips tightly pressed together, his tie slightly askew, his white beard not too well trimmed. He is identified as “R. A. Fisher, Department of Genetics, University of Cambridge.” “Studies in Crop Variation. I” is the third article in this book. It is preceded by a short note from the author identifying its importance and its place in his works:

作者在罗瑟姆斯特德早期的工作中，非常重视该研究站漫长历史中积累的大量天气、作物产量、作物分析等记录。这些资料对于诸如确定气象读数在多大程度上能够预测未来作物产量等问题，显然具有独特的价值。本文是致力于此目的的一系列文章的第一篇……

In the author’s early work at Rothamsted much attention was given to the massive records of weather, crop yields, crop analyses, etc., which had been accumulated during the long history of that research station. The material was obviously of unique value for such problems as that of ascertaining to what extent meteorological readings were capable of supplying a prediction of the crop yields to follow. The present paper is the first of a series devoted to this end … .

“致力于此目的的系列文章”最多只有六篇。“作物变异研究. II”发表于1923年。史密斯教授给我的论文题为“III. 降雨对罗瑟姆斯特德小麦产量的影响”，发表于1924年。“作物变异研究. IV”发表于1927年，“作物变异研究. VI”发表于1929年。第五篇研究并未收录在费舍尔的文集中。在科学史上，很少有哪一组论文的标题能如此糟糕地反映其内容的重要性。在这些论文中，费舍尔开发了用于数据分析的原创工具，推导出了这些工具的数学基础，描述了它们在其他领域的应用，并将它们应用于他在罗瑟姆斯特德发现的“泥泞”土壤。这些论文展现了卓越的原创性，充满了引人入胜的启示，让理论家们在整个二十世纪余下的时间里都忙于研究，而且很可能在未来的岁月里继续激发更多的研究。

There were, at most, six articles in the “series devoted to this end.” “Studies in Crop Variation. II” appeared in 1923. There is the paper Professor Smith gave me, entitled “III. The Influence of Rainfall on the Yield of Wheat at Rothamsted,” from 1924. “Studies in Crop Variation. IV” appeared in 1927 and “Studies in Crop Variation. VI” was published in 1929. Study number V does not appear in Fisher’s collected works. Seldom in the history of science has a set of titles been such a poor descriptor of the importance of the material they contain. In these papers, Fisher developed original tools for the analysis of data, derived the mathematical foundations of those tools, described their extensions into other fields, and applied them to the “muck” he found at Rothamsted. These papers show a brilliant originality and are filled with fascinating implications that kept theoreticians busy for the rest of the twentieth century, and will probably continue to inspire more work in the years that follow.

“作物变异研究. I”

“STUDIES IN CROP VARIATION. I”

费舍尔系列论文的后两篇有其他作者。而《作物变异研究》第一卷则完全由他独自完成，需要进行大量的计算。他唯一的助手是一台名为“百万富翁”的计算器。这是一台原始的手摇式机械计算器。例如，如果要计算3342乘以27，就需要将压板放在个位，输入数字3342，然后摇动七次。接着，将压板放在十位，再次输入数字3342，然后摇动两次。之所以称之为“百万富翁”，是因为它的压板足够大，可以容纳数百万的数字。

There were additional authors for two of the later papers in Fisher’s series. For “Studies in Crop Variation. I,” he worked alone. It required prodigious amounts of calculation. His only aid was a calculating machine named the Millionaire. It was a primitive, hand-cranked mechanical calculator. If one wanted to multiply, for instance, 3,342 by 27, one put the platen on the units position, set in the number 3,342, and cranked seven times. Then one put the platen on the tens position, set in the number 3,342, and cranked two times. It was a Millionaire because the platen was big enough to accommodate numbers in the millions.

为了了解其中涉及的体力劳动，请参考《作物变异研究 I》第 123 页的表 VII。假设完成一次大数乘法运算大约需要一分钟，我估计费舍尔需要大约 185 个小时才能生成该表。文章中共有 15 个类似复杂程度的表格和 4 个大型复杂图表。仅就体力劳动而言，准备这些表格至少需要 8 个月，每天工作 12 小时。这还不包括推导理论数学、整理数据、规划分析以及纠正不可避免的错误所需的时间。

To get some idea of the physical effort involved, consider Table VII that appears on page 123 of “Studies in Crop Variation. I.” If it took about one minute to complete a single large-digit multiplication, I estimate that Fisher needed about 185 hours of work to generate that table. There are fifteen tables of similar complexity and four large complicated graphs in the article. In terms of physical labor alone, it must have taken at least eight months of 12-hour days to prepare the tables for this article. This does not include the hours needed to work out the theoretical mathematics, to organize the data, to plan the analysis, and to correct the inevitable mistakes.

高尔顿回归均值广义模型

GALTON’S REGRESSION TO THE MEAN GENERALIZED

回想一下高尔顿发现的均值回归现象，以及他试图找到一个能将随机事件联系起来的数学公式。费舍尔沿用了高尔顿的回归理论，建立了年份与特定田地小麦产量之间的普遍数学关系。皮尔逊的概率分布概念由此演变为一个连接年份与产量的公式。这个更为复杂的分布的参数描述了小麦产量变化的不同方面。要理解费舍尔的数学推导，需要扎实的微积分知识、对概率分布理论的深刻理解以及对多维几何的初步认识。但理解他的结论并不难。

Recall Galton’s discovery of regression to the mean and his attempt to find a mathematical formula that linked random events to each other. Fisher took Galton’s word, regression, and established a general mathematical relationship between the year and the wheat output of a given field. Pearson’s idea of probability distribution now became a formula connecting year to output. The parameters of this more complicated distribution described different aspects of the change in wheat output. To go through Fisher’s mathematics one needs a solid knowledge of calculus, a good sense of the theory of probability distributions, and a feeling for multidimensional geometry. But it is not too difficult to understand his conclusions.

他将小麦产量的时间趋势分解为几个部分。一部分是由于土壤退化导致产量持续下降。另一部分是长期的、缓慢的变化，每个阶段都需要数年时间。第三部分是一系列快速变化，这些变化考虑到了气候的年际波动。自费舍尔最初的开创性尝试以来，时间序列统计分析在他的思想和方法基础上不断发展。如今，我们拥有能够利用巧妙算法进行海量计算的计算机，但其基本思想和方法依然存在。给定一组随时间波动的数据，我们可以将其分解为由不同来源造成的影响。时间序列分析已被用于研究美国太平洋沿岸的波浪拍击，从而识别印度洋的风暴。这些方法使研究人员能够区分地下核爆炸和地震，精确定位心跳的病理特征，量化环境法规对空气质量的影响；而且，其应用范围还在不断扩大。

He pulled the time trend of wheat output into several pieces. One was a steady overall diminution of output due to deterioration of the soil. Another was a long-term, slow change that took several years for each phase. The third was a set of faster moving changes that took into account the variations in climate from year to year. Since Fisher’s first pioneering attempts, the statistical analysis of time series has built on his ideas and methods. We now have computers that can do the immense calculations with clever algorithms, but the basic idea and methods remain. Given a set of numbers fluctuating over time, we can pull them apart into effects that are due to different sources. Time series analyses have been used to examine the lapping of waves on the Pacific shores of the United States and thereby identify storms in the Indian Ocean. These methods have enabled researchers to distinguish between underground nuclear explosions and earthquakes, pinpoint pathological aspects of heartbeats, quantify the effect of environmental regulations on air quality; and the uses continue to multiply.

费舍尔对一块名为布罗德巴尔克（Broadbalk）的田地里收获的谷物进行了分析，结果令他困惑不已。这块田地只使用了天然动物粪便，因此年际产量的变化并非实验性肥料造成的。长期产量下降是可以理解的，因为土壤中缺乏粪便中缺失的养分，而且他还能从年际变化中看出不同降雨模式的影响。那么，缓慢变化的原因是什么呢？这种缓慢变化模式表明，1876年产量开始下降，其速度超过了其他两种因素所能解释的下降速度，并且在1880年后下降速度加快。1894年至1901年间产量有所回升，之后又开始下降。

Fisher was puzzled by his analysis of grain harvested from a field named Broadbalk. Only natural animal dung had been used on this field, so the variation in yield from year to year was not the result of experimental fertilizers. The long-term deterioration made sense as the soil was depleted of nutrients missing from the dung, and he could identify the effects of differing rainfall patterns in the year-to-year changes. What was the source of the slow changes? The slow-change pattern suggested that in 1876, the output had begun to deteriorate more than would be expected from the other two effects, the deterioration getting more rapid after 1880. There was an improvement starting in 1894 and continuing until 1901, with a drop thereafter.

费舍尔还发现了另一项记录，其变化同样缓慢，但模式却截然相反。这次记录的是麦田杂草的泛滥。1876 年后，杂草愈发茂盛，新的多年生杂草品种不断涌现。到了 1894 年，杂草突然开始减少，但到了 1901 年又开始大量繁殖。

Fisher found another record with the same slow change but with the pattern reversed. This was the infestation of weeds in the wheat field. After 1876, the weeds became ever heavier, with new varieties of perennials establishing themselves. Then in 1894 the weeds suddenly began to diminish, only to start flourishing again in 1901.

原来，在1876年之前，雇佣小男孩到田里拔草是一种惯例。那时，在英国的田野里，经常可以看到疲惫的孩子们在午后穿梭于麦田和其他谷物之间，不停地拔着杂草。1876年，《教育法》规定入学是强制性的，大批小男孩开始从田间消失。1880年，第二部《教育法》规定了对让孩子辍学的家庭的处罚，最后一批小男孩也离开了田间。没有了拔草的小手，杂草开始疯长。

It turned out that it had been the practice prior to 1876 to hire small boys to go into the fields and pull weeds. It was common at that time to see weary children in the fields of England on an afternoon combing through the wheat and other grains, constantly pulling weeds. In 1876, the Education Act made attendance at school compulsory, and the legions of young boys began disappearing from the fields. In 1880, a second Education Act provided penalties for families that kept their children out of school, and the last of the young boys left the fields. Without the little fingers to pull them out, the weeds began to flourish.

1894年发生了什么事扭转了这一趋势？罗瑟姆斯特德附近有一所女子寄宿学校。新来的校长约翰·劳斯爵士坚信，积极的户外活动能够增强孩子们的健康。他与实验站的主任商定，让女孩子们在周六和晚上到田里除草。1901年约翰爵士去世后，小女孩们又回到了室内，不再进行久坐不动的活动，而布罗德巴尔克的杂草也再次遍地都是。

What happened in 1894 to reverse this trend? There was a boarding school for girls in the vicinity of Rothamsted. The new schoolmaster, Sir John Lawes, believed in vigorous outdoor activity to build up the health of his young charges. He arranged with the director of the experimental station to bring his young girls out into the fields to pull weeds on Saturdays and evenings. After Sir John died in 1901, the little girls went back to sedentary and indoor activities, and the weeds came back to Broadbalk.

随机对照实验

RANDOMIZED CONTROLLED EXPERIMENTS

第二篇关于作物变异的研究也发表于1923年的《农业科学杂志》。这篇论文并非探讨罗瑟姆斯特德以往实验的累积数据，而是描述了一系列关于不同肥料混合物对不同马铃薯品种影响的实验。自从R.A.费舍尔来到罗瑟姆斯特德后，那里的实验发生了显著的变化。他们不再像以前那样在整块田地里施用单一的实验肥料，而是将田地分割成小块试验田。每块试验田又被进一步细分为若干行，每行都采用不同的处理方法。

The second study on crop variation also appeared in the Journal of Agricultural Science in 1923. This paper does not deal with accumulated data from the past experiments at Rothamsted. Instead, it describes a set of experiments on the effects of different fertilizer mixes on different varieties of potatoes. Something remarkable had happened to the experiments at Rothamsted since the arrival of R. A. Fisher. No longer were they applying a single experimental fertilizer across an entire field. Now, they were cutting the field up into small plots. Each plot was further divided into rows of plants and each row within a plot was given a different treatment.

基本思路很简单——至少在费舍尔提出之后是如此。此前从未有人想到过这一点。任何人观察一片麦田都会发现，田地的某些部分比其他部分长得更好。在某些角落，麦苗长得高大饱满，结满了谷粒；而在另一些角落，麦苗则稀疏瘦弱。这可能是由于排水方式不同、土壤类型变化、未知养分的存在、多年生杂草丛生，或其他一些无法预料的因素造成的。如果农业科学家想要测试两种肥料成分的差异，他可以将一种肥料施在田地的一部分，另一种施在另一部分。这样就能将肥料的效果与土壤或排水特性的影响混淆起来。如果在同一块田地上，但不同年份进行测试，肥料的效果就会与每年气候变化的影响混淆起来。

The basic idea was a simple one—simple, that is, once it was proposed by Fisher. No one had ever thought of it before. It is obvious to anyone looking at a field of grain that some parts of the field are better than others. In some corners, the plants grow tall and heavy with grain. In other corners, the plants are thin and scraggly. It may be due to the way in which water drains, or to the changes in soil type, or the presence of unknown nutrients, or blocks of perennial weeds, or some other, unforeseen, effect. If the agricultural scientist wants to test the difference between two fertilizer components, he can put one fertilizer on one part of the field and another elsewhere. This will confound the effects of the fertilizer with effects due to the soil or drainage properties. If the tests are run on the same fields but in different years, the effects of fertilizer are confounded with changes in weather from year to year.

如果将肥料直接放在同一年份的相邻地块上进行比较，土壤差异的影响就会被最小化。但土壤差异仍然存在，因为处理过的植物并非生长在完全相同的土壤上。如果我们使用多组这样的对比试验，土壤差异在某种程度上会相互抵消。假设我们要比较两种肥料，其中一种的磷含量是另一种的两倍。我们将田地划分成若干小块，每块种植两行植物。我们总是将额外的磷肥施在北侧的植物上，而南侧的植物则使用另一种肥料。现在，我仿佛听到有人说，如果土壤肥力梯度呈南北走向，那么这些差异就无法“相互抵消”了，因为每个地块中北侧的土壤都会比南侧的略好一些。

If the fertilizers are compared right next to one another and in the same year, then the differences in soil will be minimized. They will still be there, since the treated plants are not on exactly the same soil. If we use many such pairs, the differences due to soil will average out in some sense. Suppose we want to compare two fertilizers, one with twice as much phosphorus as the other. We break the field into tiny plots, each with two rows of plants. We always put the extra phosphorus on the northern row of plants and treat the southern row with the other mixture. Now, I can hear someone saying, then they won’t “average” out if the fertility gradient in the soil runs north and south, for the northern row in each block will have slightly better soil than the southern row.

我们交替进行。在第一块地里，额外施磷肥的行在北侧；在第二块地里，施磷肥的行在南侧，以此类推。一位读者已经画了一张田地的简图，并用“X”标记了施过磷肥的行。他指出，如果土壤肥力梯度是从西北向东南方向的，那么施过磷肥的行土壤都会比其他行更好。另一位读者指出，如果土壤肥力梯度是从东北向西南方向的，情况则相反。另一位读者问道，到底哪种情况才是正确的？土壤肥力梯度究竟是如何变化的？对此，我们的回答是：没人知道。土壤肥力梯度是一个抽象的概念。从北到南或从东到西，土壤肥力的实际分布可能呈现出某种复杂的上下波动。

We’ll just alternate. In the first block, the extra phosphorus will be in the northern row. In the second block, it will be in the southern row, and so forth. One of my readers has now drawn a rough map of the field and put X’s to indicate the rows with extra phosphorus. He points out that if the fertility gradient runs from northwest to southeast, then the rows treated with extra phosphorus will all have better soil than the others. Someone else points out that if the gradient runs from northeast to southwest, the opposite holds. Well, demands another reader, which is it? How does the fertility gradient run? To which we reply, no one knows. The concept of a fertility gradient is an abstract one. The real pattern of fertility may run up and down in some complicated way as we go from north to south or east to west.

我能想象罗瑟姆斯特德研究所的科学家们在费舍尔指出将处理分组到小块区域可以进行更精细的实验后，展开的讨论。我能想象他们讨论如何确定生育率梯度，而费舍尔则悠闲地靠在一旁，面带微笑，任由他们越陷越深地研究复杂的模型。他早已考虑过这些问题，并给出了一个简单的答案。他从嘴里拿出烟斗。认识他的人都说，费舍尔总是静静地坐在那里，吞云吐雾，周围争论不休，他只是等待着合适的时机说出自己的答案。“随机化，”他说。

I can imagine these discussions among the scientists at Rothamsted once Fisher pointed out that setting treatments within small blocks would allow for more careful experimentation. I can imagine the discussions over how to determine the fertility gradient, while R. A. Fisher sits back and smiles, letting them get more and more involved in complicated constructs. He has already considered these questions and has a simple answer. He removes the pipe from his mouth. Those who knew him describe Fisher as sitting, quietly puffing on his pipe, while arguments raged about him, waiting for the moment when he could insert his answer. “Randomize,” he says.

费舍尔方差分析

FISHER’S ANALYSIS OF VARIANCE

这很简单。科学家将不同的处理随机分配到同一区组内的不同行中。由于随机排序没有固定模式，任何可能的肥力梯度结构平均而言都会相互抵消。费舍尔猛地站起来，开始在黑板上飞快地写写画画，用数学符号填满黑板，手臂扫过一列列数学公式，划掉等式两边相互抵消的因子，最终得到了后来可能成为生物科学最重要的工具。这是一种在精心设计的科学实验中分离不同处理效果的方法，费舍尔称之为“方差分析”。在《作物变异研究II》中，方差分析首次出现。

It is simple. The scientist assigns the treatments to different rows within a block at random. Since the random ordering follows no fixed pattern, any possible structure of fertility gradient will cancel out, on the average. Fisher springs up and begins to write furiously on the blackboard, filling it with mathematical symbols, sweeping his arms across the columns of math, crossing out factors that cancel on either side of the equations, and emerging with what was to become probably the single most important tool of biological science. This is a method for separating out the effects of different treatments in a well-designed scientific experiment, which Fisher called the “analysis of variance.” In “Studies in Crop Variation. II” the analysis of variance shows up for the first time.

《科研人员统计方法》一书中给出了一些方差分析的公式，但本文是从数学角度推导的。然而，这些公式的推导不够详细，无法满足学术数学家的要求。文中展示的代数运算是针对比较三种肥料（粪肥）、十个马铃薯品种和四块土壤的具体情况而制定的。要弄清楚如何将这些代数运算应用于两种肥料和五个品种，或者六种肥料和一个品种，需要花费数小时的艰苦工作。而要推导出适用于所有情况的通用公式，则需要付出更多的数学努力。当然，费舍尔知道这些通用公式。对他来说，这些公式如此显而易见，以至于他认为没有必要再赘述。

The formulas for some examples of analysis of variance appear in Statistical Methods for Research Workers, but in this paper they are derived mathematically. They are not worked out in sufficient detail for an academic mathematician to be satisfied. The algebra displayed is made specific to the situation of comparing three types of fertilizer (manure), ten varieties of potatoes, and four blocks of soil. It takes a few hours of painstaking work to figure out how the algebra might be adapted for two fertilizers and five varieties, or for six fertilizers and only one variety. It takes even more mathematical sweat of the brow to figure out the general formulas that would work in all cases. Fisher, of course, knew the general formulas. They were so obvious to him that he did not see the need to produce them.

难怪当时的同代人对年轻的费舍尔的作品感到困惑不解！

No wonder his contemporaries were mystified by young Fisher’s work!

《作物变异研究 IV》介绍了费舍尔所谓的“协方差分析”。这是一种将实验设计之外但实际存在且可测量的条件的影响排除在外的方法。当医学期刊上的文章描述一种“已根据性别和体重进行调整”的治疗效果时，它使用的正是费舍尔在这篇论文中开创的方法。研究 VI 对实验设计理论进行了完善。史密斯教授向我介绍的研究 III 将在本章后面讨论。

“Studies in Crop Variation. IV” introduces what Fisher called “analysis of covariance.” This is a method for factoring out the effects of conditions that are not part of the experimental design but which are there and can be measured. When an article in a medical journal describes a treatment effect that has been “adjusted for sex and weight,” it is using the methods pioneered by Fisher in this paper. Study VI produces refinements in the theory of design of experiments. Study III, to which Professor Smith introduced me, will be discussed later in this chapter.

自由度

DEGREES OF FREEDOM

1922年，费舍尔终于在《皇家统计学会杂志》上发表了他的第一篇文章。这是一篇简短的论文，谦逊地证明了卡尔·皮尔逊的一个公式是错误的。多年后，费舍尔在谈到这篇论文时说：

In 1922, Fisher finally got his first article published in the Journal of the Royal Statistical Society. It is a short note that modestly proves that one of Karl Pearson’s formulas was wrong. Writing about this paper many years later, Fisher said:

这篇短文虽然存在诸多稚嫩之处，却也起到了一定的破冰作用。任何因其犹豫不决、支离破碎的风格而感到恼火的读者都应该记住，它必须先经过那些评论家的阻挠才能得以发表。这些评论家一开始就不相信皮尔逊的作品需要修改，而且即便不得不承认这一点，他们也坚信自己已经修改过它了。

This short paper, with all its juvenile inadequacies, yet did something to break the ice. Any reader who feels exasperated by its tentative and piecemeal character should remember that it had to find its way to publication past critics who, in the first place, could not believe that Pearson’s work stood in need of correction, and who, if this had to be admitted, were sure that they themselves had corrected it.

1924年，他得以在《皇家统计学会杂志》上发表一篇篇幅更长、内容更全面的论文。后来，他在一份经济学期刊上评论了这篇论文和一篇相关论文：“（这些论文）试图借助自由度这一新概念，来调和不同作者观察到的不一致和异常的结果……”

In 1924, he was able to publish a longer and more general paper in the Journal of the Royal Statistical Society. He later comments on this and a related paper in an economics journal: “[These papers] are attempts to reconcile, with the aid of the new concept of degrees of freedom, the discrepant and anomalous results observed by different authors … .”

“自由度”这一新概念是费舍尔的发现，这与他敏锐的几何洞察力以及将数学问题转化为多维几何问题的能力直接相关。“异常结果”出现在一本由一位名叫T.L.凯利的人在纽约出版的鲜为人知的书中；凯利发现了一些数据，表明皮尔逊的部分公式似乎无法得出正确答案。似乎只有费舍尔看过凯利的书。凯利的异常结果仅仅被费舍尔用作跳板，费舍尔以此为跳板，彻底推翻了皮尔逊引以为傲的另一项成就。

The “new concept of degrees of freedom” was Fisher’s discovery and was directly related to his geometric insights and his ability to cast the mathematical problems in terms of multidimensional geometry. The “anomalous results” occurred in an obscure book published by somebody named T. L. Kelley in New York; Kelley had found data wherein some of Pearson’s formulas did not seem to produce correct answers. Only Fisher, it would seem, had looked at Kelley’s book. Kelley’s anomalous results were only used as a springboard from which Fisher utterly demolished another of Pearson’s most proud achievements.

“作物变异研究. III”

“STUDIES IN CROP VARIATION. III”

第三项关于作物变异的研究发表于1924年的《伦敦皇家学会哲学汇刊》。文章开头是这样的：

The third of the studies in crop variation appeared in 1924 in the Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society of London. It begins:

目前，人们对天气对农作物的影响知之甚少。尽管农业对国家重要产业至关重要，但这一课题的晦涩难懂，部分原因在于问题本身的复杂性……以及……缺乏在实验或工业条件下获得的定量数据……。

At the present time very little can be claimed to be known as to the effects of weather upon farm crops. The obscurity of the subject, in spite of its immense importance to a great national industry, may be ascribed partly to the inherent complexity of the problem … and … to the lack of quantitative data obtained either upon experimental or under industrial conditions … .

接下来是一篇长达五十三页的杰作。它奠定了现代统计方法的基础，这些方法被应用于经济学、医学、化学、计算机科学、社会学、天文学、药理学等领域——任何需要确定大量相互关联因素的相对影响的领域。文章中包含极其巧妙的计算方法（要知道，费舍尔当时只能依靠《百万富翁手册》），以及许多关于如何组织数据进行统计分析的宝贵建议。我永远感激史密斯教授向我推荐了这篇文章。每次阅读，我都能学到新的知识。

Then follows a masterful article of fifty-three pages. In it lie the foundations of modern statistical methods used in economics, medicine, chemistry, computer science, sociology, astronomy, pharmacology—any field where one needs to establish the relative effects of a large number of interconnected causes. It contains highly ingenious methods of calculation (recall that Fisher had only the manual Millionaire to work with), and many wise suggestions on how to organize data for statistical analysis. I am forever grateful to Professor Smith for introducing me to this paper. With each reading, I learn something new.

《R.A.费舍尔文集》第一卷（共五卷）以他1924年发表的论文结尾。卷末附有一张费舍尔的照片，此时他34岁。照片中，他双臂交叉抱于胸前，胡须修剪得整整齐齐，眼镜看起来比之前的照片中略薄。他神态自信沉稳。在之前的五年里，他在罗瑟姆斯特德研究所建立了一个卓越的统计学系。他聘请了像弗兰克·耶茨这样的同事，在费舍尔的鼓励下，耶茨后来为统计分析的理论和实践做出了重大贡献。卡尔·皮尔逊的学生几乎都销声匿迹了。他们在生物统计实验室工作时，只是皮尔逊的助手，实际上只是皮尔逊的延伸。而费舍尔的学生几乎都响应了他的鼓励，开辟了自己辉煌而独创的道路。

The first volume (of five) of the Collected Papers of R. A. Fisher ends with the papers he published in 1924. There is a photograph of Fisher, at this point thirty-four years old, near the end of the volume. His arms are folded. His beard is well trimmed. His glasses do not seem as thick as they did in earlier photos. He has a confident and secure look. In the previous five years, he has built a remarkable statistics department at Rothamsted. He has hired coworkers like Frank Yates, who would go on, under Fisher’s encouragement, to make major contributions to the theory and practice of statistical analysis. With a few exceptions, Karl Pearson’s students have disappeared. While they worked at the biometrical laboratory, they aided Pearson and were no more than extensions of Pearson. With few exceptions, Fisher’s students responded to his encouragement and plowed brilliant and original paths themselves.

1947年，费舍尔受邀在BBC广播电台就科学的本质和科学研究发表一系列演讲。在其中一次演讲的开头，他说道：

In 1947, Fisher was invited to give a series of talks on the BBC radio network about the nature of science and scientific investigation. Early in one talk, he said:

在某些方面，科学事业是特殊的。它的存在意义在于增进自然知识。因此，自然知识的增长偶尔也会发生。但这却显得不够体贴，容易伤人。因为在某种程度上，先前阐述的观点不可避免地会被证明是过时的或错误的。我认为，大多数人都能意识到这一点，如果他们教授了十年左右的内容需要稍作修正，他们也能欣然接受；但有些人无疑会难以接受，视之为自尊心的打击，甚至是对他们视为专属领地的侵犯，他们的反应就像我们春天看到的知更鸟和苍头燕雀一样激烈，它们对领地的入侵感到愤怒。我认为对此无能为力。这是我们职业的固有特性。但年轻的科学家应该被警告和劝告，当他有珍贵的发现可以献给人类以造福人类时，肯定会有人想要反过来攻击他。

A scientific career is peculiar in some ways. Its raison d’être is the increase of natural knowledge. Occasionally, therefore, an increase of natural knowledge occurs. But this is tactless, and feelings are hurt. For in some small degree it is inevitable that views previously expounded are shown to be either obsolete or false. Most people, I think, can recognize this and take it in good part if what they have been teaching for ten years or so comes to need a little revision; but some undoubtedly take it hard, as a blow to their amour propre, or even as an invasion of the territory they have come to think of as exclusively their own, and they must react with the same ferocity as we can see in the robins and chaffinches these spring days when they resent an intrusion into their little territories. I do not think anything can be done about it. It is inherent in the nature of our profession; but a young scientist may be warned and advised that when he has a jewel to offer for the enrichment of mankind some certainly will wish to turn and rend him.

C章第六章

CHAPTER 6

《百年一遇的洪水》

“THE HUNDRED-YEAR FLOOD”

W还有什么比“百年一遇的洪水”更难以预测的呢？这种洪水是指洪水以惊人的威力从河面倾泻而下，百年一遇的罕见景象。谁能为这样的事件做好准备？我们又该如何估算这种罕见洪水的规模？如果现代科学的统计模型处理的是大量观测数据的分布，那么对于从未出现过，或者即使出现过也只出现过一次的洪水，统计模型又能做些什么呢？LHC Tippett 找到了一个解决方案。

What can be more unpredictable than the “hundred-year flood,” the flood waters that come pouring down a river with a massive ferocity so great that it happens as rarely as once in a hundred years? Who can plan for such an event? How can we estimate the height of flood waters that come so infrequently? If the statistical models of modern science deal with the distribution of many observations, what can statistical models do for the problem of the flood that has never been seen or, if it has occurred, been seen only once? L. H. C. Tippett found a solution.

伦纳德·亨利·卡莱布·蒂佩特于1902年出生于伦敦，并在帝国理工学院学习物理学，于1923年毕业。他曾说，物理学吸引他的地方在于它“坚持精确测量……以及对当时科学争论的严谨态度”。回想起自己年轻时的热情，他继续说道：“我们当时倾向于认为一个假设非对即错，并将关键实验视为推进知识发展的主要工具。”然而，当他有机会进行实验时，他发现实验结果与理论预测总是存在偏差。根据他自身的经验，他说道：“我发现改进抽样技术（这里指的是统计分布）比放弃理论更为有效。”蒂佩特意识到，他所钟爱的理论只能提供关于参数的信息，而无法提供关于单个观测结果的信息。

Leonard Henry Caleb Tippett was born in 1902 in London and studied physics there at Imperial College, graduating in 1923. He was attracted, he said, to physics because of its “insistence on accurate measurement … and a disciplined approach to the scientific controversies of the day.” Looking back at his youthful enthusiasm, he continued: “We tended to think of a hypothesis as being either right or wrong, and regarded the crucial experiment as being the main instrument tor advancing knowledge.” When he had the opportunity to do experiments, he found that the experimental results never agreed exactly with what the theory would predict. By his own experience, he said, “I found it better to improve the sampling technique [here he refers to statistical distributions] than to discard the theory.” Tippett realized that the theory he so loved provided information only about parameters and not about individual observations.

就这样，LHC Tippett（他在已发表的论文中如此称呼）通过对实验的理解，逐渐适应了统计学革命。毕业后，他进入英国棉纺织工业研究协会（通常被称为雪莉研究所）担任统计学家，该研究所致力于运用现代科学方法改进棉线和棉布的生产。其中一个最棘手的问题是新纺棉线的强度。即使在相同的纺纱条件下，不同棉线的断裂所需张力也大相径庭。Tippett 进行了一系列细致的实验，在不同的张力下用显微镜观察棉线。他发现，棉线的断裂取决于其中最弱纤维的强度。

In this way, L. H. C. Tippett (as he is identified in his published papers) became attuned to the statistical revolution through his own understanding of experimentation. Upon graduation, he took a position as a statistician at the British Cotton Industry Research Association, usually called the Shirley Institute, where attempts were being made to improve the manufacture of cotton thread and cloth through the use of modern scientific methods. One of the most vexing problems dealt with the strength of newly spun cotton thread. The tension needed to break a strand of thread differed greatly from one strand to another, even when the strands had been spun under identical circumstances. Tippett ran some careful experiments, examining the thread under the microscope after different levels of tension. He discovered that the breaking of the thread depended upon the strength of the weakest fiber in it.

最弱的纤维是什么？如何用数学模型来描述最弱纤维的强度？蒂佩特无法解决这个问题，于是在1924年申请并获准休假一年，前往伦敦大学学院的加尔顿生物统计实验室，在卡尔·皮尔逊的指导下进行研究。关于这段经历，蒂佩特写道：

The weakest fiber? How would one model the mathematics of the strength of the weakest fiber? Unable to solve this problem, Tippett asked for and was given a year’s leave of absence in 1924 to study under Karl Pearson at the Galton Biometrical Laboratory at University College, London. Of that experience, Tippett wrote:

在大学学院的时光令人兴奋。卡尔·皮尔逊是一位伟人，我们都能感受到他的伟大。他勤奋热情，激励着他的教职工和学生。我在那里的时候，他还在从事研究，总是兴致勃勃地走进讲堂，分享他刚刚从书桌上摘下的成果。尽管他当时的研究方向有些过时，但这丝毫没有影响他讲课的精彩程度……他兴趣广泛，其中一门课程就是“17至18世纪统计学史”，这正体现了他的兴趣之广……他是一位充满活力的辩论家……他出版的一系列刊物就名为“时事与争论之问”……那种充满活力和辩论精神的氛围弥漫在校园里。系里的墙上装饰着格言和漫画…… [T]那里有一幅“间谍”创作的漫画，讽刺的是“肥皂山姆”——威尔伯福斯主教，他曾在1860年的英国科学促进会会议上与T·H·赫胥黎就达尔文主义展开了著名的辩论。现场还展出了过去几十年出版的出版物，从诸如《人类遗传宝库（人类体质、心理和病理特征谱系）​​》和《达尔文主义、医学进步与优生学》之类的书名中，我们可以感受到该部门的研究方向。在一次年度部门晚宴上，KP以报告的形式回顾了本年度的工作，以此提醒我们他与高尔顿的密切联系。如果高尔顿还活着，他会把这份报告交给高尔顿。最后，我们举杯缅怀“生物统计学领域的逝者”。

The time at University College was thrilling. Karl Pearson was a great man, and we felt his greatness. He was hard-working and enthusiastic and inspired his staff and students. When I was there he was still doing research, and would come into his lectures, full of excitement and enthusiasm, giving results hot from his study table. That in those years his lines of research were somewhat out of date did not make his lectures any less stimulating … . It was typical of his breadth of interests that one of his lecture courses was on “The History of Statistics in the 17th and 18th Centuries.” … He was vigorous controversialist … and one series of publications issued by him is termed “Questions of the Day and of the Fray.” … [T]he influence of the vigorous and controversial past was in the atmosphere. The walls of the department were embellished with mottoes and cartoons … . [T]here was … a cartoon by “Spy” which was a caricature of “Soapy Sam”—the Bishop Wilberforce who had the famous verbal duel with T. H. Huxley on Darwinism at the British Association meeting in 1860. There was a display of publications that had been issued over past decades, and an impression of the interests of the department was given by such titles as “Treasury of Human Inheritance (Pedigrees of Physical, Psychical, and Pathological Characters in Man)” and “Darwinism, Medical Progress and Eugenics.” K. P. reminded us of his close connection with Galton at an annual departmental dinner when he gave a description of the year’s work in the form of a report he would have given Galton, had he been alive. And we toasted “the biometric dead.”

这是卡尔·皮尔逊生命中最后活跃的几年，之后，费舍尔和皮尔逊自己的儿子所做的工作将他大部分的科学努力扫进了被遗忘的思想垃圾堆。

This was Karl Pearson in the final active years of his life, before the work of R. A. Fisher and Pearson’s own son was to sweep most of his scientific effort into the trash bin of forgotten ideas.

尽管皮尔逊的实验室令人兴奋，蒂佩特在那里也积累了丰富的数学知识，但最弱纤维的强度分布问题仍然悬而未决。回到雪莉研究所后，蒂佩特发现了一些伟大数学发现背后蕴藏的简单逻辑真理。他找到了一个看似简单的方程，这个方程将极值分布与样本数据的分布联系起来。

For all the excitement of Pearson’s laboratory, and for all the mathematical knowledge Tippett developed while there, the problem of the distribution of strength for the weakest fiber remained unsolved. After he returned to the Shirley Institute, Tippett found one of those simple logical truths that lie behind some of the greatest mathematical discoveries. He found a seemingly simple equation that had to connect the distribution of extreme values to the distribution of the sample data.

能够写出方程是一回事，解方程又是另一回事。他咨询了皮尔逊，但皮尔逊也帮不上忙。在过去的七十五年里，工程界积累了大量的方程及其解法，这些都收录在厚重的汇编中。然而，蒂佩特在这些汇编中都找不到他要解的方程。

Being able to write an equation is one thing. Solving it is another matter. He consulted with Pearson, who could offer him no help. Over the previous seventy-five years, the engineering profession had developed a large collection of equations and their solutions, which could be found in massive compendiums. Nowhere in these compendiums could Tippett find his equation.

他做了一个高中代数水平不高的同学会做的事：他猜了一个答案——结果发现这个答案竟然解出了方程。但这真的是方程的唯一解吗？这真的是他问题的“正确”答案吗？他向R.A. Fisher请教，Fisher推导出了Tippett的猜测，并给出了另外两个解，证明这才是方程的唯一解。这三个解被称为“Tippett的三个极值渐近线”。

He did what a poor high school algebra student would do. He guessed an answer—and it turned out to solve the equation. Was this the only solution to the equation? Was it even the “correct” answer for his problem? He consulted with R. A. Fisher, who was able to derive Tippett’s guess, provide two other solutions, and show that these were the only ones. They are known as “Tippett’s three asymptotes of the extreme.”

极端值的分布

THE DISTRIBUTION OF EXTREMES

了解极值分布有什么用？如果我们知道极值分布与正常值分布之间的关系，我们就可以记录每年的洪水水位，并预测百年一遇洪水的最可能水位。之所以能做到这一点，是因为对每年洪水水位的测量提供了足够的信息来估计蒂佩特分布的参数。因此，美国陆军工程兵团可以计算出河堤的高度，环境保护署可以制定排放标准来控制工业烟囱突然排放的极端气体。棉纺织业也能够确定影响最弱纤维强度分布参数的纱线生产因素。

What good does it do to know the distribution of extremes? If we know how the distribution of extreme values relates to the distribution of ordinary values, we can keep a record of the heights of yearly floods and predict the most likely height of the hundred-year flood. We can do this because the measurements of the yearly flood values give us enough information to estimate the parameters of Tippett’s distributions. Thus, the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers can calculate how high to make the dikes on rivers, and the Environmental Protection Agency can set standards for emissions that will control the extreme values of sudden plumes of gases from industrial chimneys. The cotton industry was able to determine those factors of thread production that influenced the parameters of the distribution of strengths for the weakest fiber.

1958年，时任哥伦比亚大学工程学教授的埃米尔·J·冈贝尔出版了该领域的权威著作《极值统计》。此后，该理论虽有少量补充，并将概念扩展到相关领域，但冈贝尔的著作涵盖了统计学家处理该主题所需的一切知识。该书不仅收录了蒂佩特的原始工作，还包括后来对该理论的改进，其中许多改进都出自冈贝尔本人之手。

In 1958, Emil J. Gumbel, then a professor of engineering at Columbia University, published the definitive text on the subject, entitled Statistics of Extremes. There have been minor additions to the theory since then, extending the concepts to related situations, but Gumbel’s text covers all that a statistician needs to know to deal with this subject. The book includes not only Tippett’s original work but later refinements to the theory, many of which were the work of Gumbel himself.

政治谋杀

POLITICAL MURDER

冈贝尔的生平颇为传奇。20世纪20年代末30年代初，他曾是德国一所大学的初级教员。他早期的论文表明他才华横溢，但尚未获得很高的声望。因此，他的工作远非稳定，能否养家糊口也取决于政府当局的意愿。当时纳粹在德国横行无忌。尽管名义上是一个政党，但国家社会主义者实际上是一个流氓团伙。冲锋队（褐衫队）是一个由暴徒组成的组织，他们以威胁、殴打和杀戮来强制执行党的意志。任何批评纳粹党的人都会遭到暴力袭击，而且往往是在光天化日之下，在城市街道上被公开处决，以此恐吓他人。冈贝尔的一位朋友就曾以这种方式被袭击身亡。据说有很多目击者可以指认凶手。然而，法院认为证据不足以定罪，涉案的褐衫党成员被释放。

Gumbel has an interesting biography. In the late 1920s and early 1930s he was a beginning-level faculty member at a university in Germany. His early papers indicated that he was a man of great potential, but he had not yet risen to a high level of esteem. As such, his job was far from secure, and his ability to support his wife and children was subject to the whim of government authorities. The Nazis were running rampant in Germany at that time. Although it was officially a political party, the National Socialists were really a party of gangsters. The Brown Shirts were an organization of thugs who enforced party will with threats, beatings, and killings. Anyone who criticized the Nazi party was subject to violent attack, often in the open on city streets, in order to intimidate others. Gumbel had a friend who was attacked and killed in such a public way. There were many witnesses to the murder who could supposedly identify the murderers. However, the court found that there was insufficient evidence to convict, and the Brown Shirts involved went free.

冈贝尔感到无比震惊。他旁听了审判，亲眼目睹法官如何无视所有证据，武断地做出判决，而法庭上的纳粹分子却在一旁欢呼。冈贝尔开始调查其他公开谋杀却无人被定罪的案件。他最终得出结论：司法部已被纳粹掌控，许多法官要么是纳粹同情者，要么是纳粹的走狗。

Gumbel was horrified. He attended the trial and saw the way in which the judge dismissed all evidence and made his finding arbitrarily, while the Nazis in the courtroom cheered. Gumbel began examining other cases where murders had been committed in the open and no one was found guilty. He came to the conclusion that the Ministry of Justice had been subverted by the Nazis and that many judges were Nazi sympathizers or were in the pay of the Nazis.

冈贝尔收集了大量案例，采访了证人，并记录了凶手被错误释放的情况。1922年，他将研究成果发表在《四年政治谋杀》一书中。由于许多书店不敢上架，他不得不自己安排书籍的发行。与此同时，他继续收集案例，并于1928年出版了《政治谋杀的成因》。他试图组建一个反对纳粹的政治团体，但他的大多数同事都因恐惧而不敢加入。甚至他的犹太朋友也不敢加入。

Gumbel collected a number of cases, interviewing the witnesses and documenting the false dismissals of the murderers. In 1922, he published his findings in Four Years of Political Murder. He had to arrange for the distribution of his own book, finding that many of the bookstores were afraid to carry it. In the meantime, he continued collecting cases and in 1928 published Causes of Political Murder. He tried to create a political group to oppose the Nazis, but most of his fellow academicians were too frightened. Even his Jewish friends were afraid to join him.

1933年纳粹上台时，根贝尔正在瑞士参加一个数学会议。他想立刻赶回德国，与新政府对抗。他的朋友们劝阻了他，并向他证明，他还没来得及越过边境就会被逮捕并杀害。在纳粹政权初期，新政府尚未完全控制所有边境口岸，少数犹太教授，例如德国著名概率论家理查德·冯·米塞斯，预见到了即将发生的事情，成功逃离。根贝尔的朋友们也趁着这段混乱时期，将他的家人送出了德国。他们曾在法国短暂定居，但1940年纳粹占领了法国。

When the Nazis came to power in 1933, Gumbel was attending a mathematics meeting in Switzerland. He wanted to rush back to Germany to fight against this new government. His friends dissuaded him, proving to him that he would be arrested and killed before he could barely cross the border. In the early days of the Nazi regime, before the new government was able to control all border crossings, a small number of Jewish professors, like the leading German probabilist Richard von Mises, who foresaw what was to happen, escaped. Gumbel’s friends took advantage of this time of confusion to get his family out of Germany, too. They settled briefly in France, but in 1940 the Nazis entered the country.

贡贝尔和家人逃往法国南部未被占领的地区，那里由纳粹扶植的残余政府统治，唯德国马首是瞻。贡贝尔和许多其他德国民主人士一样，生命受到威胁，因为他们被列入纳粹要求法国政府交出的“国家敌人”名单。其他被困在马赛的德国难民中，还有作家托马斯·曼的弟弟海因里希·曼和利翁·福伊希特万格。美国驻马赛领事海勒姆·宾厄姆四世违反美国国务院的规定，开始向这些德国难民发放签证。他因此受到华盛顿的训诫，最终被免职。但宾厄姆拯救了许多如果纳粹得逞就必死无疑的人。贡贝尔一家最终来到美国[8]，他在哥伦比亚大学获得了一份工作。

Gumbel and his family fled south to the unoccupied part of France, where a Nazi-installed rump government ruled, subservient to German demands. Gumbel was among many German democrats whose lives were in danger, since they were on a list of enemies of the state that the Nazis were demanding the French government turn over. Among these other German refugees trapped in Marseilles were Heinrich Mann, the brother of the writer Thomas Mann, and Lion Feuchtwanger. In violation of U.S. State Department regulations, Hiram Bingham IV, the American consul in Marseilles, began issuing visas to these German refugees. He was reprimanded by Washington and finally removed from his post because of his activities. But Bingham was able to save many who faced certain death if the Nazis had had their way. Gumbel and his family came to the United States,[8] where he was offered a position at Columbia University.

数学著作的类型多种多样。有些“权威”著作冷冰冰、内容空洞，罗列一系列定理和证明，却鲜少或根本没有阐述其背后的原因。有些著作的证明冗长晦涩，从假设到结论，步步为营，令人费解。还有一些权威著作充满了精妙的证明，将数学过程简化为看似简单的步骤，最终轻松得出结论。此外，还有极少数的权威著作，作者力求阐明问题的背景和思想，描述该学科的发展历程，并引用有趣的现实生活案例。

There are different types of mathematical writing. Some “definitive” texts are cold and sparse, presenting a sequence of theorems and proofs with little or no motivation. In some texts, the proofs are turgid and difficult, plowing with determination from hypotheses to conclusion. There are some definitive texts that are filled with elegant proofs, where the course of the mathematics has been boiled down to seemingly simple steps that move effortlessly to the final conclusions. There are also a very small number of definitive texts in which the authors try to provide the background and ideas behind the problems, in which the history of the subject is described, and in which examples are taken from interesting real-life situations.

最后这些特点恰如其分地描述了冈贝尔的《极值统计》。它以极其清晰易懂的方式阐述了一个复杂的主题，并引用了大量相关发展史。第一章题为“目标与工具”，介绍了主题并阐述了理解本书其余部分所需的数学知识。仅这一章就足以成为统计分布理论数学的绝佳入门。它的设计旨在让只有大学一年级微积分基础的人也能理解。虽然我是在获得数理统计学博士学位后才第一次阅读这本书，但我仍然从第一章中获益匪浅。作者在序言中谦逊地写道：“本书的写作，或许出乎意料地希望，即使是对科学进步的微小贡献，也能使人类受益。”

These last characteristics describe Gumbel’s Statistics of Extremes. It is a magnificently lucid presentation of a difficult subject, filled with references to the development of the subject. The first chapter, entitled “Aims and Tools,” introduces the topic and develops the mathematics needed to understand the rest of the book. This chapter alone is an excellent introduction to the mathematics of statistical distribution theory. It is designed to be understood by someone with no more background than first-year college calculus. Although I first read the book after I had received my Ph.D. in mathematical statistics, I learned a great deal from that first chapter. In the preface, the author states modestly: “This book is written in the hope, contrary to expectation, that humanity may profit by even a small contribution to the progress of science.”

这本书的贡献绝非微不足道。它堪称二十世纪最伟大教师之一的丰碑。埃米尔·冈贝尔是那种罕见的人物，他既拥有非凡的勇气，又能以清晰简洁的方式阐述一些最深奥的理念。

The contribution this book makes could hardly be called “small.” It stands as a monument to one of the great teachers of the twentieth century. Emil Gumbel was one of those rare individuals who combine extraordinary courage with an ability to communicate some of the most difficult ideas in a clear and concise fashion.

C章第七章

CHAPTER 7

费舍尔凯旋

FISHER TRIUMPHANT

T英国皇家统计学会通过其三份期刊发表文章，并全年举办会议，邀请专家学者在会上展示他们的最新研究成果。在他们的期刊上发表文章并非易事。文章必须经过至少两位审稿人的评审，以确保其内容正确；此外，副主编和主编都必须一致认为该文章显著增进了自然科学知识。而受邀在会议上发言则更是难上加难，这无疑是对该领域最杰出学者的莫大荣誉。

The Royal Statistical Society of England publishes articles in its three journals and sponsors meetings throughout each year, at which invited speakers present their latest work. It is difficult to get an article published in one of their journals. It has to be reviewed by at least two referees to see if it is correct; and both an associate editor and the main editor have to agree that it represents a significant increase in natural knowledge. It is an even higher hurdle to be invited to speak at a meeting. This is an honor reserved for only the most prominent workers in the field.

按照惯例，每次特邀报告结束后，都会安排听众进行讨论。部分成员会提前收到报告的副本，因此他们的讨论往往详尽而深刻。《皇家统计学会会刊》随后会同时发表报告和讨论者的评论。

It is the custom of the society to follow each invited talk with a discussion by the members of the audience. Selected members are provided with advance copies of the paper to be presented, so their contributions to the discussion are often detailed and trenchant. The Journal of the Royal Statistical Society then publishes both the paper and the comments of the discussants.

会议记录（如期刊所载）风格正式，带有浓厚的英式特色。会议主席（或其指定代表）起立，提议向发言者致谢，随后由主席发言。一位指定的资深学会成员起立附议致谢，并发表讲话。之后，学会中一些最知名的成员依次起立发言。会议经常邀请来自美国、英联邦国家和其他国家的访客，他们的发言也会被收录。发言者会对这些发言作出回应。讨论者和发言者都可以在发言稿发表前对其进行修改。

The discussion, as it appears in the journal, has a very formal and British tone. The chair of the meeting (or a designate) stands to move a vote of thanks for the speaker, followed by his comments. A designated senior member of the society then rises to second the vote of thanks, followed by his comments. Then, one by one, some of the most renowned members of the society rise to make their comments. Visitors are often invited from the United States, the Commonwealth, and from other nations, and their comments are included. The speaker responds to these comments. Both the discussants and the speaker are allowed to edit their words before they appear in the journal.

罗纳德·艾尔默·费舍尔，1890-1962年

Ronald Aylmer Fisher, 1890-1962

1934年12月18日，R.A.费舍尔教授（理学博士，英国皇家学会会员）荣幸地获得了发表论文的殊荣。在20世纪20年代几乎与世隔绝之后，费舍尔的才华终于得到了认可。在我们上次见到他时（在前几章中），他最高的学历是理学硕士，而他所在的“大学”是伦敦郊外一个偏远的农业实验站。到了1934年，他获得了理学博士学位，并当选为享有盛誉的英国皇家学会会员（因此有了FRS的缩写）。现在，英国皇家统计学会终于认可了他在该领域的领军人物地位。为了获得这份荣誉，费舍尔发表了一篇题为《归纳推理的逻辑》的论文。学会主席M.格林伍德教授（英国皇家学会会员）主持了会议。这篇印刷版文章共十六页，对费舍尔的最新研究成果进行了精心构建且清晰明了的总结。第一位发言者是AL Bowley教授，他起身提议致谢。他的发言继续如下：

On December 18, 1934, the singular honor of presenting such a paper was given to Professor R. A. Fisher, Sc.D., F.R.S. After his virtual isolation in the 1920s, Fisher was finally having his genius recognized. When we last saw him (in previous chapters), his most senior academic degree was an M.S., and his “university” was a remote agricultural experimental station outside of London. By 1934, he had accumulated an additional degree, doctor of science, and he had been elected a fellow of the prestigious Royal Society (hence the initials F.R.S.). Now, at last, the Royal Statistical Society was granting him a place among the leaders of the field. For this honor, Fisher presented a paper entitled “The Logic of Inductive Inference.” The president of the society, Professor M. Greenwood, F.R.S., was in the chair. The printed article runs for sixteen pages and presents a carefully constructed and very clear summary of Fisher’s most recent work. The first discussant is Professor A. L. Bowley, who rises to propose a vote of thanks. His comments continue:

我很高兴有机会感谢费舍尔教授，这不仅是因为他为我们朗读的论文，更是因为他对统计学的贡献。值此之际，我想表达我以及所有与我共事的统计学家们对费舍尔教授在统计学研究中所倾注的巨大热情、他所运用的强大数学工具、他在美国乃至世界各地广泛的影响力，以及他对正确运用数学的推动作用。

I am glad to have this opportunity of thanking Professor Fisher, not so much for the paper he has read to us, as for his contributions to statistics in general. This is an appropriate occasion to say that I, and all statisticians with whom I associate, appreciate the enormous amount of zeal he has brought to the study of statistics, the power of his mathematical tools, the extent of his influence here, in America and elsewhere, and the stimulus he has given to what he believes to be the correct application of mathematics.

卡尔·皮尔逊并未参与讨论。三年前，他从伦敦大学退休。在他的领导下，高尔顿生物统计实验室发展成为该大学正式的生物统计系。他退休后，该系被拆分为两个部门。罗纳德·艾尔默·费舍尔被任命为新成立的优生学系主任。卡尔·皮尔逊的儿子埃贡·皮尔逊则被任命为规模缩减后的生物统计系主任，同时负责高尔顿生物统计实验室，并担任《生物统计》（Biometrika）杂志的编辑。费舍尔与年轻的皮尔逊私交甚笃，这完全是费舍尔的错。他对埃贡·皮尔逊明显抱有敌意。这位温和的绅士不仅要忍受费舍尔对其父的厌恶，还要忍受费舍尔对耶日·内曼的反感——内曼与埃贡·皮尔逊的合作将在第十章中详述。然而，年轻的皮尔逊对费舍尔的工作极为尊重，并给予了高度评价。晚年，他写道，他早已习惯了费舍尔从未在出版物中提及他的名字。尽管存在这些紧张关系以及两个系之间的一些管辖权争议，费舍尔和埃贡·皮尔逊还是会互相派学生去听对方的课，并且避免公开争论。

Karl Pearson is not among the discussants. Three years earlier, he had retired from his position at the University of London. Under his leadership, the Galton Biometrical Laboratory had grown into a formal department of biometrics at the university. Upon his retirement, it was split into two departments. Ronald Aylmer Fisher was named chairman of the new Department of Eugenics.
