Psychical research refers to attempts to show, via scientific methods, the existence of clairvoyance. Psychical researchers make extensive use of statistical significance tests to show that their results are improbable in terms of the hypothesis that the results are due to purely random guesses by the subjects. In this article, Fisher condemns some writers for failing to use significance tests properly. He then states:

在运用生物学方法研究生物时，统计显著性检验至关重要。其作用在于防止我们被偶然事件所蒙蔽，这些偶然事件并非源于我们想要研究或试图发现的原因，而是由我们无法控制的诸多其他因素共同作用所致。如果一项观察结果在缺乏我们所寻求的那种真正原因的情况下极少出现，则该观察结果被认为是显著的。通常的做法是，如果一个结果的显著性水平足够大，以至于偶然出现的概率不超过二十次试验中的一次，则将其判定为显著。对于实践研究者而言，这是一个任意但方便的显著性水平，但这并不意味着他允许自己在每二十次实验中就被欺骗一次。显著性检验只是告诉他应该忽略哪些结果，即所有未获得显著结果的实验​​。只有当他知道如何设计一个几乎不会出现结果不显著的实验时，他才能声称某种现象可以通过实验证明。因此，那些他无法重复的孤立的显著结果，只能等待进一步的研究。

In the investigation of living beings by biological methods, statistical tests of significance are essential. Their function is to prevent us being deceived by accidental occurrences, due not to the causes we wish to study, or are trying to detect, but to a combination of many other circumstances which we cannot control. An observation is judged significant, if it would rarely have been produced, in the absence of a real cause of the kind we are seeking. It is a common practice to judge a result significant, if it is of such a magnitude that it would have been produced by chance not more frequently than once in twenty trials. This is an arbitrary, but convenient, level of significance for the practical investigator, but it does not mean that he allows himself to be deceived once in every twenty experiments. The test of significance only tells him what to ignore, namely all experiments in which significant results are not obtained. He should only claim that a phenomenon is experimentally demonstrable when he knows how to design an experiment so that it will rarely fail to give a significant result. Consequently, isolated significant results which he does not know how to reproduce are left in suspense pending further investigation.

注意“懂得如何设计实验……这种实验……几乎总能得出显著结果”这一表述。这正是费舍尔运用显著性检验的核心所在。在费舍尔看来，显著性检验只有在一系列旨在阐明特定处理效果的实验中才有意义。通读费舍尔的应用论文，人们会认为他运用显著性检验得出以下三种可能的结论之一：如果p值非常小（通常小于0.01），他宣称已证实存在效应；如果p值很大（通常大于0.20），他宣称即使存在效应，其强度也微乎其微，任何规模的实验都无法检测到；如果p值介于两者之间，他会探讨如何设计下一个实验以更好地了解效应。除了上述表述之外，费舍尔从未明确说明科学家应该如何解读p值。对费舍尔而言显而易见的事情，对读者而言可能并非如此。

Note the expression “knows how to design an experiment … that … will rarely fail to give a significant result.” This lies at the heart of Fisher’s use of significance tests. To Fisher, the significance test makes sense only in the context of a sequence of experiments, all aimed at elucidating the effects of specific treatments. Reading through Fisher’s applied papers, one is led to believe that he used significance tests to come to one of three possible conclusions. If the p-value is very small (usually less than .01), he declares that an effect has been shown. If the p-value is large (usually greater than .20), he declares that, if there is an effect, it is so small that no experiment of this size will be able to detect it. If the p-value lies in between, he discusses how the next experiment should be designed to get a better idea of the effect. Except for the above statement, Fisher was never explicit about how the scientist should interpret a p-value. What seemed to be intuitively clear to Fisher may not be clear to the reader.

我们将在第18章重新审视费舍尔对显著性检验的态度。这正是费舍尔犯下的一个重大错误的核心所在，即他坚持认为吸烟并未被证明有害健康。但我们暂且搁置费舍尔对吸烟与健康相关证据的精辟分析，先来看看1928年35岁的耶日·内曼的情况。

We will come back to reexamine Fisher’s attitude toward significance testing in chapter 18. It lies at the heart of one of Fisher’s great blunders, his insistence that smoking had not been shown to be harmful to health. But let us leave Fisher’s trenchant analysis of the evidence involving smoking and health for later and turn to 35-year-old Jerzy Neyman in the year 1928.

耶日·内曼的数学教育

JERZY NEYMAN’S MATHEMATICAL EDUCATION

第一次世界大战爆发时，耶日·内曼是一位前途无量的数学学生。他被迫逃往俄罗斯，在哈尔科夫大学学习。哈尔科夫大学是当时数学活动较为活跃的地区性大学。由于缺乏知识与时俱进的教师，加上战争导致他不得不中断学业，内曼便利用在哈尔科夫学到的基础数学知识，通过阅读当时能够找到的数学期刊文章来不断深造。因此，内曼接受了类似于十九世纪学生所接受的正规数学教育，然后又自学了二十世纪的数学。

Jerzy Neyman was a promising mathematics student when World War I erupted across his homeland in Eastern Europe. He was driven into Russia, where he studied at the University of Kharkov, a provincial outpost of mathematical activity. Lacking teachers who were up to date in their knowledge, and forced to miss semesters of schooling because of the war, he took the elementary mathematics he was taught at Kharkov and built upon it by seeking out articles in the mathematics journals available to him. Neyman thus received a formal mathematics education similar to that taught to students of the nineteenth century, and then educated himself into twentieth-century mathematics.

内曼能够查阅的期刊文章仅限于哈尔科夫大学图书馆以及后来波兰各省学校图书馆的馆藏。一次偶然的机会，他发现了一系列法国数学家亨利·勒贝格的文章。勒贝格（1875-1941）在二十世纪初创立了现代数学分析的许多基本思想，但他的论文晦涩难懂。勒贝格积分、勒贝格收敛定理以及这位伟大数学家的其他成就，都被后世的数学家们简化和整理，以更易于理解的形式呈现出来。如今，几乎无人再阅读勒贝格的原文。学生们都是通过这些后世的版本来学习他的思想。

The journal articles available to Neyman were limited to what he could find in the libraries of the University of Kharkov and later at provincial Polish schools. By chance, he came across a series of articles by Henri Lebesgue of France. Lebesgue (1875-1941) had created many of the fundamental ideas of modern mathematical analysis in the early years of the twentieth century, but his papers are difficult to read. Lebesgue integration, the Lebesgue convergence theorem, and other creations of this great mathematician have all been simplified and organized in more understandable forms by later mathematicians. Nowadays, no one reads Lebesgue in the original. Students all learn about his ideas through these later versions.

也就是说，除了耶日·内曼之外，没有人真正理解勒贝格的思想。内曼只有勒贝格的原始论文，他苦读这些论文，最终领略到这些对他而言全新的伟大著作的光芒。此后多年，内曼一直视勒贝格为偶像，并在20世纪30年代末，终于在法国的一次数学会议上见到了他。据内曼回忆，亨利·勒贝格是个粗鲁无礼的人，面对内曼的热情，他只是咕哝了几句，便在内曼讲话的过程中转身走开了。

No one, that is, except Jerzy Neyman, who had only Lebesgue’s original articles, who struggled through them, and who emerged seeing the brilliant light of these great new (to him) creations. For years afterward, Neyman idolized Lebesgue, and, in the late 1930s, finally got to meet him at a mathematics conference in France. According to Neyman, Henri Lebesgue turned out to be a gruff, impolite man, who responded to Neyman’s enthusiasm with a few mutterings and turned and walked away in the midst of Neyman’s talking to him.

内曼先生因这次拒绝而深受打击，或许正是以此为鉴，他总是格外注意待人，对年轻学生彬彬有礼、和蔼可亲，认真倾听他们的发言，并积极参与到他们的热情之中。这就是耶日·内曼先生。所有认识他的人都记得他的善良和体贴。他待人亲切周到，与人交往总是充满真诚的喜悦。我初次见到他时，他已年过八旬，身材矮小，举止优雅，衣着整洁，留着整齐的白色胡须。他聆听他人讲话时，蓝色的眼睛闪闪发光，与人深入交谈，无论对方是谁，他都给予同样的关注。

Neyman was deeply hurt by this rebuff, and perhaps with this as an object lesson, always went out of his way to be polite and kind to young students, to listen carefully to what they said, and to engage them in their enthusiasms. That was Jerzy Neyman, the man. All who knew him remember him for his kindness and caring manners. He was gracious and thoughtful and dealt with people with genuine pleasure. When I met him, he was in his early eighties, a small, dignified, well-groomed man with a neat white moustache. His blue eyes sparkled as he listened to others and engaged in intensive conversation, giving the same personal attention to everyone, no matter who they were.

在职业生涯早期，耶日·内曼设法在华沙大学找到了一份初级教员的工作。当时，刚刚独立的波兰资金匮乏，难以支持学术研究，数学家的职位更是稀缺。1928年夏天，他在伦敦的生物统计实验室工作，在那里结识了埃贡·S·皮尔逊及其妻子艾琳和两个女儿。埃贡·皮尔逊是卡尔·皮尔逊的儿子，父子俩的性格对比可谓天壤之别。卡尔·皮尔逊强势进取，而埃贡·皮尔逊则腼腆谦逊。卡尔·皮尔逊常常急于提出新想法，发表的文章往往数学推导模糊不清，甚至存在一些错误。而埃贡·皮尔逊则极其谨慎，对每一个计算的细节都精益求精。

In the early years of his career, Jerzy Neyman managed to find a position as a junior member of the faculty of the University of Warsaw. At that time, the newly independent nation of Poland had little money to support academic research, and positions for mathematicians were scarce. In 1928, he spent a summer at the biometrical laboratory in London and there came to know Egon S. Pearson and his wife, Eileen, and their two daughters. Egon Pearson was Karl Pearson’s son, and a more striking contrast in personalities is hard to find. Where Karl Pearson was driving and dominating, Egon Pearson was shy and self-effacing. Karl Pearson rushed through new ideas, often publishing an article with the mathematics vaguely sketched in or even with some errors. Egon Pearson was extremely careful, worrying over the details of each calculation.

埃贡·皮尔逊和耶日·内曼之间的友谊在他们1928年至1933年间的书信往来中得以保存。这些信件为我们深入了解科学社会学提供了绝佳的视角，展现了两位极具创造力的思想家如何共同探讨问题，彼此提出见解或批评对方的观点。皮尔逊谦逊的性格在他犹豫地提出内曼提出的某些方案可能行不通时显露无疑。而内曼卓越的独创性则体现在他能够洞悉复杂问题的本质，直击每个难题的根源。对于想要了解数学研究为何常常需要合作的人来说，我推荐阅读内曼-皮尔逊的信件。

The friendship between Egon Pearson and Jerzy Neyman is preserved in their exchange of letters between 1928 and 1933. These letters provide a wonderful insight into the sociology of science, showing how two original minds grapple with a problem, each one proposing ideas or criticizing the ideas of the other. Pearson’s self-effacing comes to the forefront as he hesitantly suggests that perhaps something Neyman had proposed might not work out. Neyman’s great originality comes out as he cuts through complicated problems to find the essential nature of each difficulty. For someone who wants to understand why mathematical research is so often a cooperative venture, I recommend the Neyman-Pearson letters.

埃贡·皮尔逊最初向内曼提出的问题是什么？回想一下卡尔·皮尔逊的卡方拟合优度检验。他开发该检验是为了检验观测数据是否符合理论分布。但实际上并不存在所谓的卡方拟合优度检验。分析师可以采用无数种方法将该检验应用于给定的数据集。似乎没有标准来判断如何从众多选择中“最佳”地进行选择。每次应用该检验时，分析师都必须做出任意选择。埃贡·皮尔逊向耶日·内曼提出了以下问题：

What was the problem that Egon Pearson first proposed to Neyman? Recall Karl Pearson’s chi square goodness of fit test. He developed it to test whether observed data fit a theoretical distribution. There really is no such thing as the chi square goodness of fit test. The analyst has available an infinite number of ways to apply the test to a given set of data. There appeared to be no criterion on how “best” to pick among those many choices. Every time the test is applied, the analyst must make arbitrary choices. Egon Pearson posed the following question to Jerzy Neyman:

如果我对一组数据应用了卡方拟合优度检验，并将其与正态分布进行比较，但未能得到显著的p值，我如何才能确定这些数据确实符合正态分布？也就是说，我如何才能确定另一种卡方检验方法或其他尚未发现的拟合优度检验方法可能不会产生显著的p值，从而导致我无法拒绝正态分布作为数据的拟合假设？

If I have applied a chi square goodness of fit test to a set of data versus the normal distribution, and if I have failed to get a significant p-value, how do I know that the data really fit a normal distribution? That is, how do I know that another version of the chi square test or another goodness of fit test as yet undiscovered might not have produced a significant p-value and allowed me to reject the normal distribution as fitting the data?

内曼的数学风格

NEYMAN’S STYLE OF MATHEMATICS

内曼将这个问题带回了华沙，两人开始了书信往来。内曼和年轻的皮尔逊都对费舍尔基于似然函数的估计概念印象深刻。他们首先研究了与拟合优度检验相关的似然函数。他们的第一篇合作论文描述了这些研究的结果。这篇论文是他们三篇经典论文中最难懂的一篇，这三篇论文彻底革新了显著性检验的理念。随着研究的深入，内曼敏锐的洞察力不断将问题提炼到其本质要素，他们的工作也变得更加清晰易懂。

Neyman took this question back to Warsaw with him, and the exchange of letters began. Both Neyman and young Pearson were impressed with Fisher’s concept of estimation based on the likelihood function. They began their investigation by looking at the likelihood associated with a goodness of fit test. The first of their joint papers describes the results of those investigations. It is the most difficult of the three classic papers they produced, which were to revolutionize the whole idea of significance testing. As they continued looking at the question, Neyman’s great clarity of vision kept distilling the problem down to its essential elements, and their work became clearer and easier to understand.

尽管读者可能难以置信，但文学风格在数学研究中扮演着重要的角色。有些数学作者似乎无法写出通俗易懂的文章。另一些作者则似乎乐于堆砌大量的符号，细节繁复，以至于核心思想被淹没在琐碎的细节之中。然而，有些作者却能以简洁有力的方式阐述复杂的概念，使整个过程显得自然而然。只有在回顾所学内容之后，读者才能真正领悟到这些成果的强大力量。耶日·内曼就是这样一位作者。阅读他的论文是一种享受。他的思想自然流畅，符号看似简单，结论也显得如此合情合理，以至于你会不禁疑惑，为何此前无人提出过这些成果。

Although the reader may not believe it, literary style plays an important role in mathematical research. Some mathematical writers seem unable to produce articles that are easy to understand. Others seem to get a perverse pleasure out of generating many lines of symbolic notation so filled with detail that the general idea is lost in the picayune. But some authors have the ability to display complicated ideas with such force and simplicity that the development appears to be obvious in their exposition. Only upon reviewing what has been learned does the reader realize the great power of the results. Such an author was Jerzy Neyman. It is a pleasure to read his papers. The ideas evolve naturally, the notation is deceptively simple, and the conclusions appear to be so natural that you find it hard to see why no one produced these results long before.

我在辉瑞中央研究院工作了二十七年，该研究院每年都会在康涅狄格大学举办一次学术研讨会。大学统计系会邀请一位生物统计学领域的权威人士前来，与学生们交流，并在下午晚些时候发表演讲。由于我参与了该系列研讨会的资助申请工作，我有幸通过他们结识了一些统计学界的泰斗。耶日·内曼就是其中一位受邀嘉宾。他要求演讲采用特定的形式：先发表一篇论文，然后邀请专家小组对他的论文进行点评。鉴于嘉宾是声名显赫的耶日·内曼，研讨会组织者联系了新英格兰地区一些知名的资深统计学家组成专家小组。然而，在最后一刻，一位专家组成员无法出席，于是我被邀请顶替他。

Pfizer Central Research, where I worked for twenty-seven years, sponsors a yearly colloquium at the University of Connecticut. The statistics department of the university invites a major figure in biostatistical research to come for a day, meet with students, and then present a talk in the late afternoon. Since I was involved in setting up the grant for this series, I had the honor of meeting some of the great men of statistics through them. Jerzy Neyman was one such invitee. He asked that his talk have a particular form. He wanted to present a paper and then have a panel of discussants who would criticize his paper. Because this was the renowned Jerzy Neyman, the organizers of the symposium contacted well-known senior statisticians in the New England area to constitute the panel. At the last minute, one of the panelists was unable to come, and I was asked to substitute for him.

内曼给我们发了一份他计划发表的论文。这是一项令人兴奋的研究成果，他将1939年的研究成果应用于天文学领域的一个问题。我知道那篇1939年的论文；几年前我还在读研究生的时候就发现了它，并被它深深吸引。这篇论文探讨了内曼发现的一类新的分布，他称之为“传染性分布”。论文提出的问题始于对土壤中昆虫幼虫出现情况的建模。雌性昆虫带着满载卵的昆虫在田野里飞来飞去，然后随机选择一个地点产卵。卵产下后，幼虫孵化并从产卵点向外爬出。从田野里采集一份土壤样本。求该样本中幼虫数量的概率分布。

Neyman had sent us a copy of the paper he planned to present. It was an exciting development, wherein he applied work he had done in 1939 to a problem in astronomy. I knew that 1939 paper; I had discovered it years before while still a graduate student, and I had been impressed by it. The paper dealt with a new class of distributions Neyman had discovered, which he called the “contagious distributions.” The problem posed in the paper began with trying to model the appearance of insect grubs in soil. The female insect flew about the field, laden with eggs, then chose a spot at random to lay the eggs. Once the eggs were laid, the grubs hatched and crawled outward from that spot. A sample of soil is taken from the field. What is the probability distribution of the number of grubs found in that sample?

传染性分布描述了这类情况。1939 年的一篇论文用一系列看似简单的方程推导出了这些分布。这种推导似乎显而易见且自然。当读者读到论文结尾时，就会明白没有其他方法可以解决这个问题，但这只有在阅读了内曼的著作之后才能明白。自 1939 年那篇论文发表以来，人们发现内曼的传染性分布适用于医学研究、冶金学、气象学、毒理学以及（正如内曼在辉瑞研讨会论文中所述）宇宙星系分布研究等诸多领域。

The contagious distributions describe such situations. They are derived in the 1939 paper with an apparently simple series of equations. This derivation seems obvious and natural. It is clear, when the reader gets to the end of the paper, that there is no other way to approach it, but this is clear only after reading Neyman. Since that 1939 paper, Neyman’s contagious distributions have been found to fit a great many situations in medical research, in metallurgy, in meteorology, in toxicology, and (as described by Neyman in his Pfizer Colloquium paper) in dealing with the distribution of galaxies in the universe.

内曼演讲结束后，便靠在椅背上聆听小组讨论。小组其他成员都是杰出的统计学家，他们此前因工作繁忙而无暇阅读他的论文。他们将辉瑞研讨会视为对内曼的荣誉认可。他们的“讨论”主要围绕内曼的职业生涯和过往成就展开。我是临时顶替加入小组的，因此无法提及我之前（并不存在的）与内曼的交往经历。正如他所要求的，我的发言都围绕着内曼当天的演讲展开。我特别讲述了自己多年前如何发现他1939年的那篇论文，以及为了这次研讨会而重读这篇论文的经历。我尽我所能地描述了这篇论文，并在谈到内曼巧妙地阐述分布参数含义时，表达了我的热情。

After he finished his talk, Neyman sat back to listen to the panel of discussants. All the other members of the panel were prominent statisticians who had been too busy to read his paper in advance. They looked upon the Pfizer Colloquium as a recognition of honor for Neyman. Their “discussions” consisted of comments about Neyman’s career and past accomplishments. I had come onto the panel as a last-minute replacement and could not refer to my (nonexistent) previous experiences with Neyman. My comments were directed to Neyman’s presentation that day, as he had asked. In particular, I told how I had discovered the 1939 paper years before and revisited it in anticipation of this session. I described the paper, as best I could, showing enthusiasm when I came to the clever way Neyman had developed the meaning of the parameters of the distribution.

内曼显然对我的评论非常满意。之后，我们围绕传染分布及其应用展开了热烈的讨论。几周后，我收到一个大包裹。里面是加州大学出版社出版的《J. 内曼早期统计论文选集》。扉页上写着：“致大卫·萨尔斯堡博士，衷心感谢他对我1974年4月30日报告的精彩评论。J. 内曼。”

Neyman was clearly delighted with my comments. Afterward, we had an exciting discussion about the contagious distributions and their uses. A few weeks later, a large package arrived in the mail. It was a copy of A Selection of Early Statistical Papers of J. Neyman, published by the University of California Press. On the inside cover was the inscription: “To Dr. David Salsburg, with hearty thanks for his interesting comments on my presentation of April 30, 1974. J. Neyman.”

我珍爱这本书，不仅因为上面的题词，更因为其中收录的那些优美流畅、文笔精湛的论文。此后，我有幸与内曼的许多学生和同事交流。1974年我遇到的那位友善、风趣、求知欲强的内曼，正是他们所认识和敬仰的那位。

I treasure this book both for the inscription and the set of beautiful, well-written papers in it. I have since had the opportunity to talk with many of Neyman’s students and coworkers. The friendly, interesting, and interested man I met in 1974 was the man that they knew and admired.

第11章

CHAPTER 11

假设检验

HYPOTHESIS TESTING

一个在合作之初，埃贡·皮尔逊问耶日·内曼，如果检验正态性时没有发现显著的p值，如何才能确定一组数据服从正态分布。他们的合作始于这个问题，但皮尔逊最初的问题引出了一个更广泛的问题：显著性检验中出现非显著结果意味着什么？如果我们未能反驳某个假设，我们能否得出该假设为真的结论？

At the start of their collaboration, Egon Pearson asked Jerzy Neyman how he could be sure that a set of data was normally distributed if he failed to find a significant p-value when testing for normality. Their collaboration started with this question, but Pearson’s initial question opened the door to a much broader one. What does it mean to have a nonsignificant result in a significance test? Can we conclude that a hypothesis is true if we have failed to refute it?

R.A. Fisher曾以一种间接的方式探讨过这个问题。Fisher认为，较大的p值（以及未能发现显著性）表明数据不足以做出判断。在Fisher看来，未能发现显著性并不意味着所检验的假设是正确的。引用他的话来说：

R. A. Fisher had addressed that question in an indirect way. Fisher would take large p-values (and a failure to find significance) as indicating that the data were inadequate to decide. To Fisher, there was never a presumption that a failure to find significance meant that the tested hypothesis was true. To quote him:

仅仅因为某个假设没有被现有事实反驳就断定它是正确的这种逻辑谬误，在统计学中和其他科学推理中一样，都无权置身事外……。因此，如果人们普遍理解，当显著性检验被准确使用时，它能够驳斥或否定那些与数据相悖的假设，但永远无法确凿地证明假设是正确的，那么这将大大有助于人们更清晰地理解显著性检验的意义……

For the logical fallacy of believing that a hypothesis has been proved to be true, merely because it is not contradicted by the available facts, has no more right to insinuate itself in statistical than in other kinds of scientific reasoning … . It would, therefore, add greatly to the clarity with which the tests of significance are regarded if it were generally understood that tests of significance, when used accurately, are capable of rejecting or invalidating hypotheses, in so far as they are contradicted by the data: but that they are never capable of establishing them as certainly true … .

卡尔·皮尔逊曾经常使用卡方拟合优度检验来“证明”数据服从特定的分布。费舍尔为数理统计引入了更为严谨的方法，卡尔·皮尔逊的方法不再适用。然而，问题依然存在：为了确定需要估计哪些参数，以及这些参数与当前科学问题之间的关系，必须假设数据服从特定的分布。统计学家们常常倾向于使用显著性检验来证明这一点。

Karl Pearson had often used his chi square goodness of fit test to “prove” that data followed particular distributions. Fisher had introduced more rigor into mathematical statistics, and Karl Pearson’s methods were no longer acceptable. The question still remained. It was necessary to assume that the data fit a particular distribution, in order to know which parameters to estimate and determine how those parameters relate to the scientific question at hand. The statisticians were frequently tempted to use significance tests to prove that.

在他们的通信中，埃贡·皮尔逊和耶日·内曼探讨了显著性检验中出现的几个悖论，即不加思考地使用显著性检验会否定一个显然为真的假设。费舍尔从未陷入这些悖论，因为对他来说，显然是显著性检验被错误地应用了。内曼询问判断显著性检验是否正确应用的标准是什么。在他们的通信中，随着内曼夏季访问英国以及皮尔逊访问波兰，假设检验的基本思想逐渐形成。[12]

In their correspondence, Egon Pearson and Jerzy Neyman explored several paradoxes that emerged from significance testing, cases where unthinking use of a significance test would reject a hypothesis that was obviously true. Fisher never fell into those paradoxes, because it would have been obvious to him that the significance tests were being applied incorrectly. Neyman asked what criteria were being used to decide when a significance test was applied correctly. Gradually, between their letters, with visits that Neyman made to England during the summers and Pearson’s visits to Poland, the basic ideas of hypothesis testing emerged.[12]

现在，所有基础统计学教材中都收录了简化版的奈曼-皮尔逊假设检验公式。它的结构简单明了，我发现大多数一年级学生都能轻松理解。由于该公式已被系统化，因此既精确又具有指导意义。教材暗示，这才是唯一正确的方法。这种严谨的假设检验方法已被美国食品药品监督管理局（FDA）和环境保护署（EPA）等监管机构所接受，并在医学院校中教授给未来的医学研究人员。此外，在处理某些类型的歧视案件时，它也逐渐渗透到法律诉讼程序中。

A simplified version of the Neyman-Pearson formulation of hypothesis testing can now be found in all elementary statistics text-books. It has a simple structure. I have found that it is easy for most first-year students to understand. Since it has been codified, this version of the formulation is exact and didactic. This is how it must be done, the texts imply, and this is the only way it can be done. This rigid approach to hypothesis testing has been accepted by regulatory agencies like the U.S. Food and Drug Administration and the Environmental Protection Agency, and it is taught in medical schools to future medical researchers. It has also wormed its way into legal proceedings when dealing with certain types of discrimination cases.

当以这种僵化、简化的版本来讲解内曼-皮尔逊公式时，会因为过于关注公式的错误方面而歪曲他的发现。内曼的主要发现是，除非至少存在两个可能的假设，否则显著性检验毫无意义。也就是说，除非存在某种或一组其他分布，并且你认为数据符合这些分布，否则你无法检验数据是否符合正态分布。这些备择假设的选择决定了显著性检验的运行方式。他将检测到该备择假设（如果其为真）的概率称为检验的“功效”。在数学中，清晰的思维是通过为特定概念赋予清晰、明确的名称来实现的。为了区分用于计算费舍尔p值的假设和其他可能的假设，内曼和皮尔逊将正在检验的假设称为“零假设”，将其他假设称为“备择假设”。在他们的公式中，p 值是用来检验零假设的，而功效指的是如果备择假设实际上是正确的，那么这个 p 值会如何变化。

When the Neyman-Pearson formulation is taught in this rigid, simplified version of what Neyman developed, it distorts his discoveries by concentrating on the wrong aspects of the formulation. Neyman’s major discovery was that significance testing made no sense unless there were at least two possible hypotheses. That is, you could not test whether data fit a normal distribution unless there was some other distribution or set of distributions that you believed it would fit. The choice of these alternative hypotheses dictates the way in which the significance test is run. The probability of detecting that alternative, if it is true, he called the “power” of the test. In mathematics, clarity of thought is developed by giving clear, well-defined names to specific concepts. In order to distinguish between the hypothesis being used to compute Fisher’s p-value and the other possible hypothesis or hypotheses, Neyman and Pearson called the hypothesis being tested the “null hypothesis” and the other hypotheses the “alternative.” In their formulation, the p-value is calculated for testing the null hypothesis but the power refers to how this p-value will behave if the alternative is, in fact, true.

这使内曼得出两个结论。第一，检验效能衡量的是检验的有效性。在两个检验中，效能更高的那个更好。第二，备择假设集不能太大。分析师不能断言数据来自正态分布（零假设），也不能断言数据来自任何其他可能的分布。备择假设集过大，任何检验都无法对所有可能的备择假设都有效。

This led Neyman to two conclusions. One was that the power of a test was a measure of how good the test was. The more powerful of two tests was the better one to use. The second conclusion was that the set of alternatives cannot be too large. The analyst cannot say that the data come from a normal distribution (the null hypothesis) or that they come from any other possible distribution. That is too wide a set of alternatives, and no test can be powerful against all possible alternatives.

1956年，芝加哥大学的L.J. Savage和Raj Raghu Bahadur证明，备择假设的范畴不必非常宽泛，假设检验就可能失效。他们构建了一个相对较小的备择假设集，针对该集合的任何检验都无效。20世纪50年代，Neyman发展了限制性假设检验的概念，即备择假设的集合被非常严格地定义。他证明，这种检验比处理更广泛假设集合的检验更有效。

In 1956, L. J. Savage and Raj Raghu Bahadur at the University of Chicago showed that the class of alternatives does not have to be very wide for hypothesis testing to fail. They constructed a relatively small set of alternative hypotheses against which no test had any power. During the 1950s, Neyman developed the idea of restricted hypothesis tests, where the set of alternative hypotheses is very narrowly defined. He showed that such tests are more powerful than ones dealing with more inclusive sets of hypotheses.

在许多情况下，假设检验是用来反驳一个不切实际的零假设。例如，在临床试验中比较两种药物时，待检验的零假设是两种药物的疗效相同。然而，如果零假设成立，那么这项研究根本就不会进行。两种疗法相同的零假设是一个不切实际的假设，其目的就是通过研究结果来推翻它。因此，根据内曼的观点，研究设计应该旨在最大限度地提高所得数据的统计效力，从而推翻这个不切实际的假设，并揭示两种药物疗效的差异。

In many situations, hypothesis tests are used against a null hypothesis that is a straw man. For instance, when two drugs are being compared in a clinical trial, the null hypothesis to be tested is that the two drugs produce the same effect. However, if that were true, then the study would never have been run. The null hypothesis that the two treatments are the same is a straw man, meant to be knocked down by the results of the study. So, following Neyman, the design of the study should be aimed at maximizing the power of the resulting data to knock down that straw man and show how the drugs differ in effect.

什么是概率？

WHAT IS PROBABILITY?

不幸的是，为了发展出一种内部一致的假设检验数学方法，内曼不得不面对费舍尔一直回避的问题。尽管内曼提出了简洁的纯数学解决方案，但这个问题仍然困扰着假设检验。它也是统计方法应用于科学领域的一个普遍问题。更广义地说，这个问题可以概括为：概率在现实生活中究竟意味着什么？

Unfortunately, to develop a mathematical approach to hypothesis testing that was internally consistent, Neyman had to deal with a problem that Fisher had swept under the rug. This is a problem that continues to plague hypothesis testing, in spite of Neyman’s neat, purely mathematical solution. It is a problem in the application of statistical methods to science in general. In its more general form, it can be summed up in the question: What is meant by probability in real life?

统计学的数学公式可以用来计算概率。这些概率使我们能够将统计方法应用于科学问题。就所使用的数学而言，概率的定义是明确的。然而，这个抽象的概念如何与现实联系起来？当科学家试图判断什么是真什么是假时，他们应该如何解读统计分析中的概率陈述？本书最后一章将探讨这一普遍问题以及人们为解答这些问题所做的尝试。但现在，我们将先考察迫使内曼找到他自己答案的具体情况。

The mathematical formulations of statistics can be used to compute probabilities. Those probabilities enable us to apply statistical methods to scientific problems. In terms of the mathematics used, probability is well defined. How does this abstract concept connect to reality? How is the scientist to interpret the probability statements of statistical analyses when trying to decide what is true and what is not? In the final chapter of this book I shall examine the general problem and the attempts that have been made to answer these questions. For the moment, however, we will examine the specific circumstances that forced Neyman to find his version of an answer.

回想一下，费舍尔使用显著性检验得出了一个数值，费舍尔称之为p值。这是一个计算出的概率，即在零假设为真的情况下，与观测数据相关的概率。例如，假设我们想测试一种新药预防乳腺癌复发的疗效，对象是已接受乳房切除术的患者，并将其与安慰剂进行比较。零假设（即稻草人假设）是该药物并不比安慰剂更好。假设五年后，服用安慰剂的女性中有50%出现复发，而服用新药的女性中无人复发。这是否证明新药“有效”？答案当然取决于这50%代表了多少患者。

Recall that Fisher’s use of a significance test produced a number Fisher called the p-value. This is a calculated probability, a probability associated with the observed data under the assumption that the null hypothesis is true. For instance, suppose we wish to test a new drug for the prevention of a recurrence of breast cancer in patients who have had mastectomies, comparing it to a placebo. The null hypothesis, the straw man, is that the drug is no better than the placebo. Suppose that after five years, 50 percent of the women on placebos have had a recurrence and none of the women on the new drug have. Does this prove that the new drug “works”? The answer, of course, depends upon how many patients that 50 percent represents.

如果研究每组只包含四名女性，这意味着我们有八名患者，其中两名复发。假设我们随机选取八人，标记其中两人，然后将这八人随机分成两组，每组四人。被标记的两人都落入其中一组的概率约为 0.30。如果每组只有四名女性，那么所有复发病例都发生在安慰剂组这一事实并不具有统计学意义。如果研究每组包含 500 名女性，那么除非药物有效，否则所有 250 名复发患者都服用安慰剂的可能性极低。如果药物疗效不优于安慰剂，那么所有 250 名复发患者都落入同一组的概率就是 p 值，计算结果小于 0.0001。

If the study included only four women in each group, that means we had eight patients, two of whom had a recurrence. Suppose we take any group of eight people, tag two of them, and divide the eight at random into two groups of four. The probability that both of the tagged people will fall into one of the groups is around .30. If there were only four women in each group, the fact that all the recurrences fell in the placebo group is not significant. If the study included 500 women in each group, it would be highly unlikely that all 250 with recurrences were on the placebo, unless the drug was working. The probability that all 250 would fall in one group if the drug was no better than the placebo is the p-value, which calculates to be less than .0001.

p 值是一种概率，其计算方法如下。既然它被用来证明其计算所依据的假设是错误的，那么它究竟意味着什么呢？它是与在最有可能为假的条件下观察到的结果相关的理论概率。它与现实无关。它是对合理性的间接衡量。它并非我们断言药物有效的概率，也并非任何类型错误的概率，更不是患者服用安慰剂和服用药物疗效相同的概率。但是，为了确定哪些检验方法优于其他方法，内曼必须找到一种方法，将假设检验置于一个框架内，在该框架内，可以计算与检验结果相关的决策概率。他需要将假设检验的 p 值与现实生活联系起来。

The p-value is a probability, and this is how it is computed. Since it is used to show that the hypothesis under which it is calculated is false, what does it really mean? It is a theoretical probability associated with the observations under conditions that are most likely false. It has nothing to do with reality. It is an indirect measurement of plausibility. It is not the probability that we would be wrong to say the drug works. It is not the probability of any kind of error. It is not the probability that a patient will do as well on the placebo as on the drug. But, to determine which tests are better than others, Neyman had to find a way to put hypothesis testing within a framework wherein probabilities associated with the decisions made from the test could be calculated. He needed to connect the p-values of the hypothesis test to real life.

频率学派对概率的定义

THE FREQUENTIST DEFINITION OF PROBABILITY

1872年，英国哲学家约翰·维恩提出了一个更符合现实生活的数学概率公式。他颠覆了概率论的一个重要定理——大数定律。大数定律指出，如果某个事件具有给定的概率（例如掷一枚骰子，结果为6点朝上），并且我们反复进行相同的试验，那么该事件发生的次数比例将越来越接近其给定的概率。

In 1872, John Venn, the British philosopher, had proposed a formulation of mathematical probability that would make sense in real life. He turned a major theorem of probability on its head. This is the law of large numbers, which says that if some event has a given probability (like throwing a single die and having it land with the six side up) and if we run identical trials over and over again, the proportion of times that event occurs will get closer and closer to the probability.

维恩认为，某个事件发生的概率是指该事件长期发生次数的比例。在维恩的理论中，概率论并非蕴含大数定律，而是大数定律蕴含了概率。这就是频率学派对概率的定义。1921年，约翰·梅纳德·凯恩斯[13]驳斥了这种解释，认为它既无用也无意义，并指出其存在根本性的矛盾，使得频率学派的概率定义在大多数需要用到概率的场合都无法适用。

Venn said the probability associated with a given event is the long-run proportion of times the event occurs. In Venn’s proposal, the mathematical theory of probability did not imply the law of large numbers; the law of large numbers implied probability. This is the frequentist definition of probability. In 1921, John Maynard Keynes[13] demolished this as a useful or even meaningful interpretation, showing that it has fundamental inconsistencies that make it impossible to apply the frequentist definition in most cases where probability is invoked.

在以正式的数学方式构建假设检验时，内曼采用了维恩的频率学派定义。他以此来解释他对假设检验中p值的理解。在内曼-皮尔逊公式中，科学家设定一个固定值，例如0.05，并在显著性检验的p值小于或等于0.05时拒绝原假设。这样，从长远来看，科学家拒绝一个正确的原假设的概率恰好为5%。如今的假设检验教学强调了内曼对频率学派方法的运用。然而，人们很容易将内曼-皮尔逊的假设检验公式视为频率学派概率论的一部分，而忽略了内曼提出的更重要的见解，即需要一组定义明确的备择假设来检验原假设这个“稻草人”。

When it came to structuring hypothesis tests in a formal mathematical way, Neyman fell back upon Venn’s frequentist definition. Neyman used this to justify his interpretation of the p-value in a hypothesis test. In the Neyman-Pearson formulation, the scientist sets a fixed number, such as .05, and rejects the null hypothesis whenever the significance test p-value is less than or equal to .05. This way, in the long run, the scientist will reject a true null hypothesis exactly 5 percent of the time. The way hypothesis testing is now taught, Neyman’s invocation of the frequentist approach is emphasized. It is too easy to view the Neyman-Pearson formulation of hypothesis testing as a part of the frequentist approach to probability and to ignore the more important insights that Neyman provided about the need for a well-defined set of alternative hypotheses against which to test the straw man of the null hypothesis.

费舍尔误解了内曼的见解。他只关注显著性水平的定义，却忽略了权力以及定义备选方案类别的必要性等重要概念。在批评内曼时，他写道：

Fisher misunderstood Neyman’s insights. He concentrated on the definition of significance level, missing the important ideas of power and the need to define the class of alternatives. In criticism of Neyman, he wrote:

内曼以为他是在修正和改进我早期关于显著性检验的研究，以此作为“增进自然知识”的手段，但实际上他却用一种被称为验收程序的技术和商业机制重新诠释了我的研究。验收程序在现代社会至关重要。我想，像英国皇家海军这样的大型机构从工程公司接收物资时，必然会经过足够仔细的检验和测试，以降低接收不合格或有缺陷货物的频率……但是，在我看来，这种操作与通过物理或生物实验进行科学发现的工作之间存在着巨大的逻辑差异，二者之间的类比毫无益处，将两者混为一谈显然是误导性的。

Neyman, thinking he was correcting and improving my own early work on tests of significance, as a means to the “improvement of natural knowledge,” in fact reinterpreted them in terms of that technological and commercial apparatus which is known as an acceptance procedure. Now, acceptance procedures are of great importance in the modern world. When a large concern like the Royal Navy receives material from an engineering firm it is, I suppose, subjected to sufficiently careful inspection and testing to reduce the frequency of the acceptance of faulty or defective consignments … . But, the logical differences between such an operation and the work of scientific discovery by physical or biological experimentation seem to me so wide that the analogy between them is not helpful, and the identification of the two sorts of operations is decidedly misleading.

尽管内曼的基本思想被曲解，假设检验仍然成为科学研究中最广泛使用的统计工具。耶日·内曼精妙的数学理论如今已成为许多科学领域的固定准则。大多数科学期刊都要求文章作者在数据分析中使用假设检验。其应用范围已超越科学期刊。美国、加拿大和欧洲的药品监管机构要求在提交的申请中使用假设检验。法院已接受假设检验作为一种适当的证明方法，并允许原告使用它来证明就业歧视。它渗透到统计科学的各个分支。

In spite of these distortions of Neyman’s basic ideas, hypothesis testing has become the most widely used statistical tool in scientific research. The exquisite mathematics of Jerzy Neyman have now become an idée fixe in many parts of science. Most scientific journals require that the authors of articles include hypothesis testing in their data analyses. It has extended beyond the scientific journals. Drug regulatory authorities in the United States, Canada, and Europe require the use of hypothesis tests in submissions. Courts of law have accepted hypothesis testing as an appropriate method of proof and allow plaintiffs to use it to show employment discrimination. It permeates all branches of statistical science.

奈曼-皮尔逊公式攀升至统计学巅峰并非一帆风顺。费舍尔从一开始就对其提出质疑，并终其一生都在抨击它。1955年，他在《皇家统计学会杂志》上发表了题为《统计方法与科学归纳》的论文，并在其最后一部著作《统计方法与科学推断》中对此进行了扩展。20世纪60年代末，即将担任《生物统计学》杂志主编的戴维·考克斯发表了一篇尖锐的分析文章，阐述了假设检验在科学中的实际应用，指出奈曼的频率论解释与实际情况并不相符。20世纪80年代，W·爱德华兹·戴明彻底抨击了假设检验的概念，认为其毫无意义。（我们将在第24章中再次探讨戴明对统计学的影响。）年复一年，统计学文献中不断涌现出新的文章，指出教科书中固守的奈曼-皮尔逊公式存在新的缺陷。

The climb of the Neyman-Pearson formulation to the pinnacle of statistics did not go unchallenged. Fisher attacked it from its inception and continued to attack it for the rest of his life. In 1955, he published a paper entitled “Statistical Methods and Scientific Induction” in the Journal of the Royal Statistical Society, and he expanded on this with his last book, Statistical Methods and Scientific Inference. In the late 1960s, David Cox, soon to be the editor of Biometrika, published a trenchant analysis of how hypothesis tests are actually used in science, showing that Neyman’s frequentist interpretation was inappropriate to what is actually done. In the 1980s, W. Edwards Deming attacked the entire idea of hypothesis testing as nonsensical. (We shall come back to Deming’s influence on statistics in chapter 24.) Year after year, articles continue to appear in the statistical literature that find new faults with the Neyman-Pearson formulation as frozen in the textbooks.

内曼本人并未参与内曼-皮尔逊假设检验公式的确立。早在1935年，他就在《法国数学会会刊》（Bulletin de la Société Mathématique de France）上发表的一篇法文文章中，对是否存在最优假设检验提出了严重的质疑。在他后期的论文中，内曼很少直接使用假设检验。他的统计方法通常是先从理论原理推导出概率分布，然后再根据数据估计参数。

Neyman himself took no part in the canonization of the Neyman-Pearson formulation of hypothesis tesing. As early as 1935, in an article he published (in French) in the Bulletin de la Société Mathématique de France, he raised serious doubts about whether optimum hypothesis tests could be found. In his later papers, Neyman seldom made use of hypothesis tests directly. His statistical approaches usually involved deriving probability distributions from theoretical principles and then estimating the parameters from the data.

其他人继承了奈曼-皮尔逊公式背后的思想并加以发展。二战期间，亚伯拉罕·瓦尔德扩展了奈曼对维恩频率学派定义的运用，发展了统计决策理论领域。埃里希·莱曼提出了其他检验有效性的标准，并在1959年撰写了一部关于假设检验的权威教科书，该书至今仍是文献中对奈曼-皮尔逊假设检验最完整的描述。

Others picked up the ideas behind the Neyman-Pearson formulation and developed them. During World War II, Abraham Wald expanded on Neyman’s use of Venn’s frequentist definitions to develop the field of statistical decision theory. Erich Lehmann produced alternative criteria for good tests and then, in 1959, wrote a definitive textbook on the subject of hypothesis testing, which remains the most complete description of Neyman-Pearson hypothesis testing in the literature.

就在希特勒入侵波兰、将邪恶的帷幕笼罩欧洲大陆之前，内曼来到美国，在加州大学伯克利分校创建了统计学项目。他一直留在那里，直到1981年去世，一手打造了世界上最重要的学术统计学系之一。他将一些该领域的顶尖人物招入麾下，也发掘了一些后来成就斐然的人才。例如，大卫·布莱克威尔当时独自在霍华德大学工作，与其他数理统计学家隔绝。由于种族原因，尽管他才华横溢，却无法在“白人”院校获得教职；内曼邀请布莱克威尔来到伯克利。内曼还招收了一位来自法国文盲农民家庭的研究生；吕西安·勒卡姆后来成为世界顶尖的概率论专家之一。

Just before Hitler invaded Poland and dropped a curtain of evil upon continental Europe, Neyman came to the United States, where he started a statistics program at the University of California at Berkeley. He remained there until his death in 1981, having created one of the most important academic statistics departments in the world. He brought to his department some of the major figures in the field. He also drew from obscurity others who went on to great achievements. For example, David Blackwell was working alone at Howard University, isolated from other mathematical statisticians. Because of his race, he had been unable to get an appointment at “White” schools, in spite of his great potential; Neyman invited Blackwell to Berkeley. Neyman also brought in a graduate student who had come from an illiterate French peasant family; Lucien Le Cam went on to become one of the world’s leading probabilists.

内曼教授总是对他的学生和同事们关怀备至。他们津津乐道地回忆起下午的系里茶会，内曼教授总是以彬彬有礼的风度主持茶会。他会温和地引导学生或同事们介绍一下最近的研究成果，然后亲切地在房间里走动，倾听大家的意见，并积极参与讨论。许多茶会结束时，他都会举起茶杯，高呼“敬女士们！”。他对“女士们”尤其关照，鼓励并支持女性的职业发展。他最杰出的女性门生包括伊丽莎白·斯科特博士，她曾与内曼教授共事，并在天文学、致癌作用和动物学等领域发表过多篇论文；以及伊芙琳·菲克斯博士，她在流行病学领域做出了重大贡献。

Neyman was always attentive to his students and fellow faculty members. They describe the pleasures of the afternoon departmental teas, which Neyman presided over with a courtly graciousness. He would gently prod someone, student or faculty, to describe some recent research and then genially work his way around the room, getting comments and aiding the discussion. He would end many teas by lifting his cup and toasting, “To the ladies!” He was especially good to “the ladies,” encouraging and furthering the careers of women. Prominent among his female protégées were Dr. Elizabeth Scott, who worked with Neyman and was a coauthor on papers ranging from astronomy to carcinogenesis to zoology, and Dr. Evelyn Fix, who made major contributions to epidemiology.

在R.A.费舍尔于1962年去世之前，内曼一直饱受这位尖刻天才的攻击。内曼所做的一切都成了费舍尔批评的素材。如果内曼成功地证明了费舍尔某些晦涩的论断，费舍尔就会攻击他误解了自己的著作。如果内曼扩展了费舍尔的某个观点，费舍尔就会攻击他将理论引向歧途。内曼从未以牙还牙，无论是在公开场合，还是——如果我们相信那些与他共事的人的说法——在私下里。

Until R. A. Fisher died in 1962, Neyman was under constant attack by this acerbic genius. Everything Neyman did was grist for Fisher’s criticism. If Neyman succeeded in showing a proof of some obscure Fisherian statement, Fisher attacked him for misunderstanding what he had written. If Neyman expanded on a Fisherian idea, Fisher attacked him for taking the theory down a useless path. Neyman never responded in kind, either in print or, if we are to believe those who worked with him, in private.

在晚年的一次采访中，内曼回忆起上世纪五十年代的一件事：当时他正准备在一次国际会议上用法语发表论文。当他走上讲台时，他发现费舍尔就在听众席中。在宣读论文的过程中，他做好了迎接即将到来的攻击的准备。他知道费舍尔会抓住论文中某个无关紧要的细枝末节大做文章，甚至会把他和论文一起批得体无完肤。内曼宣读完毕后，等待听众提问。确实有一些提问。但费舍尔始终纹丝不动，一言不发。后来，内曼才知道费舍尔根本不会说法语。

In an interview toward the end of his life, Neyman described a time in the 1950s when he was about to present a paper in French at an international meeting. As he went to the podium, he realized that Fisher was in the audience. While presenting the paper, he steeled himself for the attacks he knew would come. He knew that Fisher would pounce upon some unimportant minor aspect of the paper and tear it and Neyman to pieces. Neyman finished and waited for questions from the audience. A few came. But Fisher never stirred, never said a word. Later, Neyman discovered that Fisher could not speak French.

第十二章​

CHAPTER 12

自信的把戏

THE CONFIDENCE TRICK

W20世纪80年代艾滋病疫情爆发时，许多问题亟待解答。一旦确定了病原体——人类免疫缺陷病毒（HIV），卫生官员就需要了解感染人数，以便规划应对疫情所需的资源。幸运的是，过去二三十年间发展起来的流行病学数学模型[14]可以应用于此。

When the AIDS epidemic appeared in the 1980s, a number of questions needed to be answered. Once the infective agent, HIV (for human immunodeficiency virus), was identified, health officials needed to know how many infected people there were, in order to plan on the resources that would be needed to meet this epidemic. Fortunately, mathematical models of epidemiology[14] developed over the previous twenty to thirty years could be applied here.

现代科学对流行病的看法是，个体接触病原体后，部分人会被感染，经过一段称为“潜伏期”的时期后，许多感染者会出现疾病症状。一旦感染，这个人就可能成为传染源，将病原体传播给其他尚未感染的人。我们无法预测谁会接触病原体、谁会被感染，或者谁会传染给他人。因此，我们只能处理概率分布，并估计这些分布的参数。

The modern scientific view of epidemic disease is that individual patients are exposed, some of them become infected, and after a period of time called the “latency,” many of those who became infected show symptoms of the disease. Once infected, a person is a potential source of exposure to others who are not yet infected. There is no way we can predict which person will be exposed, or infected, or will infect others. Instead, we deal with probability distributions and estimate the parameters of those distributions.

其中一个参数是平均潜伏期——即从感染到出现症状的平均时间。对于艾滋病疫情期间的公共卫生官员来说，这是一个尤为重要的参数。他们无法得知有多少人已被感染，也无法得知最终会有多少人患病，但如果他们知道平均潜伏期，就可以将其与患病人数结合起来，从而估算出感染人数。此外，由于艾滋病感染模式中存在一种特殊情况，他们掌握了一部分患者的感染时间和发病时间。少数血友病患者通过受污染的血液制品感染了艾滋病毒，他们提供的数据可以用来估算平均潜伏期这一参数。

One of the parameters is the mean latency time—the average time from infection to symptoms. This was a particularly important parameter to public health officials for the AIDS epidemic. They had no way of knowing how many persons were infected and how many would eventually come down with the disease, but if they knew the mean latency time, they could combine that with the count of people who had the disease and estimate the number infected. Furthermore, due to an unusual circumstance in the infection pattern of AIDS, they had a group of patients about whom they knew both the time of infection and the time the disease appeared. A small number of hemophiliacs had been exposed to HIV through contaminated blood products, and they provided the data to estimate the parameter of mean latency time.

这个估计值到底有多好？流行病学家可能会指出，他们使用的是费舍尔意义上的最佳估计值。他们的估计值具有一致性，且效率最高。他们甚至可以校正可能的偏差，并声称他们的估计值是无偏的。但是，正如前几章所述，我们根本无法确定某个具体的估计值是否正确。

How good was this estimate? The epidemiologists could point out that they used the best estimates, in R. A. Fisher’s sense. Their estimates were consistent and had maximum efficiency. They could even correct for possible bias and claim that their estimates were unbiased. But, as pointed out in earlier chapters, there is no way of knowing if a specific estimate is correct.

如果我们无法断言估计值完全正确，那么我们能否衡量估计值与参数真实值的接近程度呢？答案就在于区间估计。点估计是一个单一的数值。例如，我们可能利用血友病研究的数据估计平均潜伏期为 5.7 年。区间估计则表明平均潜伏期介于 3.7 年和 12.4 年之间。很多时候，区间估计就足够了，因为区间两端所需的公共政策大致相同。但有时，区间估计过宽，最小值和最大值所需的公共政策就有所不同。区间过宽意味着现有信息不足以做出决策，需要获取更多信息，例如扩大研究范围或开展另一系列实验。

If we cannot say the estimate is exactly correct, is there some way we can say how close the estimate is to the true value of the parameter? The answer to that question lies in the use of interval estimates. A point estimate is a single number. For instance, we might use the data from hemophiliac studies to estimate that the mean latency time was 5.7 years. An interval estimate would state that the mean latency time lies between 3.7 and 12.4 years. Very often, it is adequate to have an interval estimate, since the public policies that would be required are about the same for both ends of the interval estimate. Sometimes, the interval estimate is too wide, and different public policies would be required for the minimum value than for the maximum value. The conclusion that can be drawn from too wide an interval is that the information available is not adequate to make a decision and that more information should be sought, perhaps by enlarging the scope of the investigation or engaging in another series of experiments.

例如，如果艾滋病的平均潜伏期长达12.4年，那么大约五分之一的感染者在感染后20年或更长时间才会发展成艾滋病。如果平均潜伏期为3.7年，几乎所有感染者都会在20年内发展成艾滋病。这两个结果差异过大，无法据此制定最佳公共政策，因此需要更多信息。

For instance, if the mean latency time for AIDS is as high as 12.4 years, then approximately one-fifth of infected patients will survive for 20 years or more after being infected, before they get AIDS. If the mean latency is 3.7 years, almost every patient will get AIDS within 20 years. These two results are too disparate to lead to a single best public policy, and more information would be useful.

20世纪80年代末，美国国家科学院召集了一批国内顶尖科学家组成委员会，探讨气溶胶喷雾剂中使用的氟碳化合物是否会破坏高层大气中的臭氧层。臭氧层保护地球免受有害紫外线辐射的侵害。该委员会（其主席约翰·图基是本书第22章的主角）并没有简单地给出“是”或“否”的答案，而是决定用概率分布模型来模拟氟碳化合物的影响。他们随后计算了臭氧层每年平均变化的区间估计值。结果表明，即使使用当时有限的数据，该区间的下限也表明臭氧层每年减少的幅度足以在50年内对人类生命构成严重威胁。

In the late 1980s, the National Academy of Sciences convened a committee of some of the country’s leading scientists to consider the possibility that fluorocarbons used in aerosol sprays were destroying the ozone layer in the upper atmosphere, which protects the Earth from harmful ultraviolet radiation. Instead of answering the question with a yes or no, the committee (whose chairman, John Tukey, is the subject of chapter 22 of this book) decided to model the effect of fluorocarbons in terms of a probability distribution. They then computed an interval estimate of the mean change in ozone per year. It turned out that, even using the small amount of data available, the lower end of that interval indicated a sufficient annual decrease in ozone to pose a serious threat to human life within fifty years.

区间估计如今几乎渗透到所有统计分析中。当一项民意调查声称44%的民众认为总统工作表现良好时，通常会有一个脚注说明该数字“误差为正负3%”。这意味着44%的受访者认为总统工作表现良好。由于这是一项随机调查，因此需要寻找的参数是所有持此观点的人所占的百分比。由于样本量较小，一个合理的估计是该参数的范围在41%（44%减去3%）到47%（44%加上3%）之间。

Interval estimates now permeate almost all statistical analyses. When a public opinion poll claims that 44 percent of the populace think the president is doing a good job, there is usually a footnote stating this figure has “an error of plus or minus 3 percent.” What that means is that 44 percent of the people surveyed thought the president is doing a good job. Since this was a random survey, the parameter being sought is the percentage of all the people who think that way. Because of the small size of the sample, a reasonable guess is that the parameter runs from 41 percent (44 percent minus 3 percent) to 47 percent (44 percent plus 3 percent).

耶日·内曼，1894-1981

Jerzy Neyman, 1894-1981

如何计算区间估计？如何解释区间估计？我们能否对区间估计做出概率陈述？我们有多大把握认为参数的真实值位于该区间内？

How does one compute an interval estimate? How does one interpret an interval estimate? Can we make a probability statement about an interval estimate? How sure are we that the true value of the parameter lies within the interval?

内曼的解决方案

NEYMAN’S SOLUTION

1934年，耶日·内曼在英国皇家统计学会发表了题为《论代表性方法的两个不同方面》的演讲。他的论文探讨了抽样调查的分析；它延续了他大多数著作的精妙之处，推导出了看似简单的数学表达式，这些表达式在内曼推导之后才显得直观易懂。这篇论文最重要的部分在附录中，内曼在其中提出了一种构建区间估计并确定其准确度的简便方法。内曼将这种新方法称为“置信区间”，并将置信区间的上下限称为“置信界限”。

In 1934, Jerzy Neyman presented a talk before the Royal Statistical Society, entitled “On the Two Different Aspects of the Representative Method.” His paper dealt with the analysis of sample surveys; it has the elegance of most of his work, deriving what appear to be simple mathematical expressions that are intuitively obvious (only after Neyman has derived them). The most important part of this paper is in an appendix, in which Neyman proposes a straightforward way to create an interval estimate and to determine how accurate that estimate is. Neyman called this new procedure “confidence intervals,” and the ends of the confidence intervals he called “confidence bounds.”

会议由鲍利教授主持，他起身提议致谢。他首先用几段话讨论了论文的主体部分，然后谈到了附录：

Professor G. M. Bowley was in the chair for the meeting and rose to propose a vote of thanks. He first discussed the main part of the paper for several paragraphs. Then he got to the appendix:

我不确定是该请求解释还是提出质疑。文章中暗示这项工作难以理解，而我可能就是被它误导的人之一[在本段后面，他举了一个例子，表明他清楚地理解了内曼提出的观点]。我只能说，这篇文章发表时我就读过，之后也读过，而且昨天我还非常仔细地阅读了内曼博士对它的阐释。我指的是内曼博士的置信区间。我完全不确定这个“置信区间”是否是一种“障眼法”。

I am not certain whether to ask for an explanation or to cast a doubt. It is suggested in the paper that the work is difficult to follow and I may be one of those who have been misled by it [later in this paragraph, he works out an example, showing that he clearly understood what Neyman was proposing]. I can only say that I have read it at the time it appeared and since, and I read Dr. Neyman’s elucidation of it yesterday with great care. I am referring to Dr. Neyman’s confidence limits. I am not at all sure that the “confidence” is not a “confidence trick.”

鲍利随后举了一个内曼置信区间的例子，并继续说道：

Bowley then works out an example of Neyman’s confidence interval and continues:

这真的能让我们更进一步吗？我们掌握的知识比托德亨特（一位十九世纪末的概率论家）更多吗？它能让我们超越卡尔·皮尔逊和埃奇沃思（一位早期数理统计学的领军人物）吗？它真的能引导我们找到所需的答案——在我们所抽样的总体中，该比例落在特定限度内的概率吗？我认为不能……我不知道我是否准确地表达了我的想法……自从这种方法被提出以来，我就一直感到困惑。该理论的表述并不令人信服，在我确信之前，我对它的有效性持怀疑态度。

Does that really take us any further? Do we know more than was known to Todhunter [a late-nineteenth-century probabilist]? Does it take us beyond Karl Pearson and Edgeworth [a leading figure in the early development of mathematical statistics]? Does it really lead us towards what we need—the chance that in the universe which we are sampling the proportion is within these certain limits? I think it does not … . I do not know that I have expressed my thoughts quite accurately … [this] is a difficulty I have felt since the method was first propounded. The statement of the theory is not convincing, and until I am convinced I am doubtful of its validity.

鲍利对这种方法的质疑，也是此后一直困扰着置信区间概念的问题。显然，内曼用来推导其方法的简洁的四行微积分公式，在抽象的概率论框架内是正确的，并且确实可以计算出一个概率。然而，这个概率究竟指的是什么却并不明确。数据已被观测到，参数是一个固定的（即使未知）数值，因此，参数取特定值的概率要么是100%（如果该值就是参数值），要么是0（如果参数值不是参数值）。然而，95%的置信区间处理的是95%的概率。这个概率指的是什么？内曼巧妙地将他的理论称为置信区间，并避免使用“概率”一词，从而回避了这个问题。鲍利和其他人一眼就识破了这种拙劣的伎俩。

Bowley’s problem with this procedure is one that has bedeviled the idea of confidence bounds since then. Clearly, the elegant four lines of calculus that Neyman used to derive his method are correct within the abstract mathematical theory of probability, and it does lead to the computation of a probability. However, it is not clear what that probability refers to. The data have been observed, the parameter is a fixed (if unknown) number, so the probability that the parameter takes on a specific value is either 100 percent if that was the value, or 0 if it was not. Yet, a 95 percent confidence interval deals with 95 percent probability. The probability of what? Neyman finessed this question by calling his creation a confidence interval and avoiding the use of the word probability. Bowley and others easily saw through this transparent ploy.

R.A. Fisher也参与了讨论，但他却忽略了这一点。他的发言杂乱无章，引用了许多Neyman论文中根本没有提及的内容。这是因为Fisher当时正对区间估计的计算方法感到困惑。在他的评论中，他提到了“置信概率”（fiducial probability），而Neyman的论文中并没有出现这个词。Fisher长期以来一直在努力解决这个问题——如何确定参数区间估计的不确定性程度。Fisher从一个与似然函数相关的复杂角度来研究这个问题。正如他很快证明的那样，这种看待公式的方式并不符合概率分布的要求。Fisher将这个函数称为“置信分布”（fiducial distribution），但他随后却违背了自己的见解，将适用于真正概率分布的数学方法应用到了这个函数上。Fisher希望，最终的结果会是一组在观测数据下对参数而言合理的数值。

R. A. Fisher was also among the discussants, but he missed this point. His discussion was a rambling and confused collection of references to things that Neyman did not even include in his paper. This is because Fisher was in the midst of confusion over the calculation of interval estimates. In his comments, he referred to “fiducial probability,” a phrase that does not appear in Neyman’s paper. Fisher had long been struggling with this very problem—how to determine the degree of uncertainty associated with an interval estimate of a parameter. Fisher was working at the problem from a complicated angle somewhat related to his likelihood function. As he quickly proved, this way of looking at the formula did not meet the requirements of a probability distribution. Fisher called this function a “fiducial distribution,” but then he violated his own insights by applying the same mathematics one might apply to a proper probability distribution. The result, Fisher hoped, would be a set of values that was reasonable for the parameter, in the face of the observed data.

这正是内曼所得出的结果，如果参数是正态分布的均值，两种方法都会得出相同的结果。由此，费舍尔得出结论：内曼窃取了他的置信分布思想，并为其取了个不同的名字。费舍尔的置信分布理论并未取得太大进展，因为该方法在处理其他更复杂的参数（例如标准差）时会失效。而内曼的方法则适用于任何类型的参数。费舍尔似乎始终未能理解这两种方法之间的区别，他终其一生都坚持认为，内曼的置信区间充其量只是他提出的置信区间的推广。他确信，内曼的这种表面上的推广在面对足够复杂的问题时也会失效——就像他自己的置信区间一样。

This was exactly what Neyman produced, and if the parameter was the mean of the normal distribution, both methods produced the same answers. From this, Fisher concluded that Neyman had stolen his idea of fiducial distribution and given it a different name. Fisher never got far with his fiducial distributions, because the method broke down with other more complicated parameters, like the standard deviation. Neyman’s method works with any type of parameter. Fisher never appeared to understand the difference between the two approaches, insisting to the end of his life that Neyman’s confidence intervals were, at most, a generalization of his fiducial intervals. He was sure that Neyman’s apparent generalization would break down when faced with a sufficiently complicated problem—just as his own fiducial intervals had.

概率与置信水平

PROBABILITY VERSUS CONFIDENCE LEVEL

无论问题多么复杂，内曼的方法都不会失效，这也是它在统计分析中被广泛应用的原因之一。内曼在置信区间上遇到的真正问题并非费舍尔所预见的，而是鲍利在讨论之初提出的问题：在这个语境下，概率究竟意味着什么？内曼的回答回归到了频率学派对现实生活中概率的定义。正如他在此处所述，并在之后一篇关于置信区间的论文中进一步阐述，置信区间不应被视为对每个结论的单独分析，而应被视为一个过程。从长远来看，始终计算95%置信区间的统计学家会发现，参数的真实值有95%的概率落在计算出的区间内。需要注意的是，对内曼而言，与置信区间相关的概率并非我们判断正确的概率，而是使用他的方法的统计学家在长期内做出正确判断的频率。它与当前估计的“准确性”无关。

Neyman’s procedure does not break down, regardless of how complicated the problem, which is one reason it is so widely used in statistical analyses. Neyman’s real problem with confidence intervals was not the problem that Fisher anticipated. It was the problem that Bowley raised at the beginning of the discussion. What does probability mean in this context? In his answer, Neyman fell back on the frequentist definition of real-life probability. As he said here, and made clearer in a later paper on confidence intervals, the confidence interval has to be viewed not in terms of each conclusion but as a process. In the long run, the statistician who always computes 95 percent confidence intervals will find that the true value of the parameter lies within the computed interval 95 percent of the time. Note that, to Neyman, the probability associated with the confidence interval was not the probability that we are correct. It was the frequency of correct statements that a statistician who uses his method will make in the long run. It says nothing about how “accurate” the current estimate is.

尽管内曼对置信区间的概念定义得十分严谨，鲍利等统计学家也竭力保持概率概念的清晰纯粹，但置信区间在科学领域的普遍应用却导致了思维上的诸多不严谨。例如，有人在使用95%置信区间时，常常会说自己“95%确定”某个参数落在该区间内。在第13章中，我们将认识L.J.萨维奇（“吉米”）和布鲁诺·德·菲内蒂，并了解他们关于个人概率的研究，这或许可以解释为何会出现类似的表述。然而，计算一个人对某事的确定程度与计算置信区间是截然不同的。统计学文献中有很多文章指出，根据萨维奇和德·菲内蒂的方法推导出的参数界限，与基于相同数据得出的内曼置信区间存在显著差异。

As careful as Neyman was to define the concept, and as careful as were statisticians like Bowley to keep the concept of probability clear and uncontaminated, the general use of confidence intervals in science has led to much more sloppy thinking. It is not uncommon, for instance, for someone using a 95 percent confidence interval to state that he is “95 percent sure” that the parameter lies within that interval. In chapter 13, we will meet L. J. (“Jimmie”) Savage and Bruno de Finetti and describe their work on personal probability, which justifies the use of statements like that. However, the calculation of the degree to which a person can be sure of something is different from the calculation of a confidence interval. The statistical literature has many articles where the bounds on a parameter that are derived following the methods of Savage and de Finetti are shown to be dramatically different from Neyman’s confidence bounds derived from the same data.

尽管在此语境下概率的含义尚存疑问，但内曼置信区间已成为计算区间估计的标准方法。大多数科学家计算90%或95%的置信区间，并假定该区间包含参数的真实值。

In spite of the questions about the meaning of probability in this context, Neyman’s confidence bounds have become the standard method of computing an interval estimate. Most scientists compute 90 percent or 95 percent confidence bounds and act as if they are sure that the interval contains the true value of the parameter.

如今已无人再谈论或撰写“信托分配”这一概念。费舍尔去世后，这个概念也随之消亡。在费舍尔努力实践这一概念的过程中，他进行了大量精辟而重要的研究。其中一些研究已成为主流；而另一些则仍保持着他留下的未完成状态。

No one talks or writes about “fiducial distributions” today. The idea died with Fisher. As he tried to make the idea work, Fisher produced a great deal of clever and important research. Some of that research has become mainstream; other parts remain in the incomplete state in which he left them.

在这项研究中，费舍尔曾多次接近跨越统计学的界限，涉足他称之为“逆概率”的领域。但他每次都及时止步。逆概率的概念起源于十八世纪的业余数学家托马斯·贝叶斯牧师。贝叶斯与当时许多顶尖科学家保持通信，并经常向他们提出复杂的数学问题。有一天，当他摆弄标准的概率数学公式时，他将其中两个公式与简单的代数运算结合起来，结果发现了一些令他震惊的事情。

In this research, Fisher came very close at times to stepping over the line into a branch of statistics he called “inverse probability.” Each time he pulled away. The idea of inverse probability began with the Reverend Thomas Bayes, an amateur mathematician of the eighteenth century. Bayes maintained a correspondence with many of the leading scientists of his age and often posed complicated mathematical problems to them. One day, while fiddling with the standard mathematical formulas of probability, he combined two of them with simple algebra and discovered Something that horrified him.

下一章，我们将探讨贝叶斯异端邪说以及费舍尔为何拒绝使用逆概率。

In the next chapter, we shall look at the Bayesian heresy and why Fisher refused to make use of inverse probability.

C第13章

CHAPTER 13

贝叶斯异端

THE BAYESIAN HERESY

T从八世纪到十八世纪初，威尼斯共和国一直是地中海地区的重要强国。在其鼎盛时期，威尼斯控制着亚得里亚海沿岸的大部分地区以及克里特岛和塞浦路斯岛，并垄断了从东方到欧洲的贸易。威尼斯由一群贵族家族统治，这些家族内部维持着某种形式的民主。名义上的国家元首是总督。从公元697年共和国建立到1797年威尼斯被奥地利吞并，共有超过150人担任过总督，有的任期只有一年或更短，有的则长达34年。在任总督去世后，共和国会进行一系列复杂的选举。首先，从贵族家族的高级成员中，通过抽签选出一小部分人作为“选帝侯”（lector）。这些选帝侯会挑选其他成员加入他们的行列，然后从这个扩大的选帝侯群体中再通过抽签选出一小部分人。这个过程持续了好几个阶段，直到最后一组诵经员从他们当中选出总督为止。

The Serene Republic of Venice was a major power in the Mediterranean from the eighth through the early eighteenth centuries. At the height of its empire, Venice had control of much of the Adriatic coast and the islands of Crete and Cyprus, and had a monopoly over trade from the Orient to Europe. It was ruled by a group of noble families who maintained a kind of democracy among themselves. The titular head of state was the doge. From the founding of the republic in 697 until Venice was taken over by Austria in 1797, over 150 men served as doge, some for a year or less, one for as long as thirty-four years. Upon the death of the reigning doge, the republic engaged in an elaborate sequence of elections. From the senior members of the noble families, a small number would be chosen by lot as lectors. These lectors would choose additional members to join them at this first stage, and then a small number of this augmented group would be chosen by lot. This continued for several stages until a final group of lectors would choose the doge from among themselves.

威尼斯鼎盛时期的总督府

The palace of the doge at the height of Venetian power

在共和国早期，选拔诵经员的方式是在每个阶段准备一组蜡球，其中一些蜡球空空如也，另一些则藏有写着“诵经员”字样的纸条。到了十七世纪，最后阶段的选拔则使用大小相同的金银球。1268年总督雷尼埃里·芝诺去世时，第二阶段共有三十位诵经员，为此准备了三十个蜡球，其中九个藏有纸条。一位小孩被带到台前。他从篮子里挑选一个蜡球，递给第一位诵经员。诵经员打开蜡球，看看自己是否会成为下一阶段的诵经员。小孩再挑选一个蜡球，递给第二位诵经员，诵经员打开蜡球，以此类推。

Early in the history of the republic, the lectors were chosen at each stage by preparing a group of wax balls, some of which had nothing in them and some of which had a slip of paper with the word lector on it. By the seventeenth century, the final stages were conducted using identically sized balls of gold and silver. When the Doge Rainieri Zeno died in 1268, there were thirty lectors at the second stage, and thirty balls of wax were prepared, nine of which had slips of paper in them. A small child was brought forward. He chose a ball from the basket and handed it to the first lector, who opened it and saw whether he would be a lector at the next stage. The child chose another ball and handed it to the second lector, who opened it, and so on.

在孩子抽取第一个球之前，小组中的每位成员都有9/30的概率成为下一阶段的朗读者。如果第一个球是空的，那么剩余成员被选中的概率为9/29。如果第一个球里装有纸条，那么剩余成员被选中的概率为8/29。第二个球被抽取并展示后，下一位成员被选中成为朗读者的概率会根据抽取结果相应增加或减少。这个过程会一直持续到所有九个标记球都被抽取完毕。此时，剩余成员成为下一阶段朗读者的概率降至零。

Before the child chose the first ball, each member of the group had a probability of 9/30 of becoming a lector for the next stage. If the first ball was empty, then each of the remaining members had a 9/29 probability of being chosen. If the first ball contained a slip of paper, then each of the remaining members had an 8/29 chance of being chosen. Once the second ball was chosen and displayed, the probability that the next member would be chosen lector would be similarly diminished or increased, depending upon the outcome of that draw. This would continue until all nine marked balls had been chosen. At that point, the chance that any of the remaining members would become a lector for the next stage dropped to zero.

这是一个条件概率的例子。某个成员在下一阶段成为朗读者的概率取决于他做出选择之前所选出的球。约翰·梅纳德·凯恩斯指出，所有概率都是条件概率。以他举的一个例子来说，从他的书架上随机选择一本书，这本书是布面精装的概率，取决于他书架上实际有哪些书，以及他如何“随机”地做出选择。病人患小细胞肺癌的概率取决于该病人的吸烟史。用于检验对照实验中治疗效果为零假设的p值，取决于实验的设计。条件概率的重要之处在于，某个给定事件发生的概率（例如，一组特定的号码在彩票中中奖的概率）会因不同的先验条件而不同。

This is an example of conditional probability. The probability that a given member would become a lector at the next stage depended upon the balls that had been chosen prior to his choice. John Maynard Keynes has pointed out that all probabilities are conditional. To use one of his examples, the probability that a book chosen at random from his library shelves will be bound in buckram is conditional on the books that are actually in his library and on how he will be making that choice “at random.” The probability that a patient will have small-cell carcinoma of the lung is conditional on the smoking history of that patient. The p-value that is calculated to test the null hypothesis of no treatment effect from a controlled experiment is conditional on the design of the experiment. The important aspect of conditional probability is that the probability of some given event (e.g., that a particular set of numbers will win in a lottery) is different for different prior conditions.

十八世纪发展起来的用于处理条件概率的公式都基于这样一个前提：条件事件发生在待求事件之前。在十八世纪后期，托马斯·贝叶斯牧师在研究条件概率公式时，有了一个惊人的发现：这些公式具有内在的对称性。

The formulas that were developed during the eighteenth century to deal with conditional probability all depended upon the idea that the conditioning events occurred prior to the event being sought. In the latter part of that century, the Reverend Thomas Bayes was playing around with the formulas for conditional probability and made a startling discovery: The formulas had an inner symmetry.

假设我们有两个事件在一段时间内发生，比如洗牌后发一手五张牌的扑克牌。我们把这两个事件分别称为“之前”和“之后”。讨论“之后”在“之前”条件下的概率是有意义的。如果我们洗牌洗得不好，就会影响拿到两张A的概率。贝叶斯发现我们也可以计算“之前”在“之后”条件下的概率。但这毫无意义。这就像在已知一手牌拿到两张A的情况下，去计算这副牌里有四张A的概率一样。或者在已知某人患有肺癌的情况下，去计算他是吸烟者的概率。又或者在已知查尔斯·A·史密斯是唯一中奖者的情况下，去计算某个州彩票是公平的概率。

Suppose we have two events that occur over a period of time, like shuffling a deck of cards and then dealing a poker hand of five. Let us call the events “before” and “after.” It makes sense to talk about the probability of “after,” conditional on “before.” If we fail to shuffle the deck well, that will influence the probability of getting two aces in the poker hand. Bayes discovered that we can also calculate the probability of “before,” conditional on “after.” This made no sense. It would be like determining the probability that the deck of cards contained four aces, given that a poker hand has been dealt with two aces. Or the probability that a patient was a smoker, given that he had lung cancer. Or the probability that a state lottery was fair, given that someone named Charles A. Smith was the only winner.

贝叶斯将这些计算搁置一旁。在他去世后，人们在他的遗稿中发现了这些计算，并最终发表。贝叶斯定理[15]此后一直困扰着统计分析的数学领域。贝叶斯对条件概率的逆运算并非毫无意义，反而常常非常合理。当流行病学家试图寻找罕见疾病（例如雷氏综合征）的可能病因时，他们通常会采用病例对照研究。在这种研究中，研究人员会收集一组病例，并将其与一组未患此病但其他方面与病例相似的患者（对照组）进行比较。流行病学家会计算在对照组患者患有此病的情况下，病例患者接受某种先前治疗或存在某种疾病的概率。吸烟对心脏病和肺癌的影响最初就是通过这种方法发现的。沙利度胺对出生缺陷的影响也是通过病例对照研究推断出来的。

Bayes put these calculations aside. They were found among his papers when he died, and were published posthumously. Bayes’s theorem[15] has since come to bedevil the mathematics of statistical analysis. Far from failing to make sense, Bayes’s inversion of conditional probability often makes a great deal of sense. When epidemiologists attempt to find the possible causes of a rare medical condition, like Reye’s syndrome, they often use a case-control study. In such a study, a group of cases of the disease is assembled, and they are compared with a group of patients (the controls) who have not had this disease but who are similar in other respects to the patients with the disease. The epidemiologists calculate the probability of some prior treatment or condition, given that the control patients had the disease. This was how the effects of smoking on both heart disease and lung cancer were first discovered. The influence of thalidomide on birth defects was also deduced from a case-control study.

贝叶斯定理在估计分布参数方面的应用，比直接用它来反演条件概率更为重要。人们很容易将分布的参数本身视为随机变量，并计算与这些参数相关的概率。例如，我们可能比较两种癌症治疗方法，并希望得出这样的结论：“我们有95%的把握认为，治疗方案A的五年生存率高于治疗方案B。” 这可以通过应用一到两次贝叶斯定理来实现。

More important than the direct use of Bayes’s theorem to invert conditional probability has been the use of the theorem for estimating parameters of distributions. There is a temptation to treat the parameters of a distribution as random themselves and to compute probabilities associated with those parameters. For instance, we might compare two treatments for cancer and want to conclude that “We are 95 percent sure that the five-year survival rate for treatment A is greater than the survival rate for treatment B.” This can be done with one or two applications of Bayes’s theorem.

关于“逆概率”的问题

QUESTIONS ABOUT “INVERSE PROBABILITY”

多年来，以这种方式使用贝叶斯定理被认为是不恰当的。当概率用于参数时，其含义究竟如何，这其中存在着一些重要的疑问。毕竟，皮尔逊革命的根本在于，科学研究不再关注测量结果本身，而是关注这些测量结果的概率分布。正如皮尔逊所证明的，科学研究的目的是估计那些控制该分布的参数（其值固定但未知）。如果将参数视为随机的（且取决于观测到的测量结果），那么这种方法就失去了其清晰的意义。

For many years, the use of Bayes’s theorem in this fashion was considered an inappropriate practice. There are serious questions about what probability means when used for parameters. After all, the whole basis of the Pearsonian revolution was that the measurements of science were no longer considered the things of interest. Rather, as Pearson showed, it was the probability distribution of those measurements, and the purpose of scientific investigation was to estimate the parameters whose (fixed but unknown) values controlled that distribution. If the parameters were considered random (and conditional on the observed measurements), this approach would cease to have such clear meaning.

在二十世纪初，统计学家们非常谨慎地避免使用所谓的“逆概率”。费舍尔在皇家统计学会的一次讨论中，因其早期论文之一而被指控使用了逆概率，他坚决地为自己辩护，驳斥了这一可怕的指控。内曼在其关于置信区间的第一篇论文中似乎使用了逆概率，但仅仅将其作为一种数学工具来绕过特定的计算。在他的第二篇论文中，他展示了如何在不使用贝叶斯定理的情况下得出相同的结果。到了20世纪60年代，这种方法的潜在威力和实用性开始吸引越来越多的研究人员。这种贝叶斯式的“异端”方法逐渐被人们所接受。到二十世纪末，它已经达到了如此高的接受度，以至于在《统计年鉴》和《生物统计学》等期刊上发表的文章中，超过一半都使用了贝叶斯方法。然而，贝叶斯方法的应用仍然常常受到质疑，尤其是在医学领域。

During the early years of the twentieth century, statisticians were very careful to avoid “inverse probability,” as it was called. In discussions before the Royal Statistical Society following one of his early papers, Fisher was accused of using inverse probability and stoutly defended himself against such a terrible charge. In the first paper on confidence intervals, Neyman seemed to be using inverse probability, but only as a mathematical device to get around a specific calculation. In his second paper, he showed how to reach the same result without Bayes’s theorem. By the 1960s, the potential power and usefulness of such an approach began attracting more and more workers. This Bayesian heresy was becoming more and more respectable. By the end of the twentieth century, it had reached such a level of acceptability that over half the articles that appear in journals like the Annals of Statistics and Biometrika now make use of Bayesian methods. The application of Bayesian methods is still often suspect, especially in medical science.

解释贝叶斯异端的一大难点在于，它包含多种不同的分析方法，而且这些方法的使用至少基于两种不同的哲学基础。人们常常会发现，完全不同的理念都被贴上了同一个标签——贝叶斯。接下来，我将探讨贝叶斯异端的两种具体表述：贝叶斯层级模型和个人概率。

One difficulty with explaining the Bayesian heresy is that there are a number of different methods of analysis and at least two different philosophical foundations to the use of these methods. It often seems as if entirely different ideas have been given the same label—Bayesian. In what follows, I will consider two specific formulations of the Bayesian heresy: the Bayesian hierarchal model, and personal probability.

贝叶斯层级模型

THE BAYESIAN HIERARCHAL MODEL

20世纪70年代初，文本分析的统计方法取得了长足进步，这始于弗雷德里克·莫斯特勒和戴维·华莱士的工作。他们运用统计方法确定了备受争议的《联邦党人文集》的作者身份。在1787年至1788年纽约州批准美国新宪法之前，詹姆斯·麦迪逊、亚历山大·汉密尔顿和约翰·杰伊撰写了一系列共70篇文章，支持批准宪法。这些文章均以笔名署名。20世纪初，汉密尔顿和麦迪逊分别确认了各自声称撰写的文章。他们都声称其中12篇文章是自己的作品。[16]

In the early 1970s, statistical methods of textual analysis made great advances, starting with work by Frederick Mosteller and David Wallace, who used statistical methods to determine the authorship of the disputed Federalist papers. Leading up to the ratification of the new Constitution of the United States by the state of New York in 1787–1788, James Madison, Alexander Hamilton, and John Jay wrote a series of seventy articles supporting ratification. The articles were signed with pseudonyms. Early in the next century, Hamilton and Madison identified the papers each claimed to have written. They both claimed[16] twelve of the papers as their own.

在对争议论文进行统计分析时，莫斯特勒和华莱士发现了数百个缺乏“实质内容”的英语单词。这些单词包括if、when、because、over、while、as 和 and 等。这些单词对于句子的语法意义至关重要，但它们本身并不包含具体含义，其使用主要取决于作者的语言运用方式。在这些数百个无实质内容的单词中，他们发现大约有三十个单词在两位作者的其他作品中的使用频率存在差异。

In their statistical analysis of the disputed papers, Mosteller and Wallace identified several hundred English words that lacked “content.” These are words like if, when, because, over, whilst, as, and. The words are needed to give a sentence grammatical meaning, but they do not carry specific meaning, and their use is dependent primarily upon the way in which the author uses language. Of these hundreds of contentless words, they found about thirty where the two authors differed in the frequency of use in their other writings.

例如，麦迪逊平均每1000个单词中使用“ upon ”一词0.23次，而汉密尔顿平均每1000个单词中使用3.24次。（在12篇有争议的文章中，有11篇完全没有使用“ upon”一词，而另一篇平均每1000个单词中使用1.1次。）这些平均频率并非描述任何特定的1000个单词的集合。它们不是整数，这意味着它们无法描述任何观察到的词语序列。然而，它们却是对两位不同作家作品中词语分布参数之一的估计。

Madison, for example, used the word upon an average of 0.23 times in 1,000 words, and Hamilton used upon an average of 3.24 times per 1,000. (Eleven of the twelve disputed papers do not use the word upon at all, and the other paper has an average of 1.1 uses per 1,000 words.) These average frequencies do not describe any specific collection of 1,000 words. The fact that they are not whole numbers means that they do not describe any observed sequence of words. They are, however, estimates of one of the parameters of the distribution of words in the writings of two different men.

在某篇论文的作者归属争议中，关键问题在于：这些词语的使用模式究竟源于与麦迪逊相关的概率分布，还是源于与汉密尔顿相关的概率分布？这些分布都具有参数，而定义麦迪逊或汉密尔顿作品的具体参数各不相同。这些参数只能根据他们的作品进行估算，而这些估算可能并不准确。正是由于这种不确定性，试图区分哪种分布适用于一篇有争议的论文变得困难重重。

The question in the disputed authorship for a given paper was: Do the patterns of usage of these words come from the probability distributions associated with Madison or from the probability distributions associated with Hamilton? These distributions have parameters, and the specific parameters that define Madison’s or Hamilton’s works differ. The parameters can only be estimated from their works, and these estimates could be wrong. The attempt to distinguish which distribution can be applied to a disputed paper is clouded with this uncertainty.

估算不确定性程度的一种方法是注意到，这两位作者的这些参数的具体数值取自一个分布，该分布描述了18世纪晚期北美所有受过教育的英语写作者所使用的参数。例如，汉密尔顿每1000个单词中使用“in”一词24次，麦迪逊每1000个单词中使用23次，而其他同时代作家则倾向于每1000个单词使用22到25次左右。

One way of getting an estimate of the level of uncertainty is to note that the exact values of these parameters for the two men are taken from a distribution that describes the parameters used by all educated people writing in English in North America in the late eighteenth century. For instance, Hamilton used the word in 24 times per 1,000 words. Madison used in 23 times per 1,000, and other contemporary writers tended to use in around 22 to 25 times per 1,000.

鉴于当时当地普遍的用词模式，每个人的用词参数都是随机的，并服从一定的概率分布。因此，汉密尔顿或麦迪逊使用空洞词语的驱动因素本身也具有参数，我们可以称之为“超参数”。通过研究同时代其他作家的作品，我们可以估算这些超参数。

Subject to the patterns associated with the general usage of words at that time and place, the parameters for each man are random and have a probability distribution. In this way, the parameters that drive the usage of contentless words by Hamilton or Madison have, themselves, parameters, which we can call “hyperparameters.” Using written works from other authors of the time and place, we can estimate the hyperparameters.

英语语言总是随着地域和时间的变化而变化。例如，在二十世纪的英语文学中，“ in”一词的使用频率往往低于千分之二十，这表明自汉密尔顿和麦迪逊时代以来的两百多年里，其使用模式发生了轻微的转变。我们可以将定义十八世纪北美参数分布的超参数视为在所有时间和地点都具有概率分布。除了十八世纪北美的文献之外，我们还可以利用其他时间和地点的文献来估计这些超参数的参数，我们可以称之为“超超参数”。

The English language is always changing from place to place and time to time. In twentieth-century English literature, for instance, the frequency of the use of the word in tends to be less than 20 per 1,000, indicating a slight shift in patterns of usage over the 200 years or more since the time of Hamilton and Madison. We can consider the hyperparameters that define the distribution of parameters in eighteenth-century North America as themselves having a probability distribution across all times and all places. We can use writings from other times and places, in addition to the writings from eighteenth-century North America, to estimate the parameters of these hyperparameters, which we can call “hyper-hyperparameters.”

通过反复运用贝叶斯定理，我们可以确定参数的分布，进而确定超参数的分布。原则上，我们可以通过寻找超超超参数的分布（以超超超参数为变量），进一步扩展这一层级结构。在这种情况下，似乎没有明显的方法来产生额外的不确定性。利用超参数和超超参数的估计值，Mosteller 和 Wallace 能够测量与以下陈述相关的概率：麦迪逊（或汉密尔顿）撰写了这篇论文。

By repeated use of Bayes’s theorem, we can determine the distribution of the parameters, then of the hyperparameters. In principle, we could extend this hierarchy further by finding the distribution of the hyper-hyperparameters in terms of hyper-hyper-hyperparameters, and so on. In this case, there is no obvious candidate for the generation of an additional level of uncertainty. Using the estimates of hyper- and hyper-hyperparameters, Mosteller and Wallace were able to measure the probability associated with the statement: Madison (or Hamilton) wrote this paper.

自 20 世纪 80 年代初以来，分层贝叶斯模型已成功应用于工程和生物学领域的许多难题。其中一个难题是当数据似乎来自两个或多个分布时。分析人员提出存在一个未观测到的变量，用于确定给定观测值来自哪个分布。这个识别标记是一个参数，但它具有概率分布（带有超参数），可以将其纳入似然函数中。Laird 和 Ware 的 EM 算法特别适用于此类问题。

Hierarchal Bayesian models have been applied very successfully since the early 1980s to many difficult problems in engineering and biology. One such problem arises when the data seem to come from two or more distributions. The analyst proposes the existence of an unobserved variable that defines which distribution a given observation comes from. This identifying marker is a parameter, but it has a probability distribution (with hyperparameters) that can be incorporated into the likelihood function. Laird and Ware’s EM algorithm is particularly suited to this type of problem.

统计文献中对贝叶斯方法的广泛应用充满了混乱和争议。不同的方法会产生不同的结果，而且没有明确的标准来判断哪种方法是正确的。传统主义者普遍反对使用贝叶斯定理，而贝叶斯主义者在模型细节上也存在分歧。这种情况亟需像费舍尔那样的天才出现，找到一个统一的原则来解决这些争论。然而，进入二十一世纪，这样的天才似乎并未出现。这个问题仍然像两百多年前托马斯·贝叶斯牧师遇到的问题一样难以解决。

This extensive use of Bayesian methods in the statistical literature is filled with confusion and disputation. Different methods producing different results can be proposed, and there are no clear criteria to determine which are correct. Traditionalists object to the use of Bayes’s theorem in general, and Bayesians disagree about the details of their models. The situation is crying for another genius like R. A. Fisher to come and find a unifying principle with which to resolve these arguments. As we enter the twenty-first century, no such genius seems to have arisen. The problem remains as elusive as it did for the Reverend Thomas Bayes more than 200 years ago.

个人概率

PERSONAL PROBABILITY

另一种贝叶斯方法似乎建立在更为坚实的基础之上，那就是个人概率的概念。这个概念早在17世纪伯努利夫妇对概率论进行初步研究时就已出现。事实上，“概率”一词的创造正是为了应对个人不确定性这一概念。

Another Bayesian approach appears to be on a much more solid foundation. This is the concept of personal probability. The idea has been around since the initial work on probability by the Bernoullis in the seventeenth century. In fact, the very word probability was created to deal with the sense of personal uncertainty.

L·J·“吉米”·萨维奇和布鲁诺·德·菲内蒂在20世纪60年代和70年代发展了个人概率背后的许多数学理论。20世纪60年代末，我参加了北卡罗来纳大学的一次统计学会议，萨维奇在会上阐述了他的一些观点。萨维奇声称，根本不存在所谓的“已证实的科学事实”，只有一些陈述，而那些自诩为科学家的人会赋予这些陈述很高的概率。例如，他说，当时在场的听众大多会认为“地球是圆的”这一陈述的概率很高。然而，如果我们对世界人口进行普查，我们很可能会发现，中国中部有很多农民会认为这个陈述的概率很低。就在这时，萨维奇不得不停止演讲，因为一群学生从礼堂外冲了出来，高喊着：“停课！罢工！停课！”他们正在抗议越南战争，呼吁学生在大学里罢课。当他们沿着小路消失，喧闹声渐渐平息时，萨维奇看着窗外说：“你知道吗，我们可能是最后一批认为地球是圆的了。”

L. J. (“Jimmie”) Savage and Bruno de Finetti developed much of the mathematics behind personal probability in the 1960s and 1970s. I attended a lecture at a statistics meeting at the University of North Carolina in the late 1960s in which Savage was propounding some of these ideas. There are no such things as proven scientific facts, Savage claimed. There are only statements, about which people who call themselves scientists associate a high probability. For instance, he said, most of the people listening to him at that talk would associate a high probability to the statement: “The world is round.” However, if we were to take a census of the world’s population, we would most likely find many peasants in the middle of China who would associate a low probability to that statement. At that moment, Savage had to stop talking because a group of students at the university went racing by outside the hall shouting, “Close it down! Strike, strike! Close it down!” They were protesting the war in Vietnam by calling for a student strike at the university. As they disappeared down the path and the tumult died away, Savage looked at the window and said, “And, you know, we may be the last generation that thinks the world is round.”
