马哈拉诺比斯认为，如果能够收集到一个足以代表总体的小样本，就有可能估算出总体的特征。此时，我们面临两种可能的方法。一种是构建所谓的“判断样本”。在判断样本中，利用对总体的已知信息来选择一小群个体，这些个体被选为代表总体中不同群体的代表。尼尔森收视率就是基于判断样本得出的，该收视率用于确定有多少人观看电视节目。尼尔森媒体研究公司根据家庭的社会经济地位和居住地区来选择样本。

Mahalanobis reasoned that it might be possible to estimate the characteristics of the larger population if one could gather a small sample that was adequately representative of the larger. At this point, we run into two possible approaches. One is to construct what is known as a “judgment sample.” In a judgment sample, whatever is known about the population is used to select a small group of individuals who are chosen to represent different groups in the larger population. The Nielsen ratings to determine how many people are watching TV shows are created from a judgment sample. Nielsen Media Research selects families based on their socioeconomic status and the region of the country in which they live.

乍看之下，判断抽样似乎是获取具有代表性的总体样本的好方法。但它存在两大缺陷。首先，只有当我们对总体有足够的了解，能够找到可以代表的特定子群体时，样本才具有代表性。如果我们对总体了解得如此透彻，或许就无需进行抽样，因为我们向样本提出的问题正是将总体划分为同质群体所必需的。第二个问题更为棘手。如果判断抽样的结果有误，我们无从得知其与真实情况的偏差有多大。2000年夏天，尼尔森媒体研究公司就因其样本中西班牙裔家庭数量不足，低估了观看西班牙语电视节目的家庭数量而受到批评。

A judgment sample seems, at first glance, to be a good way to get a representative sample of the larger population. But it has two major faults. The first is that the sample is representative only if we are absolutely sure that we know enough about the larger population to be able to find specific subclasses that can be represented. If we knew that much about the larger population, we would probably not have to sample, since the questions we ask of the sample are those that are needed to divide the larger population into homogeneous groups. The second problem is more troublesome. If the results of the judgment sample are wrong, we have no way of knowing how far from the truth they are. In the summer of 2000, Nielsen Media Research was criticized for not having enough Hispanic families in their sample and underestimating the number of families watching Spanish-language TV.

马氏的答案是随机抽样。我们使用随机化机制从总体中抽取个体。从这个随机样本中得到的数据很可能是不准确的，但我们可以利用数理统计定理来确定最佳的抽样和测量方法，确保从长远来看，我们的数据比其他任何方法都更接近真实值。此外，我们知道随机样本概率分布的数学形式，并且可以计算出我们想要估计的事物的真实值的置信区间。

Mahalanobis’s answer was the random sample. We use a randomizing mechanism to pick individuals out of the larger population. The numbers we get from this random sample are most likely wrong, but we can use the theorems of mathematical statistics to determine how to sample and measure in an optimum way, making sure that, in the long run, our numbers will be closer to the truth than any others. Furthermore, we know the mathematical form of the probability distribution of random samples, and we can calculate confidence bounds on the true values of the things we wish to estimate.

因此，随机样本比机会样本或判断样本更好，不是因为它能保证答案正确，而是因为我们可以计算出一个答案范围，其中有很高的概率包含正确答案。

Thus, the random sample is better than either the opportunity , sample or the judgment sample, not because it guarantees correct answers but because we can calculate a range of answers that will contain the correct answer with high probability.

新协议和抽样

THE NEW DEAL AND SAMPLING

20世纪30年代，抽样理论的数学发展迅猛，部分成果来自马哈拉诺比斯领导的印度统计研究所，部分来自内曼在30年代末发表的两篇论文，还有一部分来自一群在罗斯福新政初期聚集在华盛顿特区的充满热情的年轻大学毕业生。这些年轻的新政支持者在联邦政府的商务部和劳工部工作，解决了许多关于如何从庞大人群中进行抽样的实际问题。

The mathematics of sampling theory developed rapidly during the 1930s, some of it at the Indian Statistical Institute under Mahalanobis, some of it from two papers by Neyman in the late 1930s, and some of it by a group of eager young university graduates who gathered in Washington, D.C., during the early days of the New Deal. Many of the practical problems of how to sample from a large population of people were addressed and solved by these young New Dealers at the Commerce and Labor Departments of the federal government.

1932年至1939年间获得学士学位的年轻人，往往走出大学校园后会发现，社会上根本没有工作机会。大萧条造成了这种情况。玛格丽特·马丁在纽约州扬克斯长大，就读于巴纳德学院，后来成为美国预算局的一名官员，她写道：

A young man or woman who received a bachelor’s degree in the years 1932 to 1939 often walked out of the university into a world where there were no jobs. The Great Depression saw to that. Margaret Martin, who grew up in Yonkers, New York, and went to Barnard College, and who eventually became an official of the U.S. Bureau of the Budget, wrote:

1933年6月我毕业时，找不到任何工作……我的一个朋友比我晚一年，也就是1934年毕业，她觉得自己非常幸运。她在B. Altman百货公司找到了一份销售员的工作；她每周工作48小时，挣15美元。但是，即使是这样的工作也相对稀少。巴纳德学院有一位就业指导员，名叫弗洛伦斯·多蒂小姐，我去找她咨询去凯瑟琳·吉布斯秘书学校学习的可能性。我不知道从哪里弄钱，但我认为这至少是一项可以挣点钱的技能。多蒂小姐……并不容易相处，很多学生都非常敬畏她……她突然对我说道：“我绝不会建议你去学秘书！如果你学会了使用打字机，并且证明你会用打字机，那么你除了用打字机之外就什么也做不了……你应该去找一份专业工作。”

When I graduated in June of 1933 I couldn’t find any job … . A friend of mine who graduated one year later in 1934 felt very fortunate. She’d gotten a job selling at the B. Altman department store; she worked 48 hours a week and earned $15. But, even those jobs were relatively scarce. We had an occupation officer, Miss Florence Doty, at Barnard, and I went to her to talk about the possibilities of going to Katherine Gibbs, a secretarial school. I didn’t know where I’d find the money, but I thought that would be a skill that could at least earn something. Miss Doty … was not an easy person to get along with, and a lot of the students stood very much in awe of her … . She just turned on me, “I would never recommend that you take a secretarial course! If you learn to use a typewriter, and show that you can use a typewriter, you will never do anything else but use a typewriter … . You should be looking for a professional position.”

马丁最终在奥尔巴尼找到了她的第一份工作，担任纽约州就业安置和失业部门研究和统计办公室的初级经济学家，并以此为跳板继续攻读研究生学位。

Martin eventually found her first job in Albany, as a junior economist in the office of research and statistics of the New York State Division of Placement and Unemployment, and used that job as a springboard to graduate studies.

其他一些应届毕业生直接去了华盛顿。莫里斯·汉森1933年从怀俄明大学获得经济学学士学位后，进入人口普查局工作。他利用本科阶段的数学知识，并匆匆阅读了内曼的论文，设计了第一份全面的失业调查报告。内森·曼特尔从纽约市立学院（CCNY）获得生物学学位后，进入国家癌症研究所工作。杰罗姆·康菲尔德是纽约市立学院的历史系学生，毕业后在劳工部担任分析师。

Other newly graduated young people went directly to Washington. Morris Hansen went to the Census Bureau in 1933, with an undergraduate degree in economics from the University of Wyoming. He used his undergraduate mathematics and a hasty reading of Neyman’s papers to design the first comprehensive survey of unemployment. Nathan Mantel took his new City College of New York (CCNY) degree in biology and went to the National Cancer Institute. Jerome Cornfield, a history major at CCNY, took a job as an analyst at the Department of Labor.

对于身处政府部门的人来说，那是一个激动人心的时期。整个国家陷入瘫痪，大部分正常的经济活动都停滞不前，华盛顿的新政府正在寻求重启经济的方法。首先，他们必须了解全国各地的状况究竟有多糟糕。于是，就业和经济活动调查随即展开。这是美国历史上第一次尝试准确了解国家正在发生什么。显然，抽样调查正是解决这一问题的理想途径。

It was an exciting period to be in government. The nation lay prostrate, with much normal economic activity idle, and the new government in Washington was looking for ideas on how to get things started again. First they had to know how bad things were throughout the country. Surveys of employment and economic activity were begun. For the first time in the nation’s history, an attempt was being made to determine exactly what was happening in the country. It was an obvious place for sample surveys.

这些充满干劲的年轻劳动者最初不得不克服那些不懂数学的人的反对。劳工部早期的一项调查显示，不到10%的人口获得了近40%的收入，这一结果遭到了美国商会的强烈反对。这怎么可能呢？这项调查只接触了不到0.5%的劳动人口，而且这些人还是随机抽取的！商会也进行了自己的调查，收集了会员对现状的看法。商会认为这项新的调查不准确，因为它只是随机收集的数据。

These eager young workers initially had to overcome the objections of those who did not understand the mathematics. When one of the earlier surveys at the Department of Labor showed that less than 10 percent of the population received almost 40 percent of the income, it was denounced by the U.S. Chamber of Commerce. How could this be true? The survey had contacted less than one-half of 1 percent of the working population, and these people were chosen by random means! The Chamber of Commerce had its own surveys, taken from the opinions of its own members about what was happening. This new survey was dismissed by the chamber as inaccurate, since it was only a random collection of data.

1937年，政府试图全面统计失业率，国会授权开展1937年失业人口普查。国会通过的法案要求所有失业人员填写登记卡并送至当地邮局。当时，对失业人数的估计在300万到1500万之间，而唯一可靠的统计数据是纽约进行的几项随机调查。人口普查局的一群年轻社会学家，在卡尔·德德里克和弗雷德·斯蒂芬的带领下，意识到许多失业者不会参与普查，导致最终结果充满未知误差。于是，他们决定开展首次覆盖全国的严肃随机调查。年轻的莫里斯·汉森负责设计调查方案，人口普查局随机抽取了2%的邮路。这些邮路的邮递员将调查问卷分发给沿途的每一户家庭。

In 1937, the government attempted to get a full count of the unemployment rate, and Congress authorized the Unemployment Census of 1937. The bill, as passed by Congress, called for everyone who was unemployed to fill out a registration card and deliver it to the local post office. At that time, estimates of the number of unemployed ranged from three to fifteen million, and the only good counts were a few random surveys that had been taken in New York. A group of young sociologists, led by Cal Dedrick and Fred Stephan in the Census Bureau, realized that there would be many unemployed who would not respond and the result would yield numbers filled with unknown errors. It was decided that the first serious random survey across the entire country should be run. With young Morris Hansen designing the survey, the bureau chose 2 percent of all the postal routes at random. The postal carriers on those routes handed out questionnaires to every family on their routes.

即使只有2%的样本量，人口普查局仍然被数量庞大的问卷压得喘不过气来。美国邮政局试图整理这些问卷并进行初步统计。问卷旨在收集受访者的详细人口统计信息和工作经历，但没有人知道如何处理如此庞大的详细信息。要知道，当时还没有计算机，唯一能辅助纸笔统计的工具是手动机械计算器。汉森联系了耶日·内曼，他的论文是该调查设计的基础。用汉森的话说，内曼指出，“我们不必了解并匹配所有案例，也不必理解所有关系”，就能找到最重要问题的答案。采纳了内曼的建议，汉森和他的同事们忽略了问卷中大部分复杂且令人困惑的细节，只统计了失业人数。

Even with a 2 percent sample, the Census Bureau was overwhelmed with the huge number of questionnaires. The U.S. Postal Service attempted to organize them and make initial tabulations. The questionnaire had been designed to pick up detailed information about the demographics and the work history of the respondents, and no one knew how to examine such large amounts of detailed information. Recall that this was before the computer, and the only aids to pencil-and-paper tabulations were hand-operated mechanical calculators. Hansen contacted Jerzy Neyman, whose papers had formed the basis of the survey’s design. In Hansen’s words, Neyman pointed out that “we didn’t have to know and match all the cases and understand all the relationships” to find answers to the most important questions. Using Neyman’s advice, Hansen and his coworkers set aside most of the complicated and confusing details of the questionnaires and counted the numbers of unemployed.

在汉森的领导下，人口普查局进行了一系列细致的研究，最终证明这些小规模的随机抽样比之前使用的判断抽样方法准确得多。最终，美国劳工统计局和人口普查局引领了随机抽样的新时代。乔治·盖洛普和路易斯·比恩将这些方法应用于政治民意调查领域。[20]为了1940年的人口普查，人口普查局启动了在普查范围内开展抽样调查的详细计划。当时，普查局新聘了一位名叫威廉·赫维茨的年轻统计学家。汉森和赫维茨成为了亲密的合作伙伴和朋友；他们发表了一系列重要且影响深远的论文，最终汇集成1953年出版的教科书《抽样调查方法与理论》（与第三位作者威廉·马多合著）。汉森和赫维茨的论文和著作对抽样调查领域产生了如此巨大的影响，并被频繁引用，以至于该领域的许多研究人员都误以为只有一个人名叫汉森·赫维茨。

It took a series of careful studies in the Census Bureau, under Hansen, to prove that these small random surveys were much more accurate than the judgment samples that had been used before. Eventually, the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics and the Census Bureau led the way into a new world of random sampling. George Gallup and Louis Bean took these methods into the region of political polling.[20] For the 1940 census, the Census Bureau initiated elaborate plans for sample surveys within the overall census. There was a young, newly hired statistician at the bureau named William Hurwitz. Hansen and Hurwitz became close collaborators and friends; they issued a series of important and influential papers, culminating in the 1953 textbook Sample Survey Methods and Theory (written with a third author, William Madow). The Hansen and Hurwitz papers and text became so important to the field of sample surveys, and were quoted so often, that many workers in the field came to believe that there was one person named Hansen Hurwitz.

杰罗姆·康菲尔德

JEROME CORNFIELD

新政时期涌入华盛顿的许多年轻工人后来都成为了政府和学术界的领军人物。他们中的一些人忙于创造新的数学和统计方法，无暇攻读研究生学位。杰罗姆·康菲尔德就是一个典型的例子。康菲尔德参与了劳工统计局的一些早期调查，之后进入美国国立卫生研究院工作。他与一些学术界的领军人物合作发表了论文。他解决了病例对照研究中涉及的数学难题。他的科学论文涵盖了从随机抽样理论到就业模式经济学、从鸡肿瘤研究到光合作用问题以及环境毒素对人类健康的影响等诸多领域。他创建的许多统计方法如今已成为医学、毒理学、药理学和经济学领域的标准方法。

Many of the new young workers who arrived in Washington during the New Deal went on to become major figures in government and in academia. Some of them were too busy creating new mathematics and statistical methods to go for graduate degrees. A prime example is Jerome Cornfield. Cornfield participated in some of these early surveys at the Bureau of Labor Statistics and then moved to the National Institutes of Health. He published papers jointly with some of the leading figures in academia. He solved the mathematical problems involved in case-control studies. His scientific papers range from work on random sample theory to the economics of employment patterns, the investigation of tumors in chickens, problems in photosynthesis, and effects of environmental toxins on human health. He created many of the statistical methods that have now become standard in the fields of medicine, toxicology, pharmacology, and economics.

科恩菲尔德最重要的成就之一是设计并初步分析了始于1948年的弗雷明汉研究。该研究以马萨诸塞州弗雷明汉镇为“典型城镇”，测量镇上所有居民的大量健康指标，并对他们进行多年的追踪调查。这项研究至今已持续五十余年。由于政府预算削减，该研究的经费时常被削减，其发展历程可谓坎坷曲折，如同电影《宝琳的冒险》一般。尽管如此，它仍然是研究饮食和生活方式对心脏病和癌症长期影响的重要信息来源。

One of Cornfield’s most important achievements was in the design and initial analysis of the Framingham Study, begun in 1948. The idea was to take Framingham, Massachusetts, as a “typical town,” measure a large number of health variables on everyone in the town, and then follow these people for a number of years. The study has now been running for over fifty years. It has had a “Perils of Pauline” existence as, from time to time, attempts have been made to cut its funding in the interests of budget reduction in government. It remains a major source of information on the long-term effects of diet and lifestyles on heart disease and cancer.

为了分析弗雷明汉研究前五年的数据，康菲尔德遇到了理论文献中尚未解决的根本性问题。他与普林斯顿大学的教员合作，解决了这些问题。其他人继续在他开创的理论基础上发表论文，但康菲尔德对自己找到的方法感到满意。1967年，他与他人合著了该研究的第一篇医学论文，这是第一篇揭示高胆固醇对心脏病风险影响的论文。

To analyze the first five years of data from the Framingham Study, Cornfield ran into fundamental problems that had not been addressed in the theoretical literature. Working with faculty members at Princeton University, he solved these problems. Others went on to produce papers on the theoretical development he started, but Cornfield was satisfied to have found a method. In 1967, he was a coauthor of the first medical article to emerge from the study, the first article to show the effects of elevated cholesterol on the probability of heart disease.

1973年，我和杰里·康菲尔德（Jerry Cornfield）一起在一个委员会工作，该委员会是国会委员会一系列听证会的一部分。在我们工作间隙，康菲尔德接到了一个电话。电话那头是哥伦比亚大学的经济学家瓦西里·列昂惕夫（Wassily Leontief），他打电话来说自己刚刚获得了诺贝尔经济学奖，并想感谢康菲尔德在他们共同的研究工作中所发挥的作用，正是这项工作最终促成了他获奖。这项研究始于20世纪40年代末，当时列昂惕夫曾到美国劳工统计局寻求帮助。

I was on a committee with Jerry Cornfield, convened in 1973 as part of a set of hearings before a Congressional committee. During a break in our work, Cornfield was called to the phone. It was Wassily Leontief, an economist at Columbia University, calling to say that he had just been awarded the Nobel Prize in economics and wanted to thank Cornfield for the role Jerry had played in their work, which led to this prize. This work had originated in the late 1940s when Leontief had come to the Bureau of Labor Statistics for help.

列昂惕夫认为，经济可以分解为若干部门，例如农业、钢铁制造业、零售业等等。每个部门都利用其他部门的原材料和服务来生产原材料或服务，然后再将这些原材料或服务提供给其他部门。这种相互关系可以用数学矩阵的形式来描述，通常被称为“投入产出分析”。二战结束后，列昂惕夫开始研究这个模型时，他前往美国劳工统计局寻求帮助，以收集所需的数据。为了协助他，该局指派了一位当时在那里工作的年轻分析师杰罗姆·科恩菲尔德。

Leontief believed that the economy could be broken down into sectors, like farming, steel manufacturing, retailing, and so forth. Each sector uses material and services from the other sectors to produce material or a service, which it supplies to those other sectors. This interrelationship can be described in the form of a mathematical matrix. It is often called an “input—output analysis.” When he first began investigating this model at the end of World War II, Leontief went to the Bureau of Labor Statistics to help gather the data he needed. To assist him, the bureau assigned a young analyst who was working there at the time, Jerome Cornfield.

列昂惕夫可以将经济划分为几个大类，例如将所有制造业归为一类，或者他也可以将这些大类进一步细分。投入产出分析的数学理论要求描述经济的矩阵具有唯一的逆矩阵。这意味着矩阵一旦构建完成，就必须经过一个称为“矩阵求逆”的数学运算。在计算机尚未普及的年代，用计算器求矩阵逆是一项既困难又繁琐的工作。我读研究生的时候，我们每个人都必须求一个矩阵逆——我猜这是一种“为了我们灵魂的救赎”的成人礼。我记得我曾经尝试求一个5×5矩阵的逆，花了几天时间，其中大部分时间都花在了查找错误和重做上。

Leontief could break the economy down into a few broad sectors, such as putting all manufacturing in one sector, or he could subdivide the sectors into more specific ones. The mathematical theory of input–output analysis requires that the matrix that describes the economy have a unique inverse. That meant that the matrix, once assembled, had to be subjected to a mathematical procedure called “inverting the matrix.” At that time, before the widespread availability of computers, inverting a matrix was a difficult and tedious procedure on a calculator. When I was in graduate school, each of us had to invert a matrix—I suspect as a kind of rite of passage “for the good of our souls.” I remember trying to invert a 5 × 5 matrix and taking several days, most of which I spent locating my mistakes and redoing what I had done wrong.

列昂惕夫最初划分的扇区构成了一个 12 × 12 的矩阵，杰里·康菲尔德随后尝试对这个 12 × 12 的矩阵进行逆运算，以寻找唯一解。他花了大约一周时间，最终得出结论：扇区的数量必须增加。于是，康菲尔德和列昂惕夫忐忑不安地开始细分扇区，直到最终得到他们认为可行的最简单矩阵——一个 24 × 24 的矩阵。他们都明白，这远远超出了单个人的能力范围。康菲尔德估计，即使他每周七天都工作，也需要几百年才能完成一个 24 × 24 矩阵的逆运算。

Leontief’s initial set of sectors led to a 12 × 12 matrix, and Jerry Cornfield proceeded to invert that 12 × 12 matrix to see if there was a unique solution. It took him about a week, and the end result was the conclusion that the number of sectors had to be expanded. So, with trepidation, Cornfield and Leontief began subdividing the sectors until they ended with the simplest matrix they thought would be feasible, a 24 × 24 matrix. They both knew this was beyond the capacity of a single human being. Cornfield estimated that it would take him several hundred years of seven-day workweeks to invert a 24 × 24 matrix.

二战期间，哈佛大学研制出了最早的计算机之一，但那台计算机非常原始。它使用机械继电器开关，经常发生故障。战争结束后，这台庞大的机器不再用于其他用途，哈佛大学开始寻找它的用武之地。康菲尔德和列昂惕夫决定将他们研制的24×24矩阵送到哈佛，让哈佛的Mark I型计算机来完成繁琐的计算，并求出逆矩阵。当他们试图为这个项目筹集资金时，却遭到了劳工统计局会计部门的阻挠。当时政府奉行一项政策：只为商品买单，不为服务买单。其理论依据是，政府拥有各领域的专家，如果有什么事情需要做，政府里总有人能胜任。

During World War II, Harvard University had developed one of the first, very primitive computers. It used mechanical relay switches and would often jam. There was no longer any war work for it, and Harvard was looking for applications for its monstrous machine. Cornfield and Leontief decided to send their 24 × 24 matrix to Harvard where its Mark I computer would go through the tedious calculations and compute the inverse. When they sought to pay for this project, the process was stopped by the accounting office of the Bureau of Labor Statistics. The government had a policy at that time; it would pay for goods but not for services. The theory was that the government had all kinds of experts working for it. If something had to be done, there should be someone in government who could do it.

他们向政府会计师解释说，虽然理论上这在理论上是可行的，但实际上没有人能活到完成的地步。会计师表示理解，但他也想不出有什么办法可以绕过这项规定。这时，科恩菲尔德提出了一个建议。结果，该局签发了一份资本货物采购订单。什么资本货物呢？发票上要求该局从哈佛大学购买“一个倒置的矩阵”。

They explained to the government accountant that, while this was theoretically something that a person could do, no one would be able to live long enough to do it. The accountant was sympathetic, but he could not see a way around the regulation. Cornfield then made a suggestion. As a result, the bureau issued a purchase order for capital goods. What capital goods? The invoice called for the bureau to purchase from Harvard “one matrix, inverted.”

经济指数

ECONOMIC INDICES

这些在罗斯福新政初期投身政府的青年男女的工作，对国家而言至今仍具有至关重要的意义。他们的工作催生了一系列常规经济指标，这些指标如今被用于微调经济。这些指标包括消费者价格指数（用于衡量通货膨胀）、当前人口调查（用于衡量失业率）、制造业普查、人口普查局在十年一次的人口普查之间对全国人口估计的中间调整，以及许多其他鲜为人知但已被世界所有工业化国家效仿和使用的调查。

The work of these young men and women who rushed into government during the early days of the New Deal continues to be of fundamental importance to the nation. This work led to the regular series of economic indicators that are now used to fine-tune the economy. These indicators include the Consumer Price Index (for inflation), the Current Population Survey (for unemployment rates), the Census of Manufacturing, the intermediate adjustments of Census Bureau estimates of the nation’s population between decennial censuses, and many other less well-known surveys that have been copied and are used by every industrial nation in the world.

在印度，马哈拉诺比斯在新政府成立初期就与总理贾瓦哈拉尔·尼赫鲁结下了深厚的友谊。在他的影响下，尼赫鲁效仿苏联中央计划经济的尝试，常常会通过精心开展的抽样调查进行修正，从而展现出这个新生国家经济的真实状况。在苏联，官僚们为了讨好统治者，捏造生产和经济活动的虚假数据，这助长了中央经济计划中那些愚蠢的过度行为。而在印度，人们总能获得对真实情况的准确评估。尼赫鲁及其继任者或许并不喜欢这种情况，但他们不得不面对。

In India, P. C. Mahalanobis became a personal friend of Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru in the early days of the new government of India. Under his influence, Nehru’s attempts to imitate the central planning of the Soviet Union were often modified by carefully conducted sample surveys, which showed what really was happening to the new nation’s economy. In Russia, the bureaucrats produced false figures of production and economic activity to flatter the rulers, which encouraged the more foolish excesses of their central economic plans. In India, good estimates of the truth were always available. Nehru and his successors may not have liked it, but they had to deal with it.

1962年，R.A.费舍尔访问了印度。此前，他曾多次应马哈拉诺比斯的邀请到访印度。这次访问意义非凡。世界顶尖统计学家齐聚一堂，共同纪念印度统计研究所成立三十周年。费舍尔、内曼、埃贡·皮尔逊、汉森、科恩菲尔德以及其他来自美国和欧洲的统计学家都出席了会议。会议气氛热烈，因为当时数理统计领域正处于蓬勃发展阶段，许多问题尚未解决。统计分析方法正渗透到各个科学领域。新的分析技术不断涌现并被研究。当时有四个专注于统计的科学学会，以及至少八种主要期刊（其中一种是由马哈拉诺比斯创办的）。

In 1962, R. A. Fisher went to India. He had been there many times before at Mahalanobis’s invitation. This was a special occasion. There was a great meeting of the world’s leading statisticians to commemorate the thirtieth anniversary of the founding of the Indian Statistical Institute. Fisher, Neyman, Egon Pearson, Hansen, Cornfield, and others from the United States and Europe were there. The sessions were lively, for the field of mathematical statistics was still in ferment, with many unsolved problems. The methods of statistical analysis were penetrating to every field of science. New techniques of analysis were constantly being proposed and examined. There were four scientific societies devoted to the subject and at least eight major journals (one of which had been founded by Mahalanobis).

会议结束后，与会者各奔东西。在回家的路上，他们听到了噩耗：R.A.费舍尔在返回澳大利亚的船上因心脏病突发去世，享年72岁。他留下的科学论文集共五卷，七部著作至今仍对统计学领域的研究产生着深远的影响。他卓越的原创成就就此画上了句号。

When the conference closed, the attendees went their separate ways. As they arrived home, they heard the news. R. A. Fisher had died of a heart attack on the boat returning him to Australia. He was seventy-two years old. His collected scientific papers fill five volumes, and his seven books continue to influence all that is being done in statistics. His brilliant original accomplishments had come to an end.

C第18章

CHAPTER 18

吸烟会导致癌症吗？

DOES SMOKING CAUSE CANCER?

我1958年，R.A.费舍尔在《百年评论》（Centennial Review ）上发表了一篇题为《香烟、癌症与统计数据》（Cigarettes, Cancer, and Statistics）的论文，并在《自然》（ Nature）杂志上发表了两篇论文，分别题为《肺癌与香烟？》（Lung Cancer and Cigarettes?）和《癌症与吸烟》（Cancer and Smoking）。随后，他将这些论文与一篇长篇序言汇编成册，出版了一本名为《吸烟：癌症争议。对证据的一些评估》（Smoking: the Cancer Controversy. Some Attempts to Assess the Evidence）的小册子。在这些论文中，费舍尔（他经常被拍到叼着烟斗）坚称，那些声称吸烟会导致肺癌的证据存在严重缺陷。

In 1958, R. A. Fisher published a paper entitled “Cigarettes, Cancer, and Statistics” in the Centennial Review, and two papers in Nature entitled “Lung Cancer and Cigarettes?” and “Cancer and Smoking.” He then pulled these together, along with an extensive preface, in a pamphlet entitled “Smoking: the Cancer Controversy. Some Attempts to Assess the Evidence.” In these papers, Fisher (who was often photographed smoking a pipe) insisted that the evidence purported to show that smoking caused lung cancer was badly flawed.

当时，费舍尔并非唯一批评吸烟与癌症研究的人。梅奥诊所首席统计学家、美国生物统计学界的领军人物约瑟夫·伯克森也对研究结果提出了质疑。杰西·内曼也对将肺癌与吸烟联系起来的研究中使用的推理方式提出了异议。费舍尔的批评最为激烈。在接下来的几年里，随着证据的积累，伯克森和内曼似乎都确信二者之间存在关联，但费舍尔依然坚持己见，甚至指责一些顶尖研究人员篡改数据。这令许多统计学家感到尴尬。当时，烟草公司否认这些研究的有效性，指出它们仅仅是“统计相关性”，并没有证据表明吸烟会导致肺癌。表面上看，费舍尔似乎同意他们的观点。他的论证带有强烈的辩论色彩。例如，以下是他一篇论文中的一段话：

Nor was Fisher alone in his criticisms of the smoking/cancer studies at that time. Joseph Berkson, the head statistician at the Mayo Clinic and a leader among American biostatisticians, questioned the results. Jerzy Neyman had raised objections to the reasoning used in the studies that associated lung cancer and cigarette smoking. Fisher was the most strident in his criticism. As the evidence accumulated over the next few years, and both Berkson and Neyman appeared satisfied that the relationship was proved, Fisher remained adamant, actually accusing some of the leading researchers of doctoring their data. It became an embarrassment to many statisticians. At that time, the cigarette companies were denying the validity of the studies, pointing out that they were only “statistical correlations” and that there was no proof that cigarettes caused lung cancer. On the surface, it appeared that Fisher was agreeing with them. His arguments had the air of a polemic. Here, for instance, is a paragraph from one of his papers:

大约一年前，英国医学会期刊上发表的一篇评论让我深刻体会到，有必要对看似揭示了这种关联的研究进行如此严格的审查。该评论最终得出了一个近乎尖锐的结论：必须动用一切现代宣传手段，让全世界都意识到这种可怕的危险。读到这篇文章时，我并不确定自己是否赞同“一切现代宣传手段”，而且我认为此时应该做出道德上的区分……。在一个尚不确定吸烟者是否真的有必要担心这种特定习惯所带来的危害的情况下，利用公共资金支持的各种现代宣传手段，在全世界可能多达一亿的吸烟者心中植入恐惧，这并非一个好公民应尽的职责……

The need for such scrutiny [of the research that appeared to show the relationship] was brought home to me very forcibly about a year ago in an annotation published by the British Medical Association’s Journal, leading up to the almost shrill conclusion that it was necessary that every device of modern publicity should be employed to bring home to the world at large this terrible danger. When I read that, I wasn’t sure that I liked “all the devices of modern publicity,” and it seemed to me that a moral distinction ought to be drawn at this point … . [It] is not quite so much the work of a good citizen to plant fear in the minds of perhaps a hundred million smokers throughout the world—to plant it with the aid of all the means of modern publicity backed by public money—without knowing for certain that they have anything to be afraid of in the particular habit against which the propaganda is to be directed … .

不幸的是，费舍尔对政府利用宣传散布这种恐惧感到愤怒，但他并没有清楚地表达自己的反对意见。人们普遍认为，他只是在扮演一个不愿放弃心爱烟斗的古怪老人的角色。1959年，杰罗姆·康菲尔德与来自美国国家癌症研究所（NCI）、美国癌症协会和斯隆-凯特琳癌症研究所的五位顶尖癌症专家合作，撰写了一份长达30页的论文，回顾了所有已发表的研究。他们考察了费舍尔、伯克森和内曼的反对意见，以及烟草协会（代表烟草公司）提出的反对意见。他们对这场争议进行了条理清晰的阐述，并指出证据压倒性地支持“吸烟是导致人类肺癌发病率迅速上升的致病因素”这一观点。

Unfortunately, in his anger against the use of government propaganda to spread this fear, Fisher did not state his objections very clearly. It became the conventional wisdom that he was playing the role of a crotchety old man who did not want to relinquish his beloved pipe. In 1959, Jerome Cornfield joined with five leading cancer experts from the National Cancer Institute (NCI), the American Cancer Society, and the Sloan-Kettering Institute, to write a thirty-page paper that reviewed all the studies that had been published. They examined Fisher’s, Berkson’s, and Neyman’s objections, along with objections raised by the Tobacco Institute (on behalf of the tobacco companies). They provided a carefully reasoned account of the controversy and showed how the evidence was overwhelmingly in favor of showing that “smoking is a causative factor in the rapidly increasing incidence of human epidermoid carcinoma of the lung.”

这让整个医学界对这个问题有了定论。烟草协会继续在流行杂志上刊登整版广告，质疑这种关联仅仅是统计上的相关性，但1960年以后，任何权威科学期刊上都没有出现过质疑这一发现的文章。四年之内，费舍尔就去世了。他无法继续这场争论，也没有其他人接手。

That settled the issue for the entire medical community. The Tobacco Institute continued to pay for full-page advertisements in popular magazines, which questioned the association as being only a statistical correlation, but no articles that questioned this finding appeared after 1960 in any reputable scientific journal. Within four years, Fisher was dead. He could not continue the argument, and no one else took it up.

世间是否存在因果关系？

IS THERE SUCH A THING AS CAUSE AND EFFECT?

这一切究竟是一位只想安安静静抽烟斗的老人提出的无稽之谈，还是费舍尔的反对意见中蕴含着某种道理？我读过费舍尔关于吸烟与癌症的论文，并将它们与他之前关于归纳推理本质以及统计模型与科学结论之间关系的论文进行了比较。一条连贯的推理路线浮现出来。费舍尔探讨的是一个深刻的哲学问题——一个英国哲学家伯特兰·罗素在20世纪30年代初就曾探讨过的问题，一个困扰着科学思想核心的问题，一个大多数人甚至没有意识到其存在的问题：究竟什么是“因果关系”？这个问题的答案远非简单。

Was it all a lot of nonsense put forward by an old man who wanted to smoke his pipe in peace, or was there something to Fisher’s objections? I have read Fisher’s smoking and cancer papers, and I have compared them to previous papers he had written on the nature of inductive reasoning and the relationship between statistical models and scientific conclusions. A consistent line of reasoning emerges. Fisher was dealing with a deep philosophical problem—a problem that the English philosopher Bertrand Russell had addressed in the early 1930s, a problem that gnaws at the heart of scientific thought, a problem that most people do not even recognize as a problem: What is meant by “cause and effect”? Answers to that question are far from simple.

许多读者或许会记得伯特兰·罗素是一位白发苍苍、慈祥和蔼的哲学家，他曾于20世纪60年代公开批评美国卷入越南战争。那时，罗素勋爵已被官方和学术界公认为20世纪哲学界最伟大的思想家之一。他的第一部重要著作是与比他年长许多的阿尔弗雷德·诺思·怀特海合著的，探讨了算术和数学的哲学基础。这部名为《数学原理》（Principia Mathematica）的著作试图用简单的集合论公理来建立数学的基本概念，例如数和加法。

Bertrand Russell may be remembered by many readers as a white-haired, grandfatherly looking but world-renowned philosopher, who lent his voice to the criticism of United States involvement in the war in Vietnam in the 1960s. By that time, Lord Russell had received both official and scholarly recognition as one of the great minds of twentieth-century philosophy. His first major work, written with Alfred North Whitehead—who was many years his senior—dealt with the philosophical foundations of arithmetic and mathematics. Entitled Principia Mathematica, it tried to establish the basic ideas of mathematics, like numbers and addition, on simple axioms dealing with set theory.

罗素-怀特海理论的核心工具之一是符号逻辑，这种探究方法是二十世纪初最伟大的新发明之一。读者或许还记得，他们曾学习过亚里士多德的逻辑，例如“所有人都会死。苏格拉底是人。因此，苏格拉底会死。”

One of the essential tools of the Russell-Whitehead work was symbolic logic, a method of inquiry that was one of the great new creations of the early twentieth century. The reader may recall having studied Aristotelian logic with examples like “All men are mortal. Socrates is a man. Therefore, Socrates is mortal.”

尽管亚里士多德的逻辑体系已被研究了约2500年，但它却是一个相对无用的工具。它赘述显而易见的事实，随意设定逻辑与非逻辑的规则，并且未能模拟逻辑在数学推理中的运用——而数学推理恰恰是逻辑创造新知识的领域。当学生们还在一丝不苟地背诵着基于苏格拉底的死亡和乌鸦羽毛的黑色等逻辑分类时，数学家们已经运用那些无法完全归入亚里士多德分类的逻辑方法，发现了诸如微积分等新的思想领域。

Although it has been studied for about 2,500 years, Aristotle’s codification of logic is a relatively useless tool. It belabors the obvious, sets up arbitrary rules as to what is logical and what is not, and fails to mimic the use of logic in mathematical reasoning, the one place where logic has been used to produce new knowledge. While students were dutifully memorizing categorizations of logic based on Socrates’s mortality and the blackness of raven feathers, the mathematicians were discovering new areas of thought, like calculus, with the use of logical methods that did not fit neatly into Aristotle’s categories.

这一切随着十九世纪末二十世纪初集合论和符号逻辑的发展而改变。符号逻辑的早期形式，即罗素和怀特海所运用的形式，以被称为“命题”的思维原子为起点。每个命题都有一个真值，称为“T”或“F”。[21]这些命题通过“与”、“或”、“非”和“等于”等符号进行组合和比较。由于每个原子命题都有一个真值，因此它们的任意组合也都有一个真值，该真值可以通过一系列代数步骤计算得出。基于这一简单的基础，罗素、怀特海等人构建了描述数字和算术的符号组合，并且似乎能够描述所有类型的推理。

This all changed with the development of set theory and symbolic logic in the final years of the nineteenth century and early years of the twentieth. In its earliest form, the one that Russell and Whitehead exploited, symbolic logic starts with atoms of thought known as “propositions.” Each proposition has a truth value called “T” or “F.”[21] The propositions are combined and compared with symbols for “and,” for “or,” for “not,” and for “equals.” Because each of the atomic propositions has a truth value, any combination of them has a truth value, which can be computed via a series of algebraic steps. On this simple foundation, Russell, Whitehead, and others were able to build combinations of symbols that described numbers and arithmetic and seemed to describe all types of reasoning.

唯独一个例外！似乎根本无法创建一套符号来表示“A导致B”。因果关系的概念始终无法被逻辑学家们用符号逻辑规则所解释。当然，我们都知道“因果关系”的含义。如果我把玻璃杯掉在浴室地板上，这个行为会导致杯子破碎。如果主人在狗走错方向时就制止它，这个行为会导致狗学会朝正确的方向走。如果农民给庄稼施肥，这个行为会导致庄稼长得更大。如果一个女人在怀孕初期服用沙利度胺，这个行为会导致她的孩子出生时四肢发育不良。如果另一个女​​人患有盆腔炎，那是因为她使用了宫内节育器。[22]如果ABC公司的高级管理职位中女性很少，那是因为管理层存在偏见。如果我的表弟脾气暴躁，那是因为他出生在狮子座。

All except one! There seemed to be no way to create a set of symbols that meant “A causes B.” The concept of cause and effect eluded the best efforts of the logicians to squeeze it into the rules of symbolic logic. Of course, we all know what “cause and effect” means. If I drop a glass tumbler on the bathroom floor, this act causes it to break. If the master restrains the dog whenever it goes in the wrong direction, this act causes the dog to learn to go in the right direction. If the farmer uses fertilizer on his crops, this act causes the crops to grow bigger. If a woman takes thalidomide during the first trimester of her pregnancy, this act causes her child to be born with attenuated limbs. If another woman suffers pelvic inflammation, it was because of the IUD she used.[22] If there are very few women in senior management positions at the ABC firm, it was caused by prejudice on the part of the managers. If my cousin has a hair-trigger temper, this was caused by the fact that he was born under the sign of Leo.

正如伯特兰·罗素在20世纪30年代初有力地论证的那样，人们通常所理解的因果关系是自相矛盾的。不同的因果关系例子无法调和，因为它们都基于相同的推理步骤。事实上，根本不存在所谓的因果关系。它只是一种流行的幻想，一个模糊的概念，经不起纯粹理性的考验。它包含一系列自相矛盾的观点，在科学论述中几乎没有任何价值。

As Bertrand Russell showed very effectively in the early 1930s, the common notion of cause and effect is an inconsistent one. Different examples of cause and effect cannot be reconciled to be based on the same steps of reasoning. There is, in fact, no such thing as cause and effect. It is a popular chimera, a vague notion that will not withstand the batterings of pure reason. It contains an inconsistent set of contradictory ideas and is of little or no value in scientific discourse.

实质性影响

MATERIAL IMPLICATION

罗素提出用符号逻辑中一个定义明确的概念——“实质蕴涵”——来代替因果关系。利用原子命题的基本概念以及“与”、“或”、“非”和“等于”等连接符号，我们可以得出命题A蕴涵命题B的概念。这等价于命题非B蕴涵非A。这听起来有点像贝叶斯定理背后的悖论（我们在第13章讨论过）。但两者之间存在着非常深刻的差异，我们将在后面的章节中进行探讨。

In place of cause and effect, Russell proposed the use of a well-defined concept from symbolic logic, called “material implication.” Using the primitive notions of atomic propositions and the connecting symbols for “and,” “or,” “not,” and “equals,” we can produce the concept that proposition A implies proposition B. This is equivalent to the proposition that not B implies not A. This begins to sound a little like the paradox that lies behind Bayes’s theorem (which we looked at in chapter 13). But there are very deep differences, which we will examine in a later chapter.

十九世纪末，德国医生罗伯特·科赫提出了一系列假设，用于证明某种传染性病原体会导致某种特定疾病。这些假设包括：

In the late nineteenth century, the German physician Robert Koch proposed a set of postulates needed to prove that a certain infective agent caused a specific disease. These postulates required:

  1. 只要能培养出病原体，就能检测到这种疾病。
  2. Whenever the agent could be cultured, the disease was there.
  3. 当疾病不存在时，就无法培养出病原体。
  4. Whenever the disease was not there, the agent could not be cultured.
  5. 病原体清除后，疾病就消失了。
  6. When the agent was removed, the disease went away.

科赫的表述虽然有些冗余，但它实际上是在陈述实质性蕴涵的条件。这些条件或许足以确定某种特定细菌是导致传染病的原因。然而，对于吸烟与癌症这类情况，科赫法则的价值就微乎其微了。让我们来考察一下肺癌与吸烟之间的联系与科赫法则（以及罗素的实质性蕴涵）的契合程度。致病因素是吸烟史。致病疾病是人类肺鳞状细胞癌。有些吸烟者并没有患肺癌。科赫的第一条法则不成立。有些患肺癌的人声称自己从未吸烟。如果我们相信他们的说法，那么科赫的第二条法则也不成立。如果我们把癌症类型限定为小细胞癌，那么患有这种疾病的非吸烟者人数似乎为零，因此科赫的第二条法则或许成立了。如果我们把致病因素去掉，也就是说，如果病人停止吸烟，疾病仍然可能发生，科赫第三定律就得不到满足。

With some redundancy, Koch was stating the conditions for material implication. This may be adequate to determine that a particular species of bacteria caused the infectious disease. When it comes to something like smoking and cancer, however, Koch’s postulates are of little value. Let us consider how well the connection between lung cancer and cigarette smoking fits Koch’s postulates (and hence Russell’s material implication). The agent is a history of cigarette smoking. The disease is human epidermoid carcinoma of the lung. There are cigarette smokers who do not get lung cancer. Koch’s first postulate is not met. There are some people who get lung cancer who claim that they have not been smokers. If we are to believe their claims, Koch’s second postulate is not met. If we restrict the type of cancer to small oat-cell carcinoma, the number of nonsmokers with this disease appears to be zero, so maybe the second postulate has been met. If we take the agent away, that is, if the patient stops smoking, the disease may still come, and Koch’s third postulate is not met.

如果我们应用科赫法则（以及罗素的实质蕴涵），那么唯一符合这些法则的疾病就是由特定的感染性病原体引起的急性疾病，这些病原体可以从血液或其他体液中培养出来。心脏病、糖尿病、哮喘、关节炎或其他类型的癌症则不符合这一法则。

If we apply Koch’s postulates (and with them Russell’s material implication), then the only diseases that meet them are acute conditions that have been caused by specific infective agents that can be cultured from the blood or other fluids of the body. This does not hold for heart disease, diabetes, asthma, arthritis, or cancer in other forms.

康菲尔德的解决方案

CORNFIELD’S SOLUTION

让我们回到1959年康菲尔德和五位著名癌症专家的论文。[23]他们逐一描述了所有已开展的相关研究。首先是理查德·多尔和A·布拉德福德·希尔于1952年在《英国医学杂志》上发表的一项研究[24]。多尔和希尔对英国肺癌死亡人数的迅速上升感到震惊。他们找到了数百例肺癌病例，并将他们与同期入住同一家医院但未患肺癌的相似患者（年龄、性别、社会经济地位相同）进行匹配。结果显示，肺癌患者中的吸烟者人数几乎是其他患者（在此类研究中称为“对照组”）的十倍。到1958年底，又有五项类似的研究，研究对象分别来自斯堪的纳维亚半岛、美国、加拿大、法国和日本。所有研究都得出了相同的结果：癌症患者中的吸烟者比例远高于对照组。

Let us return to the 1959 paper by Cornfield and five prominent cancer specialists.[23] One by one, they describe all the studies that had been run on the subject. First there was a study by Richard Doll and A. Bradford Hill,[24] published in the British Medical Journal in 1952. Doll and Hill had been alarmed by the rapid rise in the number of patients dying from lung cancer in the United Kingdom. They located several hundred cases of such patients and matched them to similar patients (same age, sex, socioeconomic status) who had been admitted to the same hospitals at the same time, but who did not have lung cancer. There were almost ten times as many smokers among the lung cancer patients than there were among the others (called the “controls” in such a study). By the end of 1958, there were five other studies of this nature, using patients in Scandinavia, the United States, Canada, France, and Japan. All of them showed the same results: a much greater percentage of smokers among the cancer patients than among the controls.

这些研究被称为“回顾性研究”。它们从疾病入手，反向推导，寻找与该疾病相关的既往病史。为了确保这些既往病史与疾病相关，而非与患者的某些更普遍的特征相关，这类研究需要设置对照组（未患病的患者）。然而，这些对照组常常被诟病与病例组不匹配。加拿大曾开展过一项著名的回顾性研究，探讨人工甜味剂与膀胱癌的关联。该研究似乎表明人工甜味剂与膀胱癌之间存在关联，但仔细分析数据后发现，病例组几乎全部来自低收入阶层，而对照组几乎全部来自高收入阶层。这意味着病例组和对照组并不具有可比性。20世纪90年代初，耶鲁大学医学院的阿尔文·费恩斯坦和拉尔夫·霍维茨提出了非常严格的规则，用于指导此类研究，以确保病例组和对照组的匹配。如果我们把费恩斯坦-霍维茨规则应用于这些回顾性病例对照癌症与吸烟研究，那么所有这些研究都未能通过。

These are called “retrospective studies.” They start with the disease and work backward to see what prior conditions are associated with the disease. They need controls (patients without the disease) to be sure that these prior conditions are associated with the disease and not with some more general characteristic of the patients. These controls can be criticized as not matching the disease cases. One prominent retrospective study was run in Canada on the effects of artificial sweeteners as a cause of bladder cancer. The study seemed to show an association between artificial sweeteners and bladder cancer, but a careful analysis of the data showed that the disease cases were almost all from low socioeconomic classes and the controls were almost all from upper socioeconomic classes. This meant that the disease cases and the controls were not comparable. In the early 1990s, Alvan Feinstein and Ralph Horvitz at the Yale Medical School proposed very rigid rules for running such studies to ensure that the cases and controls match. If we apply the Feinstein-Horvitz rules to these retrospective case-control cancer and smoking studies, all of them fail.

另一种方法是前瞻性研究。在这种研究中，研究人员会预先选定一组个体，仔细记录他们的吸烟史，并追踪他们的健康状况。到1958年，已经开展了三项独立的前瞻性研究。第一项研究（由进行过第一项回顾性研究的希尔和多尔报告）涉及英国的5万名医生。实际上，在希尔和多尔的研究中，受试者并没有接受长期追踪。相反，研究人员对这5万名医生进行了访谈，了解他们的健康习惯，包括吸烟习惯，并对他们进行了为期五年的追踪，因为其中许多人开始患上肺癌。现在，证据不仅仅表明了吸烟与肺癌之间的关联。研究人员能够根据医生的吸烟量将他们分成不同的组。吸烟量越大的医生患肺癌的概率越高。这是一种剂量反应关系，是药理学中证明药物作用的关键证据。在美国，哈蒙德和霍恩进行了一项前瞻性研究（发表于 1958 年），对 187,783 名男性进行了为期四个月的随访。他们也发现了剂量反应关系。

An alternative approach is the prospective one. In such a study, a group of individuals is identified in advance. Their smoking histories are carefully recorded, and they are followed to see what will become of them. By 1958, three independent prospective studies had been run. The first (reported by the same Hill and Doll who had done the first retrospective study) involved 50,000 medical doctors in the United Kingdom. Actually, in the Hill and Doll study, the subjects were not followed over a long period of time. Instead, the 50,000 doctors were interviewed about their health habits, including their smoking habits, and followed for five years, as many of them began to come down with lung cancer. Now the evidence did more than suggest a relationship. They were able to divide the doctors into groups depending upon how much they smoked. The doctors who smoked more had greater probabilities of having lung cancer. This was a dose response, the key proof of an effect in pharmacology. In the United States, Hammond and Horn ran a prospective study (published in 1958) on 187,783 men, whom they followed for four months. They also found a dose response.

然而，前瞻性研究也存在一些问题。如果研究规模较小，则可能仅针对特定人群。将研究结果外推至更大人群可能并不合理。例如，早期的大多数前瞻性研究都以男性为研究对象。当时，女性肺癌的发病率太低，无法进行分析。前瞻性研究的第二个问题是，可能需要很长时间才能积累到足够多的事件（肺癌），从而进行有效的分析。这两个问题都可以通过追踪大量人群来解决。大量的样本能够增强研究结果适用于更大人群的可信度。即使事件在短时间内发生的概率很小，在短时间内追踪大量人群仍然可以获得足够的事件，从而进行分析。

There are some problems with prospective studies, however. If the study is small, it may be dealing with a particular population. It may not make sense to extrapolate the results to a larger population. For instance, most of these early prospective studies were done with males. At that time, the incidence of lung cancer in females was too low to allow for analysis. A second problem with prospective studies is that it may take a long time for enough of the events (lung cancer) to occur to allow for sensible analysis. Both these problems are dealt with by following a large number of people. The large number gives credence to the suggestion that the results hold for a large population. If the probability of the event is small in a short period of time, following a large number of people for a short period of time will still produce enough events to allow for analysis.

Doll 和 Hill 的第二项研究之所以选择医生作为研究对象，是因为他们认为医生对吸烟习惯的回忆较为可靠，而且医生的职业背景几乎可以确保该群体中所有肺癌病例都会被记录下来。我们能否将受过良好教育的专业医生的研究结果外推到学历低于高中的码头工人身上呢？Hammond 和 Horn 使用了近 20 万名男性作为研究对象，希望他们的样本更具代表性——但这样做可能会导致信息不够准确。此时，读者或许会想起卡尔·皮尔逊的数据样本曾因属于便利抽样而受到质疑。那么，这些样本难道不也是便利抽样吗？

The second Doll and Hill study used medical doctors because it was believed that their recollection of smoking habits could be relied upon and because their belonging to the medical profession made it virtually certain that all the lung cancers that occurred in the group would be recorded. Can we extrapolate the results from educated, professional doctors to what would happen to a dockhand with less than a high school education? Hammond and Horn used almost 200,000 men in hopes that their sample would be more representative—at the risk of getting less than accurate information. At this point, the reader may recall the objection to Karl Pearson’s samples of data because they were opportunity samples. Weren’t these also opportunity samples?

为了回应这一质疑，1958年，H.F. Dorn研究了三个主要城市的死亡证明，并对死者家属进行了后续访谈。这是一项涵盖所有死亡案例的研究，因此不能被视为便利抽样。研究结果再次证实了吸烟与肺癌之间的密切关系。然而，有人可能会认为对死者家属的访谈存在缺陷。在进行这项研究时，肺癌与吸烟之间的关系已广为人知。因此，肺癌患者的亲属可能比死于其他疾病的患者的亲属更容易记住患者生前吸烟。

To answer this objection, in 1958, H. F. Dorn studied the death certificates from three major cities and followed up with interviews of the surviving families. This was a study of all deaths, so it could not be considered an opportunity sample. Again, the relationship between smoking and lung cancer was overwhelming. However, the argument could be made that the interviews with surviving family members were flawed. By the time this study was run, the relationship between lung cancer and smoking was widely known. It was possible that surviving relatives of patients who had died of lung cancer would be more likely to remember that the patient had been a smoker than would relatives of patients who had died from other diseases.

大多数流行病学研究都是如此。每项研究都存在某种缺陷。对于每项研究，批评者都能设想出可能导致结论偏差的各种可能性。康菲尔德及其合作者收集了1958年以前在不同国家开展的30项流行病学研究，这些研究针对不同的人群。正如他们所指出的，正是这些涵盖各种类型的众多研究之间高度一致的结果，才使最终结论更具说服力。他们逐一讨论了这些反对意见。他们考虑了伯克森的反对意见，并展示了如何利用一项或另一项研究来回应这些意见。内曼提出，如果吸烟患者的寿命比非吸烟者长，并且肺癌是一种老年疾病，那么最初的回顾性研究可能存在偏差。康菲尔德等人提供了研究中患者的数据，表明这种描述并不合理。

Thus it is with most epidemiological studies. Each study is flawed in some way. For each study, a critic can dream up possibilities that might lead to bias in the conclusions. Cornfield and his coauthors assembled thirty epidemiological studies run before 1958 in different countries and concentrating on different populations. As they point out, it is the overwhelming consistency across these many studies, studies of all kinds, that lends credence to the final conclusion. One by one, they discuss each of the objections. They consider Berkson’s objections and show how one study or another can be used to address them. Neyman suggested that the initial retrospective studies could be biased if the patients who smoked lived-longer than the nonsmokers and if lung cancer was a disease of old age. Cornfield et al. produced data about the patients in the studies to show that this was not a sensible description of those patients.

他们从两个方面探讨了机会样本是否具有代表性的问题。首先，他们展示了所研究的患者群体范围，从而提高了结论在不同人群中适用的可能性。其次，他们指出，如果因果关系是由基础生物学因素决定的，那么患者不同的社会经济和种族背景就无关紧要了。他们回顾了毒理学研究，这些研究表明烟草烟雾对实验动物和组织培养物具有致癌作用。

They addressed in two ways the question of whether the opportunity samples were nonrepresentative. They showed the range of patient populations involved, increasing the likelihood that the conclusions held across populations. They also pointed out that, if the cause and effect relationship holds as a result of fundamental biology, then the patients’ different socioeconomic and racial backgrounds would be irrelevant. They reviewed toxicology studies, which showed carcinogenic effects of tobacco smoke on lab animals and tissue cultures.

Cornfield等人撰写的这篇论文是流行病学研究中如何证明因果关系的一个经典案例。尽管每项研究都存在缺陷，但证据却不断积累，因为一项又一项的研究都强化了相同的结论。

This paper by Cornfield et al. is a classic example of how cause is proved in epidemiological studies. Although each study is flawed, the evidence keeps mounting, as one study after another reinforces the same conclusions.

吸烟与癌症对比橙剂

SMOKING AND CANCER VERSUS AGENT ORANGE

与此形成鲜明对比的是，有人试图将越战老兵晚年遭受的健康问题归咎于橙剂。所谓的致病因素是当时使用的除草剂中的污染物。几乎所有研究都只针对少数以不同方式接触过除草剂的男性。其他人群的研究并未支持这些结论。20世纪70年代，意大利北部一家化工厂发生事故，导致大量人员接触到更高浓度的污染物，但并未造成长期影响。对新西兰草坪农场工人的研究表明，接触过除草剂的工人中，某种特定类型的出生缺陷有所增加，但这些工人大多是毛利人，而毛利人本身就具有这种特定出生缺陷的遗传倾向。

A contrast to this can be seen in the attempts to indict Agent Orange as a cause for health problems Vietnam War veterans have suffered in later life. The putative agents of cause are contaminants in the herbicide that was used. Almost all studies have dealt with the same small number of men exposed in different ways to the herbicide. Studies in other populations did not support these findings. In the 1970s, an accident in a chemical factory in northern Italy resulted in a large number of people being exposed to much higher levels of the contaminant, with no long-term effects. Studies of workers on New Zealand turf farms who were exposed to the herbicide suggested an increase in a specific type of birth defect, but the workers were mostly Maoris, who have a genetically related tendency toward that particular birth defect.

吸烟研究和橙剂研究的另一个区别在于，吸烟的假定后果具有高度特异性（肺表皮样癌）。而据称由橙剂暴露引起的事件则包括一系列神经系统和生殖系统问题。这与毒理学中通常的结论——特定物质导致特定类型损伤——相悖。橙剂研究没有剂量反应关系的迹象，但数据不足以确定个体所接触的不同剂量。这导致情况变得复杂，使得伯克森、内曼和费舍尔等人的反对意见无法得到回应。

Another difference between the smoking and the Agent Orange studies is that the putative consequences of smoking are highly specific (epidermoid carcinoma of the lung). The events that were supposedly caused by Agent Orange exposure consisted of a wide range of neurological and reproductive problems. This runs contrary to the usual finding in toxicology that specific agents cause specific types of lesions. For the Agent Orange studies, there is no indication of a dose response, but there are insufficient data to determine the different doses to which individuals have been exposed. The result is a muddied picture, where objections like those of Berkson, Neyman, and Fisher have to go unaddressed.

通过对流行病学研究的分析，我们已经远离了伯特兰·罗素那种高度精确的理论和物质层面的意义。如今，因果关系是通过许多存在缺陷的人群调查来推断的。这些关系是统计学上的，分布参数的变化似乎与特定原因相关。理智的观察者应该能够整合大量存在缺陷的研究，并从中发现潜在的共同点。

With the analysis of epidemiological studies, we have moved a long way from the highly specific exactitude of Bertrand Russell and material implication. Cause and effect are now imputed from many flawed investigations of human populations. The relationships are statistical, where changes in the parameters of distributions appear to be related to specific causes. Reasonable observers are expected to integrate a large number of flawed studies and see the underlying common threads.

发表偏倚

PUBLICATION BIAS

如果这些研究是经过筛选的呢？如果观察者所能看到的只是实际开展的研究中精心挑选出来的子集呢？如果每发表一项阳性研究，就有一项阴性研究被压制呢？毕竟，并非所有研究都能发表。有些研究因为研究人员无法或不愿完成而从未发表。有些研究因为不符合期刊的标准而被期刊编辑拒稿。很多时候，尤其是在某个主题存在争议的情况下，编辑们往往会倾向于发表那些被科学界接受的研究，而拒绝那些不被接受的研究。

What if the studies have been selected? What if all that is available to the observer is a carefully selected subset of the studies that were actually run? What if, for every positive study that is published, a negative study was suppressed? After all, not every study gets published. Some never get written up because the investigators are unable or unwilling to complete the work. Some are rejected by journal editors because they do not meet the standards of that journal. All too often, especially when there is some controversy associated with the subject, editors are tempted to publish that which is acceptable to the scientific community and reject that which is not acceptable.

这是费舍尔的指控之一。他声称希尔和多尔的早期研究遭到了审查。多年来，他一直试图让两位作者公布详细数据以佐证他们的结论。他们只发表了摘要，但费舍尔认为这些摘要掩盖了数据中实际存在的矛盾。他指出，在希尔和多尔的第一项研究中，作者询问吸烟者吸烟时是否吸入烟雾。当数据按“吸入器使用者”和“非吸入器使用者”进行分类时，非吸入器使用者的肺癌发病率反而更高。吸入器使用者的肺癌发病率似乎更低。希尔和多尔声称，这可能是由于受访者未能理解问题所致。费舍尔对此嗤之以鼻，并质问他们为何不公布研究的真正结论：吸烟有害健康，但如果必须吸烟，吸入烟雾总比不吸入要好。

This was one of Fisher’s accusations. He claimed that the initial work by Hill and Doll had been censored. He tried for years to have the authors release detailed data to back up their conclusions. They had only published summaries, but Fisher suggested that these summaries had hidden inconsistencies that were actually in the data. He pointed out that, in the first Hill and Doll study, the authors had asked if the patients who smoked inhaled when they smoked. When the data are organized in terms of “inhalers” and “noninhalers,” the noninhalers are the ones with an excess of lung cancer. The inhalers appear to have less lung cancer. Hill and Doll claimed that this was probably due to a failure on the part of the respondents to understand the question. Fisher scoffed at this and asked why they didn’t publicize the real conclusions of their study: that smoking was bad for you, but if you had to smoke, it was better to inhale than not to inhale.

令费舍尔感到愤慨的是，希尔和多尔在对医生进行前瞻性研究时，竟然没有将这个问题纳入调查范围。还有什么人被精心挑选了呢？费舍尔想知道。他震惊于政府竟然要动用权力和金钱来恐吓民众。他认为这与纳粹利用宣传来操纵舆论并无二致。

To Fisher’s disgust, Hill and Doll left that question out of their investigation when they ran their prospective study on medical doctors. What else was being carefully selected? Fisher wanted to know. He was appalled that the power and money of government was going to be used to throw fear into the populace. He considered this no different from the use of propaganda by the Nazis to drive public opinion.

费舍尔的解决方案

FISHER’S SOLUTION

费舍尔也深受伯特兰·罗素关于因果关系的论述的影响。他认识到，物质推论不足以解释大多数科学结论。他广泛论述了归纳推理的本质，并提出，只要遵循良好的实验设计原则，就可以根据具体的调查研究得出关于生命的一般性结论。他证明，将处理方式随机分配给受试者的实验方法，为归纳推理提供了逻辑和数学上坚实的基础。

Fisher had also been influenced by Bertrand Russell’s discussion of cause and effect. He recognized that material implication was inadequate to describe most scientific conclusions. He wrote extensively on the nature of inductive reasoning and proposed that it was possible to conclude something in general about life on the basis of specific investigations, provided that the principles of good experimental design were followed. He showed that the method of experimentation, where treatments were randomly assigned to subjects, provided a logically and mathematically solid basis for inductive inference.

流行病学家们正在使用费舍尔为分析设计实验而开发的工具，例如他的估计方法和显著性检验。他们将这些工具应用于机会样本，在这些样本中，治疗分配并非来自研究之外的某种随机机制，而是研究本身的一个复杂组成部分。他沉思道，假设存在某种基因导致一些人吸烟，而另一些人不吸烟。进一步假设，同样的基因倾向也与肺癌的发生有关。众所周知，许多癌症都具有家族遗传性。他假设，吸烟和肺癌之间的这种关联是因为两者都是由同一事件，即相同的基因倾向造成的。为了证明他的观点，他收集了同卵双胞胎的数据，并表明双胞胎之间存在很强的家族遗传倾向，即两人要么都吸烟，要么都不吸烟。他向其他人发起挑战，要求他们证明肺癌不受类似的基因影响。

The epidemiologists were using the tools Fisher developed for the analysis of designed experiments, such as his methods of estimation and tests of significance. They were applying these tools to opportunity samples, where the assignment of treatment was not from some random mechanism external to the study but an intricate part of the study itself. Suppose, he mused, that there was something genetic that caused some people to be smokers and others not to smoke. Suppose, further, that this same genetic disposition involved the occurrence of lung cancer. It was well known that many cancers have a familial component. Suppose, he said, this relationship between smoking and lung cancer was because each was due to the same event, the same genetic disposition. To prove his case, he assembled data on identical twins and showed that there was a strong familial tendency for both twins to be either smokers or nonsmokers. He challenged the others to show that lung cancer was not similarly genetically influenced.

一边是脾气暴躁的天才R.A.费舍尔，他将整个统计分布理论建立在坚实的数学框架之上，正在进行最后的斗争。另一边是杰里·康菲尔德，他唯一的正规教育是历史学学士学位，统计学完全是自学的，而且他忙于创造重要的新统计方法，无暇攻读更高的学位。费舍尔说：“没有随机实验设计，你什么都证明不了。”康菲尔德则说：“有些事情不适合这种设计，但证据的积累应该能够证明事实。”两人都已去世，但他们的思想后继者仍然与我们同在。这些论点在法庭上引起共鸣，人们试图在法庭上证明存在基于结果的歧视。它们在试图识别人类活动对生物圈造成的有害影响方面发挥着作用。每当医学领域出现生死攸关的重大问题时，它们都扮演着重要的角色。毕竟，因果关系并非那么容易证明。

On one side there was R. A. Fisher, the irascible genius who put the whole theory of statistical distributions on a firm mathematical setting, fighting one final battle. On the other side was Jerry Cornfield, the man whose only formal education was a bachelor’s degree in history, who had learned his statistics on his own, who was too busy creating important new statistics to pursue a higher degree. You cannot prove anything without a randomized experimental design, said Fisher. Some things do not lend themselves to such designs, but the accumulation of evidence should prove the case, said Cornfield. Both men are now deceased, but their intellectual descendants are still with us. These arguments resound in the courts, where attempts are made to prove discrimination on the basis of outcomes. They play a role in attempts to identify the harmful results of human activity on the biosphere. They are there whenever great issues of life and death arise in medicine. Cause and effect are not so simple to prove, after all.

C第十九章

CHAPTER 19

如果你想要最好的人……

IF YOU WANT THE BEST PERSON …

L1913年夏天，乔治·W·斯内德科尔离开肯塔基大学，将为数不多的行李装进一个手提箱，驱车前往爱荷华大学。他听说那里正在招聘数学教师。不幸的是，他对爱荷华州的地理一无所知，最终来到了爱荷华州立学院所在的艾姆斯，而不是他原本应该到达的爱荷华城。他被告知，学校并没有刊登招聘数学家的广告，但他们一直在招收一些数学基础薄弱的学生。他是否愿意教授一门代数课程？六年之后，他成功说服教员们让他开设一门关于统计方法新思想的课程。因此，当R·A·费舍尔关于农业实验的第一批论文开始发表时，他正好在一所农业学校任教，并且对统计学的思想非常熟悉。

Late in the summer of 1913, George W. Snedecor left the University of Kentucky, packed his few belongings in a suitcase, and set out by auto for the University of Iowa, where he had heard there was an opening for someone to teach mathematics. Unfortunately, he knew nothing about the geography of Iowa and eventually found himself in Ames, Iowa, the home of Iowa State College, rather than in Iowa City, where he would have found the University of Iowa. No, he was told, they had not advertised for a mathematician, but they had been accepting a number of students who were weak in their mathematical background. Would he be willing to teach a course in algebra? Six years later, he convinced the faculty that he should prepare a course in the new ideas of statistical methods. Thus, he was on hand at an agricultural school and attuned to the ideas of statistics when R. A. Fisher’s first papers on agricultural experiments began to appear.

斯内德科尔本人接受的数学教育中并未包含概率论课程，但他仍留在艾姆斯研究这些新进展，并创建了一个统计实验室。最终，他创建了统计学系，这是美国第一个此类学术部门。他研究了费舍尔的论文，并继续审阅了皮尔逊、“学生”、埃奇沃思、耶茨、冯·米塞斯等人的著作。虽然斯内德科尔在原创研究方面贡献不多，但他是一位杰出的综合者。20世纪30年代，他编写了教科书《统计方法》，最初以油印本的形式出版，最终于1940年正式面世，该书成为该领域的权威教材。他改进了费舍尔的《研究工作者的统计方法》，加入了基本的数学推导，整合了相似的思想，并提供了一套大量的表格，使读者能够以最小的努力计算p值和置信区间。 20 世纪 70 年代，对所有科学领域已发表的科学文章的引用情况进行回顾发现，斯内德科尔的《统计方法》是被引用次数最多的书籍。

Snedecor, whose own education in mathematics had contained no courses in probability theory, stayed on in Ames to study these new developments and to found a statistical laboratory. Eventually, he created a department of statistics that was the first such academic department in the United States. He studied Fisher’s papers and went on to review the work of Pearson, “Student,” Edgeworth, Yates, von Mises, and others. While Snedecor did not contribute much in the way of original research, he was a great synthesizer. In the 1930s, he produced a textbook, Statistical Methods, first in mimeographed form and finally published in 1940, which became the preeminent text in the field. He improved on Fisher’s Statistical Methods for Research Workers by including the basic mathematical derivations, by pulling similar ideas together, and by including an extensive set of tables that made it possible to calculate p-values and confidence intervals with a minimum of effort. In the 1970s, a review of citations in published scientific articles from all areas of science showed that Snedecor’s Statistical Methods was the most frequently cited book.

斯内德科尔也是一位高效的管理者。他邀请统计研究领域的领军人物到艾姆斯度过暑假。在20世纪30年代的大部分时间里，费舍尔本人也经常到访艾姆斯，每次停留数周，进行讲学和咨询。位于爱荷华州艾姆斯的统计实验室和统计系成为了世界上最重要的统计研究中心之一。二战前几年，在艾姆斯担任客座教授的学者名单，堪称该领域最杰出人物的荟萃。

Snedecor was also an effective administrator. He invited the leading figures in statistical research to spend summers at Ames. For most of the 1930s, Fisher himself visited for several weeks at a time, lecturing and consulting. The Statistical Laboratory and Department of Statistics at Ames, Iowa, became one of the most important centers of statistical research in the world. The list of men and women who were visiting professors at Ames during the years before World War II was a roster of the most distinguished people in the field.

格特鲁德·考克斯正是在这段时间来到爱荷华州立大学学习。她梦想成为一名传教士，去遥远的异国他乡拯救灵魂。高中毕业后的近七年时间里，她一直致力于卫理公会的社会服务。为了从事她所向往的传教工作，她需要接受大学教育。斯内德科尔说服她，统计学更有趣，于是她毕业后留在了统计实验室，与他一起工作。1931年，她获得了爱荷华州立大学授予的第一个统计学硕士学位，斯内德科尔聘请她到自己的系任教。她对费舍尔的实验设计理论尤为感兴趣，并在艾姆斯开设了第一批实验设计课程。斯内德科尔为她在加州大学心理学研究生项目中找到了一个名额，她在那里继续深造了两年。获得博士学位后，她回到艾姆斯，斯内德科尔任命她为统计实验室主任。

Gertrude Cox came to study at Iowa State during this time. She had dreams of becoming a missionary and saving souls in far-off lands. For almost seven years after high school, she had dedicated her life to social service in the Methodist Church. She needed a university education to apply to the missionary service that interested her. Snedecor convinced her that statistics was more interesting, and she stayed on after she graduated to work with him at the Statistical Laboratory. In 1931, she received the first master’s degree in statistics granted by Iowa State, and Snedecor hired her to teach in his department. She became particularly interested in Fisher’s theories of experimental design and taught the first courses on experimental design at Ames. Snedecor found a place for her in the graduate program in psychology at the University of California, where she continued her studies for two more years. She returned to Ames with her doctorate, and Snedecor put her in charge of the Statistical Laboratory.

与此同时，众多杰出的统计学家继续涌入爱荷华州的艾姆斯。威廉·科克伦也曾在此停留一段时间，担任教职。他与格特鲁德·考克斯一起教授实验设计课程（当时这类课程已经开设了好几门），并于1950年合著了一本关于该主题的教科书，名为《实验设计》。与斯内德科尔的《统计方法》一样，科克伦和考克斯的《实验设计》以扎实的数学基础为指导，带领读者逐步了解各种方法。书中包含一系列非常有用的表格，方便实验者根据具体情况修改设计并分析结果。《科学引文索引》每年都会发布科学期刊的引文列表。该索引以小字印刷，引文以五列排列；科克伦和考克斯的著作通常每年至少占据一整列。

In the meantime, the stream of prominent statisticians continued to flow through Ames, Iowa. William Cochran stopped for a while to take an appointment on the faculty. He joined Gertrude Cox in teaching experimental design courses (there were now several such courses), and together in 1950 they wrote a textbook on the subject, entitled Experimental Designs. Like Snedecor’s Statistical Methods, Cochran and Cox’s Experimental Designs takes the reader through the methods with a firm foundation of the mathematics behind it. It has a set of very useful tables to allow the experimenter to modify a design for specific situations and to analyze the results. The Science Citation Index publishes lists of citations from scientific journals each year. The Index is printed in small print with the citations ranged in five columns; Cochran and Cox’s book usually takes up at least one full column each year.

女性的贡献

THE CONTRIBUTIONS OF WOMEN

读者可能已经注意到，除了弗洛伦斯·南丁格尔·戴维之外，本书迄今为止介绍的所有统计学家都是男性。统计学发展的早期阶段由男性主导。虽然也有许多女性从事统计学工作，但她们几乎都被安排进行统计分析所需的繁琐计算，因此被称为“计算机”。由于需要进行大量的计算，而且全部都是用手摇计算器完成的，这项枯燥乏味的工作通常都交给女性。人们普遍认为，女性往往更加温顺耐心，而且比男性更值得信赖，能够反复检查计算结果的准确性。卡尔·皮尔逊领导下的高尔顿生物统计实验室的典型景象是：皮尔逊和几位男性四处走动，查看计算机的输出结果或讨论深奥的数学概念，而他们周围则挤满了正在计算的女性。

The reader will probably have noticed that, with the exception of Florence Nightingale David, all the statisticians described so far in this book have been male. The early years of statistical development were dominated by men. Many women were working in the field, but they were almost all employed in doing the detailed calculations needed for statistical analysis, and were indeed called “computers.” Since extensive computations had to be made, all of them on hand-cranked calculators, this tedious work was usually delegated to women. Women tended to be more docile and patient, so went the belief, and could be depended upon more than men to check and recheck the accuracy of their calculations. A typical picture of the Galton Biometrical Laboratory under Karl Pearson would have Pearson and several men walking around, looking at output from the computers or discussing deep mathematical ideas, while all about them rows of women were computing.

随着二十世纪的推进，情况开始发生变化。特别是耶日·内曼（Jerzy Neyman），他给予了许多女性帮助和鼓励，指导她们的博士论文，与她们合作发表论文，并在学术界为她们争取到重要的地位。到了20世纪90年代，当我参加统计学会的全国性会议时，大约一半的与会者是女性。女性在美国统计协会、生物统计学会、皇家统计学会和数理统计学会中都占据着重要地位。然而，她们的代表性仍然不及男性。统计学期刊上发表的文章中，约有30%由一位或多位女性作者撰写，而美国统计协会的会士中，女性仅占13%。这种差距正在改变。在二十世纪末，女性展现了她们在数学领域卓越的才能。

As the twentieth century progressed, the situation began to change. Jerzy Neyman, in particular, was helpful and encouraging to many women, supervising their Ph.D. theses, publishing papers jointly with them, and finding prominent places for them in the academic community. By the 1990s, when I attended national meetings of the statistical societies, about half the attendees were women. Women are prominent in the American Statistical Association, the Biometric Society, the Royal Statistical Society, and the Institute of Mathematical Statistics. They are not yet equally represented with men, however. About 30 percent of the articles published in the statistical journals have one or more female authors, and only 13 percent of the members of the American Statistical Association who have been honored as fellows are women. This disparity is changing. In the last years of the twentieth century, the female half of the human race showed that it is capable of great mathematical activity.

1940年，情况却并非如此。当时，乔治·斯内德科尔在火车上偶遇北卡罗来纳大学校长弗兰克·格雷厄姆。两人坐在一起，谈笑风生。格雷厄姆略有耳闻统计学革命，斯内德科尔便向他详细介绍了统计模型在农业和化学研究领域取得的巨大进展。格雷厄姆惊讶地发现，当时美国唯一一个拥有完整统计系的大学竟然是爱荷华州立大学。普林斯顿大学的萨姆·威尔克斯正在组建一个数学统计学家团队，但这支团队隶属于数学系。密歇根大学的情况也类似，亨利·卡弗也在那里工作。[25]格雷厄姆对这次火车之旅的所见所闻进行了深入的思考。

This was not the case in 1940, when George Snedecor happened to meet Frank Graham, the president of the University of North Carolina, on a train. They sat together and had much to talk about. Graham had heard something of the statistical revolution, and Snedecor was able to fill him in on the great advances that had been made in agricultural and chemical research with statistical models. Graham was surprised to learn that the only full-fledged statistics department in the United States was at Iowa State. At Princeton University, Sam Wilks was developing a group of mathematical statisticians, but this was within the mathematics department. A similar situation existed at the University of Michigan with Henry Carver.[25] Graham gave a great deal of thought to what he learned on that train ride.

格特鲁德·考克斯，1900–1978

Gertrude Cox, 1900–1978

几周后，格雷厄姆联系了斯内德科尔。他已经说服了位于罗利市的北卡罗来纳州立大学，认为时机成熟，可以建立一个统计实验室，最终建立一个类似于艾姆斯研究所的统计系。斯内德科尔能否推荐一位合适的人选来领导这个系？斯内德科尔坐下来，列出了十位他认为合适的人选。他叫来格特鲁德·考克斯核对名单，并询问她的意见。考克斯看了看名单，问道：“我怎么样？”

Weeks later, Graham contacted Snedecor. He had convinced his sister school, North Carolina State University at Raleigh, that the time was ripe to set up a statistical laboratory and eventually a statistics department, similar to the ones at Ames. Could Snedecor recommend a man to head such a department? Snedecor sat down and drew up a list of ten men he thought would work out. He called in Gertrude Cox to verify his list and ask what she thought of it. She looked it over and asked, “What about me?”

斯内德科尔在信中补充道：“这是我能想到的十位最优秀的人选。但是，如果你想要最好的人选，我会推荐格特鲁德·考克斯。”

Snedecor added a line to his letter: “These are the ten best men I can think of. But, if you want the best person, I would recommend Gertrude Cox.”

格特鲁德·考克斯不仅是一位杰出的实验科学家和优秀的教师，更是一位卓越的管理者。她建立了一支由杰出统计学家组成的师资队伍，这些统计学家同时也是优秀的教师。她的学生们后来在工业界、学术界和政府部门担任要职。所有人都对她充满敬意和爱戴。我第一次见到她是在美国统计协会的一次会议上，当时我坐在一位身材娇小、安静的老妇人对面。她说话时，眼神闪烁着热情，对讨论的话题总是兴致勃勃，无论是理论探讨还是具体应用。她的评论中带着一种令人愉悦却又内敛的幽默。我当时并不知道她患有白血病，不久之后便因病去世。在她去世后的这些年里，她的学生们每年夏天都会聚集在统计学会的传统联合会议上，举办一场以她名字命名的公路赛，并筹集资金设立以她名字命名的奖学金。

Gertrude Cox turned out to be not only an excellent experimental scientist and a wonderful teacher, but also a remarkable administrator. She built a faculty of renowned statisticians who were also good teachers. Her students went on to take major positions in industry, academia, and government. She was treated by all of them with great respect and affection. When I first met her at a meeting of the American Statistical Association, I found myself sitting opposite a small, quiet elderly lady. When she talked, her eyes sparkled with enthusiasm as she would warm up to the matter under discussion, whether it was theoretical or involved a particular application. Her comments were salted with a delightful, if subdued, wit. I did not realize that she was suffering from leukemia, which would end her life soon after. In the years since her death, her former students gather each summer at the traditional joint meeting of the statistical societies where they sponsor a road race in her honor and raise money for scholarships in her name.

到1946年，考克斯的应用统计系取得了巨大成功，以至于弗兰克·格雷厄姆得以在北卡罗来纳大学教堂山分校建立数理统计系，不久之后又建立了生物统计系。北卡罗来纳州立大学、北卡罗来纳大学和杜克大学组成的“三角”已成为统计研究中心，并催生了众多依托这些院校专业知识的私人研究公司。格特鲁德·考克斯所创造的成就，远超她的老师乔治·斯内德科的成就。

By 1946, Cox’s Department of Applied Statistics had been so successful that Frank Graham was able to establish a department of mathematical statistics at the University of North Carolina in Chapel Hill, and soon afterward a department of biostatistics. The “triangle” of North Carolina State, University of North Carolina, and Duke University has become a center of statistical research, spawning private research firms that draw upon the expertise of these schools. The world that Gertrude Cox wrought has dwarfed the creation of her teacher, George Snedecor.

经济指标的发展

THE DEVELOPMENT OF ECONOMIC INDICATORS

在美国，女性在政府的统计部门发挥了重要作用，她们在人口普查局、劳工统计局、国家卫生统计中心以及管理和预算局等机构担任过许多高级职位。其中资历最深的要数珍妮特·诺伍德，她于1991年从劳工统计局局长的职位上退休。

Women have played an important role in the statistical components of government in America, serving in many senior positions at the Census Bureau, the Bureau of Labor Statistics, the National Center for Health Statistics, and the Bureau of Management and Budget. One of the most senior was Janet Norwood, who retired as Commissioner of the Bureau of Labor Statistics in 1991.

珍妮特·诺伍德（右）担任劳工统计局局长

Janet Norwood (right) as Commissioner of Labor Statistics

当美国加入二战时，诺伍德正在新泽西州新布伦瑞克市的罗格斯大学女子学院——道格拉斯学院就读。她的男友伯纳德·诺伍德即将奔赴战场，于是他们决定结婚。她十九岁，他二十岁。他没有立即出国，他们得以在一起。然而，这段婚姻给相对封闭的道格拉斯学院带来了一个难题。此前，学院从未有过已婚学生。关于男性访客的规定是否也适用于珍妮特和她的丈夫？她是否需要父母的许可才能离开校园前往纽约市与他见面？这种“第一人”的经历一直伴随着珍妮特·诺伍德。1949年，她从塔夫茨大学获得博士学位——是当时最年轻的博士毕业生。“我曾多次，”她写道，“在我积极参与的组织中，我都是第一个当选高级职务的女性。”她是第一位被任命为劳工统计局局长的女性，她从 1979 年到 1991 年担任该职位。

Norwood was attending Douglass College, the women’s branch of Rutgers University in New Brunswick, New Jersey, when the United States entered World War II. Her boyfriend, Bernard Norwood, was leaving for the war, so they decided to get married. She was nineteen, he was twenty. He did not go overseas immediately, and they were able to be together. This marriage posed a problem for the sheltered world of Douglass College, however. They had never had married students before. Would the rules about male visitors hold for Janet and her husband? Would she need permission from her parents to leave campus and go to New York City to see him? This experience of being the first was to follow Janet Norwood. In 1949, she received a Ph.D. from Tufts University—the youngest person at that time ever to have done so. “From time to time,” she wrote, “I have been the first woman elected to high office in organizations in which I have been active.” She was the first woman to be named commissioner of labor statistics, a position she held from 1979 to 1991.

1979年，政府或许并未完全意识到他们任命的究竟是怎样的人。在诺伍德担任局长之前，劳工部一直有这样的惯例：由政策部门的一名代表列席劳工统计局所有新闻稿的审查会议。诺伍德告知这位代表，他不再受邀参加这些会议。她认为，劳工统计局发布的经济信息不仅必须准确、公正，而且必须给人以这样的印象。她希望劳工统计局的所有活动都能完全免受任何政治影响。

The administration may not have realized quite who they were putting in this position in 1979. Before Norwood became commissioner, it had been the practice of the Department of Labor that a representative of the policy arm of the department sat in on all reviews of the press releases planned by the Bureau of Labor Statistics. Norwood informed the representative that he would no longer be welcome at these meetings. She believed that the economic information produced by the bureau not only had to be accurate and nonpartisan, it had to appear to be so. She wanted all the bureau’s activities completely insulated from the slightest possibility of political influence.

我发现，明确表明你愿意为了原则辞职，这一点非常重要，前提是这个问题足够重要……在政府中，你拥有你所主张和坚持的独立性……在政府中保持独立性并不容易。例如，当你必须纠正美国总统的错误时，你该如何处理？我们做到了。

I found that it was important to make clear that you were willing to resign on principle if the issue were important enough … . In government, you have the independence that you assert and insist upon … . Independence in government is not easy to accomplish. For example, how do you handle situations in which you have to correct the President of the United States? We did that.

珍妮特·诺伍德和她的丈夫都拥有经济学博士学位。婚后的头几年，尤其是在她丈夫参与建立欧洲共同市场机构期间，她没有外出工作，而是在家照顾两个儿子，并通过撰写文章保持专业领域的活跃度。随着一家人定居华盛顿特区，小儿子也上了小学，珍妮特·诺伍德开始寻找一份能够让她拥有部分或全部下午空闲时间的工作，以便在儿子放学回家时陪伴在他身边。美国劳工统计局正好有一个这样的职位空缺，她可以灵活安排工作时间，每周有三个下午可以陪伴儿子。

Both Janet Norwood and her husband received Ph.D.s in economics. For the first few years of their married life, especially when her husband was involved in setting up the institutions of the European Common Market, she did not work outside the home, but raised two sons and wrote articles to keep active in her field. With the family settled in Washington, D.C., and with their second son in grade school, Janet Norwood looked for a position that would enable her to have some or all afternoons free, to be there when her son came home after school. Such a position opened at the Bureau of Labor Statistics, where she could arrange her work hours to be free three afternoons a week.

劳工统计局似乎是劳工部下属的一个不起眼的小机构，而劳工部本身也是一个鲜少登上新闻头条的政府部门。与白宫或国务院的喧嚣相比，这个不起眼的机构究竟在做些什么呢？然而，它却是政府运转机器中不可或缺的一环。政府的运作离不开信息。那些随着新政来到华盛顿的才华横溢的年轻人很快发现，如果没有关于国家经济状况的基本信息，政策就无法制定，而这些基本信息却难以获取。新政的一项重要创新，便是建立起获取这些信息所需的机制。

The Bureau of Labor Statistics would appear to be a minor bureau in the Department of Labor, which is itself a branch of government that seldom makes the headlines. What goes on in this lowly bureau, compared to the excitement of the White House or the State Department? It is, however, an essential cog in the machinery of government. Government has to run on information. The bright young men and women who came to Washington with the New Deal soon discovered that policy could not be made without some essential information about the state of the nation’s economy, and such essential information was unavailable. An important innovation of the New Deal consisted of setting up the machinery necessary to produce this information.

美国劳工统计局要么开展必要的调查来生成这些信息，要么分析从其他部门（例如人口普查局）收集的数据。珍妮特·诺伍德于1963年加入劳工统计局。到1970年，她已晋升为消费者价格指数（CPI）的负责人。CPI用于调整社会保障金、追踪通货膨胀，并用于调整联邦政府向州政府的大部分转移支付。1978年，劳工统计局对CPI进行了重大改革，珍妮特·诺伍德负责规划和监督这项工作。

The Bureau of Labor Statistics either conducts the surveys needed to produce this information or analyzes data accumulated from other departments, like the Census Bureau. Janet Norwood joined Labor Statistics in 1963. By 1970, she had risen to be in charge of the Consumer Price Index (CPI). The CPI is used to index social security payments, track inflation, and adjust most transfers of payment from the federal to the state governments. In 1978, the Bureau of Labor Statistics engaged in a major overhaul of the CPI, which Janet Norwood planned and supervised.

消费者物价指数 (CPI) 和该局生成的其他系列数据（珍妮特·诺伍德将担任该局局长）涉及复杂的数学模型，其中包含相对晦涩难懂的参数，这些参数在计量经济模型中是有意义的，但对于缺乏经济学数学方面训练的人来说，往往很难解释清楚。

The CPI and other series generated by the bureau, over which Janet Norwood was to be the commissioner, involve complicated mathematical models, with relatively arcane parameters that make sense in econometric models but are often hard to explain to someone who lacks training in the mathematical side of economics.

报纸上经常引用消费者物价指数（CPI）的数据，例如，会指出上个月通货膨胀率上升了0.2%。CPI是一组复杂的数字，它反映了经济不同部门和不同地区价格模式的变化。CPI的计算始于“市场篮子”的概念。市场篮子指的是一个典型家庭可能购买的一系列商品和服务。在构建市场篮子之前，需要进行抽样调查，以确定家庭购买哪些商品和服务以及购买频率。计算过程中会考虑各种商品和服务的权重，例如，一个家庭可能每周都买面包，但几年才买一次汽车，买房的频率则更低。

The CPI is often quoted in newspapers as noting, for example, that inflation rose 0.2 percent last month. It is a complicated set of numbers, indicating the changes in price patterns across different sectors of the economy and in different regions of the country. It starts with the concept of a market basket. This is a set of goods and services that a typical family might purchase. Before the market basket can be assembled, sample surveys are taken to determine what families purchase and how often. Mathematical weights are computed to take into account that a family might purchase bread every week but a car only once every few years and a house less frequently.

一旦市场篮子及其相应的数学权重确定下来，统计局的工作人员就会被派往随机选择的商店，记​​录篮子中商品的当前价格。记录的价格会根据数学权重公式进行加权组合，并计算出一个总值，这个总值在某种程度上可以代表特定规模家庭当月的平均生活成本。

Once the market basket and its associated mathematical weights have been assembled, employees of the bureau are sent out to randomly chosen stores, where they record the current prices for items on their list. The prices recorded are combined according to the mathematical weighting formulas and an overall number is computed that is, in some sense, the average cost of living for a family of a given size for that month.

从概念上讲，用指数来描述某种经济活动的平均模式很容易理解。但实际构建这样一个指数却困难得多。如何将新产品（例如家用电脑）的出现纳入考量？如何考虑消费者在价格过高时可能选择其他类似产品的情况（例如选择酸奶而不是酸奶油）？消费者价格指数（CPI）和其他衡量国家经济状况的指标一直在不断接受重新评估。珍妮特·诺伍德主持了CPI的上一次重大改革，但未来还会有更多改革。

In concept, the idea of an index to describe the average pattern of some economic activity is easy to understand. Attempting to construct such an index is more difficult. How does one take into account the appearance of a new product (like a home computer) on the market? How does one account for the possibility that the consumer might choose another but similar product if the price is too high (like choosing yogurt instead of sour cream)? The CPI and other measures of the nation’s economic health are constantly being subjected to reexamination. Janet Norwood oversaw the last major overhaul of the CPI, but there will be others in the future.

消费者物价指数（CPI）并非衡量国家经济健康状况的唯一指标。还有其他指数涵盖制造业活动、库存和就业模式。此外，还有社会指标、监狱人口估算等——所有这些参数都与非经济活动相关。这些确实是卡尔·皮尔逊意义上的参数。它们是概率分布和数学模型的一部分，这些参数并非描述具体的可观察事件，而是支配可观察事件模式的“事物”。因此，在美国，没有哪个家庭的月支出与CPI完全相等；失业率也无法反映实际失业人数，因为失业人数每小时都在变化。那么，究竟谁才算“失业”呢？是指从未就业且目前不寻找工作的人吗？是指那些从一份工作跳槽到另一份工作，享受五周假期和遣散费的人吗？还是指那些每周只想做几个小时工作的人？经济模型的世界充满了对这类问题的任意答案，并且涉及大量永远无法精确观察但又相互作用的参数。

The CPI is not the only index of the nation’s economic health. There are other indices generated to cover manufacturing activity, inventories, and employment patterns. There are also social indicators, estimates of prison populations—all parameters associated with other noneconomic activity. These are, indeed, parameters in Karl Pearson’s sense. They are parts of probability distributions, mathematical models, where the parameters do not describe a specific observable event but are “things” that govern the pattern of observable events. In this way, there is no family in the United States whose monthly costs are exactly equal to the CPI; and the unemployment rate cannot describe the real number of unemployed workers, which changes by the hour. Who, for that matter, is “unemployed”? Is it someone who has never been employed and is not now seeking work? Is it someone who is moving from one job to another with five weeks off and severance pay? Is it someone who is looking for only a few hours a week of work? The world of economic models is filled with arbitrary answers to such questions and involves a large number of parameters that can never be observed exactly but that interact with one another.

在经济和社会指标的推导过程中，并没有像费雪那样的权威来确立最优标准。我们试图将复杂的人际互动简化为一小堆数字，这其中必然存在人为因素。在美国首次失业人口普查中，只统计了户主（通常是男性）。而目前的失业人口统计则包括上个月正在寻找工作的所有人。珍妮特·诺伍德在主持消费者物价指数（CPI）重大改革时，不得不协调各方对类似人为定义的不同意见，而且总会有一些真诚的批评者对某些定义提出异议。

There is no R. A. Fisher to establish optimum criteria in the derivation of economic and social indicators. In each case, we are trying to reduce a complex interaction among people into a small collection of numbers. Arbitrary decisions have to be made. In the first census of unemployment in the United States, only heads of households (usually male) were counted. Current counts of the unemployed include anyone who was looking for work within the previous month. Janet Norwood, in overseeing a major overhaul of the CPI, had to reconcile the different opinions about similarly arbitrary definitions, and there will always be sincere critics who object to some of those definitions.

理论统计学中的女性

WOMEN IN THEORETICAL STATISTICS

本章介绍的两位女性——格特鲁德·考克斯和珍妮特·诺伍德——主要从事行政和教学工作。在20世纪后半叶，女性在理论统计学的发展中也发挥了重要作用。回想一下第六章中提到的LHC Tippett提出的用于预测“百年一遇洪水”的极值渐近线。该分布的一个变体，即“威布尔分布”，在航空航天工业中得到了广泛应用。威布尔分布的一个问题是它不满足费舍尔正则性条件，而且目前还没有明确的最优方法来估计其参数。也就是说，直到北美罗克韦尔公司的南希·曼发现了威布尔分布与一种更简单的分布之间的联系，并发展出如今在该领域使用的方法之前，一直没有最优方法。

Gertrude Cox and Janet Norwood, the two women described in this chapter, were primarily administrators and teachers.
