Dragging Box behind him, Gaddum stormed into the main hut of the complex and marched into a meeting of the committee in charge of reviewing reports. To use Box’s words: “I felt very embarrassed. Here’s this very distinguished guy, who reads the riot act to all these civil servants and says, ‘Put the bloody thing back in.’” And they quickly did.

战争结束后，博克斯决定认真学习统计学。因为他读过费舍尔的著作，而且知道费舍尔在伦敦大学学院任教，所以他便前往这所大学。但他并不知道，费舍尔早在1943年就离开了伦敦，前往剑桥大学担任遗传学系主任。博克斯的面试官是埃贡·皮尔逊，皮尔逊曾因与内曼合作研究假设检验而受到费舍尔的尖刻批评。博克斯兴致勃勃地描述了费舍尔的工作，并解释了他从中学到的关于实验设计的知识。皮尔逊静静地听着，然后说道：“好吧，你当然可以来。但我认为你会发现，除了费舍尔之外，统计学界还有一两位杰出人物。”

When the war ended, Box decided it would be worth his while to study statistics. Because he had read Fisher, and knew that Fisher was teaching at University College of the University of London he sought out the university. What he did not know was that Fisher had left London in 1943 to chair the Department of Genetics at Cambridge. Box found himself being interviewed by Egon Pearson, who had suffered some of Fisher’s acid disdain for his work with Neyman on hypothesis testing. Box launched into an excited description of Fisher’s work, explaining what he had learned about experimental design. Pearson listened quietly and then said, “Well, by all means you can come. But I think you’ll learn there were one or two other people besides Fisher in statistics.”

博克斯在伦敦大学学院学习，获得学士学位后继续攻读硕士学位。他展示了一些实验设计方面的研究成果，并被告知这些成果足以作为博士论文，于是他转而攻读博士学位。当时，帝国化学工业公司（ICI）是英国新化学品和药物研发领域的主要公司。博克斯受邀加入该公司的数学服务部门。1948年至1956年间，他在ICI工作，发表了一系列杰出的论文（通常与他人合作），这些论文拓展了实验设计技术，研究了逐步调整生产过程产量以提高产量的方法，并标志着他日后在柯尔莫哥洛夫随机过程理论的实际应用方面工作的开端。

Box studied at University College, took his bachelor’s degree, and then went on to study for a master’s degree. He presented some of his work in experimental design and was told that it was good enough for a doctoral thesis, so he took his Ph.D. instead. At that point, Imperial Chemicals Industry (ICI) was the major company in Great Britain in the discovery of new chemicals and drugs. Box was invited to join their mathematical services group. He worked at ICI from 1948 to 1956, turning out a remarkable series of papers (often with coauthors) that extended the techniques of experimental design, examined methods for gradually adjusting the output of a manufacturing process to improve its yield, and represented the beginnings of his later work on the practical applications of Kolmogorov’s theories of stochastic processes.

美国境内的盒子

BOX IN THE UNITED STATES

乔治·博克斯来到普林斯顿大学担任统计技术研究小组主任，之后又在威斯康星大学创建了统计系。他荣获所有主要统计组织的会士称号，并因其卓越成就获得多项著名奖项。退休后，他仍然活跃于研究和组织工作中。他的成就涵盖统计研究的诸多领域，兼具理论与应用。

George Box came to Princeton University to be the director of its Statistical Techniques Research Group and then went on to found a department of statistics at the University of Wisconsin. He has been honored by being named a fellow of all the major statistical organizations, has received several prestigious prizes for his accomplishments, and continues to be active in research and organizations since his retirement. His accomplishments range across many areas of statistical research, dealing with both theory and applications.

博克斯在费舍尔为帝国化学工业公司（ICI）工作期间就认识了他，但两人私交并不密切。费舍尔在普林斯顿大学担任统计技术研究小组主任期间，他的女儿琼有机会前往美国，经朋友介绍，她在普林斯顿大学找到了一份秘书的工作。两人相识并结婚，琼·费舍尔·博克斯于1978年出版了她父亲及其工作的权威传记。

Box got to know Fisher in his days working for ICI, but never on a close personal basis. When he was directing the Statistical Techniques Research Group at Princeton, one of Fisher’s daughters, Joan, was given the opportunity to go to the United States, where some friends found a position for her as a secretary at Princeton. They met and married, and Joan Fisher Box published a definitive biography of her father and his work in 1978.

Box对统计学的贡献之一是提出了“稳健性”这一概念。他担心许多统计方法依赖于包含数据分布特性假设的数学定理，而这些假设可能并不成立。即使定理的条件不成立，是否也能找到有效的方法？Box提议将这类方法称为“稳健性”。他进行了一些初步的数学研究，但认为稳健性的概念过于模糊。他反对赋予它更明确的含义，因为他认为拥有一个通用的、模糊的概念来指导程序的选择是有益的。然而，这个概念逐渐发展壮大。假设检验的稳健性被定义为误差概率。斯坦福大学的Bradley Efron在1968年扩展了Fisher的一个几何思想，证明了学生t检验在这个意义上是稳健的。EJG Pitman的方法被用来证明大多数非参数检验在相同的意义上也是稳健的。

One of Box’s contributions to statistics was the word robust. He was concerned that many statistical methods relied on mathematical theorems that contained assumptions about the distributional properties of the data that might not be true. Could useful methods be found even if the conditions for the theorem do not hold? Box proposed calling such methods “robust.” He made some initial mathematical investigations but decided that the concept of robustness was too vague. He opposed attempts to give it a more solid meaning, because he thought there was some advantage to having a general, vague idea to guide the choice of procedures. However, the idea took a life of its own. The robustness of a hypothesis test was defined in terms of the probability of error. By extending one of Fisher’s geometric ideas, Bradley Efron of Stanford University proved in 1968 that “Student”’s t-test was robust in this sense. E. J. G. Pitman’s methods were used to show that most of the nonparametric tests were robust in the same sense.

随后，在20世纪60年代末，普林斯顿大学的约翰·图基（John Tukey）及其同事和学生着手研究如何处理那些明显错误的测量结果。他们的成果是1972年发表的《普林斯顿稳健性研究》。这项研究的基本思想是受污染分布。研究假设所采集的测量值大部分来自我们想要估计其参数的概率分布。但是，这些测量值中混入了来自另一个分布的少量值。

Then, in the late 1960s, John Tukey at Princeton and a group of his fellow faculty members and students attacked the problem of what to do with measurements that are apparently wrong. The result of this was the Princeton Robustness Study, published in 1972. The basic idea behind this study is that of a contaminated distribution. The measurements taken are assumed to come, most of them, from the probability distribution whose parameters we wish to estimate. But the measurements have been contaminated by a few values that come from another distribution.

二战期间发生了一个典型的受污染分布案例。美国海军当时研发了一种新型光学测距仪，使用者需要观察目标的三维立体图像，并在目标“上方”放置一个大三角形。为了确定该仪器的统计误差，数百名水兵对一个已知距离的目标进行测距。在每位水兵使用测距仪之前，测距仪的位置都会根据随机数表重置，以避免之前的设置对其造成影响。

A classic example of a contaminated distribution occurred during World War II. The U.S. Navy had developed a new optical range finder, which required the user to view a three-dimensional stereoscopic image of the target and place a large triangle “on top” of the target. An attempt was made to determine the degree of statistical error in this instrument by having several hundred sailors range on a target that was a known distance away. Before each sailor looked through the range finder, the position was reset according to a table of random numbers, so the previous setting would not influence him.

设计这项研究的工程师们并不知道，有20%的人无法进行立体视觉。他们患有所谓的“弱视”。测距仪采集的测量数据中，大约有五分之一完全错误。由于只有这项研究的数据，无法确定哪些数据来自患有弱视的水手，因此无法识别出这些混杂数据中的具体测量结果。

The engineers who designed the study did not know that 20 percent of humans cannot see stereoscopically. They have what is known as lazy eye. About one-fifth of the measurements taken on the range finder were completely wrong. With only the data from the study in hand, there was no way of knowing which came from sailors with lazy eye, so the individual measurements from the contaminating distribution could not be identified.

普林斯顿大学的研究利用计算机进行了一项庞大的蒙特卡罗模拟[36]，对大量受污染的分布进行了建模。他们研究的是估计分布中心趋势的方法。他们发现，当数据受到污染时，人们常用的平均值就是一个非常糟糕的衡量标准。一个典型的例子是耶鲁大学在20世纪50年代尝试估算其校友毕业十年后的收入。如果取所有收入的平均值，结果会非常高，因为只有极少数校友是千万富翁。事实上，超过80%的校友收入低于平均水平。

The Princeton study modeled a large number of contaminated distributions in a gigantic Monte Carlo[36] study on a computer. They were looking at methods of estimating the central tendency of a distribution. One thing they learned was that the much beloved average is a very poor measure when the data have been contaminated. A classic example of such a situation was an attempt by Yale University in the 1950s to estimate the income of its alumni ten years after graduation. If they took the average of all the incomes, it would be quite high, since a very small number of the alumni were multimillionaires. In fact, over 80 percent of the alumni had incomes less than the average.

普林斯顿稳健性研究发现，即使只有一个来自污染分布的异常值，平均值也会受到严重影响。药理学家从他的大鼠溃疡研究中带给我的数据就出现了这种情况。他所学的统计方法都依赖于平均值。读者可能会质疑：假设这些极端且看似错误的测量值是真实的；假设它们来自我们正在研究的分布，而不是来自污染分布。那么，剔除它们只会使结论产生偏差。

The Princeton Robustness Study discovered that the average was heavily influenced by even a single outlier from the contaminating distribution. This is what was happening to the data the pharmacologist brought me from his rat ulcer study. The statistical methods he had been taught to use all depended upon the average. The reader may protest: Suppose these extreme and seemingly wrong measurements are true; suppose they come from the distribution we are examining and not from a contaminating distribution. Throwing them out will only bias the conclusions.

普林斯顿稳健性研究旨在寻找一种能够实现两个目标的解决方案：

The Princeton Robustness Study sought a solution that would do two things:

  1. 如果存在干扰测量值，则降低其影响。
  2. Downgrade the influence of contaminating measurements if they are there.
  3. 如果测量结果未受到污染，请给出正确答案。
  4. Produce correct answers if the measurements have not been contaminated.

我建议药理学家采用其中一种解决方案，他成功地解读了数据。后续实验得出了一致的结论，表明这种稳健的分析方法是正确的。

I proposed that the pharmacologist use one of these solutions, and he was able to make sense out of the data. Future experiments produced consistent conclusions, showing that the robust analysis was on track.

博克斯和考克斯

BOX AND COX

在帝国化学工业公司（ICI）工作期间，乔治·博克斯经常去伦敦大学学院的统计小组，在那里他结识了戴维·考克斯。考克斯后来成为统计学领域的重大创新者，并担任过卡尔·皮尔逊创办的期刊《生物统计学》（Biometrika）的编辑。两人都对彼此名字的相似之处感到好笑，也对“博克斯和考克斯”这个说法在英国戏剧界用来指代一个演员扮演的两个次要角色感到惊讶。此外，还有一出经典的英国音乐喜剧小品，讲述的是两个名叫博克斯和考克斯的男人在一家旅馆租了一张床，一个白天住，另一个晚上住。

While still at ICI, George Box often visited the statistical group at University College, where he met David Cox. Cox has gone on to become a major innovator in statistics and has been editor of Biometrika, Karl Pearson’s journal. Both men were struck by the humor of their similar names and by the fact that “Box and Cox” was a term used in English theater to describe two minor parts played by one actor. There is also a classic English musical comedy skit involving two men named Box and Cox who rent the same bed in a rooming house, one of them for the day and the other for the night.

乔治·博克斯和戴维·考克斯决定合作撰写一篇论文。然而，他们的统计学兴趣并不相同；随着时间的推移，尽管他们时常尝试完成这篇论文，但他们的兴趣却完全分道扬镳，论文不得不兼顾两种不同的统计分析哲学立场。1964年，这篇论文最终发表在《皇家统计学会杂志》上。这篇论文后来被称为“博克斯和考克斯”，并成为统计方法的重要组成部分。在论文中，他们展示了如何转换测量值，从而提高大多数统计程序的稳健性。这种被称为“博克斯-考克斯变换”的方法已被应用于分析化学物质对活细胞突变效应的毒理学研究、计量经济学分析，甚至农业研究——R.A.费舍尔的方法正是起源于农业研究领域。

George Box and David Cox resolved to write a paper together. However, their statistical interests were not in the same areas; as the years went by and they tried from time to time to finish the paper, their interests completely diverged, and the paper had to accommodate two different philosophical positions about the nature of statistical analysis. In 1964, the paper was finally published in the Journal of the Royal Statistical Society. “Box and Cox,” as the paper is known, has since become an important part of statistical methods. In the paper, they show how to convert measurements in a way that increases the robustness of most statistical procedures. “Box-Cox transformations,” as they are called, have been used in the analysis of toxicological studies of the mutational effects of chemicals on living cells, in econometric analyses, and even in agricultural research, where R. A. Fisher’s methods had originated.

C章24

CHAPTER 24

重塑工业界的人

THE MAN WHO REMADE INDUSTRY

我1980年，美国全国广播公司（NBC）播出了一部名为《日本能做到，我们为什么不能？》的纪录片。当时，美国汽车公司正面临着来自日本的挑战。日本汽车的质量远胜于20世纪70年代的美国汽车，而且价格更低。不仅在汽车领域，从钢铁到电子等其他工业领域，日本企业在质量和成本方面都超越了美国企业。NBC的这部纪录片探讨了这一切是如何发生的。实际上，这部纪录片讲述的是一位名叫W·爱德华兹·戴明的80岁美国统计学家对日本工业产生的巨大影响。

In 1980, the NBC television network aired a documentary entitled “If Japan Can, Why Can’t We?” The American automobile companies were being rocked by challenges from Japan. The quality of the Japanese automobiles was vastly superior to the American cars of the 1970s, and their prices were lower. Not only in automobiles, but in other areas of industry, from steel to electronics, the Japanese had surpassed American enterprise both in quality and cost. The NBC documentary examined how this had happened. The documentary was really a description of the influence one man had on Japanese industry. That man was an 80-year-old American statistician, W. Edwards Deming.

自1939年离开美国农业部以来，戴明一直担任工业顾问，突然间，他炙手可热。在他漫长的工业顾问生涯中，戴明曾多次受邀帮助美国汽车公司进行质量控制。他对于如何改进工业方法有着独到的见解，但这些公司的高层管理人员对质量控制的“技术”细节毫无兴趣。他们满足于聘请专家来负责质量控制——无论那是什么。1947年，道格拉斯·麦克阿瑟将军被任命为盟军最高统帅，负责指挥被征服的日本。他迫使日本接受民主宪法，并召集了“美国模式”方面的顶尖专家来教育日本。他的幕僚找到了W·爱德华兹·戴明，他是统计抽样方法方面的专家。戴明受邀前往日本，向日本人展示“我们在美国是如何做的”。

Suddenly, Deming, who had been working as a consultant to industry since leaving the U.S. Department of Agriculture in 1939, was in great demand. In his long career as a consultant to industry, Deming had been invited many times to help the American automobile companies with quality control. He had developed strong ideas about how to improve industrial methods, but senior management in these companies had no interest in the “technical” details of quality control. They were content to hire experts and have them run quality control—whatever that was. Then, in 1947, General Douglas MacArthur was named the supreme allied commander over conquered Japan. He forced Japan to adopt a democratic constitution and called in the foremost experts on the “American way” to educate the country. His staff located the name of W. Edwards Deming as an expert on statistical sampling methods. Deming was invited to go to Japan to show the Japanese “how we do it in America.”

W·爱德华兹·戴明，1900-1993

W. Edwards Deming, 1900-1993

戴明的工作给日本科学技术联盟（JUSE）会长石川一郎留下了深刻印象，他应邀回到日本，为日本各行各业举办了一系列统计方法研讨会。石川一郎在许多日本公司的高层管理人员中也颇具影响力。在他的邀请下，许多公司高管经常去听戴明的讲座。当时，“日本制造”通常意味着廉价劣质的仿冒品。戴明却出人意料地告诉听众，他们可以在五年内改变这种局面。他指出，只要正确运用统计方法进行质量控制，就能生产出质量高、价格低的产品，从而主导全球市场。戴明在后来的演讲中承认，他之前预测五年就能实现这一目标并不准确。日本人比他预想的提前了近两年。

Deming’s work impressed Ichiro Ishikawa, the president of the Japanese Union of Scientists and Engineers (JUSE), and he was invited back to teach statistical methods in a group of seminars organized for a broad range of Japanese industry. Ishikawa also had the ear of senior management in many Japanese companies. At his invitation, members of management often attended Deming’s lectures. At that time, the phrase “made in Japan” meant cheap, poor-quality imitations of other countries’ products. Deming shocked his listeners by telling them they could change that around within five years. He told them that, with the proper use of statistical methods of quality control, they could produce products of such high quality and low price that they would dominate markets all over the world. Deming noted in later talks that he was wrong in predicting this would take five years. The Japanese beat his prediction by almost two years.

日本人对戴明印象深刻，以至于日本科学技术联盟（JUSE）设立了戴明​​奖，以鼓励日本工业界开发有效的新型质量控制方法。日本政府对运用统计方法改进各项活动的前景感到振奋，文部科学省设立了年度统计日，学生们通过制作统计学演示文稿来角逐奖项。统计方法席卷日本——几乎全部源于戴明研讨会。

The Japanese were so impressed by Deming that JUSE instituted an annual Deming Prize to encourage the development of effective new methods of quality control in Japanese industry. The Japanese government became excited by the prospects of using statistical methods for improvement of all activities, and the education ministry instituted an annual Statistics Day, when students competed for prizes by creating presentations in statistics. Statistical methods swept over Japan—almost all of it derived from the Deming seminars.

戴明致高层管理人员的信息

DEMING’S MESSAGE TO SENIOR MANAGEMENT

1980年NBC的纪录片播出后，戴明受到了美国工业界的欢迎。他举办了一系列研讨会，向美国管理层介绍他的理念。然而，大多数美国公司的高层管理人员并不理解戴明的工作，他们派出的都是那些已经了解质量控制的技术专家。除了极少数例外，高层管理人员很少出席。戴明的演讲主要针对管理层，而且措辞严厉，充满批判。管理层，尤其是高层管理人员，一直未能尽职尽责。为了阐明他的观点，戴明会让研讨会的学员们参与一项生产实验。

After the 1980 NBC documentary, Deming was welcomed by American industry. He set up a series of seminars to present his ideas to American management. Unfortunately, the senior management of most American companies did not understand what Deming had done, and they sent their technical experts, who already knew about quality control, to his seminars. With very few exceptions, members of the higher levels of management seldom attended. Deming’s message was a message to management primarily. It was a nasty, critical message. Management, especially senior management, had been failing to do its job. To illustrate his message, Deming would have the students of his seminar engage in a manufacturing experiment.

学生们将被分成工厂工人、检验员和经理三个小组。工厂工人将接受一项简单的操作流程培训。他们会拿到一个装满白色珠子的大桶，其中混杂着少量红色珠子。培训内容是教他们如何通过反复翻转桶来充分搅拌珠子。培训强调，这项工作的关键在于充分搅拌。之后，他们会拿起一个带有50个小孔的桨，每个小孔刚好能容纳一颗珠子。工人们将桨在桶中来回拨动，每次拨出50颗珠子。他们被告知，市场部已经确定，顾客不能容忍这50颗珠子中超过3颗是红色的，他们的目标是达到这个标准。每位工人用桨拨出满满的珠子后，检验员会清点并记录红色珠子的数量。经理会查看记录，表扬那些红色珠子数量少或接近上限3颗的工人，并批评那些红色珠子数量多的工人。事实上，经理经常会让那些工作能力差的工人停下手中的工作，观察那些工作能力强的工人是如何做的，以便学习如何正确地完成任务。

The students would be divided into factory workers, inspectors, and managers. The factory workers would be trained in a simple procedure. They would be given a large drum of white beads, with a few red beads mixed in. The training would teach them to mix the drum vigorously by turning it over and over. The training emphasized that the crucial aspect of the job was this mixing. They would then take a paddle with 50 small pits, each big enough to hold one bead. Passing the paddle through the drum, the workers would bring up exactly 50 beads. They were told that marketing had determined that the customers would not tolerate having any more than 3 red beads in the 50, and they were to strive toward that goal. As each worker emerged with a paddle full of beads, the inspector would count the number of red beads and record it. The manager would examine the records and praise those workers whose red-bead counts were small or close to the maximum of 3, and would criticize those workers whose red-bead counts were large. In fact, the manager would often tell the poor workers to stop what they were doing and watch the good workers to see how they were doing it, in order to learn how to do the task correctly.

鼓里五分之一是红色的珠子。拿到3颗或更少红珠的概率不到1%，但拿到6颗或更少红珠的概率约为10%。因此，工人们常常能非常接近不超过3颗红珠的理想目标。平均而言，他们会拿到10颗红珠，这对于管理层来说是无法接受的；而纯粹出于运气，有些工人会拿到13到15颗红珠，这显然是工作表现极差的结果。

One-fifth of the beads in the drum were red. The chance of getting 3 or fewer red beads was less than 1 percent. But the chance of getting 6 or fewer was about 10 percent, so the workers would often get tantalizingly close to the magic goal of no more than 3 red beads. On the average, they would get 10 red beads, which was unacceptable to management; and purely by chance some workers would get 13 to 15 red beads, clearly the result of very poor work.

戴明指出，管理层常常设定不可能达到的标准，却从不去探究这些标准是否能够实现，或者需要对设备进行哪些改进才能达到这些标准。他声称，美国高层管理人员仅仅依靠质量控制专家来维持标准，却忽视了工厂工人可能遇到的种种不满。他对席卷美国工业的各种管理潮流进行了尖锐的批判。20世纪70年代，一种名为“零缺陷”的潮流盛行，他们追求产品零缺陷——戴明深知这完全不可能。到了20世纪80年代（正值戴明对美国工业产生深远影响之际），一种名为“全面质量管理”（TQM）的潮流再次出现。戴明认为这一切都不过是管理层的空洞言辞和空洞口号，他们真正应该做的是做好自己的本职工作。

Deming’s point was that, all too often, management sets impossible standards and makes no attempt to determine if the standards can be met or what has to be done to change the equipment to meet those standards. Instead, he claimed, American senior management relied on experts in quality control to keep to the standards, ignoring the frustrations the factory workers might have. He was scathingly critical of the fads of management that would sweep through American industry. In the 1970s, the fad was called “zero defect.” They would have no defects in their product—a condition that Deming knew was completely impossible. In the 1980s (just as Deming was making an imprint on American industry), the fad was called “total quality management,” or TQM. Deming viewed all of this as nothing more than empty words and exhortations from management, who should be doing their real jobs instead.

在《走出危机》一书中，戴明引用了他写给一家公司管理层的一份报告：

In his book Out of the Crisis, Deming quoted from a report he wrote to the management of one company:

这份报告是应您的要求撰写的，起因是我对您目前面临的低产量、高成本和质量不稳定等问题进行了研究……。我的首要观点是，除非高层管理人员履行其职责，否则质量改进永远无法取得持久成效……。在我看来，贵公司管理层未能承担并履行其质量责任，是造成您目前困境的根本原因……。贵公司目前的情况……并非质量控制，而是各自为政的游击战——没有系统化的体系，既没有质量控制机制，也没有对质量控制体系的重视。您就像一个只能寄希望于及时赶到阻止火势蔓延的消防队……

你们到处张贴着一句口号，敦促每个人做到完美，别无其他。我真想知道，谁能做到？难道每个人都要把自己的工作做得更好吗？如果一个人根本不知道自己的工作是什么，也不知道如何做得更好，又怎么可能做到呢？如果一个人还要面对劣质材料、供应变化、机器故障等种种问题，又怎么可能做到呢？……另一个障碍是管理层认为生产工人应该为所有问题负责：只要生产工人按照他们认为正确的方式工作，生产就不会出现问题……。以我的经验来看，大多数生产问题都源于一些共同的原因，而这些原因只有管理层才能减少或消除。

This report is written at your request after study of some of the problems you are having with low production, high costs, and variable quality … . My opening point is that no permanent impact has ever been accomplished in improvement of quality unless the top management carry out their responsibilities … . Failure of your own management to accept and act on their responsibilities for quality is, in my opinion, the prime cause of your trouble … . What you have in your company … is not quality control but guerrilla sniping—no organized system, no provision nor appreciation for control of quality as a system. You have been running a fire department that hopes to arrive in time to keep fires from spreading … .

You have a slogan, posted everywhere, urging everyone to do perfect work, nothing else. I wonder how anyone could live up to it. By every man doing his job better? How can he, when he has no way to know what his job is, nor how to do it better? How can he, when he is handicapped with defective materials, change of supply, machines out of order? … Another roadblock is management’s supposition that the production workers are responsible for all the trouble: that there would be no problems in production if only the production workers would do their jobs in the way that they know to be right … . [I]n my experience, most problems in production have their origin in common causes, which only management can reduce or remove.

戴明关于质量控制的主要观点是，生产线的产出是可变的。它必然是可变的，因为这是所有人类活动的本质。戴明坚持认为，客户想要的不是完美的产品，而是可靠的产品。客户想要的是低变异性的产品，这样他们才能知道对产品的预期。费舍尔的方差分析法使分析师能够将产品的变异性分解为两个来源。戴明将其中一个来源称为“特殊原因”，另一个来源称为“普通原因”或“环境原因”。他指出，美国工业的标准做法是设定允许的总变异性上限。如果变异性超过这些上限，生产线就会停工，并寻找造成变异性的特殊原因。但是，戴明坚持认为，特殊原因很少，而且很容易识别。环境原因则始终存在，并且是管理不善的结果，因为它们通常表现为机器维护不善、用于生产的原材料质量不稳定，或其他不受控制的工作条件。

Deming’s major point about quality control is that the output of a production line is variable. It has to be variable, because that is the nature of all human activity. What the customer wants, Deming insisted, is not a perfect product but a reliable product. The customer wants a product with low variability so he or she can know what to expect from it. R. A. Fisher’s analysis of variance allows the analyst to separate the variability of the product into two sources. One source Deming called “special causes.” The other he called “common,” or “environmental,” causes. He claimed that the standard procedure in American industry was to put limits on the total variability allowed. If the variability exceeded those limits, the production line was shut down, and they searched for the special cause of this. But, Deming insisted, the special causes are few and can be easily identified. The environmental causes are always there and are the result of poor management, since they are often in the form of poorly maintained machines, or variable quality in the stock used for manufacturing, or other uncontrolled working conditions.

戴明提出，生产线应被视为一系列活动，从原材料开始，到成品结束。每项活动都可以衡量，因此每项活动都存在因环境因素造成的变异性。管理者不应等到最终产品的变异性超出预设的限值才采取行动，而应关注每项活动的变异性。变异性最大的活动应优先处理。一旦该活动的变异性降低，就会出现另一个“变异性最大”的活动，此时应着手解决。如此一来，质量控制便成为一个持续的过程，生产线上变异性最大的环节始终处于优化改进的状态。

Deming proposed that the production line be seen as a stream of activities that start with raw material and end with finished product. Each activity can be measured, so each activity has its own variability due to environmental causes. Instead of waiting for the final product to exceed arbitrary limits of variability, the managers should be looking at the variability of each of these activities. The most variable of the activities is the one that should be addressed. Once that variability is reduced, there will be another activity that is “most variable,” and it should then be addressed. Thus, quality control becomes a continuous process, where the most variable aspect of the production line is constantly being worked on.

戴明的方法最终带来了这样的结果：日本汽车行驶 10 万英里以上无需大修，船舶只需极少的维护，钢材批次间的质量始终如一，以及其他质量波动得到控制的行业成果。

The end result of Deming’s approach was Japanese cars that ran for 100,000 miles or more without major repairs, ships that needed minimum maintenance, steel that was of consistent quality from batch to batch, and other results of an industry where the variability of quality was under control.

质量控制的本质

THE NATURE OF QUALITY CONTROL

20世纪20年代和30年代，贝尔电话实验室的沃尔特·休哈特和国家标准局的弗兰克·尤登在美国组织了首批统计质量控制项目，将统计革命引入了工业领域。戴明则将统计革命带入了高层管理者的办公室。在他为数学知识有限的管理者撰写的《走出危机》一书中，他指出许多概念过于模糊，无法应用于制造业。例如，汽车活塞应该是圆的；但如果没有办法测量特定活塞的圆度，这句话就毫无意义。为了提高产品质量，必须对产品质量进行测量。要测量产品的某个属性，就需要对该属性（在本例中为圆度）进行明确定义。由于所有测量本质上都是可变的，因此制造过程需要关注这些测量分布的参数。正如卡尔·皮尔逊试图在参数变化中寻找进化的证据一样，戴明坚持认为管理层有责任监控这些测量分布的参数，并改变制造过程的基本方面，以改进这些参数。

Walter Shewhart at Bell Telephone Laboratories and Frank Youden at the National Bureau of Standards had brought the statistical revolution into industry by organizing the first programs in statistical quality control in the United States during the 1920s and 1930s. Deming moved the statistical revolution into the offices of upper management. In Out of the Crisis, written for managers with minimal mathematical knowledge, he pointed out that many ideas are too vague to use in manufacturing. An automobile piston should be round; but this phrase means nothing unless there is a way to measure the roundness of a particular piston. To improve the quality of a product, the product’s quality has to be measured. To measure a property of a product requires that the property (roundness in this case) be well defined. Because all measurements are, by nature, variable, the manufacturing process needs to address the parameters of the distributions of those measurements. Just as Karl Pearson sought to find evidence of evolution in changes in the parameters, Deming insisted that management had the responsibility of monitoring the parameters of these measurement distributions and changing fundamental aspects of the manufacturing process in order to improve those parameters.

我第一次见到埃德·德明是在20世纪70年代的统计学会议上。他身材高大，表情严肃，尤其是在提出批评意见时，在统计学界是一位令人敬畏的人物。我很少看到他在演讲后的问答环节站起来批评别人，但他经常会在会后把人拉到一边，批评他们没能看清在他看来显而易见的事实。我遇到的这位表情严肃的批评家，与他的朋友们所认识的德明截然不同。我看到的是他为人友善、体贴周到的一面。他以对同事的友善和关怀、幽默风趣（尽管非常含蓄）以及对音乐的热爱而闻名。他曾在合唱团唱歌，演奏鼓和长笛，并出版了几首原创的宗教音乐作品。其中一首作品是新版的《星条旗永不落》——他声称这个版本比通常的版本更容易演唱。

I first met Ed Deming at statistical meetings in the 1970s. Tall and stern-looking when he had something critical to say, Deming was a formidable figure among statisticians. Seldom did I see him rise up during the questions after a talk to criticize, but he would often take a person aside after the session to criticize that person for failing to see what, to Deming, was obvious. This stern-faced critic whom I met was not the Deming that his friends knew. I saw his public persona. He was known for his kindness to and consideration for those he worked with, for his robust, if very subtle, humor, and for his interest in music. He sang in a choir, played drums and flute, and published several original pieces of sacred music. One of his musical publications was a new version of the “Star-Spangled Banner”—a version that he claimed was more singable than the usual one.

他于1900年出生于爱荷华州苏城，就读于怀俄明大学，主修数学，并对工程学有着浓厚的兴趣。之后，他在科罗拉多大学获得数学和物理学硕士学位。在科罗拉多大学期间，他结识了妻子艾格尼丝·贝尔。1927年，他们搬到康涅狄格州，他在耶鲁大学开始攻读物理学博士学位。

He was born in Sioux City, Iowa, in 1900, and attended the University of Wyoming, where he studied mathematics with a strong interest in engineering. He received a master’s degree in mathematics and physics from the University of Colorado. He met his wife, Agnes Belle, at the university. They moved to Connecticut in 1927, where he began studying for a doctorate in physics at Yale University.

戴明的第一份工业工作是在伊利诺伊州西塞罗的西电公司霍桑工厂[37]，他在耶鲁大学就读期间的暑假都在那里工作。沃尔特·休哈特当时正在新泽西州的贝尔实验室为统计质量控制方法奠定基础。西电公司隶属于同一家公司（AT&T），霍桑工厂也曾尝试应用休哈特的方法，但戴明意识到他们并没有理解休哈特的理念。质量控制逐渐沦为一套基于设定允许变异范围的机械操作。这些范围通常设定为：缺​​陷产品通过质量控制的概率不超过5%。戴明后来驳斥了这种质量控制方式，认为它等于保证了5%的客户会不满意。

Deming’s first industrial job was at the Hawthorne[37] plant of Western Electric in Cicero, Illinois, where he worked during the summers he was at Yale. Walter Shewhart had been laying the groundwork for statistical quality control methods at Bell Labs in New Jersey. Western Electric was part of the same company (AT&T), and attempts were being made to apply Shewhart’s methods at the Hawthorne plant, but Deming realized that they had failed to understand Shewhart’s message. Quality control was becoming a set of rote manipulations based on setting ranges of variability that were allowable. The ranges were often set so that 5 percent or less of the time a defective product would get through quality control. Deming was later to dismiss this version of quality control as a guarantee that 5 percent of the customers would be dissatisfied.

从耶鲁大学毕业后，戴明于1927年进入美国农业部工作，并在接下来的十二年里从事抽样技术和实验设计方面的研究。之后他离开政府部门，创办了自己的咨询公司，并开始举办关于工业质量控制应用的研讨会。二战期间，他的研讨会规模扩大，培训了约2000名设计师和工程师。这些学员后来在各自的公司举办研讨会，到二战结束时，经戴明培训的质量控制专家人数已接近3万人。

With his degree from Yale, Deming went to the U.S. Department of Agriculture in 1927, where he worked on sampling techniques and experimental design for the next twelve years. He left government to set up his own consulting company and began running seminars on the use of quality control in industry. These were expanded during World War II, when he trained about 2,000 designers and engineers. These students went on to give seminars at their own companies, until the progeny of Deming-trained quality control specialists reached almost 30,000 by the end of the war.

最后一次戴明研讨会于1993年12月10日在加利福尼亚州举行。93岁高龄的W·爱德华兹·戴明亲自参与，但研讨会的大部分内容由他的年轻助手主持。12月20日，他在华盛顿特区的家中去世。W·爱德华兹·戴明研究所由他的家人和朋友于1993年11月创立，“旨在促进人们对戴明深奥知识体系的理解，从而推动商业、繁荣与和平”。

The last Deming seminar took place on December 10, 1993, in California. Ninety-three-year-old W. Edwards Deming participated, although much of the seminar was run by his younger assistants. On December 20, he died in his home in Washington, D.C. The W. Edwards Deming Institute was founded by his family and friends in November 1993, “to foster understanding of the Deming System of Profound Knowledge to advance commerce, prosperity, and peace.”

戴明论假设检验

DEMING ON HYPOTHESIS TESTING

在第十一章中，我们了解了内曼和皮尔逊如何发展出假设检验，以及它如何主导了现代统计分析的大部分领域。戴明对假设检验持强烈的批判态度。他嘲讽其广泛应用，因为他认为假设检验关注的是错误的问题。正如他所指出的：“实践中的问题从来不是两种处理方法A和B之间的差异是否显著。即使存在差异……无论差异多么微小……人们总能找到……一定次数的实验重复……来得出显著结果。” 因此，在戴明看来，发现显著差异本身毫无意义。重要的是差异的程度。此外，戴明指出，在一种实验情境下发现的差异程度可能与在另一种情境下发现的差异程度不同。在戴明看来，标准的统计方法本身并不能解决问题。统计方法的这些局限性至关重要。正如戴明所说，“统计学家需要对问题感兴趣，学习并教授统计推断及其局限性。我们越了解从一组结果中进行推断的局限性，这种推断就越有用。”

In chapter 11, we saw the development of hypothesis testing by Neyman and Pearson, and how it came to dominate much of modern statistical analysis. Deming was highly critical of hypothesis testing. He ridiculed its widespread use because, he claimed, it focused on the wrong questions. As he pointed out: “The question in practice is never whether a difference between two treatments A and B is significant. Given a difference … however small between [them] … one can find a … number of repetitions of the experiment … that will [produce significance].” Thus, to Deming, a finding of a significant difference means nothing. It is the degree of difference found that is important. Furthermore, Deming pointed out, the degree of difference found in one experimental situation may not be the same as that found in another setting. To Deming, the standard methods of statistics could not be used, by themselves, to solve problems. These limitations of statistical methods are important. As Deming put it, “Statisticians need to become interested in problems and to learn and teach statistical inference and the limitations thereof. The better we understand the limitations of an inference … from a set of results, the more useful becomes the inference.”

本书最后一章，我们将探讨戴明所警告的统计推断的这些局限性。

In the final chapter of this book, we shall look at these limitations of statistical inference that Deming warned about.

C第25章

CHAPTER 25

黑衣女士的建议

ADVICE FROM THE LADY IN BLACK

一个尽管在二十世纪初，男性理论家主导了统计方法的发展，但到我20世纪60年代入行时，许多女性已经身居要职。这种情况在工业界和政府部门尤为突出。朱迪思·戈德堡（Judith Goldberg）曾任美国氰胺公司（American Cyanamid）的统计部门主管，宝拉·诺伍德（Paula Norwood）曾任强生制药公司（Johnson Pharmaceuticals）的统计部门主管。梅维斯·卡罗尔（Mavis Carroll）曾负责通用食品公司（General Foods）的数学和统计服务部门。在华盛顿特区，女性曾领导过人口普查局、劳工统计局和国家卫生统计中心等机构。英国和欧洲大陆的情况也类似。在第19章中，我们探讨了其中一些女性在统计方法发展中所扮演的角色。

Although male theoreticians dominated the development of statistical methods in the early years of the twentieth century, by the time I joined the profession in the 1960s, there were many women in prominent places. This was particularly true in industry and government. Judith Goldberg at American Cyanamid and Paula Norwood at Johnson Pharmaceuticals have headed up statistics departments at pharmaceutical companies. Mavis Carroll was in charge of the mathematical and statistical services division of General Foods. In Washington, D.C., women have been in charge of the Census Bureau, the Bureau of Labor Statistics, and the National Center for Health Statistics, among others. This has also been true in the United Kingdom and continental Europe. In chapter 19, we looked at the roles some ot these women have played in the development of statistical methodology.

在统计学领域取得卓越成就的女性的经历并非千篇一律。她们都是杰出的个体，各自的发展历程和成就都独一无二。正如我们无法在统计学领域找到一位典型的男性代表一样，也无法在统计学领域找到一位典型的女性代表。然而，研究一位在工业界和政府部门都取得杰出成就的女性的职业生涯却颇为有趣。英国的斯特拉·坎利夫是首位担任英国皇家统计学会主席的女性。本章的大部分内容摘自她于1975年11月12日向学会发表的年度主席致辞。

There is nothing typical about the experiences of women who have made a name for themselves in statistics. All of them are remarkable individuals whose individual developments and accomplishments are unique. One cannot pick a representative woman in statistics, any more than one can find a representative man in statistics. It would be interesting, however, to examine the career of one woman, who rose to prominence in both industry and government. Stella Cunliffe of Great Britain was the first woman to be named president of the Royal Statistical Society. Much of this chapter is taken from the annual address of the president, which she presented before the society on November 12, 1975.

凡是认识或与斯特拉·坎利夫共事过的人，都称赞她幽默风趣、思维敏锐，并且能够将最复杂的数学模型简化成合作科学家能够理解的语言。这些特质在她发表的演讲中体现得淋漓尽致。这篇演讲呼吁皇家统计学会的成员们减少在发展抽象理论上花费的时间，更多地与其他领域的科学家合作。例如，她写道：“作为统计学家，如果我们不准备引导社会学家进行更科学的思考，那么仅仅对许多社会学家草率的研究方法嗤之以鼻是毫无意义的。要做到这一点，我们必须进行互动。”她经常举例说明在实验过程中发生的意外情况。“即使在组织良好的研究站，大麦试验也可能因为某个愚蠢的拖拉机司机抄近路穿过试验田，匆匆赶回家喝茶而彻底失败。”

Those who have known or worked with Stella Cunliffe attest to her broad good humor, her keen common sense, and her ability to reduce the most complicated mathematical models to understandable terms for the scientists with whom she collaborated. Much of this comes out in her address. The address is a plea to the members of the Royal Statistical Society to spend less time developing abstract theory and more time collaborating with scientists from other fields. For example, she wrote: “It is no use, as statisticians, our being sniffy about the slapdash methods of many sociologists unless we are prepared to try to guide them into more scientifically acceptable thought. To do this there must be interaction between us.” She makes frequent use of examples where the unexpected has occurred in the process of running an experiment. “Barley trials, even on a well-organized research station, could be knocked for six by some fool of a tractor driver hurrying home to his tea via a short cut across the plot.”

斯特拉·坎利夫于20世纪30年代末在伦敦政治经济学院学习统计学。那是一个令人兴奋的时代。许多学生和一些教职员工都志愿参加了西班牙内战，对抗法西斯。一些逃离纳粹德国的著名经济学家、数学家和其他科学家被安排到学校担任临时职务。当她从学校毕业时，全世界仍在遭受大萧条的蹂躏。她唯一能找到的工作是在丹麦培根公司，“在那里，数理统计的应用微乎其微，而我，作为一名统计学家，尤其是一名女性统计学家，被视为非常另类的存在。”随着第二次世界大战的爆发，坎利夫参与了粮食分配问题的研究，她的数学技能在其中发挥了重要作用。

Stella Cunliffe studied statistics at the London School of Economics in the late 1930s. It was an exciting time to be there. Many of the students and some of the faculty had volunteered to serve in the Spanish Civil War against the fascists. Prominent economists, mathematicians, and other scientists who had escaped Nazi Germany were given temporary positions at the school. When she emerged from school with her degree, the entire world was still suffering from the Great Depression. The only job she could find was with the Danish Bacon Company, “where the use of mathematical statistics was minimal and I, as a statistician, in particular a female statistician, was looked upon as something very odd.” With the coming of World War II, Cunliffe became involved in food allocation problems, where her mathematical skills proved very useful.

战后两年，她志愿前往饱受战火蹂躏的欧洲参与救援工作。她是最早抵达荷兰鹿特丹的救援人员之一，当时德军仍在投降，当地平民百姓正遭受饥荒。贝尔根-贝尔森集中营解放后不久，她又前往那里帮助集中营的受害者。最后，她在英国占领区的难民营完成了救援工作。坎利夫结束志愿工作回到家乡时身无分文，但她得到了两份工作。一份是在食品部，负责“油脂”部门的工作。另一份是在吉尼斯啤酒公司，她接受了这份工作。值得一提的是，威廉·西利·戈塞特（笔名“学生”）在吉尼斯公司创立了统计部门。斯特拉·坎利夫在戈塞特去世约十年后才加入吉尼斯，但他的影响力在吉尼斯仍然非常强大，他的声誉备受尊崇，他创立的实验方法也主导了公司的科学研究。

For two years after the war, she volunteered to help the relief work in devastated Europe. She was one of the first to arrive in Rotterdam, in the Netherlands, while the German army was still surrendering and where the civilian population was starving. She moved on to help with the concentration camp victims at Bergen-Belsen soon after liberation. She finished her efforts working in the displaced-persons camps in the British zone of occupation. Cunliffe returned from her voluntary work penniless and was offered two jobs. One of them was at the Ministry of Food, where she would work in the “oils and fats” department. The other job was at the Guinness Brewing Company, which she accepted. Recall that William Sealy Gosset, who published under the pseudonym of “Student,” had founded the statistical department at Guinness. Stella Cunliffe arrived there about ten years after Gosset’s death, but his influence was still very strong at Guinness, where his reputation was revered and the experimental discipline he had created dominated its scientific work.

吉尼斯统计数据

STATISTICS AT GUINNESS

吉尼斯啤酒的员工们对自己的产品以及不断进行的改进实验充满信心。

The Guinness workers believed in their product and the experimentation that was used constantly to improve it. They

他们从未停止试验，力求以尽可能经济的方式，使用不同的原材料（这些原材料会因天气、土壤、啤酒花和大麦品种的不同而有所变化）生产出品质始终如一的产品。他们对自己的产品充满自信，而且，或许有人知道，或许没人知道，直到1929年，他们才开始做广告。这是因为当时人们普遍认为——在我离开时这种观念依然根深蒂固——吉尼斯是最好的啤酒，不需要广告，它的品质本身就能卖出去，而那些不喝吉尼斯的人，应该得到同情，而不是被广告吸引！坎利夫描述了她在吉尼斯的最初几天：

抵达都柏林啤酒厂接受“培训”时，我像在德国时一样，过着自由自在、精彩纷呈的生活。一天早上，我出现在都柏林啤酒厂“女员工”主管面前。她是一位面容严肃的女士，一身黑衣，领口处系着几片蕾丝，用鲸骨固定着……她让我明白，能被选中为吉尼斯工作是多么荣幸，并提醒我必须穿长袜戴帽子。如果我有幸在走廊里遇到一位被称为“酿酒师”的精英，千万不能认出他，必须低下头，直到他走过为止。

never stopped experimenting to try to produce the product as a constant one, from varying raw materials, varying because of weather, soil, varieties of hops and barley, as economically as possible. They were arrogant about their product and, as may or may not be known, there was no advertising until 1929 because of the attitude—still endemic when I left—that Guinness is the best beer available, it does not need advertising as its quality will sell it, and those who do not drink it are to be sympathized with rather than advertised to! Cunliffe described her first days at Guinness:

On arrival at the Dublin Brewery for “training,” having led, as I had in Germany, a free and in many ways exciting life, I appeared one morning before the supervisor of the “Ladies Staff” at the Dublin Brewery. She was a forbidding-looking lady, all in black, with small pieces of lace at her throat, held up by whalebones … . She impressed on me what a privilege it was to have been chosen to work for Guinness, and reminded me that I was expected to wear stockings and a hat and, if I was lucky enough to meet one of the chosen race known as “brewers” in the corridor, on no account was I to recognize him, but should lower my eyes until he had passed.

这就是 1946 年吉尼斯酿酒公司等级森严的世界中女性的地位。

Thus the position of women in the hierarchal world of the Guinness Brewing Company in 1946.

坎利夫很快在吉尼斯公司证明了自己的价值，并深入参与了爱尔兰的农业实验。她不满足于坐在办公桌前分析田野科学家发来的数据，而是亲自到田间地头去观察实际情况。（任何新晋统计学家都应该效仿她的做法。令人惊讶的是，实验室工作人员之上几级的人对实验的描述，往往与实际发生的情况大相径庭。）

Cunliffe soon proved her worth at Guinness and became deeply involved in the agricultural experiments in Ireland. She was not content to stay at her desk and analyze the data sent to her by the field scientists. She went out to the field, to see for herself what was happening. (Any new statistician would do well to follow her example. It is amazing how often the description of an experiment as relayed by somebody several layers above the laboratory workers does not agree with what has actually happened.)

许多个阴冷潮湿的清晨，早上七点，我都会在啤酒花田里瑟瑟发抖、饥肠辘辘地参与到一项至关重要的实验中。我特意用了“至关重要”这个词，因为除非统计学家认可这项实验的重要性，从而与实验者的热情产生共鸣，否则我认为他对这项工作的贡献就达不到最佳水平。作为统计学家，我们面临的主要问题之一就是必须灵活应变：我们必须随时准备转换工作，一会儿协助微生物学家培育新的酵母菌株；一会儿帮助农学家评估特定牛饲料对粪便产量的影响；一会儿与病毒学家探讨新城疫抗体的制备；一会儿帮助医务人员评估麦芽仓库中灰尘对健康的影响；一会儿为工程师提供关于传送带上批量生产产品的实验建议；一会儿将排队论应用于食堂；一会儿又帮助社会学家检验他关于群体行为的理论。

Many is the damp and chill morning that found me at 7 A.M., shivering and hungry in a hop garden, actually taking part in some vital experiment. I use the word “vital” deliberately because unless the experiment is accepted as vital by the statistician, so that the enthusiasm of the experimenter is shared by the statistician, I submit that his contribution to the work is less than optimum. One of the main problems as statisticians is that we have to be flexible: We have to be prepared to switch from helping a microbiologist in the production of a new strain of yeast; to helping an agriculturalist to assess the dung-producing qualities resulting from the intake of particular cattle feeds; to discussing with a virologist the production of antibodies to Newcastle disease; to helping a medical officer assess the effects on health of dust in malt stores; to advising an engineer about his experiments involving a mass-produced article moving along a conveyor belt; to applying queuing theory to the canteen; or to helping a sociologist test his theories about group behavior.

这份合作类型清单体现了统计学家在工业界工作的典型特征。就我个人而言，我曾与化学家、药理学家、毒理学家、经济学家、临床医生以及管理人员（我们为他们开发了用于决策的运筹学模型）进行过合作。这正是统计学家工作充满魅力的原因之一。数理统计方法应用广泛，而统计学家作为数学建模专家，几乎可以在所有领域开展合作。

This list of types of collaboration is typical of the work of a statistician in industry. In my own experience, I have had interactions with chemists, pharmacologists, toxicologists, economists, clinicians, and management (for whom we developed operations research models for decision making). This is one of the things that make the work day of a statistician fascinating. The methods of mathematical statistics are ubiquitous, and the statistician, as the expert in mathematical modeling, is able to collaborate in almost every area of activity.

意外变异性

UNEXPECTED VARIABILITY

在她的演讲中，斯特拉·坎利夫思考了最大的变异来源——智人：

In her address, Stella Cunliffe muses on that greatest source of variability—Homo sapiens:

能够负责吉尼斯黑啤研发过程中大量的品鉴和饮用实验，我感到非常荣幸。正是在这段时间里，我开始意识到，要找到一个没有偏见、没有成见、没有那些令人着迷的怪癖的人是多么不可能……我们都对某些数字、字母或颜色有偏好，而且我们都很迷信。我们都会做出一些非理性的行为。我至今仍清晰地记得一项耗资巨大的实验，目的是找出啤酒的最佳饮用温度。实验中，受试者被安排在不同温度的房间里，饮用不同温度的啤酒。穿着白大褂的小个子工作人员提着盛有不同温度水的啤酒桶在楼上楼下奔跑，温度计随处可见，现场一片忙碌景象。啤酒通过彩色瓶盖进行区分，而这项实验唯一明确的结果是……我们的饮酒小组表示，他们唯一关心的是瓶盖的颜色，而且他们不喜欢黄色瓶盖的啤酒。

It was my delight to be responsible for much of the tasting and drinking experiments that are an obvious part of the development of that delicious liquid—Guinness. It was in connection with this that I began to realize how impossible it is to find human beings without biases, without prejudices, and without the delightful idiosyncrasies which make them so fascinating … . We all have prejudices about certain numbers, letters, or colours, and all of us are very superstitious. We all behave irrationally. I well remember an expensive experiment set up to discover the temperature at which beer was preferred. This involved subjects sitting in rooms at various temperatures, drinking beers at various other temperatures. Little men in white coats ran up and downstairs with beer in buckets of water at varying temperatures, thermometers abounded and an air of bustle prevailed. The beers were identified by coloured crown seals, and the only clear-cut result of this experiment … was that our drinking panel showed that the only thing that mattered to them was the colour of the crown seals and that they did not like beer with yellow crown seals.

她描述了一项对小型啤酒桶容量的分析。这些啤酒桶是手工制作的，其容量经过测量以确定是否符合标准。负责测量的女工需要先称量空桶，然后装满水，最后再称量装满水后的重量。如果啤酒桶的实际容量与标准容量相差超过三品脱或七品脱以上，则需要退回进行修改。作为持续质量控制流程的一部分，统计人员会记录啤酒桶的装酒量以及哪些啤酒桶被淘汰。在查看装酒量图表时，坎利夫发现，有数量异常多的啤酒桶勉强达到标准容量，而数量异常少的啤酒桶则刚好超出标准容量。他们还调查了负责称量啤酒桶的女工的工作条件。她需要将淘汰的啤酒桶扔到高高的桶堆上，并将合格的啤酒桶放到传送带上。在康利夫的建议下，她的称重台被设置在了废弃酒桶的上方。这样，她只需把不合格的酒桶踢进桶里就行了。那些勉强过关的多余酒桶就这样消失了。

She describes an analysis of capacity of small beer casks. The casks were handmade and their capacity was measured to determine if they were of appropriate size. The woman who measured them had to weigh the empty cask, fill it with water, and weigh the full cask. If the cask differed from its proper size by being more than three pints below or more than seven pints above, it was returned for modification. As part of the ongoing process of quality control, the statisticians kept track of the filling size of the casks and which ones were discarded. On examining the graph of filling sizes, Cunliffe realized that there was an unusually high number of casks that were just barely making it, and an unusually low number that were just outside the limits. They examined the working conditions of the woman who weighed the casks. She was required to throw a discarded cask onto a high pile and place an accepted cask onto a conveyor belt. At Cunliffe’s suggestion, her weighing position was put on top of the bin for the discarded casks. Then all she had to do was kick the rejected cask down into the bin. The excess of casks just barely making it disappeared.

斯特拉·坎利夫一路晋升，最终成为吉尼斯统计部门的负责人。1970年，她被英国政府内政部的研究部门挖走，该部门负责监管警察部队、刑事法庭和监狱。

Stella Cunliffe rose to head the statistical department at Guinness. In 1970, she was hired away by the Research Unit of the British Home Office, which supervises police forces, criminal courts, and prisons.

我加入这个部门时，它主要关注犯罪学问题。我一下子从吉尼斯世界纪录中那些精确、精心设计、可全面分析的工作，一头扎进了社会学（如果我敢说的话，有时甚至是心理学）那虚无缥缈的世界……我绝非贬低内政部研究部门研究人员的能力……然而，令我震惊的是，我长期以来奉行的那些原则——例如设定零假设、精心设计实验、充分抽样、严谨的统计分析以及对结果进行详细评估——在社会学领域似乎远没有那么严格地应用或被接受。

This unit, when I joined it, was concerned mainly with criminological problems and I plunged … straight from the rather precise, carefully designed, thoroughly analyzable work that I had been doing at Guinness into what I can only describe as the airy-fairy world of the sociologist and, if I dare say it, sometimes of the psychologist … . I am in no way decrying the ability of the researchers in the Home Office Research Unit … . However, it came as a shock to me that those principles of setting up a null hypothesis, of careful experimental design, of adequate sampling, of meticulous statistical analysis, and of detailed assessment of results, with which I had worked for so long, appeared to be much less rigorously applied or accepted in sociological fields.

犯罪学领域的大量“研究”是通过长期积累数据并分析其对公共政策可能产生的影响来进行的。其中一项分析比较了成年男性囚犯的刑期长短与出狱两年内再次犯罪的比例。结果清楚地表明，刑期较短的囚犯再犯率要高得多。这被认为是长期监禁能够将“惯犯”绳之以法的证据。

A great deal of the “research” in criminology was run by accumulating data over time and examining it for the possible effects of public policy. One of these analyses had compared the length of sentence given to adult male prisoners versus the percentage of those men who were reconvicted within two years of discharge. The results clearly showed that the prisoners with short sentences had a much higher rate of recidivism. This was taken as proof that long sentences took “habitual” criminals off the streets.

坎利夫并不满足于简单的累犯率与刑期长短的表格。她想仔细研究表格背后的原始数据。表面上的强相关性很大程度上是由于刑期三个月或以下的囚犯累犯率很高。但仔细分析后发现，几乎所有这些囚犯都是“许多年老、可怜、悲伤和精神失常的人，他们最终被关进监狱，因为精神病院不愿接收他们。他们就像一个循环往复的队伍。”事实上，由于他们频繁入狱，同样的人反复出现，但在编制表格时却被算作不同的囚犯。长刑对累犯率的显著影响则体现在表格的另一端，即刑期十年或以上的囚犯，他们的累犯率低于15%。她写道：“这里面也存在很大的年龄因素，很大的环境因素，以及很大的犯罪情节因素。重大诈骗和伪造案件往往会判处长刑——但犯过重大诈骗罪的人很少会再次犯罪。” 因此，在她调整了表格以消除这两个极端异常情况后，刑期长短与再犯率之间的明显关联就消失了。

Cunliffe was not satisfied with a simple table of rates of recidivism versus sentence length. She wanted to look carefully at the raw data behind that table. The strong apparent relationship was due, in large part, to the high recidivism rate among prisoners given sentences of three months or less. But, upon careful examination, almost all these prisoners were “the many old, pathetic, sad and mad people [who] end up in our prisons because the mental hospitals will not take them. They represent a brigade that goes round and round.” In fact, because of their frequent incarceration, the same people kept showing up again and again but were being counted as different prisoners when the table was constructed. The rest of the apparent effect of long sentences on recidivism occurred at the other end of the table, where prisoners with sentences of ten years or more had less than a 15 percent rate. “There is a big age factor in this too,” she wrote, “a big environmental factor and a big offense one. Large frauds and forgeries tend to attract long sentences—but somebody who has committed a major fraud seldom commits another.” Thus, upon her adjusting the table for the two extreme anomalies, the apparent relationship between sentence length and recidivism disappeared.

正如她所写：

As she wrote:

我认为，即使是所谓的“枯燥乏味的内政部统计数据”也引人入胜……在我看来，统计学家的工作之一就是审视数据，探究其背后的原因……我今晚的想法可能有些简单，但我认为我们的职责是让别人觉得数据很有趣——如果我们说这话的人看起来很无聊，那么要么是我们表达得不好，要么就是数据本身并不有趣。我认为，我在内政部看到的统计数据一点也不枯燥。

I opine that even the so-called “dull old Home-Office statistics” are fascinating … . It seems to me that one of the statistician’s jobs is to look at figures, to query why they look like they do … . I am being very simpleminded tonight, but I think it is our job to suggest that figures are interesting—and, if the person to whom we say this looks bored, then we have either put it across badly or the figures are not interesting. I suggest that my statistics in the Home Office are not boring.

她谴责政府官员常常不仔细审查现有数据就做出决定的倾向：

She decried the tendency for government officials to make decisions without careful examination of the data available:

我认为这并非社会学家、社会工程师、规划师的过错……但统计学家往往难辞其咎。我们尚未学会如何服务于那些不如我们所期望的那样科学的学科，因此我们未能被接纳为能够帮助他们增进知识的人……。就我个人经验而言，统计学家在应用领域的优势在于……他们能够说服他人提出需要解答的问题；思考这些问题是否能够用实验者现有的工具来解答；帮助实验者建立合适的零假设；并将严格的设计原则应用于实验中。

I do not think this is the fault of the sociologist, the social engineer, the planner … but it must often be laid very firmly to the door of the statistician. We have not learned to serve those disciplines that are less scientific than we might like, and we have not therefore been accepted as people who can help them to further knowledge … . The strength of the statistician in applied fields, as experienced by me … lay in his or her ability to persuade other people to formulate questions which needed answering; to consider whether these questions could be answered with the tools available to the experimenter; to help him set up suitable null hypotheses; to apply rigid disciplines of design to the experiments.

就我个人经验而言，尝试用数学模型来表述问题，会迫使科学家去理解真正提出的问题是什么。仔细审视现有资源后，往往会得出结论：用这些资源无法回答这个问题。我认为，作为一名统计学家，我的主要贡献之一，就是劝阻他人不要尝试那些因资源不足而注定失败的实验。例如，在临床研究中，如果提出的医学问题需要涉及数十万名患者的研究，那么就应该重新考虑这个问题是否值得解答。

In my own experience, the attempt to formulate a problem in terms of a mathematical model forces the scientist into understanding what question is really being posed. A careful examination of resources available often produces the conclusion that it is not possible to answer that question with those resources. I think that some of my major contributions as a statistician were when I discouraged others from attempting an experiment that was doomed to failure for lack of adequate resources. For instance, in clinical research, when the medical question posed will require a study involving hundreds of thousands of patients, it is time to reconsider whether that question is worth answering.

抽象数学与实用统计学

ABSTRACT MATHEMATICS VERSUS USEFUL STATISTICS

斯特拉·坎利夫强调了使统计分析发挥作用的艰辛工作。她不屑于为了数学而进行的繁复数学推导，并谴责那些……的数学模型。

Stella Cunliffe emphasized the hard work of making statistical analyses useful. She was disdainful of elaborate mathematics for the sake of mathematics and decried mathematical models that are

丰富的想象力却缺乏现实感……大量的细节、有趣的旁支、大量的娱乐、精妙的概念，但同样缺乏稳健性和现实性。对优雅的追求，往往以牺​​牲实用性为代价，在我看来，恕我直言，这似乎是男性特有的特质……我们统计学家接受的教育是进行数学上的精确计算……[但是]我们不擅长……说服那些不了解情况的人相信我们的发现值得重视。如果我们只是郑重其事地对一个不理解的人说“p值小于0.001”，我们是无法成功说服他们的；我们必须用他们能理解的语言解释我们的发现，并培养说服力。

all the imagination and lack of reality … lots of string, interesting side-pieces, plenty of amusement, brilliant of concept, but also the same lack of robustness and of reality. The delight in elegance, often at the expense of practicality, appears to me, if I dare say so, to be rather a male attribute … . We statisticians are educated to calculate … with mathematical precision … [but] we are not good at … persuading the uninitiated that our findings are worth heeding. We shall not succeed in so doing if we solemnly quote “p less than 0.001” to an incomprehending man or woman; we must explain our findings in their language and develop the powers of persuasion.

斯特拉·坎利夫不戴帽子，也不愿屈尊屈从于酿酒大师们，她兴致勃勃地投身统计学的世界，尽情释放她旺盛的好奇心，并对前来聆听她演讲的数理统计学教授们进行辛辣的批评。截至本文撰写之时，她仍然活跃在皇家统计学会的会议上，用她那尖锐的智慧讽刺那些故作高深的数学家。

Without a hat, and refusing to make herself meekly subordinate to the master brewers, Stella Cunliffe flew into the world of statistics, jauntily indulging her lively curiosity and criticizing the professors of mathematical statistics who came to hear her speech. As of this writing, she could still be found attending meetings of the Royal Statistical Society and skewering mathematical pretensions with her tart wit.

C第26章

CHAPTER 26

鞅进行曲

THE MARCH OF THE MARTINGALES

C充血性心力衰竭是全球主要死因之一。虽然它常侵袭正值壮年的男女，但主要是一种老年疾病。在美国，65岁以上的公民中，充血性心力衰竭或其并发症导致的死亡人数几乎占一半。从公共卫生的角度来看，充血性心力衰竭不仅是死亡原因，也是导致活体患者患病的重要原因。充血性心力衰竭患者频繁住院以及用于稳定病情的复杂医疗程序是该国医疗服务总成本的主要因素。因此，人们迫切希望找到有效的门诊治疗方法，以减少住院需求并提高这些患者的生活质量。

Congestive heart failure is one of the major causes of death in the world. Although it often attacks men and women in the prime of life, it is primarily a disease of old age. Among citizens of the United States above the age of sixty-five, congestive heart failure or its complications account for almost half the deaths. From the standpoint of public health, congestive heart failure is more than a cause of death; it is also a cause of considerable illness among the living. The frequent hospitalizations and the complicated medical procedures used to stabilize patients with congestive heart failure are a major factor in the overall cost of medical services in the country. There is an intense interest in finding effective outpatient care that can reduce the need for hospitalizations and improve the quality of life for these patients.

不幸的是，充血性心力衰竭并非一种简单的疾病，它并非由单一感染因子引起，也无法通过阻断特定的酶促途径来缓解。充血性心力衰竭的主要症状是心肌逐渐衰弱。心脏对调节其收缩频率和强度的激素指令的反应越来越弱，难以满足身体不断变化的需求。心肌变得肥大松弛。肺部和踝部积液。患者稍有活动便会气喘吁吁。由于泵血量减少，当胃需要血液消化食物时，大脑供血不足，导致患者出现意识混乱或长时间嗜睡。

Unfortunately, congestive heart failure is not a simple disease that can be attributed to a single infective agent or which can be alleviated by blocking a particular enzymatic pathway. The primary symptom of congestive heart failure is the increasing weakness of the heart muscle. The heart becomes less and less able to respond to the subtle commands of hormones that regulate its rate and strength of contraction to meet the changing needs of the body. The heart muscle becomes enlarged and flabby. Fluid builds up in the lungs and ankles. The patient becomes breathless after slight exertion. The reduced amount of blood being pumped through the body means that the brain has a reduced level when the stomach demands blood to digest a meal, and the patient becomes confused or dozes for long periods of time.

为了维持体内平衡，患者的生命机能会适应心输出量的下降。在许多患者中，调节心脏和其他肌肉的激素平衡会发生改变，以达到一种相对稳定的状态，此时某些激素水平及其反应处于“异常”状态。如果医生使用β-肾上腺素能激动剂或钙通道阻滞剂等药物来治疗这种异常平衡，患者的病情可能会有所改善。然而，治疗也可能打破这种勉强稳定的状态，导致患者病情进一步恶化。过去，充血性心力衰竭患者的主要死因之一是肺部积液（以前称为水肿）。现代医学使用强效利尿剂来降低肺部积液量。然而，在这个过程中，这些利尿剂本身可能会扰乱肾脏产生的激素与心脏响应产生的激素之间的反馈平衡。

To maintain homeostasis, the life forces in the patient adapt to this decrease in heart output. In many patients, the balance of hormones that regulate the heart and other muscles changes to reach a somewhat stable state, where some of the hormonal levels and their responses are “abnormal.” If the physician treats this abnormal balance with drugs like beta-adrenergic agonists or calcium channel blockers, the result may be an improvement in the patient’s condition. Or, by tipping over that barely stable state, the treatment may drive the patient into further deterioration. One of the major causes of death among congestive heart failure patients used to be the buildup of fluid in their lungs (formerly called dropsy). Modern medicine makes use of powerful diuretics, which keep the fluid level down. In the process, however, these diuretics can, themselves, introduce problems in the feedback between hormones generated by the kidney and those generated by the heart in response.

人们仍在不断探索有效的医疗方法，以延长这些患者的寿命，减少住院次数，并提高他们的生活质量。由于某些治疗方法可能对部分患者产生反作用，因此任何针对这些治疗方法的临床研究都必须考虑患者的具体特征。这样，对这类研究数据的最终分析才能确定哪些患者接受治疗有效，哪些患者接受治疗无效。充血性心力衰竭研究的统计分析可能极其困难。

The search continues for effective medical treatments to prolong the life of these patients, reduce the frequency of hospitalizations, and improve the quality of their lives. Since some treatments may have counterproductive effects on some patients, any clinical study of these treatments will have to take specific patient characteristics into account. In this way, the final analysis of data from such a study can identify those patients for whom the treatment is effective and those patients for whom it is countereffective. The statistical analysis of congestive heart failure studies can become exceedingly difficult.

设计此类研究时，首先要考虑的是测量什么。例如，我们可以测量接受特定治疗的患者的平均住院次数。这是一种粗略的总体测量方法，忽略了患者年龄、初始健康状况以及住院频率和时长等重要因素。更好的做法是考虑每位患者的疾病进程，包括可能发生的住院情况、每次住院的持续时间、距上次住院的时间、两次住院之间的生活质量测量结果，并根据患者的年龄和其他可能存在的疾病对所有这些结果进行调整。从医学角度来看，这或许是理想的，但它会带来棘手的统计学问题。没有一个单一的数字可以与每位患者对应。相反，患者的记录是一系列事件的时间进程，其中一些事件会重复发生，而另一些事件则需要多次测量。这项实验的“测量”是多层次的，其参数需要估计的分布函数将具有多维结构。

When designing such a study, the first question is what to measure. We could, for instance, measure the average number of hospitalizations of patients on a given treatment. This is a rough overall measure that misses such important aspects as the patients’ ages, their initial health states, and the frequency and length of those hospitalizations. It would be better to consider the time course of each patient’s disease, accounting for the hospitalizations that might occur, how long they last, how long since the previous one, measurements of quality of life between hospitalizations, and adjusting all these outcomes for the patient’s age and other diseases that might be present. This might be the ideal from a medical point of view, but it poses difficult statistical problems. There is no single number to associate with each patient. Instead, the patient’s record is a time course of events, some of them repeated, others of which are measured by multiple measurements. The “measurements” of this experiment are multileveled, and the distribution function, whose parameters must be estimated, will have a multidimensional structure.

早期理论工作

EARLY THEORETICAL WORK

解决这个问题要从法国数学家保罗·列维说起。列维出身数学世家，祖辈也都是数学家。他生于1886年，很早就展现出过人的天赋。按照当时法国的惯例，他很快进入了一系列天才学校，并屡获殊荣。十几岁时，他就获得了希腊语和数学的综合竞赛奖（Prix du Concours Général）；在圣路易中学（Lycée Saint Louis）获得了数学、物理和化学的卓越奖（Prix d'Excellence）；并在巴黎高等师范学院（Ecole Normale Supérieure）和巴黎综合理工学院（Ecole Polytechnique）的入学考试中均获得一等奖（Concours d'Entrée）。1912年，26岁的他获得了理学博士学位，他的博士论文后来成为他撰写的关于抽象泛函分析的重要著作的基础。保罗·莱维33岁时已是巴黎综合理工学院的正式教授，也是法国科学院院士。他在抽象分析理论领域的研究使他享誉世界。1919年，他受学校委托准备一系列概率论讲座，并首次开始深入研究这一学科。

The solution to this problem begins with the French mathematician Paul Levy. Levy was the son and grandson of mathematicians. Born in 1886, he was identified early on as a gifted student. Following the usual procedure in France at the time, he was quickly moved through a series of special schools for the gifted and won academic honors. He received the Prix du Concours Général in Greek and mathematics while still in his teens; the Prix d’Excellence in mathematics, physics, and chemistry at Lycée Saint Louis; and a first Concours d’Entrée at the Ecole Normale Supérieure and at the Ecole Polytechnique. In 1912, at age twenty-six, he received his docteur des sciences degree, and his thesis was the basis of a major book he wrote on abstract functional analysis. By the time he was thirty-three, Paul Levy was a full professor at the Ecole Polytechnique and a member of the Académie des Sciences. His work in the abstract theories of analysis made him world famous. In 1919, he was asked by his school to prepare a series of lectures on probability theory, and he began examining that subject in depth for the first time.

保罗·列维对概率论作为一系列复杂计数方法的集合并不满意。（当时安德烈·柯尔莫戈罗夫尚未做出贡献。）列维寻求一些潜在的抽象数学概念，以期将这些方法统一起来。他被棣莫弗对正态分布的推导以及数学界流传的“民间定理”所吸引，即棣莫弗的结果应该适用于许多其他情况——这后来被称为“中心极限定理”。我们已经看到，列维（与芬兰的林德伯格合作）在20世纪30年代初最终证明了中心极限定理，并确定了其成立的必要条件。为此，列维从正态分布的公式出发，反向推导，探究这种分布的独特性质是什么，使其能够适用于如此多的情况。

Paul Levy was dissatisfied with probability theory as a collection of sophisticated counting methods. (Andrei Kolmogorov had not yet made his contribution.) Levy looked for some underlying abstract mathematical concepts that might allow him to unify these many methods. He was struck by de Moivre’s derivation of the normal distribution and by the “folk theorem” among mathematicians that de Moivre’s result should hold for many other situations—what came to be called the “central limit theorem.” We have seen how Levy (along with Lindeberg in Finland) in the early 1930s finally proved the central limit theorem and determined the conditions necessary for it to hold. In doing so, Levy started with the formula for the normal distribution and worked backward, asking what were the unique properties of this distribution that would make it rise out of so many situations.

莱维随后从另一个角度切入问题，探究究竟是什么特定情况导致了正态分布。他发现，两个简单的条件就能保证数据趋向于正态分布。这两个条件并非生成正态分布的唯一途径，但莱维对中心极限定理的证明确立了更一般的必要条件。这两个条件足以应对以下情况：我们有一个随机生成的数字序列，一个接一个地出现。

Levy then approached the problem from the other direction, asking what was it about specific situations that led to the normal distribution. He determined that a simple set of two conditions will guarantee that data tend to be normally distributed. These two conditions are not the only ways the normal distribution can be generated, but Lévy’s proof of the central limit theorem established the more general set of conditions that is always needed. These two conditions were adequate for the situation where we have a sequence of randomly generated numbers, one following on the other:

  1. 必须限制变异性，以免个别数值变得无穷大或无穷小。
  2. The variability has to be bounded so individual values do not become infinitely large or small.
  3. 下一个数字的最佳估计值是上一个数字的值。
  4. The best estimate of the next number is the value of the last number.

莱维将这样的序列称为“鞅”。

Lévy called such a sequence a “martingale.”

莱维借用了赌博术语中的“马丁格尔”一词。在赌博中，“马丁格尔”指的是赌徒每次输钱后都将赌注翻倍的策略。如果他有50%的胜率，那么预期损失就等于他之前的损失。这个词还有另外两个含义。一个是指法国农民用来固定马头、防止马匹后腿站立的装置。农民使用的“马丁格尔”将马头固定在一个可以随意移动的位置，但最有可能的未来位置就是马头当前的位置。该词的第三个定义与航海有关。“马丁格尔”是一根悬挂在帆桁上的重木条，用来防止帆桁左右摆动过大。同样，帆桁的最后位置也是预测其下一个位置的最佳指标。这个词本身来源于法国小镇马提克的居民，他们以吝啬而闻名，所以对他们下周能捐出的钱数额的最佳估计就是他们今天捐出的钱数额。

Levy appropriated the word martingale from a gambling term. In gambling, a martingale is a procedure wherein the gambler doubles his bet each time he loses. If he has a 50:50 chance of winning, the expected loss is equal to his previous loss. There are two other meanings to the word. One describes a device used by French farmers to keep a horse’s head down and to keep the animal from rearing. The farmer’s martingale keeps the horse’s head in a position so that it can be moved at random, but the most probable future position is the one the head is held in now. A third definition of the term is a nautical one. A martingale is a heavy piece of wood hung from the boom of a sail to keep the boom from swinging too far from one side to the other. Here, too, the last position of the boom is the best predictor of its next position. The word itself is derived from the inhabitants of the French town of Martique, who were legendary for their stinginess, so the best estimate of the little money they would give next week was the little they gave today.

因此，马提克岛吝啬的居民们赋予了一种数学抽象概念以名字，保罗·利维用这种概念推导出了趋向正态分布的数列的最吝啬的特征。到1940年，鞅已成为抽象数学理论中的重要工具。它简单的要求意味着许多类型的随机数序列都可以被证明是鞅。20世纪70年代，挪威奥斯陆大学的奥德·阿伦意识到，临床试验中患者的反应过程就是一个鞅。

Thus, the stingy inhabitants of Martique gave their name to a mathematical abstraction in which Paul Levy developed the stingiest possible characteristics of a sequence of numbers that tend to have a normal distribution. By 1940, the martingale had become an important tool in abstract mathematical theory. Its simple requirements meant that many types of sequences of random numbers could be shown to be martingales. In the 1970s, Odd Aalen of the University of Oslo in Norway realized that the course of patient responses in a clinical trial was a martingale.

充血性心力衰竭研究中的鞅疗法

MARTINGALES IN CONGESTIVE HEART FAILURE STUDIES

回顾一下充血性心力衰竭研究中出现的问题。患者的反应往往具有个体差异。例如，如何解读研究早期或后期（患者年龄增长后）发生的住院事件，以及如何处理住院频率和住院时长等问题，都是需要考虑的因素。所有这些问题都可以通过将随时间变化的数值流视为鞅来解答。特别是，正如Aalen指出的，住院患者可以暂时从分析中移除，出院后再重新纳入分析。多次住院可以视为一次独立的事件。在每个时间点，分析人员只需要知道仍在研究中（或已重新纳入研究）的患者人数以及最初纳入研究的患者人数即可。

Recall the problems arising from a congestive heart failure study. The patient responses tend to be idiosyncratic. There are questions about how to interpret events like hospitalizations when they occur early in the study or later (when the patients have become older). There are questions about how to deal with the frequency of hospitalizations and the length of stay in the hospitals. All of these questions can be answered by viewing as martingales the stream of numbers taken over time. In particular, Aalen noted, a patient who is hospitalized can be taken out of the analysis and returned to it when released. Multiple hospitalizations can be treated as if each one were a new event. At each point in time, the analyst need know only the number of patients still in the study (or returned to the study) and the number of patients who were initially entered into the study.

到了20世纪80年代初，阿伦开始与丹麦奥胡斯大学的埃里克·安德森和荷兰乌得勒支大学的理查德·吉尔合作，运用他所发展出的洞见。本书第一章中，我指出科学和数学研究很少是单打独斗完成的。数理统计的抽象概念非常复杂，很容易出错。只有通过同事间的讨论和批评，才能发现许多这样的错误。阿伦、安德森和吉尔三人的合作，促成了20世纪最后几十年该领域最卓有成效的发展之一。

By the early 1980s, Aalen was working with Erik Anderson of the University of Aarhus in Denmark and Richard Gill of the University of Utrecht in the Netherlands, exploiting the insight that he had developed. In the first chapter of this book, I pointed out that scientific and mathematical research is seldom done alone. The abstractions of mathematical statistics are so involved that it is easy to make mistakes. Only by discussion and criticism among colleagues can many of these mistakes be found. The collaboration among these three, Aalen, Anderson, and Gill, provided one of the most fruitful developments of the subject in the final decades of the twentieth century.

Aalen、Anderson 和 Gill 的工作得到了华盛顿大学 Richard Olshen 及其合作者以及哈佛大学 Lee-Jen Wei 的补充，从而产生了大量用于分析临床试验中事件序列的新方法。特别是 Lee-Jen Wei，他利用了两个鞅之间的差值仍然是鞅这一特性，从而避免了估计模型中许多参数的需要。如今，鞅方法在慢性病长期临床试验的统计分析中占据主导地位。

The work of Aalen, Anderson, and Gill has been supplemented by that of Richard Olshen and his collaborators at the University of Washington and by Lee-Jen Wei at Harvard University, to produce a wealth of new methods for analyzing the sequence of events that occur in a clinical trial. L. J. Wei, in particular, has exploited the fact that the difference between two martingales is also a martingale, to eliminate the need to estimate many of the parameters of the model. Today, the martingale approach dominates the statistical analysis of long-term clinical trials of chronic disease.

马提克岛居民出了名的吝啬，这便是一切的起点。法国人保罗·莱维（Paul Lévy）提出了最初的见解。数学鞅理论辗转于众多学者之手，其中包括美国人、俄罗斯人、德国人、英国人、意大利人和印度人。一位挪威人、一位丹麦人和一位荷兰人将其应用于临床研究。两位美国人（其中一位出生于台湾）进一步拓展了他们的工作。自20世纪80年代末以来，关于这一主题的论文和书籍作者名单浩如烟海，足以写满好几页，而且其中还涉及来自其他国家的学者。的确，数理统计学已然成为一项正在蓬勃发展的国际性事业。

The legendary stinginess of the inhabitants of Martique was the starting point. A Frenchman, Paul Lévy, had the initial insights. The mathematical martingale passed through many other minds, with contributions from Americans, Russians, Germans, English, Italians, and Indians. A Norwegian, a Dane, and a Dutchman brought it to clinical research. Two Americans, one born in Taiwan, elaborated on their work. A complete listing of the authors of papers and books on this topic, which have emerged since the late 1980s, would fill many pages and involve workers from still other countries. Truly, mathematical statistics has become an international work in the making.

C章27

CHAPTER 27

治疗意图

THE INTENT TO TREAT

我20世纪80年代初，英国顶尖生物统计学家之一理查德·佩托（Richard Peto）遇到了一个难题。他当时正在分析几项临床试验的结果，这些试验旨在比较不同癌症治疗方案的效果。按照R.A.费舍尔（R.A. Fisher）的实验设计，典型的临床试验会筛选出一组需要治疗的患者，并将他们随机分配到不同的实验治疗组。

In the early 1980s, Richard Peto, one of the leading biostatisticians in Great Britain, had a problem. He was analyzing the results of several clinical trials comparing different treatments for cancer among patients. Following the dictates of R. A. Fisher’s experimental design, the typical clinical trial identified a group of patients who were in need of treatment and assigned them, at random, to different experimental methods of treatment.

对这类数据的分析原本应该相对简单。可以使用费舍尔方法比较各治疗组五年生存率。也可以采用更精细的比较方法，例如使用阿伦鞅方法，以研究开始到每位患者死亡的时间作为疗效的基本指标。无论采用哪种方法，分析都基于患者最初的随机分组。根据费舍尔的原则，患者的分组与研究结果完全无关，因此可以计算假设检验的p值。

The analysis of such data should have been relatively straightforward. The percentage of patients who survived for five years would have been compared among the treatment groups, using Fisher’s methods. A more subtle comparison could have been made, using Aalen’s martingale approach to analyze the time from the beginning of the study to each patient’s death as the basic measure of effect, Either way, the analysis was based on the initial randomization of patients to treatment. Following Fisher’s dictums, the assignment of patients to treatment was completely independent of the outcome of the study, and p-values tor hypothesis tests could be calculated.

佩托的问题在于，并非所有患者都接受了随机分配的治疗。这些都是活生生的人，他们饱受痛苦，而且在很多情况下是绝症的折磨。治疗他们的医生感到有必要放弃实验性治疗，或者至少在他们认为符合患者最佳利益的情况下对其进行修改。盲目地遵循任意的治疗方案，而不考虑患者的需求和反应，是不道德的。与费舍尔的论断相反，这些研究中的患者经常接受新的治疗，而治疗方案的选择确实取决于患者的反应。

Peto’s problem was that all the patients had not been given the treatments to which they were randomized. These were human beings, suffering from painful and, in many cases, terminal disease. The physicians treating them felt impelled to abandon the experimental treatment or at least to modify it if they felt it was in the best interests of the patient. Blind following of an arbitrary treatment, without considering the patient’s needs and responses, would have been unethical. Contrary to Fisher’s dictums, the patients in these studies were often provided with new treatments wherein the choice of treatment did depend upon the patient’s response.

这是癌症研究中一个典型的问题。自20世纪50年代癌症研究兴起以来，这个问题就一直存在。在佩托出现之前，通常的做法是只分析那些坚持完成随机分组治疗的患者，而将其他患者排除在分析之外。佩托意识到这可能会导致严重的错误。例如，假设我们正在比较一种活性治疗和一种安慰剂（一种没有生物效应的药物）的疗效。假设那些对活性治疗无反应的患者被转为接受标准治疗。那么，那些对安慰剂治疗无效的患者将被转为接受标准治疗，并被排除在分析之外。唯一继续接受安慰剂治疗的患者，由于其他原因，对安慰剂治疗有反应。如果分析中只使用了那些坚持接受安慰剂治疗且有反应的患者，那么就会发现安慰剂与活性治疗一样有效（甚至可能更有效）。

This was a typical problem in cancer studies. It had been a problem in such studies since they were first started in the 1950s. Until Peto came on the scene, the usual procedure was to analyze only those patients who remained on their randomized treatment. All other patients were dropped from the analysis. Peto realized that this could lead to serious errors. For instance, suppose we were comparing an active treatment with treatment by a placebo, a drug that has no biological effect. Suppose that patients who fail to respond are switched to a standard treatment. The patients who fail on placebo will be switched over and left out of the analysis. The only patients who remain on placebo will be patients who, for some other reason, are responding. The placebo will be found to be as effective (or perhaps even more so) than the active treatment—if the patients who remained on placebo and responded are the only placebo-treated patients used in the analysis.

埃德蒙·格汉当时在德克萨斯州的安德森癌症中心工作，他比佩托更早发现了这个问题。他当时的解决方案是，这些研究不符合费舍尔的要求，因此不能被视为比较不同疗法的有效实验。相反，这些研究的记录仅仅是对接受不同类型治疗的患者进行的仔细观察。所能期望的最好结果也只是对治疗结果的总体描述，并暗示未来可能的治疗方案。后来，格汉考虑了其他几种解决这个问题的方法，但他最初的结论反映了试图将统计分析方法应用于设计或执行不佳的实验时所感受到的挫败感。

Edmund Gehan, who was at M. C. Anderson Hospital in Texas, had seen the problem before Peto. His solution at the time was to propose that these studies did not meet Fisher’s requirements, so they could not be considered useful experiments for comparing treatments. Instead, the records from these studies consisted of careful observations taken on patients given different types of treatments. The best that could be expected would be a general description of their outcome, with hints of possible future treatment. Later, Gehan considered various other solutions to this problem, but his first conclusion reflects the frustration of someone who tries to apply the methods of statistical analysis to a poorly designed or executed experiment.

佩托提出了一个简单的解决方案。患者已被随机分配接受特定治疗。正是由于随机化这一行为，才使得计算比较这些治疗方案的假设检验的p值成为可能。他建议在分析中，将每位患者视为接受了其随机分配的治疗方案。分析人员将忽略研究过程中发生的所有治疗方案变更。如果一位患者被随机分配到A治疗组，但在研究结束前不久停止了该治疗，则该患者仍被视为A治疗组的患者进行分析。如果一位被随机分配到A治疗组的患者仅接受了一周的A治疗，则该患者仍被视为A治疗组的患者进行分析。如果一位被随机分配到A治疗组的患者从未服用过A治疗的任何药物，但在入组后立即住院并接受了其他治疗，则该患者仍被视为A治疗组的患者进行分析。

Peto suggested a straightforward solution. The patients had been randomized to receive specific treatments. The act of randomization is what made it possible to calculate the p-values of hypothesis tests comparing those treatments. He suggested that each patient be treated in the analysis as if he or she had been on the treatment to which he or she had been randomized. The analyst would ignore all treatment changes that occurred during the course of the study. If a patient was randomized to treatment A and was moved off of that treatment just before the end of the study, that patient was analyzed as a treatment A patient. If the patient randomized to treatment A was on treatment A for only a week, that patient was analyzed as a treatment A patient. If the patient randomized to treatment A never took a single pill from treatment A but was hospitalized and put on alternative therapies immediately after entering the study, that patient was analyzed as a treatment A patient.

这种方法乍看之下似乎很愚蠢。我们可以设想这样一种情景：将标准疗法与实验疗法进行比较，如果实验疗法无效，患者就转而接受标准疗法。这样一来，如果实验疗法无效，所有或大部分被随机分配到实验疗法组的患者都会被转为接受标准疗法，分析结果就会显示两种疗法的效果相同。正如理查德·佩托在他的提案中所明确指出的，这种分析研究结果的方法并不能得出疗法等效的结论。它只能用于分析发现两种疗法效果不同的情况。

This approach may seem foolish at first glance. One can produce scenarios in which a standard treatment is being compared to an experimental one, with patients switched to the standard if they fail. Then, if the experimental treatment is worthless, all or most of the patients randomized to it will be switched to the standard, and the analysis will find the two treatments the same. As Richard Peto made it clear in his proposal, this method of analyzing the results of a study cannot be used to find that treatments are equivalent. It can only be used if the analysis finds that they differ in effect.

佩托的解决方案后来被称为“意向性治疗”法。其名称及其普遍应用的理由如下：如果我们关注的是推荐使用某种特定疗法的医疗政策的总体效果，那么必须赋予医生根据自身判断调整治疗方案的自由。运用佩托的解决方案分析临床试验，可以确定推荐某种疗法作为初始治疗是否符合公共政策。因此，对于旨在制定合理公共政策的大型政府资助研究而言，应用意向性治疗分析法被认为是一种明智的选择。

Peto’s solution came to be called the “intent to treat” method. The justification for this name and for its use in general was the following: If we are interested in the overall results of a medical policy that would recommend the use of a given treatment, the physician has to be given the freedom to modify treatment as she sees fit. An analysis of a clinical trial, using Peto’s solution, would determine if it is good public policy to recommend a given treatment as a starting treatment. The application of the intent to treat method of analysis was proposed as a sensible one for large government-sponsored studies designed to determine good public policies.

不幸的是，一些科学家倾向于在不了解或不理解其背后的数学原理的情况下使用统计方法。这种情况在临床研究领域尤为常见。佩托曾指出他的方法存在局限性。尽管如此，意向性治疗分析法仍然在许多大学的医学理论中得到确立，并被视为临床试验统计分析的唯一正确方法。许多临床试验，尤其是癌症临床试验，旨在证明一种新疗法至少与标准疗法一样有效，同时副作用更少。许多试验的目的是证明治疗等效性。正如佩托所指出的，他的方法只能用于发现差异，而未能发现差异并不意味着两种疗法是等效的。

Unfortunately, there is a tendency for some scientists to use statistical methods without knowing or understanding the mathematics behind them. This often appears in the world of clinical research. Peto had pointed out the limitations of his solution. In spite of this, the intent to treat method became enshrined in medical doctrine at many universities and came to be seen as the only correct method of statistical analysis of a clinical trial. Many clinical trials, especially those in cancer, are designed to show that a new treatment is at least as good as the standard, while presenting fewer side effects. The purpose of many trials is to show therapeutic equivalence. As Peto pointed out, his solution can be used only to find differences, and failure to find differences does not mean that the treatments are equivalent.

问题在某种程度上在于奈曼-皮尔逊公式的僵化性。基础统计学教科书中对奈曼-皮尔逊公式的标准表述往往将假设检验描述为一种一成不变的程序。该方法的许多纯粹人为设定的方面都被视为不可更改的。

The problem lay, to some extent, in the rigidity of the Neyman-Pearson formulation. The standard redaction of the Neyman-Pearson formulation found in elementary statistics textbooks tends to present hypothesis testing as a cut-and-dried procedure. Many purely arbitrary aspects of the methods are presented as immutable.

尽管这些任意因素中的许多可能并不适用于临床研究[38] ，但一些医学科学家坚持使用“正确”方法，这导致内曼-皮尔逊公式被赋予了极其僵化的版本。除非预先设定p值阈值并由统计程序保持，否则任何结果都不可接受。这正是费舍尔反对内曼-皮尔逊公式的原因之一。他认为p值和显著性检验的使用不应受到如此严格的限制。他尤其反对内曼预先设定假阳性概率，并且只有在p值小于该概率时才采取行动。费舍尔在其著作《统计方法与科学推断》中建议，最终决定p值是否具有显著性应取决于具体情况。我之所以用“建议”一词，是因为费舍尔从未明确说明他将如何使用p值，他只是列举了一些例子。

While many of these arbitrary elements may not be appropriate for clinical research,[38] the need that some medical scientists have to use “correct” methods has enshrined an extremely rigid version of the Neyman-Pearson formulation. Nothing is acceptable unless the p-value cutoff is fixed in advance and preserved by the statistical procedure. This was one reason why Fisher opposed the Neyman-Pearson formulation. He did not think that the use of p-values and significance tests should be subjected to such rigorous requirements. He objected, in particular, to the fact that Neyman would fix the probability of a false positive in advance and act only if the p-value is less than that. Fisher suggested in his book Statistical Methods and Scientific Inference that the final decision about what p-value would be significant should depend upon the circumstances. I used the word suggested because Fisher is never quite clear on how he would use p-values. He only presents examples.

考克斯公式

COX’S FORMULATION

1977年，戴维·R·考克斯（第23章中提到的Box和Cox）继承了费舍尔的论点并加以扩展。为了区分费舍尔对p值的运用和奈曼-皮尔逊公式，他将费舍尔的方法称为“显著性检验”，将奈曼-皮尔逊公式称为“假设检验”。在考克斯撰写论文之时，统计显著性的计算（通过使用p值）已成为科学研究中最广泛使用的方法之一。因此，考克斯认为，该方法已被证明在科学中是有用的。尽管费舍尔和奈曼之间存在激烈的争论，尽管像W·爱德华兹·戴明这样的统计学家坚持认为假设检验毫无用处，尽管贝叶斯统计学兴起，而贝叶斯统计学并不认可p值和显著性——尽管数理统计学家对显著性检验和p值存在诸多批评，但显著性检验和p值仍然被不断地使用。考克斯问道，科学家们究竟是如何运用这些检验的？他们如何确定这些检验结果是真实有效的？他发现，在实践中，科学家们主要利用假设检验来完善他们对现实的理解，例如通过排除不必要的参数，或者在两种不同的现实模型之间做出选择。

In 1977, David R. Cox (of Box and Cox from chapter 23) took up Fisher’s arguments and extended them. To distinguish between Fisher’s use of p-values and the Neyman-Pearson formulation, he called Fisher’s method “significance testing” and the Neyman-Pearson formulation “hypothesis testing.” By the time Cox wrote his paper, the calculation of statistical significance (through the use of p-values) had become one of the most widely used methods of scientific research.
