Thus, Cox reasoned, the method has proven to be useful in science. In spite of the acrimonious dispute between Fisher and Neyman, in spite of the insistence of statisticians like W. Edwards Deming that hypothesis tests were useless, in spite of the rise of Bayesian statistics, which had no place for p-values and significances—in spite of all these criticisms among mathematical statisticians, significance testing and p-values are constantly being used. How, Cox asked, do scientists actually use these tests? How do they know that the results of such tests are true or useful? He discovered that, in practice, scientists use hypothesis tests primarily for refining their views of reality by eliminating unnecessary parameters, or for deciding between two differing models of reality.

博克斯的方法

BOX’S APPROACH

乔治·博克斯（博克斯和考克斯的另一位成员）从略微不同的角度看待这个问题。他指出，科学研究并非仅仅由一次实验构成。科学家在进行实验之前，已经积累了大量的先验知识，或者至少对实验结果有所预期。研究旨在完善这些知识，而研究设计则取决于所寻求的完善类型。到目前为止，博克斯和考克斯的观点基本一致。在博克斯看来，这一次实验只是众多实验中的一部分。本次实验的数据会与其他实验的数据进行比较。然后，科学家们会根据新实验以及对旧实验的新分析，重新审视先前的知识。科学家们会不断地回顾早期的研究，并根据新的研究结果来完善他们对早期研究的解读。

George Box (the other half of Box and Cox) approached the problem from a slightly different perspective. Scientific research, he noted, consisted of more than a single experiment. The scientist arrives at the experiment with a large body of prior knowledge or at least with a prior expectation of what might be the result. The study is designed to refine that knowledge, and the design depends upon what type of refinement is sought. Up to this point, Box and Cox are saying much the same thing. To Box, this one experiment is part of a stream of experiments. The data from this experiment are compared to data from other experiments. The previous knowledge is then reconsidered in terms of both the new experiment and new analyses of the old experiments. The scientists never cease to return to older studies to refine their interpretation of them in terms of the newer studies.

以博克斯的方法为例，假设一家造纸厂正在使用博克斯的一项重要创新——操作演化变异（EVOP）。通过博克斯的EVOP，造纸厂在生产过程中引入实验。湿度、速度、硫磺用量和温度等参数都会以各种方式进行微调。由此导致的纸张强度变化并不大。这种变化不可能很大，否则就无法生产出可销售的产品。然而，这些微小的差异，经过费舍尔方差分析，可以用来设计另一个实验：略微提高所有实验的平均强度，并利用新实验的结果来寻找进一步提高强度的方向。EVOP中每个阶段的结果都会与前一阶段的结果进行比较。那些似乎产生异常结果的实验​​会被重新进行。这个过程会一直持续下去——没有最终的“正确”答案。在博克斯的模型中，科学实验及其后续对数据的反复检验永无止境——没有最终的科学真理。

As an example of Box’s approach, consider the manufacturer of paper who is using one of Box’s major innovations, evolutionary variation in operations (EVOP). With Box’s EVOP, the manufacturer introduces experiments into the production run. The humidity, speed, sulfur, and temperature are modified slightly in various ways. The resulting change in paper strength is not great. It cannot be great and still produce a salable product. Yet these slight differences, subjected to Fisher’s analysis of variance, can be used to propose another experiment, in which the average strength across all the runs has been slightly increased, and the new runs are used to find the direction of still another slight increase in strength. The results of each stage in EVOP are compared to previous stages. Experiments that seem to produce anomalous results are rerun. The procedure continues forever—there is no final “correct” solution. In Box’s model, the sequence of scientific experiments followed by examination and reexamination of data has no end—there is no final scientific truth.

戴明的观点

DEMING’S VIEW

戴明和许多其他统计学家彻底否定了假设检验的使用。他们坚持认为，费舍尔关于估计方法的研究应该构成统计分析的基础。真正应该估计的是分布的参数。通过p值和任意假设间接处理这些参数的分析毫无意义。这些统计学家仍然使用内曼的置信区间来衡量结论的不确定性；但他们认为，内曼-皮尔逊假设检验应该像卡尔·皮尔逊的矩估计法一样，被历史淘汰。值得注意的是，内曼本人在其应用论文中也很少使用p值和假设检验。

Deming and many other statisticians have rejected the use of hypothesis tests outright. They insist that Fisher’s work on methods of estimation should form the basis of statistical analyses. It is the parameters of the distribution that should be estimated. It makes no sense to run analyses that deal indirectly with these parameters through p-values and arbitrary hypotheses. These statisticians continue to use Neyman’s confidence intervals to measure the uncertainty of their conclusions; but Neyman-Pearson hypothesis testing, they contend, belongs in the waste bin of history, along with Karl Pearson’s method of moments. It is interesting to note that Neyman, himself, seldom used p-values and hypothesis tests in his own applied papers.

对假设检验的否定，以及Box和Cox对Fisher显著性检验概念的重新表述，可能会使人们对Richard Peto提出的解决其在临床癌症研究中发现的问题的方案产生怀疑。但他面临的基本问题依然存在：当实验因治疗后果而改变治疗方案时，该如何处理？Abraham Wald已经展示了如何应对特定类型的修改，从而发展出序贯分析法。在Peto的案例中，肿瘤学家并没有遵循Wald的序贯方法。他们根据自身判断，根据需要插入不同的治疗方案。

This rejection of hypothesis testing, and the Box and Cox reformulations of Fisher’s concept of significance testing may cast doubt on Richard Peto’s solution to the problem he found in clinical cancer studies. But the basic problem he faced remains. What do you do when the experiment has been modified by allowing the consequences of the treatment to change the treatment? Abraham Wald had shown how a particular type of modification can be accommodated, leading to sequential analysis. In Peto’s case, the oncologists were not following Wald’s sequential methods. They were inserting different treatments as they perceived the need.

科克伦的观察性研究

COCHRAN’S OBSERVATIONAL STUDIES

在某种程度上，约翰·霍普金斯大学的威廉·科克伦在20世纪60年代就处理过类似的问题。巴尔的摩市政府想要确定公共住房是否会对穷人的社会态度和发展产生影响。他们找到约翰·霍普金斯大学的统计学小组，希望他们能帮忙设计一个实验。约翰·霍普金斯大学的统计学家们借鉴费舍尔的方法，建议他们随机抽取一部分人（无论他们是否申请过公共住房），然后随机分配一部分人入住公共住房，另一部分人则被拒绝。这让市政府官员们感到震惊。按照惯例，当公共住房空置时，他们会按照先到先得的原则进行分配。这才是公平的。他们不能剥夺那些争先恐后想要“抢占先机”的人的权利——更何况这还是基于计算机生成的随机分配。然而，约翰·霍普金斯大学的统计学小组指出，那些争先恐后申请的人往往是最有活力、最有抱负的。如果这是真的，那么住在公屋里的人会比其他人过得更好——而住房本身并没有任何影响。

In some ways, this is a problem that William Cochran of Johns Hopkins University dealt with in the 1960s. The city of Baltimore wanted to determine if public housing had an effect on the social attitudes and progress of poor people. They approached the statistics group at Johns Hopkins to help them set up an experiment. Following Fisher’s methods, the Johns Hopkins statisticians suggested they take a group of people, whether they had applied for public housing or not, and randomly assign some of them to public housing and refuse it to the others. This horrified the city officials. When openings were announced in public housing, it was their practice to respond on a first-come first-served basis. It was only fair. They could not deny their rights to people who rushed to be “first”—and that on the basis of a computer-generated randomization. The Johns Hopkins statistics group pointed out, however, that those who rushed to apply were often the most energetic and ambitious. If this was true, then those in public housing would do better than the others—without the housing itself having any effect.

科克伦的解决方案是，他们无法使用预先设计的科学实验。相反，他们可以通过追踪入住公屋的家庭和未入住公屋的家庭来进行观察性研究，这些家庭在年龄、教育水平、宗教信仰和家庭稳定性等诸多因素上存在差异。他提出了对这类观察性研究进行统计分析的方法。具体做法是，调整特定家庭的结果测量指标，以考虑这些不同的因素。他建立了一个数学模型，其中包含年龄的影响、家庭是否完整的影响、宗教信仰的影响等等。一旦估计出所有这些影响的参数，剩余的影响差异就可以用来确定公屋的效果。

Cochran’s solution was to propose that they were not going to be able to use a designed scientific experiment. Instead, by following families who went into public housing and those who did not, they would have an observational study, where the families differed by many factors, such as age, educational level, religion, and family stability. He proposed methods for running a statistical analysis of such observational studies. He would do this by adjusting the outcome measurement for a given family to take these different factors into account. He would set up a mathematical model in which there would be an effect due to age, an effect due to whether it was an intact family, an effect due to religion, and so forth. Once the parameters of all these effects had been estimated, the remaining differences in effect would be used to determine the effect of public housing.

当一项临床研究宣布其疗效差异已根据患者年龄或性别进行调整时，这意味着研究人员应用了科克伦的一些方法来估计治疗的潜在效果，并考虑了治疗分配不平衡的影响。几乎所有社会学研究都使用了科克伦的方法。这些研究的作者可能并未意识到这些方法源自威廉·科克伦，而且许多具体技术实际上早于他的工作。科克伦为这些方法奠定了坚实的理论基础，他关于观察性研究的论文影响了医学、社会学、政治学和天文学——所有这些领域中，随机分配“治疗”要么是不可能的，要么是不道德的。

When a clinical study announces that the difference in effect has been adjusted for patient age or patient sex, this means that researchers have applied some of Cochran’s methods to estimate the underlying effect of treatment, taking into account the effect of imbalances in the assignment of treatment to patients. Almost all sociological studies use Cochran’s methods. The authors of these studies may not recognize them as coming from William Cochran, and many of the specific techniques often predate his work. Cochran put it on a solid theoretical foundation, and his papers on observational studies have influenced medicine, sociology, political science, and astronomy—all areas in which random assignment of “treatment” is either impossible or unethical.

鲁宾的模型

RUBIN’S MODELS

20世纪80年代和90年代，哈佛大学的唐纳德·鲁宾提出了一种解决佩托问题的替代方法。在鲁宾的模型中，假设每位患者对每种治疗方案都有可能产生反应。如果有两种治疗方案，那么每位患者对治疗方案A和治疗方案B都有可能产生反应。我们只能观察患者接受其中一种治疗方案（即患者被分配到的治疗方案）后的反应。我们可以建立一个数学模型，其中公式中包含代表每种可能反应的符号。鲁宾推导出了该数学模型的必要条件，以便估计如果患者接受另一种治疗方案会发生什么。

In the 1980s and 1990s, Donald Rubin of Harvard University proposed a different approach to Peto’s problem. In Rubin’s model, each patient is assumed to have a possible response to each of the treatments. If there are two treatments, each patient has a potential response to both treatment A and treatment B. We can observe the patient under only one of these treatments, the one to which the patient had been assigned. We can set up a mathematical model in which there is a symbol in the formula for each of those possible responses. Rubin derived conditions on this mathematical model that are needed to estimate what might have happened had the patient been put on the other treatment.

鲁宾模型和科克伦方法之所以能应用于现代统计分析，是因为它们利用计算机进行海量数值计算。即便在费舍尔时代就有人提出这些方法，它们也难以实现。之所以需要计算机辅助，是因为这些数学模型非常复杂。它们通常需要迭代技术，即计算机进行成千上万次的估计，最终收敛于最终结果。

Rubin’s models and Cochran’s methods can be applied in modern statistical analyses because they make use of the computer to engage in massive amounts of number crunching. Even if they had been proposed during Fisher’s time, they would not have been feasible. They require the use of the computer because the mathematical models are highly involved and complicated. They often require iterative techniques, where the computer does thousands or even millions of estimations, the sequence of estimations converging on the final answer.

科克伦和鲁宾的方法高度依赖于特定的模型。也就是说，只有当它们所使用的复杂数学模型与现实的描述非常接近时，才能得出正确的答案。这些方法要求分析人员设计一个在所有或大部分方面都与现实相符的数学模型。如果现实与模型不符，那么分析结果可能就不成立。与科克伦和鲁宾方法类似的另一个重要方面是，需要努力确定结论的稳健程度。当前的数学研究正在探讨，现实与模型之间的偏差达到多大程度，结论才会失效。威廉·科克伦在1980年去世前，一直在研究这些问题。

These Cochran and Rubin methods are highly model specific. That is, they will not produce correct answers unless the complicated mathematical models they use come close to describing reality. They require the analyst to devise a mathematical model that will match reality in all or most of its aspects. If the reality does not match the model, then the results of the analysis may not hold. A concomitant part of approaches like those of Cochran and Rubin has been the effort to determine the degree to which the conclusions are robust. Current mathematical investigations are looking at how far reality can be from the model before the conclusions are no longer true. Before he died in 1980, William Cochran was examining these questions.

统计分析方法可以被视为一个连续体，一端是高度依赖模型的方法，例如科克伦和鲁宾提出的方法。另一端是非参数方法，它们从最普遍的模式角度来分析数据。正如计算机使高度依赖模型的方法成为可能一样，统计建模的另一端——非参数方法——也发生了一场计算机革命。在非参数方法中，几乎不预设任何数学结构，数据可以自由地讲述自己的故事，而无需将其强行塞入预设的模型中。这些方法有着诸如“自助法”（bootstrap）之类的奇特名称。它们将是下一章的主题。

Methods of statistical analysis can be thought of as lying on a continuum, with highly model-bound methods like those proposed by Cochran and Rubin on one end. At the other end, there are nonparametric methods, which examine data in terms of the most general type of patterns. Just as the computer has made highly model-bound methods feasible, there has been a computer revolution at the other end of statistical modeling—this nonparametric end where little or no mathematical structure is assumed and the data are allowed to tell their story without forcing them into preconceived models. These methods go by fanciful names like “the bootstrap.” They are the subject of the next chapter.

C第28章

CHAPTER 28

计算机自相矛盾

THE COMPUTER TURNS UPON ITSELF

G乌伊多·卡斯特尔努沃出身于一个引以为傲的意大利犹太家庭。他们的祖先可以追溯到古罗马时期，也就是第一位凯撒的时代。1915年，作为罗马大学数学系的一员，卡斯特尔努沃孤军奋战。他希望将概率论和精算数学课程引入研究生课程。当时，在安德烈·柯尔莫戈罗夫奠定概率论基础之前，数学家们将概率论视为一系列运用复杂计数技巧的方法。它只是数学中一个有趣的分支，通常作为代数课程的一部分进行教授，但在纯数学那些优美而闪耀的抽象概念被系统化的时候，几乎不值得在研究生课程中加以考虑。就精算数学而言，这简直是应用数学中最糟糕的部分——用相对简单的算术计算寿命和事故发生率。至少其他教员是这么认为的。

Guido Castelnuovo came from a proud Italian Jewish family. They could trace their ancestry back to ancient Rome during the time of the first Caesars. As a member of the faculty of mathematics at the University of Rome in 1915, Castelnuovo was fighting a lonely battle. He wanted to introduce courses in probability and the mathematics of actuarial studies into the graduate program. At that time, before Andrei Kolmogorov had laid the foundations of probability theory, mathematicians viewed probability as a collection of methods that made use of complicated counting techniques. It was an interesting sidelight of mathematics, often taught as part of an algebra course, but hardly worth consideration in a graduate program at a time when the beautiful shimmering abstractions of pure mathematics were being codified. As far as actuarial mathematics were concerned, this was applied mathematics at its worst, calculations of life spans and accident frequencies computed with relatively simple arithmetic. So the other members of the faculty believed.

除了在代数几何这一抽象领域做出的开创性工作外，卡斯特尔努沃对数学的应用也抱有浓厚的兴趣，并说服了其他教员允许他开设一门应用数学课程。由于这门课程的开设，卡斯特尔努沃出版了最早的概率论及其统计应用教材之一——《概率论及其应用》（Calcolo della probabilità e applicazioni），该书于1919年出版。这本书被意大利其他大学开设的类似课程所采用。到1927年，卡斯特尔努沃在罗马大学创立了统计与精算科学学院。在整个20世纪20年代和30年代，意大利日益壮大的精算研究统计学家群体与瑞典的类似群体之间保持着活跃的交流。

In addition to his pioneering work in the abstract field of algebraic geometry, Castelnuovo had a strong interest in applications of mathematics, and he persuaded the rest of the faculty to allow him to construct such a course. As a result of teaching this course, Castelnuovo published one of the first textbooks on probability with statistical applications, Calcolo della probabilità e applicazioni, published in 1919. This book was used in similar courses that were presented at other universities in Italy. By 1927, Castelnuovo had founded the School of Statistics and Actuarial Sciences at the University of Rome, and throughout the 1920s and 1930s there were lively exchanges between the growing Italian school of statisticians involved in actuarial research and a similar group in Sweden.

1922年，贝尼托·墨索里尼将法西斯主义带到意大利，并对言论自由实施了严格的控制。大学里的学生和教职员工都要接受审查，以排除“国家敌人”。这些审查并不涉及种族因素，卡斯特尔努沃的犹太人身份也未被考虑在内。[39]在法西斯政府执政的最初十一年里，他得以继续工作。1935年，意大利法西斯与德国纳粹达成协议，导致意大利颁布了反犹太法律，70岁的圭多·卡斯特尔努沃被解雇。

In 1922, Benito Mussolini had brought fascism to Italy and imposed rigid controls on freedom of expression. Both students and faculty at universities were examined to exclude “enemies of the state.” There were no racial components to those exclusions, and the fact that Castelnuovo was Jewish was not under consideration. [39] He was able to continue his work for the first eleven years of the fascist government. In 1935, the pact between the Italian fascists and the German Nazis led to the imposition of anti-Semitic laws in Italy, and seventy-year-old Guido Castelnuovo was dismissed from his post.

但这并未终结这位不知疲倦的学者的职业生涯，他一直活到1952年。随着纳粹种族法律的颁布，许多前途无量的犹太研究生也被大学开除。卡斯特尔努沃在自己家中以及其他犹太裔前教授家中组织特别课程，帮助这些研究生继续学业。除了撰写数学史方面的著作外，卡斯特尔努沃在其87年人生的最后阶段，致力于研究决定论与偶然性之间的哲学关系，并试图诠释因果关系的概念——这些主题我们在前几章中已经有所提及，我将在本书最后一章中进一步探讨。

That did not end the career of this tireless man, who lived until 1952. With the coming of Nazi-inspired racial laws, many promising Jewish graduate students were also dismissed from the universities. Castelnuovo organized special courses in his home, and in the homes of other Jewish former professors, to enable the graduate students to continue their studies. In addition to writing books on the history of mathematics, Castelnuovo spent the last of his eighty-seven years examining the philosophical relationships between determinism and chance and trying to interpret the concept of cause and effect—topics that we have touched upon in previous chapters and that I shall examine further in the final chapter of this book.

卡斯特尔努沃开创的意大利统计学学派拥有坚实的数学基础，但其大部分研究都以实际应用问题为出发点。卡斯特尔努沃的同代人，年轻的科拉多·吉尼，领导着位于罗马的中央统计研究所（Istituto Centrale di Statistica），这是一个由保险公司组织成立的私人机构，旨在推进精算研究。吉尼对各种应用领域的浓厚兴趣，使他与20世纪30年代从事数理统计的大多数意大利青年数学家建立了联系。

The Italian school of statistics that emerged from Castelnuovo’s efforts had solid mathematical foundations but used problems from actual applications as the starting point of most investigations. Castelnuovo’s younger contemporary, Corrado Gini, headed the Istituto Centrale di Statistica in Rome, a private institution organized by the insurance companies to further actuarial research. Gini’s lively interest in all kinds of applications brought him in touch with most of the young Italian mathematicians involved in mathematical statistics during the 1930s.

格利文科-坎泰利引理

THE GLIVENKO-CANTELLI LEMMA

这些数学家中有一位是弗朗切斯科·保罗·坎泰利（1875-1966），他在建立概率论基础方面几乎比柯尔莫哥洛夫更早。坎泰利对探究基础问题（例如“概率的意义是什么？”）并不那么感兴趣，他也没有像柯尔莫哥洛夫那样深入研究其底层理论。坎泰利满足于基于自18世纪亚伯拉罕·棣莫弗将微积分方法引入概率计算以来就存在的概率计算方法，推导出基本的数学定理。1916年，坎泰利发现了后来被称为数理统计基本定理的定理。尽管它意义重大，但它却有一个朴素的名字——“格利文科-坎泰利引理”。[40]坎泰利是第一个证明该定理的人，并且他完全理解它的重要性。约瑟夫·格利文科是柯尔莫戈罗夫的学生，他之所以获得部分认可，是因为他在 1933 年发表于意大利数学期刊的一篇论文中，运用了一种新开发的数学符号——“斯蒂尔杰斯积分”——推广了柯尔莫戈罗夫的结论。格利文科的符号是现代教科书中使用最广泛的符号。

One of these mathematicians was Francesco Paolo Cantelli (1875-1966), who almost anticipated Kolmogorov in establishing the foundations of probability theory. Cantelli was not that interested in investigating foundation issues (dealing with questions like, What is the meaning of probability?), and he failed to plunge as deeply into the underlying theory as Kolmogorov. Cantelli was content to derive fundamental mathematical theorems based on the type of probability calculations that had been around since Abraham de Moivre introduced the methods of calculus into probability calculations in the eighteenth century. In 1916, Cantelli discovered what has been called the fundamental theorem of mathematical statistics. In spite of its great importance, it goes by the unassuming name of the “Glivenko-Cantelli lemma.”[40] Cantelli was the first to prove the theorem, and he fully understood its importance. Joseph Glivenko, a student of Kolmogorov, gets partial credit because he made use of a newly developed mathematical notation known as the “Stieltjes integral” to generalize the result in a paper he published (in an Italian mathematical journal) in 1933. Glivenko’s notation is the one used most often in modern textbooks.

格利文科-坎泰利引理是那种乍看之下似乎显而易见，但实际上却并非如此的结果。如果对生成一组数据的潜在概率分布一无所知，那么数据本身就可以用来构建一个非参数分布。这是一个丑陋的数学函数，充满了不连续点，缺乏任何优雅之处。但尽管结构笨拙，坎泰利仍然能够证明，随着观测数量的增加，这个丑陋的经验分布函数会越来越接近真实的分布函数。

The Glivenko-Cantelli lemma is one of those results that appears to be intuitively obvious, but only after it was discovered. If nothing is known about the underlying probability distribution that generated a set of data, the data themselves can be used to construct a nonparametric distribution. This is an ugly mathematical function, filled with discontinuities and lacking in any kind of elegance. But in spite of its awkward structure, Cantelli was able to show that this ugly empirical distribution function got closer and closer to the true distribution function as the number of observations increased.

格利文科-坎泰利引理的重要性很快就被人们所认识，在接下来的二十年里，许多重要的定理都是通过反复应用该引理而证明的。它是数学研究中几乎总能在证明中用到的工具之一。为了运用该引理，本世纪上半叶的数学家们必须巧妙地运用计数技巧。构建经验分布函数只需一系列简单的算术步骤。如果没有这些巧妙的技巧，要利用经验分布函数从大量数据样本中估计参数，就需要一台每秒能进行数百万次运算的巨型计算机。在20世纪50年代、60年代，甚至70年代，这样的机器都不存在。到了80年代，计算机的发展才使之成为可能。格利文科-坎泰利引理成为一种新型统计技术的基础，而这种技术只能在高速计算机时代才能存在。

The importance of the Glivenko-Cantelli lemma was recognized immediately, and throughout the next twenty years, many important theorems were proven by reducing them to repeated applications of this lemma. It was one of those tools of mathematical research that can almost always be used in a proof. To use the lemma, mathematicians during the first part of the century had to produce clever manipulations of counting techniques. Construction of an empirical distribution function consists of a sequence of mindless steps in simple arithmetic. Without clever tricks, use of the empirical distribution function in estimating parameters from large samples of data would take a fantastic mechanical computer capable of millions of operations a second. No such machine existed in the 1950s, or in the 1960s, or even in the 1970s. By the 1980s, computers had reached the point where this was feasible. The Glivenko-Cantelli lemma became the basis of a new statistical technique that could only exist in a world of high-speed computers.

埃夫隆的“自力更生”

EFRON’S “BOOTSTRAP”

1982年，斯坦福大学的布拉德利·埃夫隆发明了“自助法”。它基于格利文科-坎泰利引理的两个简单应用。这些应用在概念上很简单，但却需要大量使用计算机进行计算、反复计算。即使使用最强大的计算机，对中等规模的数据集进行典型的自助分析也可能需要几分钟时间。

In 1982, Bradley Efron of Stanford University invented “the bootstrap.” It is based on two simple applications of the Glivenko-Cantelli lemma. The applications are simple conceptually, but they require extensive use of the computer to calculate, recalculate, and recalculate again. A typical bootstrap analysis on a moderately large set of data might take several minutes even with the most powerful computer.

埃夫隆将这种方法称为“自举法”，因为它相当于数据通过自身的“自举”过程提升到更高的层次。这种方法之所以有效，是因为计算机并不介意进行机械的重复运算。它会一遍又一遍地重复同样的操作，而不会抱怨。使用现代晶体管芯片，只需几微秒即可完成。埃夫隆的自举法背后蕴含着一些复杂的数学原理。他最初的论文证明，如果对真实的底层分布做出某些假设，那么这种方法等价于标准方法。这种方法的影响如此广泛，以至于自1982年以来，几乎每一期数理统计期刊都刊登了一篇或多篇涉及自举法的文章。

Efron called this procedure the bootstrap because it was a case of the data lifting themselves up by their own bootstraps, so to speak. It works because the computer does not mind doing mindless, repetitive arithmetic. It will do the same thing over and over and over again and never complain. With the modern transistor-based chip, it will do so in a few microseconds. There are some complicated mathematics behind Efron’s bootstrap. His original papers proved that this method is equivalent to the standard methods if certain assumptions are made about the true underlying distribution. The implications of this method have been so extensive that almost every issue of the mathematical statistical journals since 1982 has contained one or more articles involving the bootstrap.

重采样和其他计算密集型方法

RESAMPLING AND OTHER COMPUTER-INTENSIVE METHODS

除了自助法之外，还有其他一些相关方法，它们统称为重采样。事实上，埃夫隆已经证明，费舍尔的许多标准统计方法都可以看作是重采样的一种形式。而重采样本身又是更广泛的统计方法的一部分，这些方法被称为“计算机密集型”方法。计算机密集型方法利用现代计算机强大的计算能力，反复处理相同的数据，从而实现海量计算。

There are alternative versions of the bootstrap and related methods, all of which go under the general name of resampling. In fact, Efron has shown that many of R. A. Fisher’s standard statistical methods can be viewed as forms of resampling. In turn, resampling is part of a wider range of statistical methods, which are called “computer intensive.” Computer-intensive methods make use of the ability of the modern computer to engage in vast amounts of calculations, working the same data over and over again.

其中一种方法是由美国国家标准局的琼·罗森布拉特和爱荷华州立大学的伊曼纽尔·帕岑在20世纪60年代各自独立开发的。他们的方法被称为“核密度估计”。核密度估计又衍生出了基于核密度的回归估计。这些方法包含两个任意参数，分别称为“核”和“带宽”。这些思想出现后不久，在1967年（远在计算机性能足以充分利用这些方法之前），哥伦比亚大学的约翰·范·雷津利用格利文科-坎泰利引理确定了这些参数的最优配置。

One such procedure was developed during the 1960s by Joan Rosenblatt at the National Bureau of Standards and by Emmanuel Parzen at Iowa State University, working independently of one another. Their methods are known as “kernel density estimation.” Kernel density estimation, in turn, led to kernel density-based regression estimation. These methods include two arbitrary parameters, called the “kernel” and the “bandwidth.” Soon after these ideas appeared, in 1967 (long before there were computers powerful enough to take full advantage of them), John van Ryzin of Columbia University used the Glivenko-Cantelli lemma to determine the optimal configuration of these parameters.

当数理统计学家们忙于构建理论并在期刊上发表文章时，罗森布拉特和帕尔岑的基于核密度的回归方法已被工程界独立发现。在计算机工程师中，它被称为“模糊逼近”。它使用范·雷津所说的“非最优核”，并且带宽的选择非常随意。工程实践并非基于寻求理论上的最佳方法，而是基于实际可行的方法。当理论家们还在纠结于抽象的最优性准则时，工程师们已经在现实世界中运用模糊逼近方法构建计算机模糊系统。模糊工程系统被应用于智能相机，它可以自动调节焦距和光圈。它们也被应用于新建建筑中，以维持恒定舒适的温度，而不同房间的温度可能因需求而异。

While the mathematical statisticians were generating theories and writing in their own journals, Rosenblatt’s and Parzen’s kernel density-based regression was discovered independently by the engineering community. Among computer engineers, it is called “fuzzy approximation.” It uses what van Ryzin would have called a “nonoptimal kernel” and there is only one, very arbitrary, choice of bandwidth. Engineering practice is not based on seeking the theoretically best possible method. Engineering practice is based on what will work. While the theoreticians were worrying about abstract optimality criteria, the engineers were out in the real world, using fuzzy approximations to produce computer-based fuzzy systems. Fuzzy engineering systems are used in smart cameras, which automatically adjust the focus and iris. They are also used in new buildings to maintain constant and comfortable temperatures, which may vary according to different needs in different rooms.

巴特·科斯科是一位工程领域的私人顾问，也是模糊系统领域最多产的推广者之一。查阅他著作的参考文献，我发现其中提到了19世纪的主流数学家，例如戈特弗里德·威廉·冯·莱布尼茨，以及对随机过程理论及其在工程方法中的应用做出贡献的数理统计学家诺伯特·维纳。然而，我却找不到罗森布拉特、帕岑、范·雷津，以及任何后来对基于核的回归理论做出贡献的学者。模糊系统和基于核密度的回归几乎都采用了完全相同的计算机算法，但它们似乎是完全独立发展起来的。

Bart Kosko, a private consultant in engineering, is one of the most prolific popularizers of fuzzy systems. As I look over the bibliographies in his books, I can find references to mainstream mathematicians of the nineteenth century like Gottfried Wilhelm von Leibniz, along with references to the mathematical statistician Norbert Wiener, who contributed to the theory of stochastic processes and their applications to engineering methods. I cannot find references to Rosenblatt, Parzen, van Ryzin, or any of the later contributors to the theory of kernel-based regression. Coming up with almost exactly the same computer algorithms, fuzzy systems and kernel density-based regression appear to have been developed completely independently of one another.

统计模型的胜利

THE TRIUMPH OF STATISTICAL MODELS

将计算机密集型统计方法扩展到标准工程实践中，正是20世纪末科学统计革命如何变得如此普遍的一个例证。数理统计学家不再是其发展的唯一参与者，甚至不再是最重要的参与者。过去70年来，他们期刊上发表的许多精妙理论，即便如此，仍然被科学家和工程师所使用，而这些科学家和工程师却对此一无所知。最重要的定理往往会被反复重新发现。[41]

This extension of computer-intensive statistical methods to standard engineering practice is an example of how the statistical revolution in science had become so ubiquitous by the end of the twentieth century. The mathematical statisticians are no longer the sole or even the most important participants in its development. Many of the fine theories that have appeared in their journals over the past seventy years are unknown to the scientists and engineers who, even so, make use of them. The most important theorems are often rediscovered, again and again.[41]

有时，一些基本定理甚至未经重新证明，但用户却想当然地认为它们是正确的，因为它们似乎符合直觉。在少数情况下，用户甚至会引用已被证明是错误的定理——同样，也是因为它们似乎符合直觉。这是因为概率分布的概念已经深深植根于现代科学教育中，以至于科学家和工程师们习惯于用分布的视角思考问题。一百多年前，卡尔·皮尔逊提出，所有观测结果都源于概率分布，而科学的目的就是估计这些分布的参数。在此之前，科学界普遍认为宇宙遵循着诸如牛顿运动定律之类的规律，任何观测结果的明显偏差都源于误差。渐渐地，皮尔逊的观点成为了主流。因此，任何在二十世纪接受过科学方法训练的人都会理所当然地接受皮尔逊的观点。它已经深深地融入现代数据分析的科学方法中，以至于很少有人会费心去阐述它。许多从事科研工作的科学家和工程师在使用这些技术时，从未思考过这种观点的哲学含义。

Sometimes the basic theorems are not even re-proved, but the users assume them to be true because they seem intuitively true. In a few cases, the users invoke theorems that have been proved to be false—again, because they seem intuitively true. This is because the concepts of probability distributions have become so ingrained in modern scientific education that the scientists and engineers think in terms of distributions. Over a hundred years ago, Karl Pearson proposed that all observations arise from probability distributions and that the purpose of science is to estimate the parameters of those distributions. Before that, the world of science believed that the universe followed laws, like Newton’s laws of motion, and that any apparent variations in what was observed were due to errors. Gradually, Pearson’s view has become the predominant one. As a result, anyone trained in scientific methods in the twentieth century takes the Pearsonian view for granted. It is so ingrained in modern scientific methods of data analysis that few even bother to articulate it. Many working scientists and engineers use these techniques without ever thinking about the philosophical implications of this view.

然而，随着概率分布才是科学真正研究对象这一概念的传播，哲学家和数学家们也发现了一些严重的根本性问题。我在前几章中已经略微探讨过其中一些问题。下一章将专门讨论这些问题。

Yet, as the concept has spread that probability distributions are the true “things” that science investigates, the philosophers and mathematicians have uncovered serious fundamental problems. I have examined some of these in passing in previous chapters. The next chapter is devoted to those problems.

C第29章

CHAPTER 29

拥有泥足的偶像

THE IDOL WITH FEET OF CLAY

我1962年，芝加哥大学的托马斯·库恩出版了《科学革命的结构》。这本书对哲学家和实践者看待科学的方式产生了深远的影响。库恩指出，现实极其复杂，永远无法用一个系统化的科学模型完全描述。他提出，科学倾向于构建一个看似符合现有数据且有助于预测新实验结果的现实模型。由于任何模型都不可能完全正确，数据的积累开始要求对模型进行修正，以适应新的发现。模型变得越来越复杂，出现了特殊的例外情况和一些直觉上难以理解的扩展。最终，模型将不再具有实用价值。届时，具有创新精神的思想家将提出一个完全不同的模型，从而引发科学革命。

In 1962, Thomas Kuhn of the University of Chicago published The Structure of Scientific Revolutions. This book has had a profound influence on the way in which science is viewed by both philosophers and practitioners. Kuhn noted that reality is exceedingly complicated and can never be completely described by an organized scientific model. He proposed that science tends to produce a model of reality that appears to fit the data available and is useful for predicting the results of new experiments. Since no model can be completely true, the accumulation of data begins to require modifications of the model to correct it for new discoveries. The model becomes more and more complicated, with special exceptions and intuitively implausible extensions. Eventually, the model can no longer serve a useful purpose. At that point, original thinkers will emerge with an entirely different model, creating a revolution in science.

统计革命正是这种模型交流的一个例证。在十九世纪科学的决定论观点中，牛顿物理学有效地描述了行星、卫星、小行星和彗星的运动——所有这些都基于几条明确的运动定律和万有引力定律。人们在寻找化学定律方面取得了一些成功，达尔文的自然选择定律似乎为理解进化论提供了一个有用的起点。人们甚至尝试将对科学定律的探索扩展到社会学、政治学和心理学领域。当时人们认为，寻找这些定律的主要问题在于测量的不精确性。

The statistical revolution was an example of this exchange of models. In the deterministic view of nineteenth-century science, Newtonian physics had effectively described the motion of planets, moons, asteroids, and comets—all of it based on a few well-defined laws of motion and gravity. Some success had been achieved in finding laws of chemistry, and Darwin’s law of natural selection appeared to provide a useful start to understanding evolution. Attempts were even made to extend the search for scientific laws into the realms of sociology, political science, and psychology. It was believed at the time that the major problem with finding these laws lay in the imprecision of the measurements.

天体运行仪，一种用来显示行星绕太阳运行的机械装置

An orrery, a clockwork mechanism designed to show the movement of the planets about the Sun

十九世纪初的数学家皮埃尔·西蒙·拉普拉斯等人提出，天文测量会存在微小误差，这些误差可能是由于大气条件或观测者自身的局限性造成的。他提出这些误差服从概率分布，从而开启了统计革命的大门。用托马斯·库恩的话来说，这是对“机械宇宙”理论的一种修正，而修正的必要性源于新的数据。十九世纪比利时博学家兰伯特·阿道夫·雅克·凯特莱预见了统计革命的到来，他提出人类行为规律本质上是概率性的。然而，他没有卡尔·皮尔逊的多参数方法，也没有意识到最优估计方法的重要性，因此他的模型显得非常简单。

Mathematicians like Pierre Simon Laplace of the early nineteenth century developed the idea that astronomical measurements involve slight errors, possibly due to atmospheric conditions or to the human fallibility of the observers. He opened the door to the statistical revolution by proposing that these errors would have a probability distribution. To use Thomas Kuhn’s view, this was a modification of the clockwork universe made necessary by new data. The nineteenth-century Belgian polymath Lambert Adolphe Jacques Quételet anticipated the statistical revolution by proposing that the laws of human behavior were probabilistic in nature. He did not have Karl Pearson’s multiple parameter approach and was unaware of the need for optimum estimation methods, and his models were exceedingly naive.

最终，决定论的科学方法崩溃了，因为随着测量精度的提高，模型预测与实际观测结果之间的差异越来越大。拉普拉斯认为误差会干扰对行星真实运动的观测，但更精确的测量结果非但没有消除这些误差，反而显示出越来越多的变化。至此，科学界迎来了卡尔·皮尔逊及其参数分布。

Eventually, the deterministic approach to science collapsed because the differences between what the models predicted and what was actually observed grew greater with more precise measurements. Instead of eliminating the errors that Laplace thought were interfering with the ability to observe the true motion of the planets, more precise measurements showed more and more variation. At this point, science was ready for Karl Pearson and his distributions with parameters.

本书前几章已阐述了皮尔逊的统计学革命如何主导了整个现代科学。尽管分子生物学表面上看似具有决定论的特征——基因决定细胞产生特定蛋白质——但实际上，该学科的数据充满了随机性，基因实际上只是这些结果分布的参数。现代药物对身体机能的影响，例如1或2毫克的剂量就能引起血压或精神神经症状的显著变化，看起来似乎非常精确。但证明这些作用的药理学研究的设计和分析都基于概率分布，而这些作用本身也只是这些分布的参数。

The preceding chapters of this book have shown how Pearson’s statistical revolution has come to dominate all of modern science. In spite of the apparent determinism of molecular biology, where genes are found that cause cells to generate specific proteins, the actual data from this science are filled with randomness and the genes are, in fact, parameters of the distribution of those results. The effects of modern drugs on bodily functions, where doses of 1 or 2 milligrams cause profound changes in blood pressure or psychic neuroses, seem to be exact. But the pharmacological studies that prove these effects are designed and analyzed in terms of probability distributions, and the effects are parameters of those distributions.

同样，计量经济学的统计方法被用来模拟一个国家或一家企业的经济活动。我们理所当然地认为的电子和质子等亚原子粒子，在量子力学中被描述为概率分布。社会学家通过对不同人群的平均值进行加权求和来描述个体间的互动——但这些描述也仅仅基于概率分布。在许多此类科学领域，统计模型的使用已成为其方法论的重要组成部分，以至于人们谈论分布的参数时，仿佛它们是真实可测量的实体。这些科学的起点——那些不断变化且充满不确定性的测量数据——被淹没在计算之中，而最终的结论却以永远无法直接观测的参数来表述。

Similarly, the statistical methods of econometrics are used to model the economic activity of a nation or of a firm. The subatomic particles we confidently think of as electrons and protons are described in quantum mechanics as probability distributions. Sociologists derive weighted sums of averages taken across populations to describe the interactions of individuals—but only in terms of probability distributions. In many of these sciences, the use of statistical models is so much a part of their methodology that the parameters of the distributions are spoken of as if they were real, measurable things. The uncertain conglomeration of shifting and changing measurements that are the starting point of these sciences is submerged in the calculations, and the conclusions are stated in terms of parameters that can never be directly observed.

统计学家们失去了控制

THE STATISTICIANS LOSE CONTROL

统计学革命已深深融入现代科学，以至于统计学家们对这一进程失去了控制。分子遗传学家的概率计算发展独立于数理统计学文献。信息科学这门新兴学科的出现，源于计算机积累海量数据的能力以及从这些庞大信息库中提取意义的需求。信息科学新期刊的文章很少提及数理统计学家的工作，许多多年前在《生物统计学》（Biometrika）或《数理统计年鉴》（Annals of Mathematical Statistics）上探讨过的分析技术如今正被重新发现。统计模型在公共政策问题上的应用催生了一门名为“风险分析”的新学科，而风险分析新期刊也往往忽略数理统计学家的工作。

So ingrained in modern science is the statistical revolution that the statisticians have lost control of the process. The probability calculations of the molecular geneticists have been developed independently of the mathematical statistical literature. The new discipline of information science has emerged from the ability of the computer to accumulate large amounts of data and the need to make sense out of those huge libraries of information. Articles in the new journals of information science seldom refer to the work of the mathematical statisticians, and many of the techniques of analysis that were examined years ago in Biometrika or the Annals of Mathematical Statistics are being rediscovered. The applications of statistical models to questions of public policy have spawned a new discipline called “risk analysis,” and the new journals of risk analysis also tend to ignore the work of the mathematical statisticians.

现在几乎所有学科的科学期刊都要求结果表格包含与结论相关的统计不确定性的衡量标准，并且大学在这些科学的研究生课程中教授标准统计分析方法，通常不会涉及这些大学可能存在的统计系。

Scientific journals in almost all disciplines now require that the tables of results contain some measure of the statistical uncertainty associated with the conclusions, and standard methods of statistical analysis are taught in universities as part of the graduate courses in these sciences, usually without involving the statistics departments that may exist at these same universities.

自卡尔·皮尔逊发现偏态分布以来的一百多年里，统计学革命不仅扩展到了科学的绝大部分领域，而且其许多理念也渗透到了大众文化中。当电视新闻主播宣布一项医学研究表明，被动吸烟会使非吸烟者的死亡风险“翻倍”时，几乎所有听众都认为自己明白这意味着什么。当民意调查显示65%的公众认为总统工作表现良好（误差在正负3%以内）时，我们大多数人都认为自己理解这65%和这3%的含义。当天气预报员预测明天有95%的降雨概率时，我们大多数人都会带上雨伞。

In the more than a hundred years since Karl Pearson’s discovery of the skew distributions, the statistical revolution has not only been extended to most of science, but many of its ideas have spread into the general culture. When the television news anchor announces that a medical study has shown that passive smoking “doubles the risk of death” among nonsmokers, almost everyone who listens thinks he or she knows what that means. When a public opinion poll is used to declare that 65 percent of the public think the president is doing a good job, plus or minus 3 percent, most of us think we understand both the 65 percent and the 3 percent. When the weatherman predicts a 95 percent chance of rain tomorrow, most of us will take along an umbrella.

统计学革命对大众思想和文化的影响远不止于我们随意使用概率和比例，仿佛我们真的理解它们的含义。我们接受基于参数估计的科学研究结论，即便实际测量结果与这些结论并不完全吻合。我们愿意利用海量数据的平均值来制定公共政策和规划个人生活。我们理所当然地认为，收集出生和死亡数据不仅是正确的程序，而且是必要的程序，我们丝毫不担心统计人口会触怒神灵。在语言层面，我们使用“相关性”和“相关”这样的词语，仿佛它们具有某种意义，仿佛我们自认为理解它们的含义。

The statistical revolution has had an even more subtle influence on popular thought and culture than just on the way we bandy probabilities and proportions about as if we knew what they meant. We accept the conclusions of scientific investigations based upon estimates of parameters, even if none of the actual measurements agrees exactly with those conclusions. We are willing to make public policy and organize our personal plans using averages of masses of data. We take for granted that assembling data on deaths and births is not only a proper procedure but a necessary one, and we have no fear of angering the gods by counting people. On the level of language, we use the words correlation and correlated as if they mean something, and as if we think we know their meaning.

本书旨在向非数学专业人士解释这场革命。我尝试阐述这场革命背后的核心思想，它如何被应用于众多科学领域，最终又如何主导了几乎所有科学领域。我力求用通俗易懂的语言和实例来解读一些数学模型，避免读者陷入抽象的数学符号体系。

This book has been an attempt to explain to the nonmathematician something about this revolution. I have tried to describe the essential ideas behind the revolution, how it came to be adopted in a number of different areas of science, how it eventually came to dominate almost all of science. I have tried to interpret some of the mathematical models with words and examples that can be understood without having to climb the heights of abstract mathematical symbolism.

统计学革命是否已经结束了？

HAS THE STATISTICAL REVOLUTION RUN ITS COURSE?

“外部世界”是一个极其复杂的感觉、事件和动荡交织的集合体。我赞同托马斯·库恩的观点，认为人类的思维无法构建出一个能够接近描述“外部世界”真实面貌的观念体系。任何试图这样做的尝试都存在根本性的缺陷。最终，这些缺陷会变得如此显而易见，以至于科学模型必须不断修正，并最终被一种更为精妙的模型所取代。我们可以预见，统计学革命终将走向终结，并被其他理论所取代。

The world “out there” is an exceedingly complicated mass of sensations, events, and turmoil. With Thomas Kuhn, I do not believe that the human mind is capable of organizing a structure of ideas that can come even close to describing what is really out there. Any attempt to do so contains fundamental faults. Eventually, those faults will become so obvious that the scientific model must be continuously modified and eventually discarded in favor of a more subtle one. We can expect the statistical revolution will eventually run its course and be replaced by something else.

本书结尾，我理应探讨一下随着统计方法在人类活动的各个领域不断拓展而涌现出的哲学问题。接下来，我们将开启一段哲学探索之旅。读者或许会疑惑，哲学与科学和现实生活有何关联？我的答案是，哲学并非一群被称为哲学家的怪人所从事的晦涩难懂的学术活动。哲学探究的是我们日常文化观念和活动背后的潜在假设。我们从文化中习得的世界观，正是由一些微妙的假设所支配的。而我们当中鲜有人意识到这些假设的存在。哲学研究使我们能够揭示这些假设，并检验它们的有效性。

It is only fair that I end this book with some discussion of the philosophical problems that have emerged as statistical methods have been extended into more and more areas of human endeavor. What follows will be an adventure in philosophy. The reader may wonder what philosophy has to do with science and real life. My answer is that philosophy is not some arcane academic exercise done by strange people called philosophers. Philosophy looks at the underlying assumptions behind our day-to-day cultural ideas and activities. Our worldview, which we learn from our culture, is governed by subtle assumptions. Few of us are even aware of them. The study of philosophy allows us to uncover these assumptions and examine their validity.

我曾在康涅狄格学院数学系教过一门课。这门课有个正式名称，但系里的同学都戏称它为“诗人的数学”。这门课为期一个学期，旨在让文科专业的学生了解数学的基本概念。学期伊始，我向学生们介绍了十六世纪意大利数学家吉罗拉莫·卡尔达诺的著作《大艺术》（Ars Magna） 。《大艺术》首次公开描述了新兴的代数方法。卡尔达诺在引言中呼应了书中的观点，认为这种代数方法并非新颖。他暗示自己并非无知愚昧之人。他深知自人类堕落以来，知识一直在衰退，而亚里士多德的知识远胜于他那个时代的任何人。他明白，不可能存在全新的知识。然而，由于他的无知，他未能从亚里士多德的著作中找到某个特定观点的出处，因此他向读者提出了这个看似新颖的观点。他确信，一些更有学识的读者会在古代著作中找到这个看似新颖的观点。

I once taught a course in the mathematics department of Connecticut College. The course had a formal name, but the department members referred to it as “math for poets.” It was designed as a one-semester course to acquaint liberal arts majors with the essential ideas of mathematics. Early in the semester, I introduced the students to the Ars Magna of Girolamo Cardano, a sixteenth-century Italian mathematician. The Ars Magna contains the first published description of the emerging methods of algebra. Echoing his tome, Cardano writes in his introduction that this algebra is not new. He is no ignorant fool, he implies. He is aware that ever since the fall of man, knowledge has been decreasing and that Aristotle knew far more than anyone living at Cardano’s time. He is aware that there can be no new knowledge. In his ignorance, however, he has been unable to find reference to a particular idea in Aristotle, and so he presents his readers with this idea, which appears to be new. He is sure that some more knowledgeable reader will locate where, among the writings of the ancients, this idea that appears to be new can actually be found.

我的学生们成长于一种不仅相信可以发现新事物，而且鼓励创新的文化氛围中，他们听到我的文章后都感到震惊。这简直是愚蠢至极！我向他们指出，十六世纪欧洲人的世界观受到一些基本哲学假设的制约。他们世界观的重要组成部分是人类的堕落以及随之而来的世界的持续衰败——道德的衰败、知识的衰败、工业的衰败，以及万物的衰败。这被认为是真理，以至于人们很少会公开表达出来。

The students in my class, raised in a cultural milieu that not only believes new things can be found but that actually encourages innovation, were shocked. What a stupid thing to write! I pointed out to them that in the sixteenth century, the worldview of Europeans was constrained by fundamental philosophical assumptions. An important part of their worldview was the idea of the fall of man and the subsequent continual deterioration of the world—of morals, of knowledge, of industry, of all things. This was known to be true, so true that it was seldom even articulated.

我问学生们，他们世界观中哪些基本假设在500年后的学生看来可能会显得荒谬可笑。他们一个也想不出来。

I asked the students what underlying assumptions of their worldview might possibly seem ridiculous to students 500 years in the future. They could think of none.

随着统计革命的表面思想在现代文化中传播开来，越来越多的人不加思考地相信其真理，而不去思考其潜在的假设，让我们来思考一下统计宇宙观存在的三个哲学问题：

As the surface ideas of the statistical revolution spread through modern culture, as more and more people believe its truths without thinking about its underlying assumptions, let us consider three philosophical problems with the statistical view of the universe:

  1. 统计模型能否用于决策？
  2. Can statistical models be used to make decisions?
  3. 概率在现实生活中意味着什么？
  4. What is the meaning of probability when applied to real life?
  5. 人们真的理解概率吗？
  6. Do people really understand probability?

统计模型能否用于决策？

CAN STATISTICAL MODELS BE USED TO MAKE DECISIONS?

牛津大学的L·乔纳森·科恩一直以来都对所谓的“帕斯卡式”观点持尖锐的批评态度，他所指的“帕斯卡式”观点是指用统计分布来描述现实。在他1989年出版的《归纳与概率哲学导论》一书中，他提出了彩票悖论，并将此悖论归功于康涅狄格州米德尔敦卫斯理大学的西摩·凯伯格。

L. Jonathan Cohen of Oxford University has been a trenchant critic of what he calls the “Pascalian” view, by which he means the use of statistical distributions to describe reality. In his 1989 book, An Introduction to the Philosophy of Induction and Probability, he proposes the paradox of the lottery, which he attributes to Seymour Kyberg of Wesleyan University in Middletown, Connecticut.

假设我们接受假设检验或显著性检验的概念。我们同意，如果与某个假设相关的概率非常小，我们可以拒绝该假设。具体来说，我们把 0.0001 设定为一个非常小的概率。现在，我们组织一场公平的彩票活动，发行 10,000 张编号彩票。考虑“1 号彩票会中奖”的假设。它的概率是 0.0001。我们拒绝这个假设。考虑“2 号彩票会中奖”的假设。我们也可以拒绝这个假设。对于任何特定的编号彩票，我们都可以拒绝类似的假设。根据逻辑规则，如果 A 不成立，B 不成立，C 也不成立，那么（A 或 B 或 C）也不成立。也就是说，根据逻辑规则，如果每张特定的彩票都不会中奖，那么就没有彩票会中奖。

Suppose we accept the ideas of hypothesis or significance testing. We agree that we can decide to reject a hypothesis about reality if the probability associated with that hypothesis is very small. To be specific, let’s set 0.0001 as a very small probability. Let’s now organize a fair lottery with 10,000 numbered tickets. Consider the hypothesis that ticket number 1 will win the lottery. The probability of that is 0.0001. We reject that hypothesis. Consider the hypothesis that ticket number 2 will win the lottery. We can also reject that hypothesis. We can reject similar hypotheses for any specific numbered ticket. Under the rules of logic, if A is not true, and B is not true, and C is not true, then (A or B or C) is not true. That is, under the rules of logic, if every specific ticket will not win the lottery, then no ticket will win the lottery.

在早前的著作《概率与可证明性》中， LJ·科恩基于普通法实践提出了这一悖论的一个变体。在普通法中，民事诉讼中，如果原告的主张基于“优势证据”看起来属实，则原告胜诉。法院普遍接受“优势证据”的含义是原告主张的概率大于50%。科恩提出了“擅闯者悖论”。假设在一个可容纳1000人的大厅里举办摇滚音乐会。主办方售出了499张门票，但音乐会开始时，所有1000个座位都已坐满。根据英国普通法，主办方有权向所有1000名观众收取费用，因为其中任何一人是擅闯者的概率为50.1%。因此，主办方将向1499名观众收取费用，而大厅只能容纳1000人。

In an earlier book, The Probable and the Provable, L. J. Cohen proposed a variant of this paradox based on common legal practice. In the common law, a plaintiff in a civil suit wins if his claim seems true on the basis of the “preponderance” of the evidence. This has been accepted by the courts to mean that the probability of the plaintiffs claim is greater than 50 percent. Cohen proposes the paradox of the gate-crashers. Suppose there is a rock concert in a hall with 1,000 seats. The promoter sells tickets for 499 of the seats, but when the concert starts, all 1,000 seats are filled. Under English common law, the promoter has the right to collect from each of the 1,000 persons at the concert, since the probability that any one of them is a gate-crasher is 50.1 percent. Thus, the promoter will collect money from 1,499 patrons for a hall that holds only 1,000.

这两个悖论都表明，基于概率论证的决策并非逻辑决策。逻辑与概率论证是相互矛盾的。费舍尔（R.A. Fisher）通过诉诸基于精心设计的实验的显著性检验来为科学中的归纳推理辩护。科恩悖论则表明，这种归纳推理是不合逻辑的。杰里·康菲尔德（Jerry Cornfield）通过大量证据来证明吸烟会导致肺癌，他列举了一系列研究，这些研究的结果除非假定吸烟是致癌因素，否则几乎不可能出现。那么，认为吸烟会导致癌症的观点是不合逻辑的吗？

What both paradoxes show is that decisions based on probabilistic arguments are not logical decisions. Logic and probabilistic arguments are incompatible. R. A. Fisher justified inductive reasoning in science by appealing to significance tests based on well-designed experiments. Cohen’s paradoxes suggest that such inductive reasoning is illogical. Jerry Cornfield justified the finding that smoking causes lung cancer by appealing to a piling up of evidence, where study after study shows results that are highly improbable unless you assume that smoking is the cause of the cancer. Is it illogical to believe that smoking causes cancer?

逻辑与基于统计的决策之间的这种不匹配，并非科恩悖论中某个错误假设就能解释的。它恰恰是逻辑本质的核心所在。（科恩提议用一种被称为“模态逻辑”的复杂数学逻辑来取代概率模型，但我认为这种方案弊大于利。）在逻辑中，真命题与假命题之间存在着清晰的界限。但概率论引入了某些命题可能为真或几乎为真的概念。这种不确定性阻碍了我们在处理因果关系时运用实质性蕴涵的精确性。针对临床研究中提出的一个解决方案是，将每项临床研究都视为提供关于特定治疗效果的信息。这些信息的价值可以通过对研究的统计分析来确定，也可以通过研究本身的质量来确定。研究质量这一额外指标被用来决定哪些研究将在结论中占据主导地位。研究质量的概念模糊不清，难以量化。这一悖论依然存在，并一直困扰着统计方法的核心。这种矛盾是否会在二十一世纪引发一场新的革命？

This lack of fit between logic and statistically based decisions is not something that can be accounted for by finding a faulty assumption in Cohen’s paradoxes. It lies at the heart of what is meant by logic. (Cohen proposes that probabilistic models be replaced by a sophisticated version of mathematical logic known as “modal logic,” but I think this solution introduces more problems than it solves.) In logic, there is a clear difference between a proposition that is true and one that is false. But probability introduces the idea that some propositions are probably or almost true. That little bit of resulting unsureness blocks our ability to apply the cold exactness of material implication in dealing with cause and effect. One of the solutions proposed for this problem in clinical research is to look upon each clinical study as providing some information about the effect of a given treatment. The value of that information can be determined by a statistical analysis of the study but also by the quality of the study. This extra measure, the quality of the study, is used to determine which studies will dominate in the conclusions. The concept of quality of a study is a vague one and not easily calculated. The paradox remains, eating at the heart of statistical methods. Will this worm of inconsistency require a new revolution in the twenty-first century?

概率在现实生活中意味着什么？

WHAT IS THE MEANING OF PROBABILITY WHEN APPLIED TO REAL LIFE?

安德烈·柯尔莫戈罗夫确立了概率的数学定义：概率是抽象事件空间中集合的度量。概率的所有数学性质都可以从这个定义推导而来。当我们想将概率应用于现实生活时，我们需要针对具体问题确定这个抽象事件空间。当天气预报员说明天下雨的概率是95%时，他所测量的抽象事件集合是什么？是明天所有出门的人的集合，其中95%的人会被淋湿吗？是所有可能的时间点的集合，其中95%的时间点我都会被淋湿吗？是给定区域内所有一平方英寸大小的土地的集合，其中95%的土地会被淋湿吗？当然，都不是。那么，它究竟是什么呢？

Andrei Kolmogorov established the mathematical meaning of probability: Probability is a measure of sets in an abstract space of events. All the mathematical properties of probability can be derived from this definition. When we wish to apply probability to real life, we need to identify that abstract space of events for the particular problem at hand. When the weather forecaster says that the probability of rain tomorrow is 95 percent, what is the set of abstract events being measured? Is it the set of all people who will go outside tomorrow, 95 percent of whom will get wet? Is it the set of all possible moments in time, 95 percent of which will find me getting wet? Is it the set of all one-inch-square pieces of land in a given region, 95 percent of which will get wet? Of course, it is none of those. What is it, then?

卡尔·皮尔逊早于柯尔莫哥洛夫，他认为只要收集大量数据就能观测到概率分布。我们已经看到了这种方法的弊端。

Karl Pearson, coming before Kolmogorov, believed that probability distributions were observable by just collecting a lot of data. We have seen the problems with that approach.

威廉·S·戈塞特试图描述一个设计实验的事件空间。他认为事件空间是该实验所有可能结果的集合。这或许在理论上令人满意，但却毫无用处。我们需要足够精确地描述实验结果的概率分布，以便计算进行统计分析所需的概率。那么，如何从“所有可能结果的集合”这一模糊概念中推导出具体的概率分布呢？

William S. Gosset attempted to describe the space of events for a designed experiment. He said it was the set of all possible outcomes of that experiment. This may be intellectually satisfying, but it is useless. It is necessary to describe the probability distribution of outcomes of the experiment in sufficient exactitude so we can calculate the probabilities needed to do statistical analysis. How does one derive a particular probability distribution from the vague idea of the set of all possible outcomes?

费舍尔最初同意戈塞特的观点，但后来他提出了一个更完善的定义。在他的实验设计中，处理方式被随机分配给实验单元。如果我们想比较两种治疗肥胖大鼠动脉硬化的方法，我们会随机将A处理分配给一部分大鼠，将B处理分配给其余大鼠。实验进行后，我们观察结果。假设两种处理方式具有相同的潜在效果。由于动物是随机分配到不同处理方式的，任何其他分配方式都会产生类似的结果。处理方式的随机标签是无关紧要的，可以在动物之间互换——只要处理方式具有相同的效果。因此，对费舍尔来说，事件空间是所有可能的随机分配的集合。这是一个有限的事件集合，所有事件发生的概率均等。我们可以计算在零假设（即两种处理方式具有相同效果）下结果的概率分布。这被称为“置换检验”。费舍尔提出置换检验时，计算所有可能的随机分配是不可能的。费舍尔证明，他的方差分析公式能够很好地近似正确的置换检验。

R. A. Fisher first agreed with Gosset, but then he developed a much better definition. In his experimental designs, treatments are assigned to units of experimentation at random. If we wish to compare two treatments for hardening of the arteries in obese rats, we randomly assign treatment A to some rats and treatment B to the rest. The study is run, and we observe the results. Suppose that both treatments have the same underlying effect. Since the animals were assigned to treatment at random, any other assignment would have produced similar results. The random labels of treatment are irrelevant tags that can be switched among animals—as long as the treatments have the same effect. Thus, for Fisher, the space of events is the set of all possible random assignments that could have been made. This is a finite set of events, all of them equally probable. It is possible to compute the probability distribution of the outcome under the null hypothesis that the treatments have the same effect. This is called the “permutation test.” When Fisher proposed it, the counting of all possible random assignments was impossible. Fisher proved that his formulas for analysis of variance provided good approximations to the correct permutation test.

那是在高速计算机出现之前的事了。如今，我们可以进行置换检验（计算机在进行简单的算术运算时毫不费力），不再需要费舍尔的方差分析公式。多年来证明的许多精妙的数理统计定理也不再适用。只要数据来自随机对照实验，所有显著性检验都可以在计算机上通过置换检验完成。

That was before the day of the high-speed computer. Now it is possible to run permutation tests (the computer is tireless when it comes to doing simple arithmetic), and Fisher’s formulas for analysis of variance are not needed. Nor are many of the clever theorems of mathematical statistics that were proved over the years. All significance tests can be run with permutation tests on the computer, as long as the data result from a randomized controlled experiment.

当对观察性数据进行显著性检验时，这是不可能的。这正是费舍尔反对吸烟与健康研究的主要原因。这些研究的作者使用统计显著性检验来证明他们的论点。费舍尔认为，除非与随机实验结合使用，否则统计显著性检验是不恰当的。美国法院通常以统计显著性检验为依据来判决歧视案件。美国最高法院裁定，这是确定是否存在因性别或种族歧视造成的差别影响的可接受方法。费舍尔肯定会强烈反对。20世纪80年代末，美国国家科学院资助了一项关于在法庭上使用统计方法作为证据的研究。该研究委员会由卡内基梅隆大学的斯蒂芬·芬伯格和明尼苏达大学的塞缪尔·克里斯洛夫担任主席，并于1988年发布了报告。该报告中的许多论文都批评了在歧视案件中使用假设检验的做法，其论点与费舍尔反对吸烟致癌证据时的论点类似。如果最高法院想要批准诉讼中的重要性检验，就应该明确产生这些概率的事件空间。

When a significance test is applied to observational data, this is not possible. This is a major reason Fisher objected to the studies of smoking and health. The authors were using statistical significance tests to prove their case. To Fisher, statistical significance tests are inappropriate unless they are run in conjunction with randomized experiments. Discrimination cases in American courts are routinely decided on the basis of statistical significance tests. The U.S. Supreme Court has ruled that this is an acceptable way of determining if there was a disparate impact due to sexual or racial discrimination. Fisher would have objected loudly. In the late 1980s, the U.S. National Academy of Sciences sponsored a study of the use of statistical methods as evidence in courts. Chaired by Stephen Fienberg of Carnegie Mellon University and Samuel Krislov of the University of Minnesota, the study committee issued its report in 1988. Many of the papers included in that report criticized the use of hypothesis tests in discrimination cases, with arguments similar to those used by Fisher when he objected to the proof that smoking caused cancer. If the Supreme Court wants to approve significance tests in litigation, it should identify the space of events that generate the probabilities.

解决柯尔莫哥洛夫事件空间问题的第二个方案出现在抽样调查理论中。当我们想要从一个总体中抽取一个随机样本来确定其某些特征时，我们会明确地确定要调查的总体，建立一种抽样方法，并根据该方法进行随机抽样。结论存在不确定性，我们可以应用统计方法来量化这种不确定性。这种不确定性源于我们处理的是总体的一个样本。被调查的总体的真实值，例如支持总统政策的美国选民的真实比例，是固定的，只是我们不知道而已。使我们能够使用统计方法的事件空间是所有可能被选中的随机样本的集合。同样，这是一个有限的集合，其概率分布是可以计算的。概率在抽样调查中的实际意义已得到充分证实。

A second solution to the problem of finding Kolmogorov’s space of events occurs in sample survey theory. When we wish to take a random sample of a population to determine something about it, we identify exactly the population of people to be examined, establish a method of selection, and sample at random according to that method. There is uncertainty in the conclusions, and we can apply statistical methods to quantify that uncertainty. The uncertainty is due to the fact that we are dealing with a sample of the populace. The true values of the universe being examined, such as the true percentage of American voters who approve of the president’s policies, are fixed. They are just not known. The space of events that enables us to use statistical methods is the set of all possible random samples that might have been chosen. Again, this is a finite set, and its probability distribution can be calculated. The real-life meaning of probability is well established for sample surveys.

在天文学、社会学、流行病学、法学或天气预报等领域，统计方法何时应用于观测研究尚无定论。这些领域出现的争议往往源于不同的数学模型会得出不同的结论。如果我们无法确定产生所计算概率的事件空间，那么任何模型的有效性都与其他模型无异。正如许多法庭案例所示，两位统计学专家即使使用相同的数据，也可能对数据分析结果产生分歧。随着统计模型越来越多地被政府和倡导团体用于观测研究以辅助社会决策，这种无法明确推导出概率的根本性缺陷将使人们对这些方法的实用性产生质疑。

It is not well established when statistical methods are used for observational studies in astronomy, sociology, epidemiology, law, or weather forecasting. The disputes that arise in these areas are often based on the fact that different mathematical models will give rise to different conclusions. If we cannot identify the space of events that generate the probabilities being calculated, then one model is no more valid than another. As has been shown in many court cases, two expert statisticians working with the same data can disagree about the analyses of those data. As statistical models are used more and more for observational studies to assist in social decisions by government and advocacy groups, this fundamental failure to be able to derive probabilities without ambiguity will cast doubt on the usefulness of these methods.

人们真的理解概率吗？

DO PEOPLE REALLY UNDERSTAND PROBABILITY?

关于概率在现实生活中的意义，一种解决方案是“个人概率”的概念。美国的L.J.萨维奇（人称“吉米”）和意大利的布鲁诺·德·菲内蒂是这一观点的主要倡导者。萨维奇在其1954年出版的《统计学基础》一书中对这一观点进行了最详尽的阐述。在这种观点看来，概率是一个普遍存在的概念。人们自然而然地运用概率来指导自己的生活。在进行一项活动之前，人们会凭直觉判断各种可能结果的概率。例如，如果人们认为危险的概率过大，就会避免采取该行动。对萨维奇和德·菲内蒂而言，概率是一个普遍的概念，它无需与柯尔莫哥洛夫的数学概率联系起来。我们只需要建立一些通用的规则，使个人概率具有内在一致性。为此，我们只需假设人们在判断事件概率时不会出现矛盾。萨维奇正是基于这一假设推导出了内部一致性的规则。

One solution to the question of the real-life meaning of probability has been the concept of “personal probability.” L. J. (“Jimmie”) Savage of the United States and Bruno de Finetti of Italy were the foremost proponents of this view. This position was best presented in Savage’s 1954 book, The Foundations of Statistics. In this view, probability is a widely held concept. People naturally govern their lives using probability. Before entering on a venture, people intuitively decide on the probability of possible outcomes. If the probability of danger, for instance, is thought to be too great, a person will avoid that action. To Savage and de Finetti, probability is a common concept. It does not need to be connected with Kolmogorov’s mathematical probability. All we need do is establish general rules for making personal probability coherent. To do this, we need only to assume that people will not be inconsistent when judging the probability of events. Savage derived rules for internal coherence based upon that assumption.

根据萨维奇-德·菲内蒂的方法，个人概率对每个人而言都是独一无二的。完全有可能一个人认为下雨的概率是95%，而另一个人基于相同的数据却认为下雨的概率是72%。萨维奇和德·菲内蒂运用贝叶斯定理证明，如果两个拥有一致个人概率的人面对一系列相同的数据，他们最终会得出相同的概率估计值。这是一个令人满意的结论。他们似乎在说：人虽不同，但都是理性的。只要有足够的数据，即使最初意见相左，这些理性的人最终也会达成一致。

Under the Savage-de Finetti approach, personal probability is unique to each person. It is perfectly possible that one person will decide that the probability of rain is 95 percent and that another will decide that it is 72 percent—based on their both observing the same data. Using Bayes’s theorem, Savage and de Finetti were able to show that two people with coherent personal probabilities will converge to the same final estimates of probability if faced with a sequence of the same data. This is a satisfying conclusion. People are different but reasonable, they seemed to say. Given enough data, such reasonable people will agree in the end, even if they disagreed to begin with.

约翰·梅纳德·凯恩斯在其1921年出版的博士论文《概率论》中，对个人概率提出了不同的见解。在凯恩斯看来，概率是所有受过特定文化教育的人在特定情况下都会采用的不确定性度量。概率是个人文化的产物，而不仅仅是个人的直觉。如果我们试图在72%的概率和68%的概率之间做出选择，这种方法就难以成立。普遍的文化共识永远无法达到如此精确的程度。凯恩斯指出，在决策过程中，我们很少（甚至几乎不需要）知道某个事件的确切数值概率。通常，能够对事件进行排序就足够了。根据凯恩斯的观点，我们可以根据“明天下雨的概率比下冰雹的概率更大”或者“下雨的概率是下冰雹的概率的两倍”这样的信息做出决策。凯恩斯指出，概率可以是一种偏序关系。我们不必将所有事物都与其他事物进行比较。可以忽略洋基队能否赢得联赛冠军与明天是否会下雨之间的概率关系。

John Maynard Keynes in his doctoral thesis, published in 1921 as A Treatise on Probability, thought of personal probability as something else. To Keynes, probability was the measure of uncertainty that all people with a given cultural education would apply to a given situation. Probability was the result of one’s culture, not just of one’s inner, gut feeling. This approach is difficult to support if we are trying to decide between a probability of 72 percent and one of 68 percent. General cultural agreement could never reach such a degree of precision. Keynes pointed out that for decision making we seldom, if ever, need to know the exact numerical probability of some event. It is usually sufficient to be able to order events. According to Keynes, decisions can be made from knowing that it is more probable that it will rain tomorrow than that it will hail, or that it is twice as probable that it will rain as that it will hail. Keynes points out that probability can be a partial ordering. One does not have to compare everything with everything else. One can ignore the probability relationships between whether the Yankees will win the pennant and whether it will rain tomorrow.

由此可见，概率意义问题的两种解决方案都基于人类普遍渴望量化不确定性，或者至少以粗略的方式进行量化。在《论概率》中，凯恩斯为他个人概率的偏序关系构建了一个正式的数学结构。这项工作完成于柯尔莫戈罗夫奠定数学概率基础之前，并且凯恩斯并未尝试将他的公式与柯尔莫戈罗夫的工作联系起来。凯恩斯声称，他对概率的定义与1921年代表概率论的数学计数公式集不同。为了使凯恩斯的概率理论能够被使用，引用这些概率的人仍然需要满足萨维奇的一致性标准。

In this way, two of the solutions to the problem of the meaning of probability rest on the general human desire to quantify uncertainty, or at least to do so in a rough fashion. In his Treatise, Keynes works out a formal mathematical structure for his partial ordering of personal probability. He did this work before Kolmogorov laid the foundations for mathematical probability, and there is no attempt to link his formulas to Kolmogorov’s work. Keynes claimed that his definition of probability was different from the set of mathematical counting formulas that represented the mathematics of probability in 1921. For Keynes’s probabilities to be usable, the person who invokes them would still have to meet Savage’s criteria for coherence.

这种概率观或许能为运用统计模型进行决策奠定基础。这种观点认为，概率并非基于事件空间，而是由相关人员的个人感受所生成的概率数值。随后，耶路撒冷希伯来大学的心理学家丹尼尔·卡尼曼和阿莫斯·特沃斯基开始了对个人概率心理学的研究。

This makes for a view of probability that might provide the foundations for decision making with statistical models. This is the view that probability is not based on a space of events but that probabilities as numbers are generated out of the personal feelings of the people involved. Then psychologists Daniel Kahneman and Amos Tversky of Hebrew University in Jerusalem began their investigations of the psychology of personal probability.

在20世纪70年代和80年代，卡尼曼和特沃斯基研究了人们如何解读概率。他们的研究成果总结在他们与P. Slovic合编的著作《不确定性下的判断：启发式和偏差》中。他们向大学生、大学教师和普通民众展示了一系列概率情境。他们发现，没有人符合萨维奇提出的连贯性标准。相反，他们发现大多数人甚至无法对不同的概率数值的含义保持一致的理解。他们所能发现的最好情况是，人们能够对“50:50”和“几乎肯定”的含义保持一致的理解。从卡尼曼和特沃斯基的研究中，我们不得不得出结论：天气预报员试图区分90%的降雨概率和75%的降雨概率，但实际上他们无法真正分辨二者的区别。同样，收听天气预报的听众也无法对这种区别的含义保持一致的理解。

Through the 1970s and 1980s, Kahneman and Tversky investigated the way in which individuals interpret probability. Their work was summed up in their book (coedited by P. Slovic) Judgment Under Uncertainty: Heuristics and Biases. They presented a series of probabilistic scenarios to college students, college faculty, and ordinary citizens. They found no one who met Savage’s criteria for coherence. They found, instead, that most people did not have the ability to keep even a consistent view of what different numerical probabilities meant. The best they could find was that people could keep a consistent sense of the meaning of 50:50 and the meaning of “almost certain.” From Kahneman and Tversky’s work, we have to conclude that the weather forecaster who tries to distinguish between a 90 percent probability of rain and a 75 percent probability of rain cannot really tell the difference. Nor do any of the listeners to their forecast have a consistent view of what that difference means.

1974年，特沃斯基在英国皇家统计学会的一次会议上发表了这些研究成果。会后，斯坦福大学的帕特里克·苏佩斯提出了一个简单的概率模型，该模型既符合柯尔莫哥洛夫的公理，也与卡尼曼和特沃斯基的研究结果相符。这意味着使用该模型的人在计算个人概率时会保持逻辑一致。在苏佩斯的模型中，只有五个概率：

In 1974, Tversky presented these results at a meeting of the Royal Statistical Society. In the discussion afterward, Patrick Suppes of Stanford University proposed a simple probability model that met Kolmogorov’s axioms and that also mimicked what Kahneman and Tversky had found. This means that people who used this model would be coherent in their personal probabilities. In Suppes’s model, there are only five probabilities:

确实如此

可能性大于

极有可能

可能性较小

肯定是假的

surely true

more probable than not

as probable as not

less probable than not

surely false

这导致了一种乏味的数学理论。从这个模型中只能推导出大约六个定理，而且它们的证明几乎都是不言自明的。如果卡尼曼和特沃斯基的观点正确，那么唯一有用的个人概率模型根本无法为精彩的数学抽象提供任何素材，而且它只能产生最有限的统计模型。如果苏佩斯的模型实际上是唯一符合个人概率的模型，那么许多常用的统计分析技术就毫无用处，因为它们只能产生低于人类感知水平的区分。

This leads to an uninteresting mathematical theory. Only about a half-dozen theorems can be derived from this model, and their proofs are almost self-evident. If Kahneman and Tversky are right, the only useful version of personal probability provides no grist at all for the wonderful abstractions of mathematics, and it generates the most limited versions of statistical models. If Suppes’s model is, in fact, the only one that fits personal probability, many of the techniques of statistical analysis that are standard practice are useless, since they only serve to produce distinctions below the level of human perception.

概率真的有必要吗？

IS PROBABILITY REALLY NECESSARY?

统计学革命背后的基本思想是，科学的本质是数字分布，而这些分布可以用参数来描述。将这一概念融入概率论并处理概率分布在数学上是十分便捷的。通过将数字分布视为概率论数学理论的组成部分，我们可以建立这些参数估计量的最优准则，并解决在用数据描述分布时出现的数学问题。由于概率似乎是分布概念的内在组成部分，因此人们投入了大量精力来使人们理解概率，试图将概率的数学概念与现实生活联系起来，并运用条件概率的工具来解释科学实验和观察的结果。

The basic idea behind the statistical revolution is that the real things of science are distributions of numbers, which can be described by parameters. It is mathematically convenient to embed that concept into probability theory and deal with probability distributions. By considering the distributions of numbers as elements from the mathematical theory of probability, it is possible to establish optimum criteria for estimators of those parameters and to deal with the mathematical problems that arise when data are used to describe the distributions. Because probability seems inherent in the concept of a distribution, much effort has been spent on getting people to understand probability, trying to link the mathematical idea of probability to real life, and using the tools of conditional probability to interpret the results of scientific experiments and observations.

分布的概念可以存在于概率论之外。事实上，非正常分布（之所以说是非正常分布，是因为它们不满足概率分布的所有要求）已经在量子力学和一些贝叶斯方法中得到应用。排队论的发展，使得队列中平均到达间隔时间等于平均服务时间的情况，导致了等待时间的非正常分布。这是一个将概率论的数学原理应用于现实生活，却导致我们超出概率分布范畴的例子。

The idea of distribution can exist outside of probability theory. In fact, improper distributions (which are improper because they do not meet all the requirements of a probability distribution) are already being used in quantum mechanics and in some Bayesian techniques. The development of queuing theory, a situation where the average time between arrivals at the queue equals the average service time in the queue, leads to an improper distribution for the amount of time someone entering the queue will have to wait. Here is a case where applying the mathematics of probability theory to a real-life situation leads us out of the set of probability distributions.

二十一世纪将会发生什么？

WHAT WILL HAPPEN IN THE TWENTY-FIRST CENTURY?

柯尔莫戈罗夫最后的洞见在于用有限符号序列的性质来描述概率，其中信息论并非概率计算的结果，而是概率本身的起源。或许有人会接过他留下的火炬，发展出一种新的分布理论，将数字计算机的本质融入其哲学基础之中。

Kolmogorov’s final insight was to describe probability in terms of the properties of finite sequences of symbols, where information theory is not the outcome of probabilistic calculations but the progenitor of probability itself. Perhaps someone will pick up the torch where he left it and develop a new theory of distributions where the very nature of the digital computer is brought into the philosophical foundations.

谁知道在何处，或许还潜藏着另一个R.A. Fisher，在主流科学的边缘地带默默耕耘，即将以前所未有的洞见和理念横空出世？或许，在中国某个偏远的地方，又一个吕西安·勒卡姆出生在一个不识字的农户家庭；又或许，在北非，另一个乔治·博克斯中学毕业后便辍学，如今正以机械师的身份，自学探索。或许，另一个格特鲁德·考克斯即将放弃成为传教士的梦想，转而投身于科学和数学的奥秘；又或许，另一个威廉·S·戈塞特正试图找到解决啤酒酿造难题的方法；又或许，另一个内曼或皮特曼正在印度某个偏僻的省立学院任教，进行着深刻的思考。谁又能预知下一个伟大的发现会来自何方呢？

Who knows where there may be another R. A. Fisher, working on the fringes of established science, who will soon burst upon the scene with insights and ideas that have never been thought before? Perhaps, somewhere in the center of China, another Lucien Le Cam has been born to an illiterate farm family; or in North Africa, another George Box who stopped his formal education after secondary school may now be working as a mechanic, exploring and learning on his own. Perhaps another Gertrude Cox will soon give up her hopes of being a missionary and become intrigued with the puzzles of science and mathematics; or another William S. Gossett is trying to find a way to solve a problem in the brewing of beer; or another Neyman or Pitman is teaching in some obscure provincial college in India and thinking deep thoughts. Who knows from where the next great discovery will come?

进入二十一世纪，科学领域的统计学革命取得了辉煌的胜利。它几乎彻底击败了决定论，只在少数几个鲜为人知的角落里苟延残喘。统计学的应用如此广泛，以至于其基本假设已经成为西方世界不言而喻的流行文化的一部分。然而，它的胜利并非稳固，而是建立在坚实的基础之上。在未来某个隐秘的角落，另一场科学革命正蓄势待发，准备推翻它，而那些即将开启这场革命的人们，或许就生活在我们身边。

As we enter the twenty-first century, the statistical revolution in science stands triumphant. It has vanquished determinism from all but a few obscure corners of science. It has become so widely used that its underlying assumptions have become part of the unspoken popular culture of the Western world. It stands triumphant on feet of clay. Somewhere, in the hidden corners of the future, another scientific revolution is waiting to overthrow it, and the men and women who will create that revolution may already be living among us.

后记

AFTERWORD

B在撰写本书的过程中，我将对该领域做出贡献的男性和女性分为两类：我提及的和我没有提及的。第一类人可能会反对我只描述了他们工作的一小部分。第二类人可能会反对我完全没有提及他们的工作。出于对两类人感受的尊重，我需要解释我选择描述哪些内容、省略哪些内容的方法。

By writing this book, I have divided the women and men who have contributed to the field into two groups: those whom I mention and those whom I do not mention. The first group may object to the fact that I have tended to describe only a small part of their work. The second group may object that I have mentioned nothing at all about their work. A decent respect for the feelings of both groups requires that I explain my methods of choosing what to describe and what to leave out.

第一类遗漏源于现代科学的浩瀚无垠，任何个人都不可能了解其所有分支。因此，有些研究领域运用了统计方法，但我充其量也只是略知一二。20世纪70年代初，我曾就计算机在医学诊断中的应用进行文献检索，发现了三个独立的研究方向。在这些方向内部，研究人员彼此之间相互引用，并发表在同一期刊上。没有任何迹象表明，这些研究方向的科学家对其他方向的研究工作有任何了解。而这仅仅是在相对较小的医学领域。在更广泛的科学领域，可能存在着许多运用统计方法并在期刊上发表文章的研究团队，但我却闻所未闻。我对统计革命的了解主要来源于我对主流数理统计学著作的阅读。有些科学家，比如发展模糊集理论的工程师，他们不阅读或不向我阅读的期刊投稿，他们可能正在做着杰出的工作，但如果他们没有按照我所了解的科学/数学传统发表文章，我就无法将他们纳入其中。

The first group of omissions is due to the fact that modern science has become too extensive for any one person to know about all its ramifications. As a result, there are areas of research in which statistical methods have been used but of which I am, at best, barely aware. In the early 1970s, I ran a literature search on the use of computers in medical diagnoses. I found three independent traditions. Within any one of them, the workers all referred to one another and published in the same journals. There was no indication that any of the scientists in each of these groups had the slightest knowledge of the work being done by the other groups. This was within the relatively small world of medicine. In the wider range of science in general, there are probably groups using statistical methods and publishing in journals that I have never heard of. My knowledge of the statistical revolution results from my reading in the mainstream of mathematical statistics. Scientists who do not read or contribute to the journals I read, like the engineers developing fuzzy set theory, may be doing notable work, but if they do not publish in the scientific/mathematical tradition of which I am aware, I could not include them.

即使是我了解的内容，也难免有所遗漏。我并非有意撰写一部统计方法发展史的全集。由于本书面向几乎没有数学基础的读者，我必须选择能够用文字解释的例子，而不能使用数学符号。这进一步限制了我可以描述的人物范围。此外，我还希望保持贯穿全书的连贯性。如果可以使用数学符号，我就可以展示众多主题之间的联系。但如果没有这些符号，本书很容易沦为一堆看似毫无关联的想法。本书需要一条相对系统化的主题路径。我选择的这条路径，或许并非其他人会选择的路径，它带领读者穿越二十世纪统计学的重重迷雾。一旦选择了这条路径，就不得不忽略统计学的许多其他方面，即便它们对我来说也颇为有趣。

There are omissions even of material I do know about. I did not set out to write a comprehensive history of the development of statistical methodology. Since this book is aimed at readers with little or no mathematical training, I had to choose examples that I could explain in words, without the use of mathematical symbols. This further limited the choice of individuals whose work I could describe. I also wanted to keep a sense of connection running through the book. If I had had the use of mathematical notation, I could have shown the relationships among a large collection of topics. But without that notation, the book could easily have degenerated into a collection of ideas that do not seem to have any connection. This book needed a path of somewhat organized topics. The path I have chosen through the immense complications of twentieth-century statistics may not be the path that others would choose. Once chosen, it forced me to ignore many other aspects of statistics, as interesting as they might be to me.

我书中未收录某些人的作品，并不意味着该人的作品不重要，甚至也不意味着我认为它不重要。这仅仅意味着，随着本书结构的不断完善，我最终未能找到合适的方式将该人的作品纳入其中。

The fact that I have left someone out of my book does not mean that that person’s work is unimportant, or even that I think it unimportant. It only means that I could not find a way to include that work in this book as its structure developed.

我希望本书能启发一些读者更深入地研究统计学革命。我甚至希望有读者能因此受到启发，投身统计学研究领域。在参考书目中，我精选了一些我认为非数学专业人士也能理解的书籍和文章。在这些作品中，其他统计学家试图阐释统计学吸引他们的原因。那些想要进一步了解统计学革命的读者不妨阅读这些著作。

I hope some readers will be inspired by this book to look into the statistical revolution more thoroughly. It is my hope that a reader may even be inspired to study the subject and join the world of statistical research. In the bibliography, I have isolated a small group of books and articles that I think are accessible to the nonmathematician. In these works, other statisticians have tried to explain what excites them about statistics. Those readers who would like to examine the statistical revolution further will want to read some of these.

我要感谢WH Freeman出版社所有为本书最终定稿做出卓越贡献的人员。我衷心感谢Don Gecewicz先生细致入微的事实核查和编辑工作；感谢Eleanor Wedge和Vivien Weiss女士的最终校对（以及进一步的事实核查）；感谢Patrick Farace先生慧眼识珠，看到了本书的潜在价值；还要感谢Victoria Tomaselli、Bill Page、Karen Barr、Meg Kuhta和Julia DeRosa女士在本书的艺术创作和制作方面所做出的贡献。

I wish to acknowledge the efforts of those at W. H. Freeman who were instrumental in producing the final polished version of this book. I am indebted to Don Gecewicz for a thorough job of fact checking and editing; to Eleanor Wedge and Vivien Weiss for the final copyediting (and further fact checking); to Patrick Farace who saw the potential value of this book; and to Victoria Tomaselli, Bill Page, Karen Barr, Meg Kuhta, and Julia DeRosa for the artistic and production components of the effort.
